```json
						}
					],
					"protocolProfileBehavior": {
						"disabledSystemHeaders": {
							"content-type": true
						}
					},
					"request": {
						"method": "DELETE",
						"header": [],
						"url": {
							"raw": "{{baseUrl}}/whisper-manage-callback?webhook_name={{webhookName}}",
							"host": [
								"{{baseUrl}}"
							],
							"path": [
								"whisper-manage-callback"
							],
							"query": [
								{
									"key": "webhook_name",
									"value": "{{webhookName}}",
									"description": "The name of the webhook."
								}
							]
						}
					},
					"response": []
				}
			],
			"description": "# Webhooks\n\nLLMWhisperer from V2 onwards supports webhooks. You can now register a webhook and use it to receive the processed document.\n\n### Requirements[​](https://docs.unstract.com/llmwhisperer/llm_whisperer/llm_whisperer_webhooks/#requirements)\n\n- A publicly accessible URL to receive the webhook. (Can be internal in on-prem installations)\n    \n- The URL must be able to receive POST requests.\n    \n- The URL must be able to handle the payload sent by the webhook.\n    \n- Only Bearer token authentication is supported for webhooks.\n    \n- The webhook must return a 200 status code to acknowledge receipt of the payload.\n    \n- A maximum of 3 retries will be made in case of a failure. (Can be changed in on-prem installations)\n    \n\n### Payload[​](https://docs.unstract.com/llmwhisperer/llm_whisperer/llm_whisperer_webhooks/#payload)\n\nThe payload sent to the webhook will be a JSON object with the following structure:\n\n``` markup\n{\n   \"payload_status\":{\n      \"status\":\"success\",\n      \"# The status of the payload.\"\"message\":\"\"\"# Message in case of error\"\"whisper_hash\":\"<WHISPER_HASH>\"\"# Whisper hash of intiated request.\"\n   },\n   \"line_metadata\":[\n      \n   ],\n   \"# Refer to retrieve API for details\"\"confidence_metadata\":[\n      \n   ],\n   \"# Refer to retrieve API for details\"\"result_text\":\"extracted_text\",\n   \"# The extracted text\"\"metadata\":{\n      \n   }\"# Refer to retrieve API for details\"\n}\n\n ```\n\n### Setting up a webhook[​](https://docs.unstract.com/llmwhisperer/llm_whisperer/llm_whisperer_webhooks/#setting-up-a-webhook)\n\nTo set up a webhook, you need to provide the following details:\n\n- `url` (str, required): The URL of the webhook to call after the document is processed.\n    \n- `auth_token` (str, required): The Bearer token to use for authentication. Note: Pass the token alone without the 'Bearer' keyword.\n    \n- `webhook_name` (str, required): The name of the webhook to register.\n    \n\nOnce webhook submission successful the `webhook_name` value will automatically assigned to `webhookName` variable."
		},
		{
			"name": "Usage",
			"item": [
				{
					"name": "Check the usage metrics of your LLMWhisperer account.",
					"request": {
						"method": "GET",
						"header": [],
						"url": {
							"raw": "{{baseUrl}}/get-usage-info",
							"host": [
								"{{baseUrl}}"
							],
							"path": [
								"get-usage-info"
							]
						},
						"description": "[https://docs.unstract.com/llmwhisperer/llm_whisperer/apis/llm_whisperer_usage_api/](https://docs.unstract.com/llmwhisperer/llm_whisperer/apis/llm_whisperer_usage_api/)"
					},
					"response": []
				},
				{
					"name": "Check the usage stats of your LLMWhisperer account based on the tag provided.",
					"request": {
						"method": "GET",
						"header": [],
						"url": {
							"raw": "{{baseUrl}}/usage?tag=default",
							"host": [
								"{{baseUrl}}"
							],
							"path": [
								"usage"
							],
							"query": [
								{
									"key": "tag",
									"value": "default",
									"description": "The tag with user need to filter the usage data. i.e credit, invoice, statement"
								},
								{
									"key": "from_date",
									"value": "<YYYY-MM-DD>",
									"description": "Format required is YYYY-MM-DD\n",
									"disabled": true
								},
								{
									"key": "to_date",
									"value": "<YYYY-MM-DD>",
									"description": "Format required is YYYY-MM-DD\n",
									"disabled": true
								}
							]
						},
						"description": "[https://docs.unstract.com/llmwhisperer/llm_whisperer/apis/llm_whisperer_usage_stats/](https://docs.unstract.com/llmwhisperer/llm_whisperer/apis/llm_whisperer_usage_stats/)"
					},
					"response": []
				}
			],
			"description": "### For Paid Plans[​](https://docs.unstract.com/llmwhisperer/llm_whisperer/apis/llm_whisperer_usage_api/#for-paid-plans)\n\n``` json\n{\n   \"subscription_plan\":\"<Plan Name>\",\n   \"monthly_quota\":\"<Monthly Quota>\",\n   \"current_page_count\":\"<Current Page Count>\",\n   \"current_page_count_native_text\":\"<For Native Text Mode>\",\n   \"current_page_count_low_cost\":\"<For Low Cost Mode>\",\n   \"current_page_count_high_quality\":\"<For High Quality Mode>\",\n   \"current_page_count_form\":\"<For Form Mode>\",\n   \"daily_quota\":-1,\n   \"overage_page_count\":\"<Overage Page Count>\",\n   \"today_page_count\":\"<Page Count For Day>\"\n}\n\n ```\n\n`daily_quota` will be `-1` for paid plans. There is no daily quota for paid plans.\n\n### For Free Plan[​](https://docs.unstract.com/llmwhisperer/llm_whisperer/apis/llm_whisperer_usage_api/#for-free-plan)\n\n``` json\n{\n   \"subscription_plan\":\"<Plan Name>\",\n   \"daily_quota\":\"<Daily Quota>\",\n   \"today_page_count\":\"<Page Count For Day>\",\n   \"monthly_quota\":-1,\n   \"current_page_count\":-1,\n   \"overage_page_count\":-1\n}\n\n ```\n\nFields marked `-1` for free plans indicate that there's no quota or it doesn't apply to these plans.\n\n# **Usage Stats**  \nExample `200` Response[​](https://docs.unstract.com/llmwhisperer/llm_whisperer/apis/llm_whisperer_usage_stats/#example-200-response)\n\n```\n{\n    \"end_date\": <end date>,\n    \"start_date\": <start date>,\n    \"subscription_id\": <susbscription-id>,\n    \"tag\": \"credit\",\n    \"usage\": [       \n        {\n            \"pages_processed\": <count>,\n            \"service_type\": \"form\"\n        },\n        {\n            \"pages_processed\": <count>,\n            \"service_type\": <mode>\n        }\n    ]    \n}\n\n ```"
		}
	],
	"auth": {
		"type": "apikey",
		"apikey": [
			{
				"key": "key",
				"value": "unstract-key",
				"type": "string"
			},
			{
				"key": "value",
				"value": "{{apiKey}}",
				"type": "string"
			}
		]
	},
	"event": [
		{
			"listen": "prerequest",
			"script": {
				"type": "text/javascript",
				"exec": [
					"",
					""
				]
			}
		},
		{
			"listen": "test",
			"script": {
				"type": "text/javascript",
				"exec": [
					""
				]
			}
		}
	],
	"variable": [
		{
			"key": "baseUrl",
			"value": "https://llmwhisperer-api.us-central.unstract.com/api/v2"
		},
		{
			"key": "apiKey",
			"value": "<API_KEY>"
		}
	]
}

```

# Human in the Loop (HITL) | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Enterprise Feature

This feature is available in [Unstract Cloud Edition](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/cloud_edition/)
 and [Unstract On-Premise Edition](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/on_prem_edition/)
.

Human in the Loop (HITL) is a feature that allows humans to review, verify and if required, modify the output of your extraction projects."

# Introduction to the Unstract Platform | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/unstract_intro/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

![img Unstract Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio-1d330fad5f477c34e710771d5f9c5a6b.png)

Unstract is an open source, no-code platform that helps you automate complex business processes that involve long documents and thus a human in the loop. Unstract is able to go beyond what current IDP (Intelligent Document Processing) and RPA (Robotic Process Automation) systems are capable of achieving by leveraging the latest in AI: Large Language Models.

The easiest way to get to know the Unstract Platform is to build a project to extract structured information from unstructured documents. Going through this exercise should give you a very good grasp of Unstract's capabilities.

But, before you begin, let's set up come accounts you'll need to start using the platform.

---

# Enterprise SSO — Azure AD | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

This guide walks through configuring Azure AD (Microsoft Entra ID) as an identity provider for Unstract using OpenID Connect (OIDC). Unstract uses [Auth0 as an identity broker](https://auth0.com/docs/authenticate/identity-providers/enterprise-identity-providers/azure-active-directory-native)
, which connects to your Azure AD tenant for enterprise SSO.

Highlight Color Coding

Throughout this document and in the screenshots, highlights are used to indicate items that need to be shared between parties:

*   **Green highlight** — Items the **Customer shares with Unstract** (e.g., Domain, Client ID, Client Secret, Directory Groups)
*   **Yellow highlight** — Items **Unstract shares with Customer** (e.g., Redirect URI)
*   **Red box** — Key value to locate in the Azure portal screenshot

1\. Unstract Configuration to be Used[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#1-unstract-configuration-to-be-used "Direct link to 1-unstract-configuration-to-be-used")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Unstract team will share a redirect URI specific to your deployment type. Use this when registering the application in Azure AD.

| Deployment Type | Redirect URL |
| --- | --- |
| **Enterprise Cloud** | `https://auth.unstract.com/login/callback` |
| **Enterprise On-prem** | `https://zipstack-on-prem.us.auth0.com/login/callback` |

2\. Steps to be Performed by the Customer[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#2-steps-to-be-performed-by-the-customer "Direct link to 2. Steps to be Performed by the Customer")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 2.1 Create App Registration in Azure AD[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#21-create-app-registration-in-azure-ad "Direct link to 2.1 Create App Registration in Azure AD")

**REF**: [Microsoft — Register an application](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/quickstart-register-app)

1.  Go to **Entra ID > App registrations** and select **New registration** (refer [Section 3.1](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#31-register-application)
    ).
2.  Enter a meaningful **Name** for your app. **Users can see this name**, and it can be changed at any time.
3.  Under **Supported account types**, specify who can use the application. We recommend **Accounts in this organizational directory only** for most applications.
4.  Fill in the **redirect URI** field with the redirect URI shared by the **Unstract team** (refer [Section 1](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#1-unstract-configuration-to-be-used)
    ). Select the platform as **Web**.
5.  Click **Register** to submit the App registration.
6.  Once registered, share **Domain**, **Client ID**, **Client Secret** with the Unstract team:
    *   **Domain** (refer [Section 3.2](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#32-find-the-ad-domain)
        )
    *   **Client ID** (refer [Section 3.3](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#33-find-the-client-id)
        )
    *   **Client Secret** (refer [Section 3.4](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#34-create-a-client-secret)
        )

### 2.2 Configure User Directory Groups[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#22-configure-user-directory-groups "Direct link to 2.2 Configure User Directory Groups")

Existing or new **user directory groups** need to be configured and shared with Unstract. These user directory groups are then mapped to Unstract-defined roles in the application.

The following roles are defined in the Unstract application:

*   `unstract_user`
*   `unstract_admin`
*   `unstract_supervisor`
*   `unstract_reviewer`

**Example mapping** of customer Directory Groups to Unstract Roles:

| Unstract Roles | Customer Directory Groups |
| --- | --- |
| `unstract_user` | `customer_unstract_user_dev` |
| `unstract_admin` | `customer_unstract_admin_dev` |
| `unstract_supervisor` | `customer_unstract_supervisor_dev` |
| `unstract_reviewer` | `customer_unstract_reviewer_dev` |

note

*   The customer directory groups to Unstract roles mapping needs to be done for each of the **development**, **test** and **production** environments.
*   If either there are no directory groups associated with a user in a customer environment or the associated directory group is not mapped to an Unstract-side application role, then the **user will be forbidden** to access the application.

### 2.3 Configuration to be Shared with Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#23-configuration-to-be-shared-with-unstract "Direct link to 23-configuration-to-be-shared-with-unstract")

| Config | Where to Obtain |
| --- | --- |
| Domain | See **2.1** step 6 / [Section 3.2](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#32-find-the-ad-domain) |
| Client ID | See **2.1** step 6 / [Section 3.3](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#33-find-the-client-id) |
| Client Secret | See **2.1** step 6 / [Section 3.4](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#34-create-a-client-secret) |
| User directory groups | See **2.2** |

3\. Screenshots for Reference[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#3-screenshots-for-reference "Direct link to 3. Screenshots for Reference")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 3.1 Register Application[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#31-register-application "Direct link to 3.1 Register Application")

Navigate to **Entra ID > App registrations** and select **New registration** to open the registration form shown below.

![Register Application](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_ad_register_app-dcddf9556bb3357b8171480d2e338183.png)

### 3.2 Find the AD Domain[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#32-find-the-ad-domain "Direct link to 3.2 Find the AD Domain")

Navigate to the **Overview** page of your Azure AD tenant. The **Primary domain** field (highlighted with a red box in the screenshot) contains the domain value to share with the Unstract team.

![Find the AD Domain](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_ad_domain-25e61502137e3c8edf2757a2b778aa78.png)

### 3.3 Find the Client ID[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#33-find-the-client-id "Direct link to 3.3 Find the Client ID")

Go to **Entra ID > App registrations** and select your registered application. On the **Overview** page, locate the **Application (client) ID** (highlighted with a red box in the screenshot). Share this value with the Unstract team.

![Find the Client ID](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_ad_client_id-f26b8078375ef72debcbff878cca25b0.png)

### 3.4 Create a Client Secret[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#34-create-a-client-secret "Direct link to 3.4 Create a Client Secret")

Go to **Entra ID > App registrations** and select your registered application. Navigate to **Certificates & secrets** (highlighted with a red box in the screenshot) in the left sidebar.

![Navigate to Client Secret](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_ad_client_secret_link-0e71fd84f309a59acc0722c602a135c5.png)

Under **Client secrets**, click **\+ New client secret**. Add a description and set an expiration period. Click **Add** and copy the secret **Value** (not the Secret ID). Share this value with the Unstract team.

![Create a Client Secret](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_ad_client_secret-48be887c44631adb5859503efadfb33c.png)

warning

The client secret value is only shown once at creation time. Make sure to copy and securely store it before navigating away.

### 3.5 Configure the Redirect URI[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#35-configure-the-redirect-uri "Direct link to 3.5 Configure the Redirect URI")

Go to **Entra ID > App registrations** and select your registered application. Navigate to **Authentication (Preview)** in the left sidebar. Under **Redirect URI configuration**, click **Add Redirect URI**. Select platform type **Web** and enter the redirect URI shared by the Unstract team.

![Configure the Redirect URI](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_ad_redirect_uri-0cfb9ff802e2fb3d7b75881c1c80974e.png)

4\. Auth Architecture[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#4-auth-architecture "Direct link to 4. Auth Architecture")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 4.1 Auth0 as Identity Broker[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#41-auth0-as-identity-broker "Direct link to 4.1 Auth0 as Identity Broker")

Unstract uses Auth0 as an identity broker that sits between the application and your identity provider. Auth0 handles:

*   **Universal Login Page** — Centralized authentication experience
*   **Auth Engine & Token Service** — Token issuance and validation
*   **User Management** — User profile and session management
*   **MFA** — Multi-factor authentication support
*   **Rules/Actions** — Custom authentication logic

Auth0 connects to multiple identity provider types:

| Connection Type | Protocol | Supported Providers |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enterprise IDPs | OIDC/SAML | Azure AD, Okta, PingFed, ADFS |
| Social IDPs | OAuth 2.0 | Google, GitHub, LinkedIn, Microsoft |
| Database Connections | Direct Auth | Auth0 DB, Custom DB, LDAP/AD |

![Auth0 as Identity Broker](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/auth0_broker-caeb6d419d2944ab7d6a2ebba660af2b.png)

### 4.2 Enterprise Connection Flow[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#42-enterprise-connection-flow "Direct link to 4.2 Enterprise Connection Flow")

In a multi-tenant setup, Auth0 Organizations with Home Realm Discovery routes users to their correct identity provider based on their email domain. The flow produces a unified JWT token containing:

    {  "org_id": "org_xxx",  "sub": "auth0|123",  "permissions": [...]}

The application backend services use the `org_id` claim for tenant isolation.

![Enterprise Connection Flow](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/enterprise_flow-013d2b6e05d64582d800e2082655d7c5.png)

*   [1\. Unstract Configuration to be Used](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#1-unstract-configuration-to-be-used)
    
*   [2\. Steps to be Performed by the Customer](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#2-steps-to-be-performed-by-the-customer)
    *   [2.1 Create App Registration in Azure AD](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#21-create-app-registration-in-azure-ad)
        
    *   [2.2 Configure User Directory Groups](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#22-configure-user-directory-groups)
        
    *   [2.3 Configuration to be Shared with Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#23-configuration-to-be-shared-with-unstract)
        
*   [3\. Screenshots for Reference](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#3-screenshots-for-reference)
    *   [3.1 Register Application](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#31-register-application)
        
    *   [3.2 Find the AD Domain](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#32-find-the-ad-domain)
        
    *   [3.3 Find the Client ID](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#33-find-the-client-id)
        
    *   [3.4 Create a Client Secret](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#34-create-a-client-secret)
        
    *   [3.5 Configure the Redirect URI](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#35-configure-the-redirect-uri)
        
*   [4\. Auth Architecture](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#4-auth-architecture)
    *   [4.1 Auth0 as Identity Broker](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#41-auth0-as-identity-broker)
        
    *   [4.2 Enterprise Connection Flow](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/#42-enterprise-connection-flow)

# MCP Server | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Unstract Platform MCP Server[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#unstract-platform-mcp-server "Direct link to Unstract Platform MCP Server")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Unstract has support for MCP server which can be used to integrate the functionality of connecting to an API deployment with a MCP host.

Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#features "Direct link to Features")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Connects to a deployed Unstract API to process files and extract structured data. Handles asynchronous processing and polling for completion.

Tools[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#tools "Direct link to Tools")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`unstract_tool`: Submits a file to the Unstract API, polls for completion, and returns structured extraction results. Handles errors, timeouts, and environment-based configuration. The tool also supports optional paramters to include metadata and metrics.

Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#configuration "Direct link to Configuration")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Getting an API Key[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#getting-an-api-key "Direct link to Getting an API Key")

To use the tool, one needs to set the UNSTRACT\_API\_KEY and API\_BASE\_URL environment variables. For obtaining these, please check the details under [Getting Started](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)

### Usage with Claude Desktop[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#usage-with-claude-desktop "Direct link to Usage with Claude Desktop")

One can integrate the Unstract tool with Claude Desktop by adding the following configuration to the `claude_desktop_config.json` replacing the values of UNSTRACT\_API\_KEY and API\_BASE\_URL with the right values. Also, since the tool will need a file to be processed, the file needs to be mounted in the container. The sample below uses /tmp as the mount point. This can be changed to any other location.

### Docker[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#docker "Direct link to Docker")

    {  "mcpServers": {        "unstract_tool":{            "command": "/usr/local/bin/docker",            "args": [                "run",                "-i",                "--rm",                "-v",                "/tmp:/tmp",                "-e",                "UNSTRACT_API_KEY",                "-e",                "API_BASE_URL",                "unstract/mcp-server",                "unstract"            ],            "env": {                    "UNSTRACT_API_KEY": "",                    "API_BASE_URL": "https://us-central.unstract.com/deployment/api/...../"            }        }    }}

Once the configuration is added, user can use extract\_text as a tool to extract text from the supported documents.

### Sample prompt[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#sample-prompt "Direct link to Sample prompt")

Process the document /tmp/sample-bank\_statement.pdf

This should invoke the tool and get the response from the API deployed at the mentioned location. Optionally, one can request for including metadata and metrics also.

*   [Unstract Platform MCP Server](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#unstract-platform-mcp-server)
    
*   [Features](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#features)
    
*   [Tools](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#tools)
    
*   [Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#configuration)
    *   [Getting an API Key](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#getting-an-api-key)
        
    *   [Usage with Claude Desktop](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#usage-with-claude-desktop)
        
    *   [Docker](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#docker)
        
    *   [Sample prompt](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/mcp/unstract_platform_mcp_server/#sample-prompt)

---

# Getting Started | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

This guide walks you through a practical example of how easily Unstract can help you extract structured data from unstructured documents that have 4 different variants that look very different from each other. We'll do with this minimal effort, leveraging the power of Large Language Models.

Choose an Unstract edition[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/#choose-an-unstract-edition "Direct link to Choose an Unstract edition")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To get started with Unstract, you'll first need to get access to one of the available Unstract editions:

*   [Unstract Cloud Edition](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/cloud_edition/)
     (Easiest to get started with; offers a free trial.)
*   [Unstract Open Source Edition](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/open_source_edition/)
    
*   [Unstract On-Premise Edition](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/on_prem_edition/)
    

The following pages contain common instructions for you to get started with any edition of Unstract. Where there are differences in features, they are clearly pointed out.

Your trial comes with batteries included[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/#your-trial-comes-with-batteries-included "Direct link to Your trial comes with batteries included")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you chose to go the Unstract Cloud route, to have you hit the ground running, when you sign up for Unstract's 14-day free trial, your account comes pre-configured with an LLM, a vector database, an embedding model and LLMWhisperer for text extraction. Of course, you are welcome to [configure additional services](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/index/)
 that are integrated with Unstract if you plan to run a deeper trial.

The Quick Start Exercise[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/#the-quick-start-exercise "Direct link to The Quick Start Exercise")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Let's consider that you want to extract structured data from a bunch of credit card statements from different issuers. We're taking credit card statements as an example since no matter what your background, it's safe to assume that you'll know what a typical credit card statement looks like and what key points of information it might contain, since credit cards are fairly common part of our lives. At the same time, we know that every issuer has their own format and even for the same issuer, the format of the statement can keep changing from time to time. So, it's a pretty decent challenge to use such statements to build our first Unstract project.

![img Credit Card Statement Samples](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/cc_statements-4a0faea8a3eb9b82ade7db0c87bc40bc.png)

Credit card statements are typically emailed to users as PDF documents. Like most unstructured documents, these statements, although most of them consist of the same bits of key information (customer name, customer address, issuer name, statement date, list of spends, etc), they come in wildly different formatting and lengths like we discussed before. It has never been easy to get data from these varied types of statements into a database or into an application in structured form for easy querying, analysis or visualization. The Unstract Platform lets you do this with no code needed by leveraging the power of Large Language Models.

Not only will we build a simple, generic parser for credit card statements, we will also deploy this parser as both an API (to which you can send a PDF statement and get JSON data back) and also as an ETL pipeline (which can structure PDF statements and push data into a data warehouse or database for further analysis).

*   [Choose an Unstract edition](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/#choose-an-unstract-edition)
    
*   [Your trial comes with batteries included](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/#your-trial-comes-with-batteries-included)
    
*   [The Quick Start Exercise](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/#the-quick-start-exercise)

---

# What's needed to get started | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/whats_needed/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

This guide helps you set up your Unstract environment. You have:

*   Purchased an Unstract Cloud subscription after your trial got over.
*   You have an on-prem version of Unstract set up and ready to use.
*   You've set up the open source version of Unstract on your laptop or cloud environment.

Initial set up[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/whats_needed/#initial-set-up "Direct link to Initial set up")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You'll need access to four services to started (Don't worry, these are easy to set up and are available for free to get started!):

*   **LLM or Large Language Model service**: this service takes in raw text, helps reason and structure specific data/fields we care about. We will use OpenAI for this. While this guide walks you through setting up a specific LLM, you can look at [the documentation for all supported LLMs](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/index/)
     to configure specific LLMs.
*   **Embedding Model**: this service assigns special codes to words based on their meaning and helps organize data within a document or data source. This is useful when we need to extract relevant portions—based on a user's query or specific data we need to extract—from large documents. We will use OpenAI for this as well. While this guide walks you through setting up a specific embedding model, you can look at the [documentation for all supported embedding models](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/index/)
     to configure specific ones.
*   **Vector Database**: this service works in conjunction with the embedding model to actually store special representations of documents and retrieve portions we're interested in from them. To put it in simple words, the embedding model contains the logic to organize data and vector databases help in storing and retrieving it. While this guide walks you through setting up a specific vector database, you can look at the [documentation for all supported vector databases](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/index/)
     to configure specific ones. Usually the data gets persisted in the vector database under a table named unstract\_embedding-dimension. Incase of Postgres, this table is called data\_unstract\_embedding-dimension. For example, if you are using an embedding model with dimension 1536, then the nodes get stored under unstract\_1536. For Postgres, this will be data\_unstract\_1536.
*   **Text Extractor**: This service, much like its name implies, extracts text from documents and images, typically PDFs. These PDFs can be native text or composed simply of scanned images. OCR is typically built into these services. However, in the near future, we're also adding the ability to used 3rd party OCR services. While this guide walks you through setting up a specific text extractor, you can look at the [documentation for all supported text extraction services](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/text_extractors/index/)
     to configure specific ones.

*   [Initial set up](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/whats_needed/#initial-set-up)

---

# SinglePass Extraction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/index/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

[📄️ SinglePass Extraction Introduction\
--------------------------------------\
\
This feature is available in Unstract Cloud Edition and Unstract On-Premise Edition.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/single_pass_extraction_intro/)

[📄️ Developing and verifying SinglePass Extraction\
--------------------------------------------------\
\
When developing your prompts in Prompt Studio, you can enable SinglePass Extraction and see how it affects your extractions. Of course, in Prompt Studio, you're only developing prompts and defining the extraction schema. Ultimately, SinglePass Extraction runs as part of a deployed Prompt Studio project as one of the following integration types:](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/developing_and_verifying_singlepass/)

[📄️ Deploying SinglePass Extraction\
-----------------------------------\
\
We discussed earlier in Developing and verifying SinglePass Extraction that while in Prompt Studio, you're only verifying if SinglePass Extraction is doing a good job of the required structured extraction. SinglePass Extraction needs to be deployed as part of API Deployments, ETL Pipelines, Human Quality Review or Task Pipelines workflow. In this section, let's see how, when deploying one of the previously mentioned types of workflows, we can enable SinglePass Extraction.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/singlepass_deployment/)

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Enterprise Feature

 and [Unstract On-Premise Edition](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/on_prem_edition/)
.

SummarizedExtraction is a cost-saving feature that reduces the context sent to the Large Language Model by summarizing the input document based on your prompts.

# Supported File Formats | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/supported_file_types/list_of_file_types_supported/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Here is the list of the file formats supported in Unstract:

### Word Processing Formats:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/supported_file_types/list_of_file_types_supported/#word-processing-formats "Direct link to Word Processing Formats:")

*   **DOCX** (Microsoft Word Open XML)
*   **DOC** (Microsoft Word)
*   **ODT** (OpenDocument Text)

### Presentation Formats:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/supported_file_types/list_of_file_types_supported/#presentation-formats "Direct link to Presentation Formats:")

*   **PPTX** (Microsoft PowerPoint Open XML)
*   **PPT** (Microsoft PowerPoint)
*   **ODP** (OpenDocument Presentation)

### Spreadsheet Formats:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/supported_file_types/list_of_file_types_supported/#spreadsheet-formats "Direct link to Spreadsheet Formats:")

*   **XLSX** (Microsoft Excel Open XML)
*   **XLS** (Microsoft Excel)
*   **XLSM** (Microsoft Excel Macro-Enabled)
*   **ODS** (OpenDocument Spreadsheet)

### Document and Plain Text Formats:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/supported_file_types/list_of_file_types_supported/#document-and-plain-text-formats "Direct link to Document and Plain Text Formats:")

*   **PDF** (Portable Document Format)
*   **TXT** (Plain Text)
*   **CSV** (Comma-Separated Values)
*   **JSON** (JavaScript Object Notation)

### Image Formats:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/supported_file_types/list_of_file_types_supported/#image-formats "Direct link to Image Formats:")

*   **BMP** (Bitmap Image)
*   **GIF** (Graphics Interchange Format)
*   **JPEG** (Joint Photographic Experts Group)
*   **JPG** (Joint Photographic Experts Group)
*   **PNG** (Portable Network Graphics)
*   **TIF** (Tagged Image File Format)
*   **TIFF** (Tagged Image File Format)
*   **WEBP** (Web Picture Format)

*   [Word Processing Formats:](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/supported_file_types/list_of_file_types_supported/#word-processing-formats)
    
*   [Presentation Formats:](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/supported_file_types/list_of_file_types_supported/#presentation-formats)
    
*   [Spreadsheet Formats:](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/supported_file_types/list_of_file_types_supported/#spreadsheet-formats)
    
*   [Document and Plain Text Formats:](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/supported_file_types/list_of_file_types_supported/#document-and-plain-text-formats)
    
*   [Image Formats:](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/supported_file_types/list_of_file_types_supported/#image-formats)

# Working with Connectors | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Connectors in Unstract allow you to integrate with various data sources and destinations, including file systems and databases. This guide covers the common workflow for adding and configuring connectors.

Adding Connectors[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#adding-connectors "Direct link to Adding Connectors")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can add connectors in Unstract in two different ways:

### Method 1: From the Connectors Page[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#method-1-from-the-connectors-page "Direct link to Method 1: From the Connectors Page")

You can also manage all connectors from a dedicated page:

1.  Navigate to **Settings** → **Connectors** from the side navigation
2.  Click the **\+ Add Connector** button
3.  Select your connector type from the available options

![Add connector modal](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_connector_modal-5dc826db020574ed79b6d5ccb71624ca.png)

### Method 2: From a Workflow[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#method-2-from-a-workflow "Direct link to Method 2: From a Workflow")

When creating or editing a workflow, you can add connectors directly during the source or destination configuration:

1.  Navigate to **Workflows** from the side navigation
2.  Click **\+ New Workflow** or select an existing workflow
3.  In the workflow setup, locate **Configure Source Connector** or **Configure Destination Connector**
4.  Select your connector type (File System or Database) that you wish to connect to
5.  Click **Configure** to open the below modal and add a connector

![Add connector from workflow](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_from_workflow-dfcf7d8e0792e5519022465bd0a43f7c.png)

Regardless of which method you choose, the rest of the process follows these steps

### Step 1: Choose Specific Connector[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#step-1-choose-specific-connector "Direct link to Step 1: Choose Specific Connector")

From the connector selection modal, choose your specific connector (e.g., Azure Cloud Storage, PostgreSQL).

### Step 2: Configure Connection Details[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#step-2-configure-connection-details "Direct link to Step 2: Configure Connection Details")

Fill in the required fields specific to your chosen connector:

*   **Name of the connector**: A descriptive name for easy identification
*   **Service-specific fields**: Each connector requires different authentication and connection details

This is an example to configure the S3 / Minio connector

![Connector Configuration Modal](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/minio-connector-form-filled-9a13287294b539988f3ef5bf0be59cc9.png)

### Step 3: Test Connection[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#step-3-test-connection "Direct link to Step 3: Test Connection")

Click **Test Connection** to verify your configuration is correct and Unstract can successfully connect to your service.

### Step 4: Submit[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#step-4-submit "Direct link to Step 4: Submit")

Once the test is successful, click **Submit** to save your connector for use in workflows.

Next Steps[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#next-steps "Direct link to Next Steps")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once your connectors are configured, you can:

*   Use them in your workflows for data processing
*   Edit their configuration if connection details change
*   Monitor their usage across different workflows

For specific configuration details, refer to the individual connector documentation:

*   [Filesystem Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/index/)
    
*   [Database Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/index/)
    

### Need Help?[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#need-help "Direct link to Need Help?")

Talk to us on [Slack](https://join.slack.com/t/unstract/shared_invite/zt-2fyv2bkye-RVfk2kxZQbB9PDEizOMh1g)
! We're here to help.

*   [Adding Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#adding-connectors)
    *   [Method 1: From the Connectors Page](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#method-1-from-the-connectors-page)
        
    *   [Method 2: From a Workflow](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#method-2-from-a-workflow)
        
    *   [Step 1: Choose Specific Connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#step-1-choose-specific-connector)
        
    *   [Step 2: Configure Connection Details](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#step-2-configure-connection-details)
        
    *   [Step 3: Test Connection](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#step-3-test-connection)
        
    *   [Step 4: Submit](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#step-4-submit)
        
*   [Next Steps](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#next-steps)
    *   [Need Help?](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/#need-help)

# Unstract Cloud Edition | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/cloud_edition/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Unstract Cloud](https://us-central.unstract.com/)
 is a fully managed, hosted edition of Unstract. It's the easiest way for you to get started with nothing for you to manage.

Free Trial[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/cloud_edition/#free-trial "Direct link to Free Trial")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Unstract Cloud](https://us-central.unstract.com/)
 offers a free 14-day trial with no credit card required.

Enterprise features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/cloud_edition/#enterprise-features "Direct link to Enterprise features")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Unstract Cloud features a host of enterprise-only features that let you use LLMs in production with confidence and accuracy:

*   [LLMChallenge](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_intro/)
    . Uses two Large Language Models to give you output you can trust. You either get the right response or no response at all.
*   [SinglePass Extraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/single_pass_extraction_intro/)
    : Reduces LLM token usage in your projects by up to 8x, saving you costs.
*   [SummarizedExtraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/summarized_extraction_intro/)
    : Reduces LLM token usage in your projects by up to 6x, saving you costs.
*   [Human Quality Review](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
    : Side-by-side comparison of extracted value and source document with source segment highlighting for human review / tweaking.
*   SSO Support: Supports a variety of authentication options to easy onboarding and off-boarding of your enterprise users.

Security and compliance[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/cloud_edition/#security-and-compliance "Direct link to Security and compliance")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Unstract Cloud](https://us-central.unstract.com/)
 is secure, with the following compliance certifications in place by a 3rd party assessor:

*   SOC 2 Type II
*   HIPAA
*   GDPR
*   ISO 27001

*   [Free Trial](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/cloud_edition/#free-trial)
    
*   [Enterprise features](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/cloud_edition/#enterprise-features)
    
*   [Security and compliance](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/cloud_edition/#security-and-compliance)

# Contributing to Unstract Open Source Edition | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

**Welcome fellow contributor!** Really glad to see you here. Even if this is your first time contribution, we are happy to get you started.

Join our LLM-powered automation [community on Slack](https://join-slack.unstract.com/)
 to have great conversations and reach out to the Unstract team.

Finally before you begin, please review and follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
 to make this a good experience for everyone involved.

Pull Request Workflow[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/#pull-request-workflow "Direct link to Pull Request Workflow")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Clone either the Unstract repo or your fork locally.
2.  Create a branch for your feature/fix with the format `feat|fix/name` (e.g. `fix/workflow-execution`).
3.  Make and commit your changes.
4.  Push your local branch to the Unstract repo or your fork.
5.  If maintaining a fork, submit a Pull Request from your forked repo `main` branch to the Unstract repo `main` branch. Otherwise submit a Pull Request from your feature branch to the Unstract repo `main` branch.
6.  Have a helpful PR title like `feat: Improve workflow execution` or `fix: Workflow execution`.  
    Follow the PR template for detailed description.  
    Link any relevant Issues.
7.  Unstract maintainers trigger the CI tests and review the PR.  
    Collaborate to resolve any open conversations.
8.  PR is merged once all status checks pass and required number of approvals are obtained.

New Issue[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/#new-issue "Direct link to New Issue")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Report a Bug[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/#report-a-bug "Direct link to Report a Bug")

Go to [New Issue page](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract/issues/new/choose)
 and click `Get started` button for `Bug report`. Follow the PR template for filing a bug report.

### Request new Feature[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/#request-new-feature "Direct link to Request new Feature")

Go to [New Issue page](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract/issues/new/choose)
 and click `Get started` button for `Feature request`. Follow the PR template for raising a feature request.

### Report a Security Vulnerability[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/#report-a-security-vulnerability "Direct link to Report a Security Vulnerability")

Go to [New Issue page](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract/issues/new/choose)
 and click `Report a vulnerability` button. This allows a vulnerability reporter to privately collaborate with Unstract maintainers following GitHub community best practices.

*   [Pull Request Workflow](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/#pull-request-workflow)
    
*   [New Issue](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/#new-issue)
    *   [Report a Bug](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/#report-a-bug)
        
    *   [Request new Feature](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/#request-new-feature)
        
    *   [Report a Security Vulnerability](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/#report-a-security-vulnerability)

# User Guides | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/index/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

[📄️ Chunk size and overlap\
--------------------------\
\
In the context of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), chunking refers to the division of a large input data, typically a large document or documents, into smaller, more manageable segments or "chunks" for efficient information retrieval. RAG techniques leverage these chunks to retrieve relevant context that enhances the generation of responses or text outputs from large language models or LLMs](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/chunking/)

[📄️ Run Platform\
----------------\
\
Use the following available options of run-platform.sh launch script to run Unstract Open Source Edition in Docker containers.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/run_platform/)

[📄️ Calculating extraction costs\
--------------------------------\
\
As a customer planning to use Unstract to extract structured data from unstructured documents, you might want to be sure about total cost of ownership. While it's easy to understand how much the Unstract Cloud or Unstract On-Prem editions might cost for the kind of volume you expect to deal with, calculating how much you might expect to spend on Large Language Models is a little bit more involved, but fairly easy and straightforward to calculate.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/calculating_extraction_cost/)

---

# Unstract Open Source Edition | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/open_source_edition/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

If you love to tinker, [Unstract Open Source](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract)
 is for you.

**Note:** The following advanced features are unavailable in Unstract Open Source Edition, and are included in Unstract's [Cloud](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/cloud_edition/)
 and [On-Premise](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/on_prem_edition/)
 offerings:

*   [LLMChallenge](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_intro/)
    . Uses two Large Language Models to give you output you can trust. You either get the right response or no response at all.
*   [SinglePass Extraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/single_pass_extraction_intro/)
    : Reduces LLM token usage in your projects by up to 8x, saving you costs.
*   [SummarizedExtraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/summarized_extraction_intro/)
    : Reduces LLM token usage in your projects by up to 6x, saving you costs.
*   [Human Quality Review](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
    : Side-by-side comparison of extracted value and source document with source segment highlighting for human review / tweaking.
*   SSO Support: Supports a variety of authentication options to easy onboarding and off-boarding of your enterprise users.

These features are all available in the [Unstract Cloud Edition](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/cloud_edition/)
 as well.

Installation[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/open_source_edition/#installation "Direct link to Installation")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### System requirements[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/open_source_edition/#system-requirements "Direct link to System requirements")

*   You'll need 8GB of free RAM to ensure smooth operation

### Prerequisites[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/open_source_edition/#prerequisites "Direct link to Prerequisites")

*   Linux or MacOS (Intel or M-series)
*   Docker
*   Docker Compose (sometimes you need to install this separately)
*   Git (if you plan to clone the repo rather than download a release)

### Get up and running[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/open_source_edition/#get-up-and-running "Direct link to Get up and running")

You can find the Unstract Open Source Edition source code repository [on GitHub](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract)
. To run Unstract you can either [download one of the releases](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract/releases)
 (recommended) or clone the [Unstract repository](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract)
. The package should contain the `run-platform.sh` script, which you need to then run to get all the required containers pulled and started. The steps look something like this:

*   **Step 1**: Run the script: `$ ./run-platform.sh`
*   **Step 2**: Now visit [http://frontend.unstract.localhost](http://frontend.unstract.localhost/)
     in your browser.
*   **Step 3**: Use user name and password `unstract` to login

Support and Community[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/open_source_edition/#support-and-community "Direct link to Support and Community")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   On Slack, [join great conversations](https://join-slack.unstract.com/)
     around LLMs, their ecosystem and leveraging them to automate the previously unautomatable!
*   [Follow us on X/Twitter](https://twitter.com/GetUnstract)
    
*   [Follow us on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/unstract/)
    

*   [Installation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/open_source_edition/#installation)
    *   [System requirements](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/open_source_edition/#system-requirements)
        
    *   [Prerequisites](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/open_source_edition/#prerequisites)
        
    *   [Get up and running](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/open_source_edition/#get-up-and-running)

# Platform API Reference (v1) | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

API Version:v1 (latest)

Overview[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1/#overview "Direct link to Overview")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Unstract Platform API provides programmatic access to all platform resources. All tenant-scoped endpoints share a common base URL and authentication scheme.

**Base URL:** `{host}/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}`

**Authentication:** `Authorization: Bearer <platform-api-key>`

Permission Model[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1/#permission-model "Direct link to Permission Model")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Platform API keys carry a permission level that restricts which HTTP methods are allowed:

| Permission | Allowed Methods |
| --- | --- |
| `read` | `GET`, `HEAD`, `OPTIONS` |
| `read_write` | All methods except `DELETE` |

`DELETE` operations are **blocked** for all platform API keys (enforced at middleware). Service accounts created for API keys can read/write all resources within the organization.

API Sections[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1/#api-sections "Direct link to API Sections")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Section | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Platform API Keys](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-platform-keys/) | Manage API keys (admin only, session auth) |
| [Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-prompt-studio/) | Manage Prompt Studio projects, prompts, profiles, and documents |
| [Workflows](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-workflows/) | Create and manage workflows, executions, and file history |
| [API Deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-api-deployments/) | Deploy workflows as APIs, manage deployment keys, execute |
| [ETL Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-etl-pipelines/) | Create and manage ETL/Task pipelines |
| [Adapters](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-adapters/) | Manage LLM, embedding, vector DB, and text extractor adapters |
| [Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-connectors/) | Manage filesystem and database connectors |
| [Cascading Workflows](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-cascading-workflows/) | Promote projects and deployments across environments |

Authentication Examples[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1/#authentication-examples "Direct link to Authentication Examples")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### curl[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1/#curl "Direct link to curl")

    curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <platform-api-key>" \  https://your-domain.com/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/

### Python[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1/#python "Direct link to Python")

    import requestsheaders = {"Authorization": "Bearer <platform-api-key>"}resp = requests.get(    "https://your-domain.com/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/",    headers=headers,)print(resp.json())

*   [Overview](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1/#overview)
    
*   [Permission Model](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1/#permission-model)
    
*   [API Sections](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1/#api-sections)
    
*   [Authentication Examples](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1/#authentication-examples)
    *   [curl](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1/#curl)
        
    *   [Python](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1/#python)

# Setting up HITL ETL | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) ETL extends standard ETL pipelines by adding manual review and approval capabilities for extracted data before it reaches the destination database.

Prerequisites[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#prerequisites "Direct link to Prerequisites")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  [Export a Prompt Studio project as a tool](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/exporting_a_project/)
    
    ![Export Prompt Studio Project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_1-6ab3d0574b28394f84f167c928a0ff59.png) ![Export Confirmation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_2-1857530f7e6a1ef819a6ff10300015c0.png)
    
2.  Configure your [source filesystem connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
    
3.  Configure your [destination database connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
    

Creating the HITL Workflow[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#creating-the-hitl-workflow "Direct link to Creating the HITL Workflow")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Step 1: Create Workflow with HITL Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#step-1-create-workflow-with-hitl-configuration "Direct link to Step 1: Create Workflow with HITL Configuration")

Follow the [Workflow Setup Guide](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/)
 to create your workflow:

1.  Navigate to **Workflows** in the side navigation
2.  Create a new workflow
3.  Configure source, destination, and select your exported tool
4.  **Enable HITL**: In the destination connector configuration, access the **Human in the Loop** tab
5.  Configure HITL rules and settings:
    *   **Percentage of files sent to Manual Review**: The user can set the percentage of files to be sent to HITL (Human-in-the-Loop). Based on this percentage, files will be sent to HITL accordingly. For example, if the percentage is set to 50 and 2 files are processed, then 1 file will be sent to HITL, while the other will be sent directly to the Destination DB.
    *   **Rule Logic**: The user can set the logic to either `AND` or `OR`
    *   **Add Rule**: Here, the user can set rules based on specific conditions. All prompt keys (used in Prompt Studio) will be listed, allowing the user to validate conditions. For example, if the key is 'Name,' the user can set a rule such as 'Name starts with Joseph.' Only files that satisfy the condition will be sent to HITL.
    *   **After approval, send result to**: Configure approval destination (Destination DB or Queue)

For detailed HITL rule configuration options, see the [HITL Rule configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/)
 guide.

### Step 2: Deploy as ETL[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#step-2-deploy-as-etl "Direct link to Step 2: Deploy as ETL")

Click **Deploy as ETL** to create your HITL-enabled ETL pipeline.

![HITL ETL Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_3-e6ddaab86dcf618f8239a1c44db1a817.png)

Once deployed, files matching your HITL rules will be routed to the review queue.

User Roles and Permissions[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#user-roles-and-permissions "Direct link to User Roles and Permissions")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Invite users with appropriate access rights:

*   **Unstract Admin** - Full access to Human Quality Review
*   **Unstract User** - No access to Human Quality Review
*   **Unstract Reviewer** - Can review documents in the queue
*   **Unstract Supervisor** - Can review and approve documents

![Invite Users](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_4-ea67a16b6e1ba3810ac35876b75a7cd9.png) ![User Roles](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_5-f51c3559e5200b7c21a9e3790e455ef3.png)

Reviewing Documents[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#reviewing-documents "Direct link to Reviewing Documents")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Accessing the Review Queue[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#accessing-the-review-queue "Direct link to Accessing the Review Queue")

1.  Navigate to **Human Quality Review** in the side navigation
2.  Select the project from the dropdown
3.  Click **Fetch Next** to retrieve the next document for review
4.  Click **Queue Details** to view queue status

![Review Queue](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_6-c79efe0848714bd845554153a1b30176.png) ![Document Review](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_7-21b066f8e05b885240a1c243840db9ff.png)

### Interacting with Results[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#interacting-with-results "Direct link to Interacting with Results")

*   **Single-click** on a result to highlight the corresponding text in the document ![Highlight Result](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_8-4dff3e6010c0ee96dc72fe5b787865ef.png)
    
*   **Double-click** on a result to edit it ![Edit Result](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_9-208bea82b6e7b9b72875864a95af880b.png)
    
*   Click **Finish Review** to send the document to the approval queue ![Finish Review](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_10-f3247fb4a005f3317bbf61103c995ca6.png)
    

Approving Documents[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#approving-documents "Direct link to Approving Documents")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Reviewed files move to the Approval Queue where supervisors can:

1.  Review the extracted data
2.  Highlight and verify results
3.  Make final edits if needed
4.  Approve the document for final processing

![Approval Queue](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_11-271ea24984abbb5f88579b670ba99cf0.png) ![Approver View](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_12-57b412ab72081dbdb9dd974434fad027.png) ![Approval Highlighting](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_13-9b2639135f9f054651a8fffb7a7c27fa.png) ![Final Approval](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_14-faaed33086e00340ffd624894a14605c.png)

Retrieving Approved Results[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#retrieving-approved-results "Direct link to Retrieving Approved Results")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Option 1: Destination Database[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#option-1-destination-database "Direct link to Option 1: Destination Database")

If you configured **"After approval, send result to"** as **"Destination DB"**, approved results are automatically inserted into your configured database table.

### Option 2: Queue (API Retrieval)[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#option-2-queue-api-retrieval "Direct link to Option 2: Queue (API Retrieval)")

If you configured the approval destination as **"Queue"**, retrieve results using the API endpoint:

    curl --location 'https://us-central.unstract.com/mr/api/<organization_id>/approved/result/<class_id>/' \--header 'Authorization: Bearer <api_key>'

#### Getting Required Parameters:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#getting-required-parameters "Direct link to Getting Required Parameters:")

**Organization ID:**

The organization ID is available in your ETL endpoint URL.

![Organization ID](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_15-d20e92951f0480aa08bb3bbead8f532d.png)

**API Key:**

Create an API key for Human Review:

1.  Navigate to **Human Quality Review**
2.  Click **Create API Key** (follow screenshots)

![Create API Key](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_16-43a7239e11f63f6d85910aaf0bcbb6f0.png) ![API Key Created](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_17-e6cc2973440f96e226a7448757fa12db.png)

**Class ID:**

Get the class ID from Download and Sync Manager:

1.  Click on **Profile Icon** → **Download and Sync Manager**
2.  Find your class ID in the list

![Access Sync Manager](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_18-ea8615208b9d8c04338672b9a2196b1e.png) ![Get Class ID](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hqr_19-fb6fc62fd36c9f57fcef219120703bf3.png)

Bulk Downloads

For downloading large numbers of approved records, use the [Bulk Download API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/bulk_download/)
. It supports pagination, async downloads for large files, and email notifications when downloads are ready.

Data Removal After Download

Once approved records are successfully downloaded via the API and acknowledged, they are **automatically removed** from the Download and Sync Manager. As per our data-handling policy, user data is not retained on our system after it has been downloaded/acknowledged. This means:

*   Records will no longer be visible in the Download and Sync Manager
*   Data cannot be recovered from our system after acknowledgment
*   **Important**: Ensure the data was successfully pulled and stored on your side before acknowledging, as this action is irreversible

*   [Prerequisites](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#prerequisites)
    
*   [Creating the HITL Workflow](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#creating-the-hitl-workflow)
    *   [Step 1: Create Workflow with HITL Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#step-1-create-workflow-with-hitl-configuration)
        
    *   [Step 2: Deploy as ETL](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#step-2-deploy-as-etl)
        
*   [User Roles and Permissions](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#user-roles-and-permissions)
    
*   [Reviewing Documents](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#reviewing-documents)
    *   [Accessing the Review Queue](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#accessing-the-review-queue)
        
    *   [Interacting with Results](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#interacting-with-results)
        
*   [Approving Documents](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#approving-documents)
    
*   [Retrieving Approved Results](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/#retrieving-approved-results)
