# Human in the Loop (HITL) Rule Configuration | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Overview[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#overview "Direct link to Overview")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Human in the Loop (HITL) allows you to introduce manual review steps into your workflow. This ensures data accuracy by routing specific files to human reviewers based on configurable rules.

Accessing HITL Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#accessing-hitl-configuration "Direct link to Accessing HITL Configuration")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  After setting up tools in Step 3 of your workflow
2.  Click on **"Configure"** in Step 2 (Destination Connector)
3.  Navigate to the **"Human in the Loop"** tab

HITL Rule Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#hitl-rule-configuration "Direct link to HITL Rule Configuration")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Rules Tab[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#rules-tab "Direct link to Rules Tab")

#### 1\. Percentage of Files Sent to Manual Review[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#1-percentage-of-files-sent-to-manual-review "Direct link to 1. Percentage of Files Sent to Manual Review")

Control what portion of your processed files should be routed to manual review:

*   Set a percentage value (0-100%)
*   **Example**: If you set 50% and process 2 files:
    *   1 file will be sent to HITL for manual review
    *   1 file will be sent directly to the database ![HITL rule Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/percentage_of_files-4ead3b1597a29aad541d55fb9b7a21d5.png)

* * *

#### 2\. Add Rules[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#2-add-rules "Direct link to 2. Add Rules")

Create custom rules to determine which files require manual review. All prompt keys created in the Prompt Studio will be available in the **Field** dropdown.

##### Rule Types[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#rule-types "Direct link to Rule Types")

You can configure rules using four different filter methods:

1.  **Filter by Confidence**
    
    *   Route files based on confidence scores
    *   Enter percentage threshold (0-100)
    *   Example: `80` (for 80% confidence threshold) ![HITL rule Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hitl_confidence_1-5d8a4ed7a19a32041a83b9189af12186.png) ![HITL rule Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hitl_confidence_2-6db3cb8bf5b5c226a2072f0f2352820f.png)
2.  **Filter by Value**
    
    *   Route files based on specific field values
    *   Supports text, numbers, booleans, and dates
    *   Examples:
        *   Text: `approved`, `NAREN`
        *   Numbers: `1900`, `100.50`
        *   Booleans: `true`, `false`
        *   Dates: `2020-01-01` ![HITL rule Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hitl_value_1-98a555ea4b6302fc3ed547767ac9d4b7.png) ![HITL rule Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hitl_value_2-046f86482e9e7009d85f4e20b233b551.png)
3.  **Filter by Value JSON Sub Field**
    
    *   Route files based on nested JSON field values
    *   Two input fields: path and value
    *   Supports text, numbers, booleans, and dates
    
    **Examples:**
    
    | Path | Value | Data Type | Description |
    | --- | --- | --- | --- |
    | `address.city` | `Boston` | Text | Checks city field value |
    | `customer.age` | `35` | Number | Checks age field value |
    | `account.active` | `true` | Boolean | Checks boolean status |
    | `transaction.date` | `2020-01-01` | Date | Checks transaction date |
    | `status` | `approved` | Text | Checks status field value |
    

tip

When working with nested JSON fields, select the parent field from the **Field** dropdown first (e.g., `due_details`), then specify only the nested path in the value field (e.g., `due_date`). Do not include the parent field name in the path value.

**Example**: If checking `city_name` within `address`:

*   Field dropdown: Select `address`
*   Value field: Enter `city_name` (NOT `address.city_name`)

![HITL rule Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hitl_json_value_1-804bbf97e6d0d937e6d3eebfb473d8ec.png) ![HITL rule Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hitl_json_value_2-8348601fb47554d793a3b7e0b4d7eb70.png)

**4\. Filter by Confidence JSON Path**

*   Route files based on confidence at specific JSON paths
*   Enter as path:threshold
*   Threshold should be a number (0-100)

**Simple Object Examples:**

| JSON Path | Threshold | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `address.city:80` | 80  | Checks if confidence for address.city is 80% or below |
| `status:75` | 75  | Checks if confidence for status field is 75% or below |
| `customer.name:85` | 85  | Checks if confidence for customer.name is 85% or below |
| `key1.key2:75` | 75  | Checks confidence at nested key path |
| `invoice.total_amount:90` | 90  | Checks if confidence for invoice total is 90% or below |

tip

When specifying JSON paths for confidence checks, select the parent field from the **Field** dropdown first (e.g., `due_details`), then specify only the nested path with threshold in the format `path:threshold` (e.g., `due_date:80`). Do not include the parent field name in the path.

**Example**: If checking confidence of `city_name` within `address`:

*   Field dropdown: Select `address`
*   Path:Threshold: Enter `city_name:80` (NOT `due_details.city_name:80`)

![HITL rule Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hitl_json_confidence_1-aae858e5377da1ca25c31f67aa60e050.png) ![HITL rule Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hitl_json_confidence_2-b951b81ef8a66fd3d18a6bcc3f9fba47.png)

* * *

#### Setting Up a Rule[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#setting-up-a-rule "Direct link to Setting Up a Rule")

1.  **Select Field**: Choose from the dropdown (all Prompt Studio fields available)
    
    *   Examples: `account_holder_name`, `cc_issuer_name`, `line_items`
2.  **Choose Operator**: Select comparison method
    
    *   `==` (equals)
    *   `!=` (not equals)
    *   `<` (less than)
    *   `<=` (less than or equal)
    *   `>` (greater than)
    *   `>=` (greater than or equal)
    *   `Starts with`
    *   `Ends with`

tip

**Important**: Choose operators appropriate to your value type. For text values, use text-based operators like `Starts with`, `Ends with`, or `Contains`. For numbers and dates, use comparison operators like `<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=`.

3.  **Set Value**: Enter the comparison value as a string (including numbers and booleans)

![HITL rule Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/how_to_set_rule-d1f76df486ca18335f505edc4fe2c934.png)

##### Example Rules[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#example-rules "Direct link to Example Rules")

    filterByValue.account_holder_name STARTS WITH "NAREN"

    filterByValue.line_items[0].quantity == "5"

    filterByValue.account.active == "true"

These rules send files to manual review based on the specified conditions.

* * *

##### Rule Logic[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#rule-logic "Direct link to Rule Logic")

*   **Rule Logic**: Choose between `OR` and `AND` logic to combine multiple rules
*   **Not**: Toggle to negate a rule condition

![HITL rule Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/rulelogic-8e36ebb70c59a06cae192b949ac06395.png)

##### Managing Rules[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#managing-rules "Direct link to Managing Rules")

*   **Add rule**: Click the `+ Add rule` button to create additional rules
*   **Delete**: Click the red trash icon to remove a rule
*   **Save Rules**: Click the `Save Rules` button to apply your configuration

* * *

### Settings Tab[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#settings-tab "Direct link to Settings Tab")

Configure what happens after manual review approval:

![HITL rule Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/hitl_sub_settings-cb7de62c009197d3dbca90f0a6d2a4ba.png)

#### After Approval Send Result To[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#after-approval-send-result-to "Direct link to After Approval Send Result To")

Choose where approved data should be sent:

1.  **Destination DB**
    
    *   Approved results are sent directly to the configured destination database
2.  **Queue**
    
    *   Approved results are sent to a queue
    *   Extract values using API calls

#### Unfinalized Records Lifetime[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#unfinalized-records-lifetime "Direct link to Unfinalized Records Lifetime")

Control how long unreviewed records are retained:

*   **Unlimited**: Keep unreviewed records forever (default)
*   **Custom**: Auto-delete unreviewed records after a specified number of hours
    *   Maximum: 8,640 hours (1 year)
    *   Helps manage storage and compliance requirements

**Note**: Click `Save Settings` to apply changes.

* * *

*   [Overview](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#overview)
    
*   [Accessing HITL Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#accessing-hitl-configuration)
    
*   [HITL Rule Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#hitl-rule-configuration)
    *   [Rules Tab](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#rules-tab)
        
    *   [Settings Tab](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/#settings-tab)

---

# Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/notification/unstract_notification_intro/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Unstract provides notification capabilities that allow you to monitor the status of your APIs, ETL Pipelines, and Task Pipelines. When configured, notifications will be sent to your specified webhook endpoints upon completion of executions.

Setting Up Notifications[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/notification/unstract_notification_intro/#setting-up-notifications "Direct link to Setting Up Notifications")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Step 1: Access Notification Settings[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/notification/unstract_notification_intro/#step-1-access-notification-settings "Direct link to Step 1: Access Notification Settings")

![img Notification Option](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/notification_demo_1-9b34790c92a7f8427b804edae924b365.png)

1.  Navigate to the desired section (API Deployments, ETL Pipelines, or Task Pipelines)
2.  Locate the pipeline or API you want to configure notifications for
3.  Click the "Actions" menu (...) on the right side of the item
4.  Select "Setup Notification" from the dropdown menu

### Step 2: Create a New Notification[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/notification/unstract_notification_intro/#step-2-create-a-new-notification "Direct link to Step 2: Create a New Notification")

![img Create New Notification](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/notification_demo_2-48483c02f6a78ff215e0f4dc1c70588d.png)

1.  In the notification setup page, click the "Create Notification" button

### Step 3: Configure Notification Settings[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/notification/unstract_notification_intro/#step-3-configure-notification-settings "Direct link to Step 3: Configure Notification Settings")

![Notification Form](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/notification_demo_3-6fac66381fa71067795bf92593b4530c.png)

1.  Fill out the notification configuration form:
    
    *   **Name**: Give your notification a clear, descriptive name (e.g., "Slack-API-Status")
    *   **URL**: Enter your webhook URL where notifications will be sent (e.g., Slack webhook URL).
    *   **Notification Type**: Select WEBHOOK (default option).
    *   **Platform**: Choose your notification platform
        *   SLACK: For Slack workspace notifications
        *   API: For custom API endpoints
    *   **Authorization Type**: Select the required authentication method.
        *   BEARER: For Bearer token authentication
        *   API\_KEY: For Api key header
        *   CUSTOM\_HEADER: For custom API key in header
        *   NONE: For webhooks without authentication
    *   **Max Retries**: Set the number of retry attempts (maximum 4) in case of delivery failure.
2.  Click "Create Notification" to save your configuration
    

Note: Make sure your webhook URL is active and properly configured in your chosen platform (Slack, Webhook API, etc.) before proceeding.

### Step 4: Activate the Notification[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/notification/unstract_notification_intro/#step-4-activate-the-notification "Direct link to Step 4: Activate the Notification")

![img Activation Of Notification](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/notification_demo_4-45cdc014263dcf17d0e7a5daa651171d.png)

1.  After creating the notification, toggle the "Active" switch to enable it
2.  The notification is now ready to send updates to your webhook endpoint

*   [Setting Up Notifications](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/notification/unstract_notification_intro/#setting-up-notifications)
    *   [Step 1: Access Notification Settings](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/notification/unstract_notification_intro/#step-1-access-notification-settings)
        
    *   [Step 2: Create a New Notification](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/notification/unstract_notification_intro/#step-2-create-a-new-notification)
        
    *   [Step 3: Configure Notification Settings](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/notification/unstract_notification_intro/#step-3-configure-notification-settings)
        
    *   [Step 4: Activate the Notification](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/notification/unstract_notification_intro/#step-4-activate-the-notification)

---

# Download sample documents | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/unstract_download_sample_docs/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

So you can get started quickly, we've put together a zip file consisting of a set of sample credit card statements.

info

Download the sample set of credit card statements [from here](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/files/sample_cc_statements-10e75656a552521b8ff7cc5687562262.zip/)
.

There are a total of 4 sample statements in the provided zip file.

![img Structure of Examples](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/examples_dir_structure-2c9e5c3426af51242a268e2a192dcc62.png)

Once you unzip the files, you'll find two folders inside the parent folder. One named `prompt_enineering` and the other named `testing`. We will use 3 statements inside the `prompt engineering` folder to prompt engineer structuring of the documents and then we'll use the statement inside the `testing` folder to see how well our project works on new files not seen by the system. To create generic structuring APIs, you'll probably need dozens of documents, but this simple project should help you wet your feet.

Now that you've downloaded the statements, let's go ahead, structure them and look at how to achieve different integrations so we can automate the processing of credit card statements.

---

# Enterprise SSO — Okta | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

This guide walks through configuring Okta as an identity provider for Unstract using either OpenID Connect (OIDC) or SAML 2.0. Unstract uses [Auth0 as an identity broker](https://auth0.com/docs/authenticate/identity-providers/enterprise-identity-providers/okta)
, which connects to your Okta tenant for enterprise SSO.

Highlight Color Coding

Throughout this document, highlights are used to indicate items that need to be shared between parties:

*   **Green highlight** — Items the **Customer shares with Unstract** (e.g., Client ID, Client Secret, Okta Domain Name, Sign in URL, Signing Certificate, Directory Groups)
*   **Yellow highlight** — Items **Unstract shares with Customer** (e.g., Authentication Server Domain, Audience URI Domain, Redirect URI)

1\. Unstract Configuration to be Used[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#1-unstract-configuration-to-be-used "Direct link to 1-unstract-configuration-to-be-used")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Unstract team will share the following configuration values specific to your deployment type:

| Deployment Type | Authentication Server Domain | Audience URI Domain |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Enterprise Cloud** | `auth.unstract.com` | `dev-3xlzwou1raoituv0` |
| **Enterprise On-prem** | `zipstack-on-prem.us.auth0.com` | `zipstack-on-prem` |

The redirect URI follows this pattern:

*   **OIDC**: `https://<unstract_authentication_server_domain>/login/callback`
*   **SAML**: `https://<unstract_authentication_server_domain>/login/callback?connection=marsoktasaml`

2\. Steps to be Performed by the Customer[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#2-steps-to-be-performed-by-the-customer "Direct link to 2. Steps to be Performed by the Customer")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 2.1 Create App in Okta[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#21-create-app-in-okta "Direct link to 2.1 Create App in Okta")

**REF**: [Auth0 — Okta Identity Provider](https://auth0.com/docs/authenticate/identity-providers/okta)

#### A. OIDC Flow[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#a-oidc-flow "Direct link to A. OIDC Flow")

1.  **Create an App Integration** from the Okta **Admin Console**.
2.  Select **Sign-in method** as **OIDC (OpenID Connect)**.
3.  Enter the **App Integration Name** following your organization's naming convention.
4.  Choose the **Grant Type** as **Authorization Code** (default).
5.  In the **Sign-in redirect URI** field, enter the URI shared by the **Unstract team** which is applicable to your deployment type (refer [Section 1](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#1-unstract-configuration-to-be-used)
    ): `https://<unstract_authentication_server_domain>/login/callback`
6.  In the **Assignment** section:
    *   You can either **limit access to specific groups** or **allow access to everyone** in your organization.
    *   Even if access is granted to everyone, Unstract provides an option to **restrict users** based on predefined groups as agreed.
7.  Click **Save** to create the App Integration.
8.  After creation, share the following details with the **Unstract team**:
    *   **Client ID**
    *   **Client Secret**
    *   **Okta Domain Name**
9.  Once the app is created, navigate to the **Sign On** tab of the application.
10.  Under **OpenID Connect ID Token** settings, **edit the Group Claims** configuration to add **claims**. This enables Unstract to **sync user groups** and **map them to roles** within the application _(refer screenshots)_.

#### B. SAML Flow[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#b-saml-flow "Direct link to B. SAML Flow")

1.  **Create an App Integration** from the Okta **Admin Console**.
2.  Select **Sign-in method** as **SAML 2.0**.
3.  Provide **App Name** following your naming convention.
4.  In the **Configure SAML** tab:
    *   Add the **Single sign-on URL** (refer [Section 1](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#1-unstract-configuration-to-be-used)
        ): `https://<unstract_authentication_server_domain>/login/callback?connection=marsoktasaml`
    *   For **Audience URI (SP Entity ID)** fill as (refer [Section 1](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#1-unstract-configuration-to-be-used)
        ): `urn:auth0:<unstract_audience_uri_domain>:marsoktasaml`
    *   In **Group Attribute Statements**, give the name as _**group**_, format as **basic**, and filter to include the relevant **groups**. This enables Unstract to **sync user groups** and **map them to roles** within the application.
5.  Click next and finish the App creation.
6.  After creation, share the following details with the **Unstract team**:
    *   **Sign in URL** (_IdP Single Sign-On URL, refer screenshot_)
    *   **Okta tenant public key signing certificate** (_refer screenshot_)

### 2.2 Configure User Directory Groups[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#22-configure-user-directory-groups "Direct link to 2.2 Configure User Directory Groups")

Existing or new **user directory groups** need to be configured and shared with Unstract. These user directory groups are then mapped to Unstract-defined roles in the application.

The following roles are defined in the Unstract application:

*   `unstract_user`
*   `unstract_admin`
*   `unstract_supervisor`
*   `unstract_reviewer`

**Example mapping** of customer Directory Groups to Unstract Roles:

| Unstract Roles | Customer Directory Groups |
| --- | --- |
| `unstract_user` | `customer_unstract_user_dev` |
| `unstract_admin` | `customer_unstract_admin_dev` |
| `unstract_supervisor` | `customer_unstract_supervisor_dev` |
| `unstract_reviewer` | `customer_unstract_reviewer_dev` |

note

*   The customer directory groups to Unstract roles mapping needs to be done for each of the **development**, **test** and **production** environments.
*   If either there are no directory groups associated with a user in a customer environment or the associated directory group is not mapped to an Unstract-side application role, then the **user will be forbidden** to access the application.
*   Unstract uses Auth0 for handling authentication at our end. [Auth0 — Okta Identity Provider](https://auth0.com/docs/authenticate/identity-providers/enterprise-identity-providers/okta)
    

### 2.3 Configuration to be Shared with Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#23-configuration-to-be-shared-with-unstract "Direct link to 23-configuration-to-be-shared-with-unstract")

| Config | Okta App Flow | Where to Obtain |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Client ID, Client Secret, Okta Domain Name | OIDC Flow | See **2.1.A** step 8 |
| Sign in URL, Okta tenant public key signing certificate | SAML Flow | See **2.1.B** step 6 |
| User directory groups | OIDC Flow | See **2.1.A** step 10 |
| User directory groups | SAML Flow | See **2.1.B** step 4 (Group Attribute Statements) |

3\. Screenshots for Reference[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#3-screenshots-for-reference "Direct link to 3. Screenshots for Reference")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 3.4 Auth0 Okta Workforce Enterprise Connection[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#34-auth0-okta-workforce "Direct link to 3.4 Auth0 Okta Workforce Enterprise Connection")

This section shows how the Unstract team configures the Auth0 Okta Workforce Enterprise Connection using the details shared by the customer.

![Auth0 — Okta Workforce Connection](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/okta_auth0_workforce_connection-0c775d40c58d8ac178a551c8f69ca737.png)

![Auth0 — New Okta Workforce Connection](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/okta_auth0_new_connection-63111d99972465fdb9406e41df7e8d21.png)

![Auth0 — OIDC Connection Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/okta_auth0_connection_oidc-cde70f1dcb485693273302a1349926ce.png)

![Auth0 — SAML Connection Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/okta_auth0_connection_saml-6deea938c8bab779d445840d37a5fb34.png)

4\. Auth Architecture[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#4-auth-architecture "Direct link to 4. Auth Architecture")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 4.1 Auth0 as Identity Broker[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#41-auth0-as-identity-broker "Direct link to 4.1 Auth0 as Identity Broker")

Unstract uses Auth0 as an identity broker that sits between the application and your identity provider. Auth0 handles:

*   **Universal Login Page** — Centralized authentication experience
*   **Auth Engine & Token Service** — Token issuance and validation
*   **User Management** — User profile and session management
*   **MFA** — Multi-factor authentication support
*   **Rules/Actions** — Custom authentication logic

Auth0 connects to multiple identity provider types:

| Connection Type | Protocol | Supported Providers |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enterprise IDPs | OIDC/SAML | Azure AD, Okta, PingFed, ADFS |
| Social IDPs | OAuth 2.0 | Google, GitHub, LinkedIn, Microsoft |
| Database Connections | Direct Auth | Auth0 DB, Custom DB, LDAP/AD |

![Auth0 as Identity Broker](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/auth0_broker-caeb6d419d2944ab7d6a2ebba660af2b.png)

### 4.2 Enterprise Connection Flow[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#42-enterprise-connection-flow "Direct link to 4.2 Enterprise Connection Flow")

In a multi-tenant setup, Auth0 Organizations with Home Realm Discovery routes users to their correct identity provider based on their email domain. The flow produces a unified JWT token containing:

    {  "org_id": "org_xxx",  "sub": "auth0|123",  "permissions": [...]}

The application backend services use the `org_id` claim for tenant isolation.

![Enterprise Connection Flow](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/enterprise_flow-013d2b6e05d64582d800e2082655d7c5.png)

*   [1\. Unstract Configuration to be Used](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#1-unstract-configuration-to-be-used)
    
*   [2\. Steps to be Performed by the Customer](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/#2-steps-to-be-performed-by-the-customer)

# Create new Prompt Studio project | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_new_ps_project/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

For every new document type or class you need to structure, you start first by creating a Prompt Studio project. Prompt Studio is a no-code prompt engineering environment in which you can load sample documents, select any combination of LLM, Vector DB, embedding model and Text Extractor and see what kind of results you're able to get.

Let's first create a new Prompt Studio project where we'll structure credit card statements.

*   Sign into the Unstract Platform
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Prompt Studio`
*   Click on the `New Project` button, which should bring up the below dialog box

![img New Prompt Studio project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_new_project-7a0b2b20078ecaa30d130210f65dcc9a.png)

*   For the `Tool Name`, enter `Credit Card Statements Parser`
*   For `Author/Org Name`, enter your name
*   For `Description`, enter `This project helps parse US Credit Card Statements`.
*   Click on the `Choose Icon` button, search for "credit card" and select the credit card icon.
*   Click on the `Save` button to create this new project.
*   You should see your newly created project in the list of Prompt Studio projects. Click on the project from the list and that should take you into the Prompt Studio.

Next, let's look at how the Prompt Studio is laid out.

---

# Prompt Studio Layout | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/prompt_studio_quick_overview/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Let's look at how the Prompt Studio is laid out and become familiar with the most common features we'll use.

![img Prompt Studio layout](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/new_prompt_project_updated-1b68dfbddb9a5c91a83375ef93f6155a.png)

Let's look at what each numbered feature is for:

1.  **Project Settings**: lets you control various aspects of the Prompt Studio project like LLM Profiles, among other settings.
2.  **Document Pane**: this is where you can view documents in their native, raw and summary forms.
3.  **Document Manager**: this feature lets you manage uploaded documents within the Prompt Studio project.
4.  **Prompt Engineering Pane**: this is where you add and manage prompts that extract information from various types of documents.
5.  **Add New Prompt**: this button lets you create new prompts.
6.  **Export Project**: when you're done creating or editing a Prompt Studio project. You can export your project as a custom tool which you can then use as part of any workflow.

---

# Introduction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Unstract provides an API-driven interface for executing workflows securely and efficiently using appropriate authentication methods.

Creating API Deployments[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/#creating-api-deployments "Direct link to Creating API Deployments")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

API deployments can be created directly from Prompt Studio, which automatically generates the necessary workflow behind the scenes. For detailed instructions, see [Creating API Deployments from Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/)
.

To understand how workflows power API deployments, refer to the [Workflow Setup Guide](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/)
.

Once a workflow is deployed as an API, several actions become available, including triggering executions and checking the execution status. The deployment also offers a convenient option to download the Postman collection for each API, simplifying integration into your workflow. This collection is accessible from the `Download Postman Collection` option found in the `Actions` menu of each listed API in the **API Deployment** tab.

info

*   **Replace Tools**: The existing tool can be replaced with new one just by clicking `Change Prompt studio project` (step 3) and selecting the new project from the Tools list.
*   **Live Updates**: Any modifications to the tool or its settings will instantly update the behavior of your deployed API
*   **Seamless Integration**: The API endpoint remains the same, ensuring no disruption to applications already using your API
*   **Real-time Configuration**: Changes take effect immediately, allowing for quick iterations and improvements

These RESTful APIs can seamlessly integrate with your existing systems, offering flexibility and ease of use.

Smart File Handling[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/#smart-file-handling "Direct link to Smart File Handling")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Unstract's API deployment is designed for independent execution processing:

*   Supports up to **32** files in a single call
*   Each API execution is independent of the previous runs and there's no caching involved

Important

Results are reported based on the file name, hence ensure all files have unique names within each API execution to avoid any unexpected behavior.

### API Endpoints[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/#api-endpoints "Direct link to API Endpoints")

| Endpoint | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Execution API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_execution_api/) | Execute a deployed API with the desired inputs. |
| [Status API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_status_api/) | Retrieve the status of an execution. Useful for checking execution status in async mode. |

### Base URL[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/#base-url "Direct link to Base URL")

The base URL for all the APIs is

    https://us-central.unstract.com/

### Authentication[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/#authentication "Direct link to Authentication")

All APIs require an API key for authentication. You can obtain your API key from the Postman collection or from the `Manage Keys` section within the **API Deployments** page. The key must be included in the request header (Authorization) as a Bearer token.

### Example Request[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/#example-request "Direct link to Example Request")

    curl -X POST --location 'https://us-central.unstract.com/deployment/api/organization123/apiname/' \--header 'Authorization: Bearer <Api Key>' \--form 'files=@"/home/user/Downloads/file_1.pdf"' \--form 'files=@"/home/user/Downloads/file_2.pdf"'

*   [Creating API Deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/#creating-api-deployments)
    
*   [Smart File Handling](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/#smart-file-handling)
    *   [API Endpoints](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/#api-endpoints)
        
    *   [Base URL](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/#base-url)
        
    *   [Authentication](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/#authentication)
        
    *   [Example Request](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/#example-request)

---

# Add Documents to Document Manager | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/add_docs_to_documents_manager/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Now that our Prompt Studio project is set up, let's add documents to the Documents Manager. Click on the `Manage Documents` button to bring up the Documents Manager.

![img Documents Manager](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/documents_manager-7d0de2e6c0e8fd8f80e255e33d829a89.png)

We'll now add the three credit card statements from the set of sample files we downloaded for this quick start project.

*   Click on the `Upload New File` button and add the three PDFs from the `prompt_engineering` folder one by one.
*   You'll see `Not Indexed` and `Not Summarized` for the files you uploaded, but that's alright. The documents will get automatically indexed when you run your first prompt. Summarization is an advanced cost saving feature we'll look at later.
*   Next, select the first document so that it is loaded into view in the Document Pane.

We're done setting up the project and adding documents to it. We're now ready to write our first prompt and get going!

---

# Set up OpenAI LLM Profile | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_llm/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

info

When you sign up for OpenAI, you'll get $5 in free credits. This credit limit is more than sufficient to run examples listed here.

Sign up[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_llm/#sign-up "Direct link to Sign up")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

First off, you'll need to sign up for the [OpenAI Platform](https://platform.openai.com/overview)
. Please note that this is different from signing up for ChatGPT. The idea here is to get an API key for the GPT API, which is the underlying technology for ChatGPT. Once you successfully do so, you should have $5 is free credits. Now, let's fetch an API key we can use with the Unstract Platform.

![img OpenAI free credits](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/openai_free_credits-bdd84b9a76fb3ea669bfa6863ce865df.png)

Create a New OpenAI API Key[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_llm/#create-a-new-openai-api-key "Direct link to Create a New OpenAI API Key")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Clicking on `API Keys` from the side menu, enter `Unstract` as the name for the new API key while keeping permissions unchanged to "All".

![img Create new OpenAI API key](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_api_key_1-28ed5bbfe1d99934112d4e6a96d5899a.png)

Click on `Create secret key` to create a new API key. An important thing to remember is that you need to copy the key immediately. If you dismiss that dialog box, there's no way to retrieve that key. You can only create a new one.

![img Copy new API key](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_api_key_2-27e094af64eed90851bd72321d2102dc.png)

Setting up the OpenAI LLM in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_llm/#setting-up-the-openai-llm-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the OpenAI LLM in Unstract")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from OpenAI, we can use it to set up an LLM profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `LLMs`
*   Click on the `New LLM Profile` button
*   From the list of LLMs, choose `OpenAI`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.

![img Add an OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_openai_llm_profile-efc232087d8620967b7bf5435e6a79c6.png)

*   For `Name`,enter a name for this connector.
*   In the `API Key` field, paste the API key we created on the OpenAI website.
*   For `Model`, refer to [OpenAI model names](https://platform.openai.com/docs/models)
    
*   Leave the `Max retries`,`Timeout` and the `API Base` field to their default values.
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new LLM Profile for use in your Unstract projects.

We still need to create profiles for an Embedding Model, a Vector database and a Text Extractor. Let's continue doing that.

*   [Sign up](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_llm/#sign-up)
    
*   [Create a New OpenAI API Key](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_llm/#create-a-new-openai-api-key)
    
*   [Setting up the OpenAI LLM in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_llm/#setting-up-the-openai-llm-in-unstract)

---

# Set up the Prompt Studio project | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/prompt_studio_project_setup/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Let's set up basic settings now so we can get started with our prompt engineering to structure credit card statements.

Creating an LLM Profile[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/prompt_studio_project_setup/#creating-an-llm-profile "Direct link to Creating an LLM Profile")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

First off, let's create an LLM Profile. LLM Profiles allow you to easily manage various settings related to how a document is indexed and structured from a single place. LLM Profiles typically contain a Triad, which is a combination of an LLM, a Vector Database and an embedding model apart from a Text Extractor. You can create multiple LLM Profiles per project and switch between them on a per prompt basis. This let you test how your prompts are performing for various LLMs you've configured in your platform.

*   In Prompt Studio, click on Settings 🞂 `Manage LLM Profiles` 🞂 `Add New LLM Profile`.

![img Create new LLM Profile](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/new_llm_profile-545b37c35c7f92232e1fe6230effe256.png)

*   For `Name`, enter `OpenAI GPT 3.5 + Qdrant + Ada + LLMWhisperer`, which provides a descriptive name for our LLM profile.
*   Set `LLM` to the OpenAI LLM we added earlier.
*   Set `Vector Database` to the Qdrant vector database we created earlier.
*   Set `Chunk Size` and `Overlap` to `0`. We will discuss later on why these values make sense for credit card statements.
*   Set `Embedding Model` to the OpenAI embedding model we created earlier.
*   Set `Text Extractor` to the LLMWhisperer option we created earlier.
*   Click on the `Add` button to create this new profile.

Since this is the first LLM Profile we're creating, it should automatically set itself as the default profile as well.

Create a Preamble[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/prompt_studio_project_setup/#create-a-preamble "Direct link to Create a Preamble")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Preambles are prefixed to any prompt instruction that is sent to the LLM. They can be very useful to avoid any unnecessary repetition.

![img Add Preamble](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_preamble-5e4c255d87aea8763c6093df12141b73.png)

*   Enter the following for the Preamble: `Your ability to extract and summarize this information accurately is essential for effective credit card statement analysis. Pay close attention to the credit card statement's language, structure, and any cross-references to ensure a comprehensive and precise extraction of information. Do not use prior knowledge or information from outside the context to answer the questions. Only use the information provided in the context to answer the questions.`
*   Click on `Save`

Create a Postamble[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/prompt_studio_project_setup/#create-a-postamble "Direct link to Create a Postamble")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Postambles are like preables and serve much of the same purpose except that they are suffixed to each prompt you enter.

![img Add Postamble](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_postamble-653cf1eb00fb546eddd10db75abbb004.png)

*   Enter the following for the Postamble: `Do not include any explanation in the reply. Only include the extracted information in the reply.`
*   Click on `Save`

These are all the settings we need for us to get started. Let's add our sample documents to the project next.

*   [Creating an LLM Profile](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/prompt_studio_project_setup/#creating-an-llm-profile)
    
*   [Create a Preamble](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/prompt_studio_project_setup/#create-a-preamble)
    
*   [Create a Postamble](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/prompt_studio_project_setup/#create-a-postamble)

---

# Making an API call | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/making_an_api_call/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Let's make an API call to our newly deployed API endpoint with [Postman](https://www.postman.com/)
. This section assumes that you have the [Postman client](https://www.postman.com/downloads/)
 installed locally.

Of course, this is simply a basic POST API call which can be performed with the API testing tool of your choosing.

![img Postman success](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/postman_success-a15c98998bbc76673a268d6aad7b0096.png)

Making the API call in Postman:

*   In Postman, open your workspace and click on the `Import` button, choose the Postman Collection you downloaded in the previous step from Unstract to import it.
*   You should not have a collection with 2 endpoints:
    *   An [Extraction endpoint](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_execution_api/)
         which returns extracted, structured JSON data given a credit card statement
    *   A [Status Check endpoint](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_status_api/)
         which you can use to check the status of an extraction job
*   Optionally, under `Body`, you can create the `include_metadata` key and set it to `True` if you want Unstract to return LLM cost and other debug information to you.
*   From the file input against the `files` key, select the `Chase Freedom.pdf` file from the `testing` folder in the downloaded sample files.
*   Click `Send`. This should post the document to your newly deployed API. After several seconds, you should see the data output in JSON format.

Congratulations! You've now deployed an API that can parse credit card statements!

### Further Reading[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/making_an_api_call/#further-reading "Direct link to Further Reading")

For more information on API deployment and related APIs, refer to the [API Deployment](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
 documentation.

*   [Further Reading](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/making_an_api_call/#further-reading)

---

# Adding rest of the prompts for our quick start project | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/completing_quick_start/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Now that we know how to add new prompts and run them, let's add a few more prompts to extract more fields from credit card statements.

Customer name[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/completing_quick_start/#customer-name "Direct link to Customer name")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Field name: `customer_name`
*   Field type: `Text`
*   Field prompt: `What is the name of the customer to whom this credit card statement belongs to? Format the name of the customer well with the fist letter of each name capitalized.`

![img Customer Address](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/customer_name-aa5a63ffec7df73316fcb2b40dc0800f.png)

Customer address[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/completing_quick_start/#customer-address "Direct link to Customer address")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Field name: `customer_address`
*   Field type: `json`
*   Field prompt:

    Since there might be multiple addresses in the context provided to you, first gather all addresses. Try to understand whom this credit card statement is being addressed to or in other words, the name of the customer. Find the address that matches that person's. Be sure to return the customer's address, for whom this credit card statement is for. Do not respond with any other address.For the customer's address, form a simple JSON object with the following fields:- full_address: should have the customer's full address-city: should hold's the city name from the address- zip: should contain the zip alone

![img Customer Address](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/customer_address-d4d61e483529a65603f17642c64efd0c.png)

Payment Info[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/completing_quick_start/#payment-info "Direct link to Payment Info")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Field name: `payment_info`
*   Field type: `json`
*   Field prompt:

    Payment information is important part of any credit card statement and it consists of the new balance or the full amount due for the current statement, the minimum payment due and the payment due date.Respond with a JSON object with the following fields:new_balance: the total new balance amount that can be paidminimum_payment: the minimum amount that is duedue_date: the payment due date in yyyy-mm-dd format

![img Payment Info](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/payment_info-cf5dd2da0a088e8dc80e8117daf1e7a4.png)

Spend line items[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/completing_quick_start/#spend-line-items "Direct link to Spend line items")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Field name: `spend_line_items`
*   Field type: `json`
*   Field prompt:

    This credit card statement contains spending details line items. Spend details can be split across the provided context. Respond with details of all the spend items by looking at the whole context always.For each of these spend details line items, form a simple JSON object with the following fields:- spend_date: date of the spend in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the year part isn't mentioned in the line item explicitly, pick up the year from the statement date and use it instead.- spend_description: description of the spend- amount: amount of the spend without the dollar sign formatted to 2 decimal pointsPut all of these items into a JSON array and return that.

![img Spend Line Items](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/spend_line_items-4e668d06d3cc086467a0dc999063c999.png)

*   [Customer name](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/completing_quick_start/#customer-name)
    
*   [Customer address](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/completing_quick_start/#customer-address)
    
*   [Payment Info](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/completing_quick_start/#payment-info)
    
*   [Spend line items](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/completing_quick_start/#spend-line-items)

---

# Creating and running prompts | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/creating_and_running_prompts/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Let's create our very first Unstract prompt in Prompt Studio to fetch the name of the credit card issuer.

*   Click on the `+ Prompt` button to add a new prompt cell

Let's understand the different components that make up a prompt cell:

![img Prompt Cell](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/new_prompt_updated-30cb545b4a7e632e0a65b8bdc4498b7d.png)

1.  **Field Name**: This is the field name of the prompt. This will be the field name used in the JSON output. You'll need to make sure this is JSON key syntax compatible.
2.  **Field Prompt**: Prompt used to extract the field.
3.  **Coverage Info**: We have 3 documents loaded into this Prompt Studio project. Coverage Info shows you the number of documents for which this prompt created any output. In this screenshot, we see that our prompt returned some output for all 3 documents, making our coverage 100%. Coverage only checks for `null`. You'll need to manually check if the values themselves make sense.
4.  **LLM Profile Settings**: Shows the currently selected LLM Profile for this prompt. You can use the LLM Profile Switcher (highlighted by `9`) to switch profiles and then re-run the prompt.
5.  **Output type selector**: You can switch between types of output you need. Prompt Studio takes care of adding the prompts required to generate that type of output behind the scenes automatically for you. You can choose between text, data, number, JSON object, etc. When a new prompt is created, the default is always set to `Text`.
6.  **Run all LLM for current document**: Runs the prompt only for current selected document and all added LLM profiles
7.  **Run all LLMs for all documents**: Runs the prompts for all document and for all added LLM profiles
8.  **LLM Response**: This is the area which shows you the response from the LLM.

The `Run Prompt` button is highlighted in red. As you enter the field name and field prompt, they're auto-saved. You can then click on the Run button to run the prompt.

For this example prompt, here's what we enter:

*   Prompt Field: `account_holder_name`
*   Field Prompt: `What is the name of the account holder`
*   Click on the Run button

Understanding Prompt Coverage[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/creating_and_running_prompts/#understanding-prompt-coverage "Direct link to Understanding Prompt Coverage")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

While you can switch documents via the Documents Manager and examine the LLM output for each of them, it's way easier to see what output we got for each document for a particular prompt using Coverage Info.

In the above prompt cell, we see that we have coverage for all 3 documents, meaning the LLM selected responded with some output for all three documents for the given prompt.

Clicking on the Coverage link brings up the Coverage Panel, which shows the output against the document's file name.

![img Coverage Panel](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/coverage_panel-79ad1325c23f9ddd9280231a2d9f5b5b.png)

*   [Understanding Prompt Coverage](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/creating_and_running_prompts/#understanding-prompt-coverage)

---

# Set up OpenAI Embedding Model | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_embedding/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

info

When you sign up for OpenAI, you'll get $5 in free credits. This credit limit is more than sufficient to run examples listed here.

Setting up the OpenAI Embedding model in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_embedding/#setting-up-the-openai-embedding-model-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the OpenAI Embedding model in Unstract")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from OpenAI, we can use it to set up an Embedding profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `Embedding`
*   Click on the `New Embedding Profile` button
*   From the list of Embeddings, choose `OpenAI`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.

![img Add new Embedding](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_openai_embedding_model-6a3c2a849f6a85a97545a89019289d61.png)

*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
*   In the `API Key` field, paste the API key we created on the OpenAI website.
*   Leave the `Embed Batch Size` field and the `API Base` field to their default values.
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new Embedding Profile for use in your Unstract projects.

One final step now is creating a profile for a Vector database. Let's continue doing that.

*   [Setting up the OpenAI Embedding model in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_embedding/#setting-up-the-openai-embedding-model-in-unstract)
