# Exporting a Prompt Studio project | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/exporting_a_project/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Once a Prompt Studio project is able to extract the intended information well for a given set of documents, it can be exported and used in various types of Workflows.

Export Options[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/exporting_a_project/#export-options "Direct link to Export Options")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you click the **Export** button in Prompt Studio, you'll see three available options:

![img Workflow Studio Layout](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/export_button_updated-290114d3cd51761ea0f3c47474834e10.png)

### 1\. Export as Tool[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/exporting_a_project/#1-export-as-tool "Direct link to 1. Export as Tool")

Export your project as a reusable tool that can be integrated into Workflow Studio. This option allows you to:

*   **Build Task Pipelines** that can pick up documents from network/cloud file systems or object stores, structure them, and push the structured results into cloud file systems
*   **Build Unstructured data ETL Pipelines** that can pick up documents from network/cloud file systems or object stores, structure them, and push the structured results into a data warehouse or database

The exported tool becomes available in Workflow Studio, where you can create comprehensive workflows and deploy them as APIs.

**Export Process:**

*   Export via the "Export" button in Prompt Studio
*   System shows confirmation: "Custom tool exported successfully"
*   Creates a "Parser Tool" in Unstract
*   Tool becomes available in Workflow Studio for creating API/ETL and TASK workflows

### 2\. Export as JSON[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/exporting_a_project/#2-export-as-json "Direct link to 2. Export as JSON")

Export your project configuration as a JSON file that can be easily shared and imported. This option is perfect for:

*   **Sharing projects** with team members or other users
*   **Backup and version control** of your project configurations
*   **Template creation** for similar document types

To use an exported JSON file:

1.  Go to Prompt Studio
2.  Click **Import Project**
3.  Select the exported JSON file

The project will be imported with all its prompts, preamble, postamble and grammar

### 3\. Create/Manage Public Sharing[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/exporting_a_project/#3-createmanage-public-sharing "Direct link to 3. Create/Manage Public Sharing")

The Public Sharing feature in Unstract allows you to share your Prompt Studio projects with anyone in a read-only mode. Recipients can view extraction results and documents without requiring an Unstract account, and without the ability to modify prompts or use LLM profiles. This feature is perfect for stakeholders who need to see extraction results but don't need editing capabilities.

For detailed steps on creating an API deployment, see the [Creating API Deployments from Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/)
.

*   [Export Options](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/exporting_a_project/#export-options)
    *   [1\. Export as Tool](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/exporting_a_project/#1-export-as-tool)
        
    *   [2\. Export as JSON](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/exporting_a_project/#2-export-as-json)
        
    *   [3\. Create/Manage Public Sharing](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/exporting_a_project/#3-createmanage-public-sharing)

---

# Viewing response JSON | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/viewing_response_json/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Now that we've added prompts to extract multiple fields, let's see how to view response JSON in its entirety. Prompt Studio has two separte features that allow you to examine JSON from a single document and also JSON outputs from all documents in the Prompt Studio peoject side-by-side.

Combined Output[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/viewing_response_json/#combined-output "Direct link to Combined Output")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This option can be used to examine the response JSON per document. Let's look at the various options we have in Prompt Studio for this purpose:

![img Viewing JSON response](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/viewing_combined_output-093db1c85243a38f1b6096d25195de4a.png)

Legend:

1.  **Combined Output tab**: switch to this tab to see the combined output for the currently loaded document.
2.  **Show Output Analyzer**: this button brings up the Output Analyzer (see next section for a description of what it is)
3.  **Documents Manager**: this button brings up the Documents Manager, which you can use to switch from one document to the other
4.  **Document Switcher**: this option lets you easily switch from one project document to the next

Output Analyzer[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/viewing_response_json/#output-analyzer "Direct link to Output Analyzer")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Output Analyzer makes it easy to view documents and their full JSON output side-by-side for easy referencing, examination and debugging.

![img Output Analyzer](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/output_analyzer-53f3c793d86dca01e7ec46583798f9c3.png)

*   [Combined Output](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/viewing_response_json/#combined-output)
    
*   [Output Analyzer](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/viewing_response_json/#output-analyzer)

---

# Viewing Text Extractor Output | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/viewing_text_extractor_output/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

While you can view documents you've loaded into a Prompt Studio project in the `Doc View` tab, it is not this document that's passed on to the LLM. Any input document is first send to a Text Extraction service so that raw text is extracted from it. That text is then further processed and sent to the LLM. As a result, the quality of the raw text extraction is very important. Like other things in computing, even for LLMs: garbage in, garbage out.

Below is a side-by-side comparison of `Doc View` and `Raw View`.

![img Raw View](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/raw_view-03b4b7f0c4560b5e063f149e7c487627.png)

Debugging with Raw View[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/viewing_text_extractor_output/#debugging-with-raw-view "Direct link to Debugging with Raw View")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If a prompt response is wrong, one of the very first places you should check is the Raw View to ensure that the output to the LLM is indeed correct. Switch to Raw View and check portions of the input document to see why a prompt response might be the way it is. Especially in OCR mode, a lot of mistakes can be made with text extraction even in the cleanest looking documents.

*   [Debugging with Raw View](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/viewing_text_extractor_output/#debugging-with-raw-view)

---

# Set up LLMWhisperer Text Extractor-v2 | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_text_extractor_v2/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

LLMWhisperer is a text extraction service that extracts and presents data from complex documents (different designs and formats) to LLMs in a way that they can best understand.

**Advantages of using LLMWhisperer v2:**

*   The main benefit is parallel page processing, which results in faster extraction of large documents.
*   Webhooks are introduced to notify once extraction is complete.This will be useful when LLMWhisperer v2 API is used separately.

Getting started with LLMWhisperer Text Extractor-v2[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_text_extractor_v2/#getting-started-with-llmwhisperer-text-extractor-v2 "Direct link to Getting started with LLMWhisperer Text Extractor-v2")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

info

When you sign up for LLM whisperer-v2, you will be provided with lifetime free access with a limit of 100 pages per day.

1.  [Sign-up for an LLM Whisperer account](https://us-central.unstract.com/)
     and choose the product as "**LLM Whisperer**"
    
    ![img LLMWhisperer Text Extractor Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llmwhisperer_v2_1-e4d52522f052cf7517f21ca7335421a7.png)
    
2.  Click on "**Get Started**" to start the free subscription to LLM Whisperer account
    
    ![img LLMWhisperer Text Extractor Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llmwhisperer_v2_2-7f4a901e816d4ea5163fe95fd28a1acc.png)
    
3.  Click on "**API Keys**" and copy the `LLM Gateway URL` and `API Key` as shown below.
    
    ![img LLMWhisperer Text Extractor Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llmwhisperer_v2_3-9bb988302eab5e06f475eada8587853b.png)
    

Setting up the LLM Whisperer connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_text_extractor_v2/#setting-up-the-llm-whisperer-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the LLM Whisperer connector in Unstract")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from LLM Whisperer, we can use it to set up a Text Extractor profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `Text Extractor`
    
*   Click on the `New Text Extractor` button.
    
*   From the list of Text Extractor, choose `LLMWhisperer V2`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img LLMWhisperer Text Extractor Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llmwhisperer_v2_4-c3bf88f9a5f23874d2e7cffab90707a3.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
    
*   Leave the `URL` field to the default value.
    
*   In `Unstract Key`, enter the `key` created in the above section.
    
*   In `Mode`, choose
    
    *   `Native Text` - To process text based files.
    *   `Low Cost` - To process high quality pdf documents.
    *   `High Quality` - To process Medium/low quality scanned PDFs and Handwritten documents.
    *   `Form` - To process pdf with Checkbox and radio button detection
    *   Check the below image for the comparison of modes ![img LLMWhisperer Text Extractor Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llmwhisperer_v2_5-28143586fc22b0c7060a32c3a3a5dd64.png)
*   In `Output Mode`, choose
    
    *   `layout_preserving` - To extract the text line by line.
    *   `text` - Keeps the context of the document in place.
*   `Line Splitter Tolerance` - Default value is 0.4 .Reduce this value to split lines less often, increase to split lines more often. Useful when PDFs have multi-column layout with text in each column that is not aligned.
    
*   `Horizontal Stretch Factor` - Default value is 1 .Increase this value to stretch text horizontally, decrease to compress text horizontally. Useful when multi-column text merge with each other.
    
*   `Page number(s) or range to extract`\- Specify the range of pages to extract (e.g., 1-5, 7, 10-12, 50-). Leave it empty to extract all pages.
    
*   `Page separator` - Specify a pattern to separate the pages in the document `(e.g., <<< {{page_no}} >>>, <<< >>>)`. This pattern will be inserted at the end of every page. Omit page\_no if you don't want to include the page number in the separator.
    
*   `Mark Vertical Lines` & `Mark Horizonatal Lines`
    
    *   Both buttons must be in the same state (either both checked or both unchecked) for the operation to proceed.
    *   If checked, it will extract the tables structure(border and internal lines) with dotted lines.Check this buttons if the table is outlined properly in the document.
*   Leave other values to its default.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new Text Extractor Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with LLMWhisperer Text Extractor-v2](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_text_extractor_v2/#getting-started-with-llmwhisperer-text-extractor-v2)
    
*   [Setting up the LLM Whisperer connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_text_extractor_v2/#setting-up-the-llm-whisperer-connector-in-unstract)

---

# Set up Qdrant Vector Database | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_vector_db/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

info

Qdrant Cloud features a "forever free" managed cloud plan that is plenty enough for us to cover our example use cases.

Sign up for Qdrant Cloud[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_vector_db/#sign-up-for-qdrant-cloud "Direct link to Sign up for Qdrant Cloud")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Go to the Qdrant website and click on the `Cloud` icon on the top right. That should take you to a sign up / login page. If you're logging in for the first time, a new account is automatically created. You should land in a page similar to the one in the screenshot below.

![img Create free Qdrant Vector DB cluster](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_qdrant_vector_db-3c7d022c2291867361f1e9f4550eb7e9.png)

*   In the `Create your first free tier cluster` section, provide `for_unstract` as the cluster name.
*   Click on the `Create free tier cluster` button.
*   You should be greeted with a success message like the one below if everything went well.

![img Qdrant success message](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_qdrant_vector_db_2-241e051d6a4102284a53a81172d01f1a.png)

*   Click on `Continue`.
*   We'll now generate and copy an API key for your new Qdrant account which we'll use in the Unstract Platform.

![img Copy Qdrant API key](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_qdrant_vector_db_3-2877f2d93538fcbb4edd81b3747e5129.png)

*   Copy the API key and keep hold it safely somewhere until we're ready to enter details into the Unstract Platform in subsequent steps. If you lose this key, while you can't retrieve the same, you can always create a new one.
*   Click on `Continue` to eventually go to the dashboard where you can fine details of your newly created free Qdrant cluster.

![img Qdrant cluster details](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_qdrant_vector_db_4-bde565f9fea5049c39fa6bd4607f424e.png)

*   Click on the little arrow / triangle to disclose more details about the cluster.
*   Copy the `Cluster URL`. This along with the already copied API key is what we'll need to set up our new Vector DB Profile on the Unstract Platform. Let's do that now.

Setting up the Qdrant Vector DB in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_vector_db/#setting-up-the-qdrant-vector-db-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Qdrant Vector DB in Unstract")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from Qdrant, we can use it to set up a Vector DB profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign into the Unstract Platform
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `Vector DBs`
*   Click on the `New Vector DB Profile` button
*   From the list of supported Vector DBs, choose `Qdrant`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.

![img Add new Vector Database](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_qdrant_vector_db_5-77c5caabbf8e115085617d44d10fc5e3.png)

*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
*   In the `URL` field, paste in the `Cluster URL` copied earlier from the Qdrant console.
*   In the `API Key` field, paste the API key we created and copied earlier from the Qdrant console.
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new Vector DB Profile for use in your Unstract projects.

We now have everything we need to try out a simple structured data extraction project on the Unstract Platform!

*   [Sign up for Qdrant Cloud](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_vector_db/#sign-up-for-qdrant-cloud)
    
*   [Setting up the Qdrant Vector DB in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_vector_db/#setting-up-the-qdrant-vector-db-in-unstract)

---

# Set up LLMWhisperer Text Extractor-v1 | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_trial_text_extractor/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Reliable answers need reliable input data. This is where LLMWhisperer comes in. It can extract text from text-based PDFs, perform OCR on image based PDFs or even extract text in layout-preserving mode in any PDF, text-based on otherwise.

[LLMWhisperer](https://llmwhisperer.unstract.com/)
 is a specialized document text extractor developed by the Unstract team to specially targeting Large Language Models. It is available as a free service with a generous daily quota, but also available as a paid service should you need to process documents more than the free quota will allow.

In this section, we'll see how to create a free LLMWhisperer account, create an LLMWhisperer API key which we'll use to let the Unstract Platform connect and utilize the LLMWhisperer service. So, let's get started.

*   Head to the [LLMWhisperer portal](https://llmwhisperer.unstract.com/)
     and sign up for a free account after clicking on the "Sign Up" link from the top navigation. It's a pretty standard sign up process. Once you fill out the sign-up form, you should get a verification email. This should contain a link for you to click on, verify and then login with the email and password you specified.

info

When you sign up for LLM whisperer, you will be automatically subscribed to **Tier-1: Free 100 Pages Per Day** plan.By using this plan you can, **Process up to 100 pages a day completely free! No credit card required**.To increase the usage limit,visit [LLM whisperer plans](https://llmwhisperer.unstract.com/products)

*   Once you're logged into the service successfully, let's get you an API key now. Click on "Profile" from the top navigation menu and then click on "show" to reveal the API key.
    
    ![img Choose LLMWhisperer Product](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_llm_whisperer_3-f75a8c8db0b39bab03923c7d624a893f.png)
    

Now that we have an API Key for the LLMWhisperer Text Extractor service, let's connect it to the Unstract Platform.

*   Signin to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `Text Extractor`
    
*   Click on the `New Text Extractor` button
    
*   From the list of supported Text Extractors, choose `LLMWhisperer`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img Choose LLMWhisperer Product](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_llm_whisperer_4-2ca439eedd928d8cb9ad6b27839f6342.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
    
*   Leave the `URL` field to the default value.
    
*   In `Unstract Key`, enter the `key` created in the above section.
    
*   In `Mode`, choose
    
    *   `Native Text` - To process text based files.
    *   `Low Cost` - To process high quality pdf documents.
    *   `High Quality` - To process Medium/low quality scanned PDFs and Handwritten documents.
    *   `Form` - To process pdf with Checkbox and radio button detection
    *   Check the below image for the comparison of modes ![img LLMWhisperer Text Extractor Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llmwhisperer_v2_5-28143586fc22b0c7060a32c3a3a5dd64.png)
*   In `Output Mode`, choose
    
    *   `layout_preserving` - To extract the text line by line.
    *   `text` - Keeps the context of the document in place.
*   `Line Splitter Tolerance` - Default value is 0.4 .Reduce this value to split lines less often, increase to split lines more often. Useful when PDFs have multi-column layout with text in each column that is not aligned.
    
*   `Horizontal Stretch Factor` - Default value is 1 .Increase this value to stretch text horizontally, decrease to compress text horizontally. Useful when multi-column text merge with each other.
    
*   `Page number(s) or range to extract`\- Specify the range of pages to extract (e.g., 1-5, 7, 10-12, 50-). Leave it empty to extract all pages.
    
*   `Page separator` - Specify a pattern to separate the pages in the document `(e.g., <<< {{page_no}} >>>, <<< >>>)`. This pattern will be inserted at the end of every page. Omit page\_no if you don't want to include the page number in the separator.
    
*   `Mark Vertical Lines` & `Mark Horizonatal Lines`
    
    *   Both buttons must be in the same state (either both checked or both unchecked) for the operation to proceed.
    *   If checked, it will extract the tables structure(border and internal lines) with dotted lines.Check this buttons if the table is outlined properly in the document.
*   Leave the `Median Filter Size` and `Gaussian Blue Radius` to their default values.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new Text Extractor Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

This was the last step connecting various dependencies for you to start using the Unstract Platform!

---

# Creating API Deployments from Prompt Studio | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

The API deployment feature allows you to deploy your Prompt Studio project directly as an API without needing to create a separate workflow. This streamlined approach gets your document processing API up and running quickly.

Getting Started[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#getting-started "Direct link to Getting Started")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before creating an API deployment, ensure you have:

*   A completed Prompt Studio project that extracts the intended information well
*   Tested your project with sample documents
*   Verified the output quality meets your requirements

Step-by-Step Process[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#step-by-step-process "Direct link to Step-by-Step Process")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Step 1: Access the Export Options[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#step-1-access-the-export-options "Direct link to Step 1: Access the Export Options")

1.  **Open your Prompt Studio project** - Navigate to the project you want to deploy as an API
2.  **Deploy as API** - Click on Deploy as API and click on proceed.

![img Export API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_api_from_prompt_updated-b80a01350919a2b790f63516c752104c.png)

### Step 2: Deployment Details Setup[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#step-2-deployment-details-setup "Direct link to Step 2: Deployment Details Setup")

Configure the basic details for your API deployment:

1.  **Display Name**: Enter a descriptive name for your API deployment
    
    *   Example: `Credit Card Parser API`
2.  **Description**: Add a detailed description of what this API deployment does
    
    *   Example: `API deployment for Credit Card Parser`
3.  **API Name**: Specify the unique API name (this will be part of your API endpoint)
    
    *   Example: `credit_card_parser_1752483494579`
    *   Note: This name must be unique across your deployment
4.  Click **Next** to proceed to tool settings
    

![img Export API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/api_deploy_from_prompt_setup-2e0bf7efd083e67f27c72ce54f304983.png)

### Step 3: Tool Settings Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#step-3-tool-settings-configuration "Direct link to Step 3: Tool Settings Configuration")

Configure the tool settings for your API deployment:

1.  **Configure Tool Settings**: Enter the tool configuration name
    
    *   Example: `credit_card_parser`
2.  **Challenger LLM**: Select the LLM to use for LLMChallenge from the dropdown menu
    
    *   Description: LLM to use for LLMChallenge
3.  **Enable Options**: Check the boxes for features you want to enable:
    
    *   **Enable LLMChallenge**: Enables LLMChallenge functionality
    *   **Enable highlight**: Enables highlight feature
    *   **Enable SummarizedExtraction**: Enables SummarizedExtraction
    *   **Enable SinglePass Extraction**: Enables SinglePass Extraction
4.  Click **Create Deployment** to finalize the API deployment
    

![img Export API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/api_deploy_from_prompt_tool_settings-4f4e97b2991c51f7305e7908c3974bcb.png)

Navigation Options[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#navigation-options "Direct link to Navigation Options")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Previous**: Go back to the Deployment Details step
*   **Cancel**: Cancel the API deployment process
*   **Create Deployment**: Complete the API deployment

### Step 4: API Deployment Success[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#step-4-api-deployment-success "Direct link to Step 4: API Deployment Success")

After clicking **Create Deployment**, you'll see a success confirmation screen with the following information:

#### Deployment Confirmation[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#deployment-confirmation "Direct link to Deployment Confirmation")

🎉 **Your API deployment has been created successfully!**

The success screen displays:

*   **API Name**: The unique identifier for your API (e.g., `credit_card_parser_1752486232785`)
*   **Display Name**: Your chosen display name (e.g., `Credit Card Parser API`)
*   **Description**: The description you provided (e.g., `API deployment for Credit Card Parser`)

![img Export API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/api_deploy_from_prompt_success-9bdb9aaf46670c775da3684d55e72827.png)

#### Next Steps Options[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#next-steps-options "Direct link to Next Steps Options")

**What would you like to do next?**

You have two options to proceed:

1.  **View API Deployment** (Primary action)
    
    *   Takes you to the API deployment details page
    *   Shows deployment status, endpoint information, and usage details
2.  **Configure Workflow** (Secondary action)
    
    *   Allows you to create additional workflows using this deployed API
    *   Useful for building more complex processing pipelines

Click **Close** to dismiss the success dialog and return to Prompt Studio, or choose one of the next step options to continue working with your newly deployed API.

Using Your Deployed API[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#using-your-deployed-api "Direct link to Using Your Deployed API")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![API Deployments Page](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/api_deploy_from_prompt_postman-79417ebb86a525fd3165b22661a7cec4.png)

Once your API is deployed, you can access it from the API Deployments page:

1.  Navigate to **API Deployments** from the left sidebar
2.  Find your deployed API in the table showing API Name, Description, API Endpoint, Workflow, and status
3.  Click on the **Actions** button (⋮) for your API deployment
4.  Select **Download Postman Collection** to get a ready-to-use Postman collection for testing your API

The Postman collection will include all necessary API endpoints and configuration to start testing your document processing API immediately.

*   [Getting Started](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#getting-started)
    
*   [Step-by-Step Process](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#step-by-step-process)
    *   [Step 1: Access the Export Options](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#step-1-access-the-export-options)
        
    *   [Step 2: Deployment Details Setup](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#step-2-deployment-details-setup)
        
    *   [Step 3: Tool Settings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#step-3-tool-settings-configuration)
        
*   [Navigation Options](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#navigation-options)
    *   [Step 4: API Deployment Success](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#step-4-api-deployment-success)
        
*   [Using Your Deployed API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/#using-your-deployed-api)

---

# Optional: Set up a Challenger LLM | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_eval_llm/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

info

LLMEval is only part of Unstract Cloud and Unstract Enterprise offerings. This section assumes that you have a working Google Cloud Platform (GCP) account, using which we'll configure a Gemini Pro LLM for use with the Unstract Platform as an evaluator LLM.

Unstract Cloud and Enterprise editions feature LLMEval, an advanced feature that improves accuracy by employing a second LLM to evaluate the output of the first one that does the structuring. In this section, we'll look at how to set up Google's Gemini Pro LLM for use in the Unstract Platform as an Evaluator LLM.

note

Please note that it is always a good idea to choose an LLM from a completely different provenance or origin as the evaluator LLM as compared to the LLM that is used to structure documents. When two LLMs from very different origins that feature potentially different internal architectures agree that a field extraction is right, the probability that it is indeed right, is high.

Set up a GCP Service Account for Vertex AI[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_eval_llm/#set-up-a-gcp-service-account-for-vertex-ai "Direct link to Set up a GCP Service Account for Vertex AI")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

What we're looking for is to gain access to Google's Gemini Pro LLM. It is made available under the Vertex AI umbrella. We'll create a service account in GCP which will have permissions to use Vertex AI and thus the Gemini Pro APIs.

Although it might seem as if this is a long and confusing process, it is not if you understand what we're trying to achieve. GCP uses Service Accounts to associate roles and services. It's a great security practice that lets you granularly control which services a Service Account has access to while also controlling who has access to it. Here, we'll create a Service Account with access to the Vertex APIs. We'll create and download a private key with which we're able to access that Service Account. Finally, any service you'll want to use under GCP has to be explicitly enabled on a per project basis. After we do all this, we'll add the Gemini Pro LLM to Unstract.

Let's get started.

info

Everything described here is done in the context of a project. Make sure you've selected the right project to be the active one in the GCP console.

First off, let's create a new Service Account. Go to:

`Main menu` (top left hamburger menu) 🞂 `IAM & Admin` 🞂 `Service Accounts` 🞂 `Create Service Account`. You should now see a screen similar to the following:

![img Create new Service Account](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_gcp_service_account_1-8be972b2b63b80b85a7514d6e949c7a2.png)

*   For `Service account name`, enter `Unstract`
*   For `Service account ID`, enter `unstract`
*   For `Service account description`, enter `Unstract LLM Profile`
*   Click on `Create and Continue`

Now, we'll grant this service account access to the Vertex AI services.

![img Grant service account access to Vertex AI](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_gcp_service_account_2-7f3c9e7508a8613ca119b3445b047e3b.png)

*   In the `Select a role` field, type `vertex ai service agent` and choose it from the list.
*   This should take you to the optional next step.

![img Grant user access to this service account](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_gcp_service_account_3-4dab4547f61f72d25eaccbbcb56f3684.png)

*   We will ignore this optional step by simple clicking on `Done`.
*   This should take us to the service accounts list page which should look similar to the screenshot below.

![img service accounts list page](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_gcp_service_account_4-363aef06dbce1de82de1f4b52dcdbf61.png)

*   You might recall that we named our service account `Unstract`. Find it and click on the `Actions` disclosure, which reveals a menu from where you can click on `Manage keys` to create a key which will allow us to access this service account.

![img Manage service account keys](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_gcp_service_account_5-8fba4246669964c3e4798e1f702b9882.png)

*   We'll need to create a key to access this service account. For this, click on the `Add key` button and choose `Create new key`.
*   This should bring you to the `Create private key for "Unstract"` dialog.

![img Choose private key type](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_gcp_service_account_6-c9521ff20c85620f73208d0d6a9b329a.png)

*   Choose the key type to be `JSON` and click `Create`. This should download the key to your computer.
    
*   Excellent! We now have a way to access the service account. We now need to enable Vertex AI for your in your GCP project.
    
*   Go to `Main menu` (top left hamburger menu) 🞂 `APIs & Services` 🞂 `Enabled APIs & services`. You should now see a screen similar to the following:
    

![img Go to Enabled APIs and services](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_gcp_service_account_7-d35620dcb6682f101e4784de730b8864.png)

*   You should see a screen similar to the one below:

![img Go to Enabled APIs and services](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_gcp_service_account_8-43a583a0a9fd893713ae107d89d5c2f1.png)

*   Now, click on the `Enable APIs and Services` button. You'll be taken to a screen where you can search for APIs and services that you can enable for your project, much like the one below:

![img Go to Enabled APIs and services](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_gcp_service_account_9-ea4883b4d3e83783938d8f4d31d140a8.png)

*   Search for "vertext ai api" and select it from the list below. You should now be taken to the Vertex AI API page.

![img Go to Enabled APIs and services](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_gcp_service_account_10-63669aabf36c1b15b01c861cc34a50c4.png)

*   Click on the `Enable` button to enable the Vertex AI APIs for your project.
*   This concludes our work in the GCP console. Let's now move on to the Unstract console.
*   Open the downloaded private key in a text editor. It should look something like this:

![img Go to Enabled APIs and services](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_gcp_service_account_11-4b696369c4de96407c9d073cef3afe26.png)

*   You'll need to select the whole text and copy it, you'll also need to copy the value of the `project_id` key as well, which in example is `smiling-rythm-168501`. Yours will be something else, of course.
*   Sign into the Unstract Platform
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `LLMs`
*   Click on the `New LLM Profile` button
*   From the list of supported LLMs, choose `VertexAI`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.

![img Go to Enabled APIs and services](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_gcp_service_account_12-f34a3ab69dddeb64632c210b3ee0cae9.png)

*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
*   In `Credentials`field, paste in the whole downloaded service account key we copied from the text editor earlier.
*   For `Models` field,enter `gemini-1.5-pro`.For more models refer to [Gemini Models](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/learn/models#gemini-models)
    .
*   For `Max output tokens`,enter `8192`. For more models and its token limit refer to [Gemini Models](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/learn/models#gemini-models)
    .
*   For `Project`, enter the Google project name in which the Vertex AI is enabled (or) Check the `project_id` in credentials json.
*   Leave `Max Retries` value to its default.
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new Evaluator LLM Profile for use in your Unstract projects.

*   [Set up a GCP Service Account for Vertex AI](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/setup_accounts/set_up_eval_llm/#set-up-a-gcp-service-account-for-vertex-ai)

---

# On-Prem Release Notes - 2025 | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2025/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Jump to version:Select versionv0.158.4v0.158.0v0.148.3v0.139.3v0.130.0v0.128.1v0.128.0v0.122.8v0.118.18v0.116.20v0.116.18v0.113.0

### v0.148.3 — January 19, 2026[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2025/#v0-148-3 "Direct link to v0.148.3 — January 19, 2026")

**New Features**

*   **SDK / platform evolution**: Migration from the legacy SDK to the new SDK (SDK1), now using LiteLLM instead of LlamaIndex, with retry and exponential backoff in the SDK
*   **API deployment rate limiting** for cloud and on-prem (per-org limits, cache-based locking). See [API Deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
     for details.
*   **Maximum file execution count** configuration with [file history](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/)
     viewer
*   **Improved execution tracking** configuration and worker rollout strategy
*   **Organization-level sharing** for workflows, adapters, pipelines ([ETL](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/)
     and Task), [API deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
    , and [Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_intro/)
     projects
*   **Packet-processing based HITL** manual review and workflows. See [HITL documentation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
     for details.
*   **HITL rule engine support** for [API deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
    . See [HITL Rule Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/)
     for details.
*   **Word-level confidence** with rule-engine support and confidence highlighting in JSON view
*   **Bulk download** of HITL-approved records. See [Bulk Download](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/bulk_download/)
     for details.
*   **Okta enterprise SAML connection** using Auth0, including on-prem [Okta](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/)
     integration
*   **Enterprise SSO organization mapping** for custom domains
*   **Custom data support in Prompt Studio**, including [custom data variables](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/)
    
*   **Export reminders** when a [Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_export_project/)
     project is already used in deployments
*   **Support for [XLSM files](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/supported_file_types/list_of_file_types_supported/)
    ** in Prompt Studio, workflows, and pipelines
*   **Improved prompt logs and confidence display** in Prompt Studio
*   **Search capability** across LLMs, Vector databases, embeddings, and connectors pages
*   **Auto-refresh and controls** for execution logs
*   **SIGTERM handling** in sidecar tool containers
*   **Security headers and HTTP method restrictions** added to nginx
*   **Updated worker deployment strategies**: pod disruption budgets, autoscaling annotations, and rollout tuning

**Fixes & Improvements**

*   **Active file cache** and related settings to avoid duplicate file processing and race conditions in distributed workers
*   **Worker and Celery hardening**: improved broker heartbeats, chord retry behavior, and duplicate-detection handling
*   **Improved PgBouncer, RabbitMQ, and autoscaling** configurations to reduce timeouts and make rollouts safer
*   **Safer migrations and error handling** for database and destination connectors, with better surfacing of errors in the UI
*   **Reliability and precision improvements** for MariaDB, BigQuery, Google Drive, and other connectors
*   **Clearer [File History](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/)
     UI**, including improved column labels and correct "last attempt" timestamps
*   **Improved platform settings** menu behaviour and navigation
*   **Better surfacing** of underlying library and connector errors to users

* * *

### v0.139.3 — November 4, 2025[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2025/#v0-139-3 "Direct link to v0.139.3 — November 4, 2025")

**New Features**

*   **Organization-level sharing** for workflows, adapters, and [Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_intro/)
     projects
*   **Smart table extractor** implementation for Excel files in [API deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
    
*   **Packet processing support** for [HITL](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
     workflows
*   **Celery workers improvements**
*   **Custom [Anthropic](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anthropic_llm/)
     API key and LLMWhisperer key** support from request headers
*   **FIFO/LIFO file processing order** with round-robin batching

**Fixes & Improvements**

*   Improved settings navigation and modal component styling
*   Added retry logic with exponential backoff to SDK1
*   Optimized Docker images and addressed high-priority vulnerabilities
*   Fixed API deployment and workflow execution directory cleanup after execution
*   General performance optimizations and stability improvements

**Helm Configuration Changes**

*   `INTERNAL_SERVICE_API_KEY` now required as global anchor for worker communication
*   Complete worker secrets section added including database credentials, Redis credentials for both main and manual review, RabbitMQ/Celery broker credentials, and file storage credentials (`WORKFLOW_EXECUTION_FILE_STORAGE_CREDENTIALS`, `API_FILE_STORAGE_CREDENTIALS`)

* * *

### v0.130.0 — July 24, 2025[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2025/#v0-130-0 "Direct link to v0.130.0 — July 24, 2025")

**New Features**

*   **Enhanced [HITL](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
     management capabilities** and [HQR auto approval](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_auto_approval/)
     functionality
*   **Push from API to HITL** support
*   **Auth0 Cache TTL variables** added
*   **Configurable [ETL](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/)
     schedule intervals** with conflict prevention and 30-minute support
*   **Confirmation modal** to display existing [API deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
     before creating new deployment

**Fixes & Improvements**

*   Fixed Mistral AI test connection failures for certain models
*   Enhanced validation error handling across the platform
*   Improved authentication API rate limiting and throttling prevention
*   Replaced Celery worker autoscale with concurrency configuration in Helm values
*   Fixed Django migration conflict for AutoApprovalSettings model
*   Deprecated connector instance fields and refactored endpoint handling
*   Fixed ToolMetadataNotFound error when `use_file_history` is true

* * *

### v0.128.1 — July 18, 2025[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2025/#v0-128-1 "Direct link to v0.128.1 — July 18, 2025")

*   Multiple fixes and improvements
*   **Fix**: Export tool issues in [API deployment](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
     (Prompt Studio) — dynamic tool export and metadata handling
*   **Fix**: Platform key support in text extractor

* * *

### v0.128.0 — July 10, 2025[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2025/#v0-128-0 "Direct link to v0.128.0 — July 10, 2025")

**New Features**

*   **Text and image file support** in [HITL](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
    
*   **Improved file deduplication** in [ETL/Task pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/)
    
*   **File validation** in API and ETL workflows (based on extension and MIME type)
*   **Dynamic LLM profile override** in [API deployment](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
     invocation
*   **Enhanced [preamble/postamble](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/preamble_and_postamble/)
     UX**: auto-resize text areas and expandable view
*   **Drag-and-drop file upload** support in [Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/adding_and_managing_documents/)
    

Known Issues

Error occurs when exporting a tool as an API deployment from Prompt Studio (this is a new feature; the existing way of API deployment is unaffected).

* * *

### v0.122.8 — June 10, 2025[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2025/#v0-122-8 "Direct link to v0.122.8 — June 10, 2025")

Deprecated

Do not deploy this version. Use **v0.127.1** or later instead.

*   Introduced **RabbitMQ** as Celery broker for improved worker robustness
*   **ETL parallel processing** support with batches
*   Helm values changes required: add RabbitMQ credentials and YAML anchor pointers under backend, runner, and prompt secrets
*   Disabled probes for Celery due to stability issues

* * *

# Embeddings supported by Unstract | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/index/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

[📄️ Azure OpenAI Embedding\
--------------------------\
\
Azure OpenAI Embeddings are a way to convert text data into numerical representations that capture semantic meaning. This allows you to find similar text content or compare documents based on their ideas, even if the wording differs.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/azure_openai_embedding/)

[📄️ OpenAI Embeddings\
---------------------\
\
OpenAI embedding is used for converting text into numerical representations, enabling machines to understand and process language efficiently in various natural language processing tasks such as sentiment analysis, text classification, and language translation.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/openai_embedding/)

[📄️ Palm Embeddings\
-------------------\
\
Palm embedding is a technique that converts text into numerical vectors, capturing meaning and context. Similar text snippets have close embeddings, allowing for tasks like semantic search and text classification.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/palm_embedding/)

# Vector DBs supported by Unstract | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/index/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

[📄️ Milvus\
----------\
\
img Milvus Vector DB Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/milvus_vectordb/)

[📄️ Pinecone Vector Database\
----------------------------\
\
Pinecone is a cloud-based vector database designed for machine learning applications. It excels at storing and searching high-dimensional vector data, enabling fast and accurate similarity searches for tasks like recommendation systems and image recognition.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/pinecone_vectordb/)

[📄️ Postgres Vector Database\
----------------------------\
\
Postgres itself isn't a vector database, but the open-source pgvector extension lets it store and search vector data efficiently, like finding similar text embeddings.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/postgres_vectordb/)

[📄️ Qdrant Vector Database\
--------------------------\
\
Qdrant vectorDB is a high-performance database for storing and searching high-dimensional data like image and text encodings. It lets you find similar items using vector similarity search and filter results based on additional data.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/qdrant_vectordb/)

[📄️ Weaviate Vector Database\
----------------------------\
\
Weaviate is an open-source vector database that stores both objects and vectors, allowing for the combination of vector search with structured filtering with the fault tolerance and scalability of a cloud-native database.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/weaviate_vectordb/)

# LLMs supported by Unstract | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/index/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

[📄️ Anthropic\
-------------\
\
Anthropic LLM, also known as Claude, is a large language model focused on safety and helpfulness. It can generate text, answer questions, and perform many other tasks with a focus on avoiding harmful or misleading outputs.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anthropic_llm/)

[📄️ Anyscale\
------------\
\
Running your own GPU instance can be a pretty expensive affair. Services like Anyscale or Replicate allow you to be charged for the exact duration of your API calls and let you use open source LLMs on their infrastructure.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anyscale_llm/)

[📄️ Azure AI Foundry\
--------------------\
\
Azure AI Foundry is Microsoft's unified platform for building, deploying, and managing AI models. It provides access to a wide catalog of models from various providers — including OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, Meta, and more — all deployable as managed endpoints within your Azure subscription.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_ai_foundry_llm/)

[📄️ Azure OpenAI\
----------------\
\
Azure OpenAI LLM is a cloud-based service from Microsoft that provides access to OpenAI's powerful large language models (LLMs). These AI systems can generate text, translate languages, write different kinds of creative content, and answer your questions in an informative way, all within the secure environment of Microsoft Azure.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_openai_llm/)

[📄️ Gemini (Google AI Studio)\
-----------------------------\
\
Gemini is Google's family of multimodal large language models, accessible through Google AI Studio. It can generate text, analyze documents, reason over complex inputs, and handle a wide range of tasks across text, code, and images.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_llm/)

[📄️ Google / VertexAI Gemini Pro\
--------------------------------\
\
Vertex AI Gemini Pro is a powerful large language model (LLM) by Google, accessible through their Vertex AI platform. It excels in various tasks like text generation, translation, and code analysis, making it a versatile tool for developers and businesses.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_pro_llm/)

[📄️ Mistral AI\
--------------\
\
Mistral LLM is a powerful AI model trained on massive amounts of text data. It can generate different creative text formats, translate languages, and answer your questions in an informative way. It's known for its strong performance on text-based tasks.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/mistral_llm/)

[📄️ Ollama\
----------\
\
Ollama is a user-friendly platform that lets you run powerful large language models (LLMs) directly on your own computer, even utilizing your graphics card for faster processing.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/)

[📄️ Ollama in Unstract Open source\
----------------------------------\
\
Ollama is a user-friendly platform that lets you run powerful large language models (LLMs) directly on your own computer, even utilizing your graphics card for faster processing.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm_in_oss/)

[📄️ OpenAI\
----------\
\
OpenAI LLMs are powerful AI systems trained on massive amounts of text data, allowing them to generate text, translate languages, write different kinds of creative content, and answer your questions in an informative way.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/openai_llm/)

[📄️ Google Palm\
---------------\
\
Google PaLM LLM (Large Language Model) is a super-sized AI model trained on a massive dataset of text and code. It can understand and generate human language, translate languages, write different kinds of creative content, and answer your questions in an informative way. It's known for its ability to learn new things from just a few examples.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/palm_llm/)

[📄️ Amazon Bedrock\
------------------\
\
Amazon Bedrock is a fully managed service that provides access to powerful foundation models (FMs) from leading AI providers like Anthropic, Meta, and Amazon. It allows developers to easily build and scale generative AI applications without managing infrastructure.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/)

[📄️ Amazon Bedrock Cross-Region Inference\
-----------------------------------------\
\
📘 Introduction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/)

# Prompt Studio: Introduction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_intro/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Current Intelligent Document Processing or IDP system are based on classical machine learning and NLP technology and have several, very limiting disadvantages:

*   The most difficult part of current IDP systems is manually annotating fields for extraction.
*   Handling variations within the same document type needs more manual annotations. The more different the variants look, the more annotations are needed.
*   There is a need to create a "training set" manually to identify and deal with document variants.
*   Dealing with extraction of fields from documents that involve natural language text, not just from simple documents like invoices/passports/driver’s licenses/standard forms is either exceedingly cumbersome or outright impossible in practice
*   Accurately extracting structured data from documents that may be dozens of pages long with the information to be extracted scattered across the document is a huge challenge even with features like Segmentation.
*   The need for post-processing is very common. Date formats, stripping out of extra characters need to be handled separately.

Let's call these IDP 1.0 systems.

Intelligent Document Processing 2.0[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_intro/#intelligent-document-processing-20 "Direct link to Intelligent Document Processing 2.0")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By leveraging the power of Large Language Models, computers can deal with unstructured documents not unlike how humans deal with them—with natural language.

Given the input or source document, prompts that describe what fields to extract, how to format them and the schema to output the response in, we can indeed achieve structured data extraction from unstructured documents pretty much how a human would approach it. This was not possible before LLMs became commonplace. In essence, we’re relying on two powerful attributes Large Language Models have to achieve structured data extraction from unstructured documents:

*   Their ability to reason
*   They ability to follow instructions

With these abilities as a foundation, Large Language Models usher in the era of IDP 2.0.

A purpose-build prompt engineering environment[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_intro/#a-purpose-build-prompt-engineering-environment "Direct link to A purpose-build prompt engineering environment")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Central to the concept of Large Language Models enabling IDP 2.0 is the ability to use prompt engineering so we can:

*   Extract the fields we need from unstructured documents while also formatting them with just the use of natural language.
*   Specify a schema for the extraction through the use of prompt engineering.

![img Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio-1d330fad5f477c34e710771d5f9c5a6b.png)

In subsequent sections, let's look at Prompt Studio in detail.

*   [Intelligent Document Processing 2.0](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_intro/#intelligent-document-processing-20)
    
*   [A purpose-build prompt engineering environment](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_intro/#a-purpose-build-prompt-engineering-environment)

---

# Document Data Extraction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/document_data_extraction/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Prompt Studio's primary reason for existence is so that you can develop the necessary prompts for document data extraction. It is a purpose-built environment that makes this very easy for you. The document sample, its variants, the prompts you are developing, outputs from different LLMs, the schema you're developing, costing details of the extraction and various tools that let you measure the effectiveness of your prompts are just a click away and easily accessible. Prompt Studio is designed for effective and high speed development and iteration of prompts for document data extraction.

Not built for other types of prompt engineering[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/document_data_extraction/#not-built-for-other-types-of-prompt-engineering "Direct link to Not built for other types of prompt engineering")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

While prompt engineering is used in many different areas, Prompt Studio specifically, is purpose built for document data extraction use cases. There are many features in Prompt Studio that make it easy to do prompt engineering for document data extraction, while also taking care of aspects like accuracy (see [LLMChallenge](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_intro/)
) cost management (see [SinglePass Extraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/single_pass_extraction_intro/)
 and [SummarizedExtraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/summarized_extraction_intro/)
), dealing with document variants (see [Prompt Coverage](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_coverage/)
 and [Output Analyzer](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/)
) and more.

*   [Not built for other types of prompt engineering](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/document_data_extraction/#not-built-for-other-types-of-prompt-engineering)

---

# What is Prompt Studio? | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/what_is_prompt_studio/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Prompt Studio is a purpose-built prompt engineering environment for document data extraction. It has two primary functions:

1.  It allows you to write generic prompts to extract data from any unstructured document that work across variants of the document type.
2.  It allows you to define a schema for the extraction

In the following sections, we'll look at the details of how Prompt Studio affords these to you, the user.

What Prompt Studio is not[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/what_is_prompt_studio/#what-prompt-studio-is-not "Direct link to What Prompt Studio is not")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To better understand Prompt Studio, it's important to understand what it's not.

While Prompt Studio provides an easy way to quickly check if Unstract is able to extract data from some document, it's not what it's meant for. Like mentioned above, the primary purpose of Prompt Studio is to allow you to develop generic prompts while also allowing you to specify a schema for the extraction. To do the actual extraction, the Prompt Studio project you develop is deployed as one of the following:

*   [API Deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
    
*   [ETL Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/set_up_etl_pipeline/)
    
*   [Human Quality Review](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
    
*   [Task Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/set_up_task_pipeline/)
    

warning

Please note that Prompt Studio is not for document data extraction. It is for developing your prompts and for specifying your extraction schema. Please use API Deployments, ETL Pipelines or Human Quality Review for the actual data extraction.

*   [What Prompt Studio is not](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/what_is_prompt_studio/#what-prompt-studio-is-not)

---

# Create new Prompt Studio project | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_new_prompt_studio_project/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Follow these steps to create a new project in Unstract.

### Step 1: Navigate to "Prompt Studio"[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_new_prompt_studio_project/#step-1-navigate-to-prompt-studio "Direct link to Step 1: Navigate to "Prompt Studio"")

*   On the left sidebar, under the **BUILD** section, click on **Prompt Studio**.
*   This opens the "Prompt Studio" screen where you can manage your projects.

### Step 2: Click on "New Project"[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_new_prompt_studio_project/#step-2-click-on-new-project "Direct link to Step 2: Click on "New Project"")

*   On the top right corner of the "Prompt Studio" screen, click the **\+ New Project** button.

### Step 3: Fill in Project Details[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_new_prompt_studio_project/#step-3-fill-in-project-details "Direct link to Step 3: Fill in Project Details")

*   A dialog box titled **Add Tool Information** will appear.
*   Fill in the following fields:
    *   **Tool Name**: Enter the name of your project (e.g., Document Extraction)
    *   **Author/Org Name**: Provide the name of the author or organization (e.g., Unstract)
    *   **Description**: Add a brief description of the project (e.g., To extract the data from unstructured documents)

### Step 4: (Optional) Add an Icon[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_new_prompt_studio_project/#step-4-optional-add-an-icon "Direct link to Step 4: (Optional) Add an Icon")

*   Click **Choose Icon** to upload or select an icon for your project.

### Step 5: Save the Project[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_new_prompt_studio_project/#step-5-save-the-project "Direct link to Step 5: Save the Project")

*   After entering the details, click the **Save** button to create the project.

* * *

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_new_project-2d8db74a69742e9d5c0aa180db923a23.png)

**Note:** You can cancel the process anytime by clicking the **Cancel** button in the dialog box.

*   [Step 1: Navigate to "Prompt Studio"](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_new_prompt_studio_project/#step-1-navigate-to-prompt-studio)
    
*   [Step 2: Click on "New Project"](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_new_prompt_studio_project/#step-2-click-on-new-project)
    
*   [Step 3: Fill in Project Details](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_new_prompt_studio_project/#step-3-fill-in-project-details)
    
*   [Step 4: (Optional) Add an Icon](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_new_prompt_studio_project/#step-4-optional-add-an-icon)
    
*   [Step 5: Save the Project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_new_prompt_studio_project/#step-5-save-the-project)
