# Setting up LLM profiles | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/llm_profiles/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Overview[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/llm_profiles/#overview "Direct link to Overview")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Unstract provides a sample profile with pre-configured LLM, embedding, vectorDB, and Text Extractor components with a quota of 1 million tokens. Users can also configure their own custom LLM profiles with preferred components.

Accessing LLM Profile Settings[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/llm_profiles/#accessing-llm-profile-settings "Direct link to Accessing LLM Profile Settings")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Click on the Settings gear icon (⚙️) in the top-right corner of the Unstract interface.
2.  In the Settings menu, select "LLM Profiles" from the left sidebar.

Default Sample Profile[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/llm_profiles/#default-sample-profile "Direct link to Default Sample Profile")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By default, Unstract provides a sample profile with the following pre-configured components:

*   **Name**: sample profile
*   **LLM**: Azure GPT-4o LLM Free Trial
*   **Embedding Model**: Azure OpenAI Free Trial Embedding
*   **Vector Database**: Postgres Free Trial VectorDB
*   **Text Extractor**: LLMWhisperer Text Extractor Free Trial

Creating a New LLM Profile[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/llm_profiles/#creating-a-new-llm-profile "Direct link to Creating a New LLM Profile")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  From the LLM Profile Settings page, click the "Add New LLM Profile" button.
    
2.  Fill in the required fields in the form:
    
    *   **Name**: Enter a name for your profile (e.g., "Profile 1")
    *   **LLM**: Select your preferred Language Model (e.g., Azure GPT-4o LLM Free Trial). Refer [LLM's section](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/index/)
         to configure your preferred LLM.
    *   **Vector Database**: Choose your preferred vector database (e.g., Postgres Free Trial VectorDB).Refer [VectorDB section](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/index/)
         to configure your preferred VectorDB.
    *   **Embedding Model**: Select your preferred embedding model (e.g., Azure OpenAI Free Trial Embedding).Refer [Embedding section](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/index/)
         to configure your preferred Embedding.
    *   **Text Extractor**: Choose your preferred text extraction tool (e.g., LLMWhisperer Text Extractor Free Trial).Refer [Text Extractor section](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/text_extractors/index/)
         to configure your preferred Text Extractor.
    *   **Chunk Size**: Set the chunk size (set to 0 if documents are small)
    *   **Overlap**: Configure the overlap value
    *   Before setting **Chunk Size** and **Overlap**, refer [Chunk and overlap section](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/chunking/)
        .
3.  Advanced settings are applicable only when **Chunk Size** and **Overlap** are set.
    

*   **Retrieval Strategy**:
    *   **Simple**: Based on your prompt, the chunks will be retrieved.
    *   **Subquestion**: LLM will generate a set of specific subquestions from the prompt which can be used to retrieve relevant context from vector db. Fewer questions for simpler prompt, with a maximum subquestions of 10.
*   **Matching count Limit(Similarity top-k)**: Based on the count, the top number of chunks will be sent as an input context to LLM. For example if top-k is 3, then first 3 chunks will be sent to LLM as input context.

4.  Click the "Add" button to create your new LLM profile.

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_add_llm_profiles-31e3f95202184876acab4533f7a54bdc.png) ![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_add_llm_profiles_1-257d63455678d92fc6af09bf8d87558b.png)

Managing LLM Profiles[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/llm_profiles/#managing-llm-profiles "Direct link to Managing LLM Profiles")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   You can edit existing profiles using the edit button next to each profile.
*   Set a profile as default by selecting the radio button in the "Select Default" column.

*   [Overview](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/llm_profiles/#overview)
    
*   [Accessing LLM Profile Settings](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/llm_profiles/#accessing-llm-profile-settings)
    
*   [Default Sample Profile](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/llm_profiles/#default-sample-profile)
    
*   [Creating a New LLM Profile](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/llm_profiles/#creating-a-new-llm-profile)
    
*   [Managing LLM Profiles](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/llm_profiles/#managing-llm-profiles)

---

# Creating and managing prompts | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/creating_and_managing_prompts/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Adding a New Prompt[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/creating_and_managing_prompts/#adding-a-new-prompt "Direct link to Adding a New Prompt")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Open the Prompt studio project. At the bottom of the Document Parser section, click on the "+ Prompt" button.
    
2.  After clicking the "+ Prompt" button, you can enter a new prompt in the text field that appears. Each prompt should have a **key** and the **Question**.
    
    *   The example shown in the screenshot has prompts like:
        *   "account\_holder\_name" is the `key` (followed by a question "What is the name of the account holder?")
        *   "balance" is the `key` (followed by a question "What is the new available balance?")

info

The `Key` should follow JSON standards: only lowercase characters, numbers, and underscores are allowed.

3.  Run the prompt to get the results.

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_add_prompt-f9590cfed8cdf5d3320d581fc902488b.png)

Creating Effective Prompts[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/creating_and_managing_prompts/#creating-effective-prompts "Direct link to Creating Effective Prompts")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Enter a descriptive prompt key**: First, enter a descriptive key for your prompt (e.g., "account\_holder\_name").
    
2.  **Add a descriptive question**: Below the key, add a clear question that explains what information you're trying to extract (e.g., "What is the name of the account holder?").
    

Working with Prompt Results[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/creating_and_managing_prompts/#working-with-prompt-results "Direct link to Working with Prompt Results")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   The system will process your prompt using the configured LLM (in the example, it shows "gpt-4o").
*   Results appear below each prompt (e.g., "JOSEPH PAULSON" for the account holder name).
*   You can see performance metrics like **token usage, processing time, and cost**.
*   Prompts can be enabled/disabled using the toggle button labeled "Enabled".

*   [Adding a New Prompt](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/creating_and_managing_prompts/#adding-a-new-prompt)
    
*   [Creating Effective Prompts](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/creating_and_managing_prompts/#creating-effective-prompts)
    
*   [Working with Prompt Results](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/creating_and_managing_prompts/#working-with-prompt-results)

# SinglePass Extraction Introduction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/single_pass_extraction_intro/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Enterprise Feature

This feature is available in [Unstract Cloud Edition](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/cloud_edition/)
 and [Unstract On-Premise Edition](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/editions/on_prem_edition/)
.

In Prompt Studio, when you are working on field extractions, it's way easier for you to think of one prompt as something used for extracting one field, or a group of closely related fields (like the individual items in a line item) from a document. This also means that you can tweak a prompt to make it better without having to worry about it affecting the quality of other prompts. This one to one relationship between a prompt and a field is a design feature of Prompt Studio that makes long-term maintenance of projects with dozens of complex extractions easier.

Cost vs. prompt complexity[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/single_pass_extraction_intro/#cost-vs-prompt-complexity "Direct link to Cost vs. prompt complexity")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By default, each prompt in Unstract is run against the full context of the input document. This means that, if there are a lot of prompts, it can get pretty expensive in terms of LLM token costs. One obvious technique is to combine all your prompts into one huge prompt that outputs a large JSON with all the fields you need extracted. This works, but like we discussed earlier, this prompt can become unmaintainable very quickly, especially if you need to tweak prompts from time to time because you keep seeing new variants of the input document. How can we strike a balance?

SinglePass Extraction to the rescue[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/single_pass_extraction_intro/#singlepass-extraction-to-the-rescue "Direct link to SinglePass Extraction to the rescue")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Unstract uses the default Large Language Model configured in the Prompt Studio project to combine all your prompts into a single, large prompt automatically. This has the advantage that you can continue to easily maintain smaller, atomic prompts, while, during actual deployment, all your prompts are combined using an LLM into a single prompt that is run against the full context of your input document.

Limitations[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/single_pass_extraction_intro/#limitations "Direct link to Limitations")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

What we're doing with prompt engineering essentially is instructing the LLM we're using to fetch and format the data we need to extract. However, there can be simple prompts like the following:

> What is the name of the customer?

When a project has even dozens of such prompts, and when SinglePass Extraction combined all of them into one large prompt, there is no problem. But, consider prompts like the following:

> Extract the customer name from the given context. Note that the customer’s middle name should always be ignored. Also, respond with the name formatted as “Last name, First name”. Note that the context might contain the name of the customer’s relationship manager. Please note that their name needs to be ignored. We are interested in the customer’s name only.

When SinglePass Extraction combines dozens of prompts like these to form a single, large prompt, many LLMs might have trouble following the instructions in it just because of how dense it might be. Before any Prompt Studio project is [exported and deployed](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_export_project/)
 to be used in production, you'll need to verify that it's working well with a representative sample of variants. This is what we will discuss in the next section [Developing and verifying SinglePass Extraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/developing_and_verifying_singlepass/)
.

A note on chunking[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/single_pass_extraction_intro/#a-note-on-chunking "Direct link to A note on chunking")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For extraction use cases that Unstract is designed for, it's always a good idea to avoid chunking. You should only consider it if the document in question will never fit into the input context window of the chosen LLM.

You need to think about document data extraction from unstructured documents differently from regular RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) use cases. Most high volume document data extraction uses cases use documents that are a few pages long and so and almost always, 100% accuracy is targeted and is achievable as well. This is the reason, we need to operate with full context vs. retrieved chunked context.

This advice against chunking is for accuracy reasons. While Unstract supports chunking, retrieval is generally the weakest link in any RAG application and can severely impact the overall quality of the extraction.

To understand more about chunking, please read our [Chunking Guide](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/chunking/)
.

*   [Cost vs. prompt complexity](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/single_pass_extraction_intro/#cost-vs-prompt-complexity)
    
*   [SinglePass Extraction to the rescue](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/single_pass_extraction_intro/#singlepass-extraction-to-the-rescue)
    
*   [Limitations](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/single_pass_extraction_intro/#limitations)
    
*   [A note on chunking](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/single_pass_extraction_intro/#a-note-on-chunking)

---

# Adding and indexing sample documents | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/adding_and_managing_documents/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

1.  Click on the "Manage Documents" button located in the top-right section of the document view area.
    
2.  In the "Manage Document Variants" dialog that appears, click on the "Upload New File" button (with the plus icon).
    
3.  Select the document you wish to upload from your computer's file system when prompted.
    
4.  Wait for the document to be uploaded and processed. Once complete, it will appear in the "Uploaded files" section.
    
5.  The document will be available for analysis, extraction, and other document processing features within Unstract.
    

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_add_documents-8e3c370c24f68a49e465734c7d51b477.png)

After Upload[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/adding_and_managing_documents/#after-upload "Direct link to After Upload")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   After uploading the document,do the indexing(Click Reload button under `Actions`) as shown below

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_add_documents_1-563d8d48ac3e6eb3d885fe7dff2694c6.png) ![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_add_documents_2-3e0a6c6fd76c4546f4ceef68fbc5e2f6.png) ![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_add_documents_3-be0b00bf5ef93878d8bc914f47442396.png)

*   For multiple uploaded documents, you can manage and switch between them through the same Manage Documents interface.

*   [After Upload](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/adding_and_managing_documents/#after-upload)

# Prompt Chaining | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Prompt chaining is a powerful technique that allows you to connect multiple prompts in sequence, where the output of one prompt becomes the input for the next. This enables complex data processing workflows and multi-step analysis.

How Prompt Chaining Works[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#how-prompt-chaining-works "Direct link to How Prompt Chaining Works")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In prompt chaining, you can:

*   Pass extracted data from one prompt to another
*   Build complex processing pipelines
*   Create sequential analysis workflows
*   Transform data through multiple stages

Prompt Chaining Scenarios[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#prompt-chaining-scenarios "Direct link to Prompt Chaining Scenarios")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are two main scenarios for how data flows between chained prompts:

### 1\. Direct Value Passing[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#1-direct-value-passing "Direct link to 1. Direct Value Passing")

The raw output from the first prompt is directly sent as input to the second prompt without any modifications using the syntax: `{{prompt_key}}`

### 2\. Processed Value Passing[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#2-processed-value-passing "Direct link to 2. Processed Value Passing")

The output from the first prompt undergoes processing through a post-processing URL before being sent to the next prompt using the syntax: `{{[prompt_key] <post_processing_url>}}`

Examples[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#examples "Direct link to Examples")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Example 1: Direct Value Passing[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#example-1-direct-value-passing "Direct link to Example 1: Direct Value Passing")

In this scenario, the extracted value is **directly sent** from one prompt to the next without any intermediate processing.

**Scenario:** Direct sending the values

**Syntax:** `{{prompt_key}}`

**First Prompt key: `transaction_amount`**

    What is the amount for the transaction named "SAFEWAY" is processed. Reply only the amount

**Direct Result:** `23.11`

**Second Prompt key: `transaction_date`**

    What is the transaction date for this {{transaction_amount}}

**Flow:**

1.  First prompt extracts: `23.11`
2.  Value `23.11` is **directly passed** to the second prompt
3.  Second prompt uses `{{transaction_amount}}` = `23.11` to find the transaction date
4.  Final result: `09/30/23`

![Prompt Chaining Example 1](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_chaining_1-0a3cdc699a292610c67036e30b4c0282.png)

### When to use Direct Value Passing When:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#when-to-use-direct-value-passing-when "Direct link to When to use Direct Value Passing When:")

*   You need to preserve the exact format of extracted data
*   The next prompt requires the raw, unmodified value
*   Building simple sequential queries
*   Maintaining data integrity is crucial

### Example 2: Processed Value Passing[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#example-2-processed-value-passing "Direct link to Example 2: Processed Value Passing")

In this scenario, the extracted value is **processed/transformed** before being sent to the next prompt or external system.

**Scenario:** Sending the value after processing

**Syntax:** `{{[prompt_key] <post_processing_url>}}`

**First Prompt: `transaction_amount`**

    What is the amount for the transaction named "SAFEWAY" is processed. Reply only the amount

**Initial Result:** `23.11`

**Second Prompt: `amount`**

    print this {{transaction_amount}}https://webhook.site/28656756-3c25-4e57-8ad0-4896e64581a2}}

**Processing Stage:**

    {  "value": "23.11",  "new_value": "33.11"}

**Flow:**

1.  First prompt extracts: `23.11`
2.  Value undergoes **post-processing** (adds 10 to original value)
3.  Processed result: `33.11`
4.  Processed value can be used in subsequent prompts

![Prompt Chaining Example 2](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_chaining_2-4bbcc0a55561e63497dcea7c99745870.png))

### When to use Processed Value Passing When:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#when-to-use-processed-value-passing-when "Direct link to When to use Processed Value Passing When:")

*   You need to transform or enrich the data
*   Performing calculations on extracted values
*   Integrating with external systems via webhooks
*   Adding business logic between prompts
*   Formatting data for specific requirements

*   [How Prompt Chaining Works](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#how-prompt-chaining-works)
    
*   [Prompt Chaining Scenarios](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#prompt-chaining-scenarios)
    *   [1\. Direct Value Passing](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#1-direct-value-passing)
        
    *   [2\. Processed Value Passing](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#2-processed-value-passing)
        
*   [Examples](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#examples)
    *   [Example 1: Direct Value Passing](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#example-1-direct-value-passing)
        
    *   [When to use Direct Value Passing When:](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#when-to-use-direct-value-passing-when)
        
    *   [Example 2: Processed Value Passing](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#example-2-processed-value-passing)
        
    *   [When to use Processed Value Passing When:](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/#when-to-use-processed-value-passing-when)

# Deploying SinglePass Extraction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/singlepass_deployment/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

We discussed earlier in [Developing and verifying SinglePass Extraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/developing_and_verifying_singlepass/)
 that while in Prompt Studio, you're only verifying if SinglePass Extraction is doing a good job of the required structured extraction. SinglePass Extraction needs to be deployed as part of [API Deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
, [ETL Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/set_up_etl_pipeline/)
, [Human Quality Review](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
 or [Task Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/set_up_task_pipeline/)
 workflow. In this section, let's see how, when deploying one of the previously mentioned types of workflows, we can enable SinglePass Extraction.

To understand this section fully, you need to already know how [Workflows](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflows_intro/)
 work in Unstract.

Enabling SinglePass Extraction[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/singlepass_deployment/#enabling-singlepass-extraction "Direct link to Enabling SinglePass Extraction")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

It's pretty simple, really:

1.  First, open any Workflow project in which you want to enable SinglePass Extraction.
2.  Select the exported Prompt Studio project Tool from the Workflow steps.
3.  Enable the SinglePass Extraction checkbox from the Tool Settings panel on the right.

![img Enable SinglePass Extraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/singlepass_deployment-f6599d84a25fd5f5cb55d1cc7791c5d5.png)

Once this is done, this workflow has SinglePass Extraction enabled. Irrespective of whether it's an API, ETL Pipeline, Human Quality Review or Task Pipeline, when the extraction actually occurs, all your prompts will be combined into one using the default, configured LLM and the final prompt is executed against the context exactly once.

*   [Enabling SinglePass Extraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/singlepass_deployment/#enabling-singlepass-extraction)

---

# LLMChallenge: Introduction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_intro/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Large Language Models can work really well for document data extraction use cases. The trouble with them however, is that they can hallucinate. And worse, there is no reliably way to detect if an LLM has indeed hallucinated. If Large Language Models are to be deployed in production for at-scale use cases, users need to be sure that there are no hallucinations happening. Wrong extractions undermine trust in the system.

What is LLMChallenge[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_intro/#what-is-llmchallenge "Direct link to What is LLMChallenge")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

LLMChallenge is a type of LLM-as-a-judge implementation, a technique that is regarded as one of the most reliable available today to ensure accuracy and fight hallucinations.

How does it work?[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_intro/#how-does-it-work "Direct link to How does it work?")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

LLMChallenge uses not one, but two Large Language Models. The first one is an extraction model and the other the challenger. For every field extraction, the challenger model is used to score the results produced by the extractor model. They "converse" with each other and the system observes if they arrive at a consensus. If they don't arrive at a consensus, the value of the field being extracted is set to `null`. Unstract's philosophy is: A `null` value is better than a wrong value. Like discussed above, wrong values undermine trust in the system and they need to be avoided. If a system is found to be extracting wrong values often, users involved will be forced to second guess all extractions. They might as well do the extraction fully manually.

Increased accuracy[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_intro/#increased-accuracy "Direct link to Increased accuracy")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

While the primary use of LLMChallenge is to detect and avoid hallucinations, wrong and ambiguous extractions, it has a very powerful side-effect. As the challenge "conversation" happens between the extractor and the challenger LLMs, if for whatever reason the extracted value was off the first time, in many occasions, the extraction LLM corrects itself, responding with the right value. While, after this, the challenger LLM still has to reach consensus for the value to be accepted, the percentage of right values extracted goes up, apart from the detection of hallucinations. This makes LLMChallenge a powerful ally when using Large Language Models at scale in production.

Cost and latency impact[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_intro/#cost-and-latency-impact "Direct link to Cost and latency impact")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

While LLMChallenge increases operating costs, it is fully justified by the tremendous value it provides in terms of increased accuracy. Users can rest assured that LLM hallucinations are not leading to wrong field values being extracted. As to how much the cost increase might be is use case dependent.

It should also be expected that extractions taken longer than when LLMChallenge is disabled because of the involvement of two LLMs. Again, the added latency is absolutely worth the increase in accuracy and the actual latency depends on the use case.

*   [What is LLMChallenge](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_intro/#what-is-llmchallenge)
    
*   [How does it work?](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_intro/#how-does-it-work)
    
*   [Increased accuracy](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_intro/#increased-accuracy)
    
*   [Cost and latency impact](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_intro/#cost-and-latency-impact)

---

# SummarizedExtraction Introduction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/summarized_extraction_intro/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

In Prompt Studio, when you are working on field extractions, it's way easier for you to think of one prompt as something used for extracting one field, or a group of closely related fields (like the individual items in a line item) from a document. This also means that you can tweak a prompt to make it better without having to worry about it affecting the quality of other prompts. This one to one relationship between a prompt and a field is a design feature of Prompt Studio that makes long-term maintenance of projects with dozens of complex extractions easier.

Comparison with SinglePass Extraction[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/summarized_extraction_intro/#comparison-with-singlepass-extraction "Direct link to Comparison with SinglePass Extraction")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By default, each prompt in Unstract is run against the full context of the input document. This means that, if there are a lot of prompts, it can get pretty expensive in terms of LLM token costs. One powerful feature available in Unstract is SinglePass Extraction, which uses an LLM to construct a single, large prompt by combining all your prompts and executing it just once against the full context, saving LLM costs. In the SinglePass Extraction Introduction section, we discuss how it works, but also how it has limitations around the prompt getting too big and complex, thereby increasing the chances the extraction quality suffers.

SummarizedExtraction on the other hand, uses a completely different technique. Let's see how it works.

How SummarizedExtraction works[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/summarized_extraction_intro/#how-summarizedextraction-works "Direct link to How SummarizedExtraction works")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SummarizedExtraction is a four pass system.

*   Pass #1: An LLM is used for subquestion retrieval to understand what the user is trying to extract, completely ignoring the formatting instructions in each prompt, thus creating relatively simple prompts out of potentially complex prompts.
*   Pass #2: All these simple prompts are then combined into one single prompt.
*   Pass #3: This prompt is then executed against the full context of the input document to create a highly summarized version of the input document with only the information needed to satisfy the extractions the user needs.
*   Pass #4: Each of the users prompts is then executed against this summarized context. The individual outputs are combined to form the final response JSON.

The cost of SummarizedExtraction is more than the cost of SinglePass Extraction, but less than that of using the full context against each of the prompts (the default). SummarizedExtraction avoids the limitations of SinglePass Extraction, while still maintaining good accuracy and keeping costs down.

A note on chunking[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/summarized_extraction_intro/#a-note-on-chunking "Direct link to A note on chunking")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For extraction uses case that Unstract is designed for, it's always a good idea to avoid chunking. You should only consider it if the document in question will never fit into the input context window of the chosen LLM.

You need to think about document data extraction from unstructured documents differently from regular RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) use cases. Most high volume document data extraction uses cases use documents that are a few pages long and so and almost always, 100% accuracy is targeted and is achievable as well. This is the reason, we need to operate with full context vs. retrieved chunked context.

This advice against chunking is for accuracy reasons. While Unstract supports chunking, retrieval is generally the weakest link in any RAG application and can severely impact the overall quality of the extraction.

To understand more about chunking, please read our [Chunking Guide](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/chunking/)
.

*   [Comparison with SinglePass Extraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/summarized_extraction_intro/#comparison-with-singlepass-extraction)
    
*   [How SummarizedExtraction works](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/summarized_extraction_intro/#how-summarizedextraction-works)
    
*   [A note on chunking](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/summarized_extraction_intro/#a-note-on-chunking)

---

# Developing and verifying SinglePass Extraction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/developing_and_verifying_singlepass/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

When developing your prompts in [Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_intro/)
, you can enable SinglePass Extraction and see how it affects your extractions. Of course, in Prompt Studio, you're only developing prompts and defining the extraction schema. Ultimately, SinglePass Extraction runs as part of a deployed Prompt Studio project as one of the following integration types:

*   [API Deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
    
*   [ETL Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/set_up_etl_pipeline/)
    
*   [Human Quality Review](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
    
*   [Task Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/set_up_task_pipeline/)
    

However, while developing prompts, it is important to verify the performance of SinglePass Extraction. Prompt Studio provides various tools for you to be able to do that.

Turning on SinglePass Extraction in Prompt Studio[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/developing_and_verifying_singlepass/#turning-on-singlepass-extraction-in-prompt-studio "Direct link to Turning on SinglePass Extraction in Prompt Studio")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can turn on SinglePass Extraction by clicking on the `SinglePass Extraction` toggle as shown below.

![img SinglePass Extraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/singlepass_turned_on-b144145f2b7a63310baede010e2dffac.png)

You might have also noticed that the individual prompt cells no longer have `Run` buttons. That has moved up top. You also get the tokens used for the default LLM.

Please note that this only enables SinglePass Extraction for you to verify if everything is working well during prompt engineering. For SinglePass Extraction to be deployed, you need to turn it on in the deployment workflow. Please see the [Deploying SinglePass Extraction section](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/singlepass_deployment/)
 for details on how to do this.

Verifying SinglePass Extraction output[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/developing_and_verifying_singlepass/#verifying-singlepass-extraction-output "Direct link to Verifying SinglePass Extraction output")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

While you can view and verify the output of each of the prompts, you can also verify extraction quality by switching to [Combined Output](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/combined_output/)
 as shown below.

![img SinglePass Extraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/singlepass_combied_output-6f1169a30577539a52f1011f71024ead.png)

*   [Turning on SinglePass Extraction in Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/developing_and_verifying_singlepass/#turning-on-singlepass-extraction-in-prompt-studio)
    
*   [Verifying SinglePass Extraction output](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/single_pass_extraction/developing_and_verifying_singlepass/#verifying-singlepass-extraction-output)

---

# Post-deployment verification | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_post_deployment_verification/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

In this section, we'll see how to request metadata as part of the API response so that we can get the LLMChallenge log and costs.

Enabling return of metadata in the API response[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_post_deployment_verification/#enabling-return-of-metadata-in-the-api-response "Direct link to Enabling return of metadata in the API response")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once the [Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_intro/)
 project has been [deployed as an API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/create_api_workflow/)
 and you [make an API call](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/making_an_api_call/)
, you can include the `include_metadata` parameter to it so that it returns details on LLMChallenge and also how much the challenge cost you.

LLMChallenge metadata in the API response[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_post_deployment_verification/#llmchallenge-metadata-in-the-api-response "Direct link to LLMChallenge metadata in the API response")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In the screenshot below, you can see that as part of the API response metadata, the LLMChallenge log is included. This can be very useful in debugging LLMChallenge should you need to. You can see that the LLMChallenge log is contained under the `challenge_data` key in the response JSON.

![img LLMChallenge log in the API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llm_challenge_api_1-166cfa120cc804f4ef934ef1574aafe6.png)

You can also see the scores that the challenger LLM is generating against the responses of the extractor LLM:

![img LLMChallenge scores](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llm_challenge_api_2-b08079bceec116f05ce440c47324bd33.png)

LLMChallenge cost[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_post_deployment_verification/#llmchallenge-cost "Direct link to LLMChallenge cost")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When API metadata is enabled, you'll also get the tokens spent on the challenger LLM along with a cost estimate, as seen in the screenshot below.

![img LLMChallenge cost](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llm_challenge_api_cost-9829af236beb2913fd6c77353197cbd6.png)

For a more detailed guide on calculating the total cost of extraction, please see the [Calculating Extraction Costs](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/calculating_extraction_cost/)
 user guide.

*   [Enabling return of metadata in the API response](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_post_deployment_verification/#enabling-return-of-metadata-in-the-api-response)
    
*   [LLMChallenge metadata in the API response](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_post_deployment_verification/#llmchallenge-metadata-in-the-api-response)
    
*   [LLMChallenge cost](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_post_deployment_verification/#llmchallenge-cost)
