# Configuring Preamble and Postamble Settings in Unstract | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/preamble_and_postamble/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Understanding Preamble and Postamble[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/preamble_and_postamble/#understanding-preamble-and-postamble "Direct link to Understanding Preamble and Postamble")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Preamble**: Text that is added before your prompt questions to guide the LLM's approach to extraction
*   **Postamble**: Text that is added after your prompt questions to specify how responses should be formatted

Steps for Configuring Preamble Settings[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/preamble_and_postamble/#steps-for-configuring-preamble-settings "Direct link to Steps for Configuring Preamble Settings")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Access the Settings menu by clicking the gear icon (⚙️) in the top-right corner of the Unstract interface.
    
2.  In the Settings sidebar on the left, click on "Preamble".
    
3.  You'll see the "Preamble Settings" panel with a text area containing the default preamble:
    
    *   The default text instructs the model to pay close attention to context, language, structure, and cross-references
    *   It emphasizes using only information from the provided context and not external knowledge
4.  Modify the preamble text to suit your specific requirements:
    
    *   You can customize the instructions to better align with your extraction needs
    *   The preamble sets the context and guidelines for how the LLM should approach all prompts
5.  You can reset to the default prompt by clicking "Reset with default prompt" if needed.
    
6.  Click the "Save" button to apply your changes.
    

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_preamble-d2b4e16c36e0ba65d5dbecce9acd5b89.png)

Steps for Configuring Postamble Settings[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/preamble_and_postamble/#steps-for-configuring-postamble-settings "Direct link to Steps for Configuring Postamble Settings")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Access the Settings menu by clicking the gear icon (⚙️) in the top-right corner.
    
2.  In the Settings sidebar on the left, click on "Postamble".
    
3.  You'll see the "Postamble Settings" panel with a text area containing the default postamble:
    
    *   The default postamble instructs the model to "not include any explanation in the reply. Only include the extracted information in the reply."
4.  Modify the postamble text according to your requirements:
    
    *   You can specify how you want the extracted information to be formatted
    *   Add instructions for response style, brevity, or specific output format
5.  You can reset to the default prompt by clicking "Reset with default prompt" if needed.
    
6.  Click the "Save" button to apply your changes.
    

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_postamble-9077f0ab6cf4e60df42ee12c58125a90.png)

Best Practices for Preamble and Postamble[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/preamble_and_postamble/#best-practices-for-preamble-and-postamble "Direct link to Best Practices for Preamble and Postamble")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Preamble**: Focus on setting extraction guidelines, context handling, and general approach
*   **Postamble**: Focus on response formatting, output style, and presentation of results

*   [Understanding Preamble and Postamble](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/preamble_and_postamble/#understanding-preamble-and-postamble)
    
*   [Steps for Configuring Preamble Settings](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/preamble_and_postamble/#steps-for-configuring-preamble-settings)
    
*   [Steps for Configuring Postamble Settings](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/preamble_and_postamble/#steps-for-configuring-postamble-settings)
    
*   [Best Practices for Preamble and Postamble](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/preamble_and_postamble/#best-practices-for-preamble-and-postamble)

---

# Introduction to Workflows | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflows_intro/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

A **workflow** is the foundation for configuring data processing pipelines. It defines how files are collected, processed, and stored by seamlessly integrating **input connectors**, **processing logic**, and **output connectors**.

How It Works[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflows_intro/#how-it-works "Direct link to How It Works")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Input Connectors** – The workflow begins by picking up files from a designated input source. This could be a cloud storage location, an API, or a local directory.
2.  **Processing Stage** – Once the files are ingested, your configured prompts (exported tools) run in sequence, extracting, transforming, or enriching the data as required.
3.  **Output Connectors** – Finally, the processed data is stored in the configured destination, such as a database, a file system, or another service.

Why Use Workflows?[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflows_intro/#why-use-workflows "Direct link to Why Use Workflows?")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Automation** – Streamline repetitive tasks without manual intervention.
*   **Scalability** – Handle large datasets efficiently with structured processing.
*   **Flexibility** – Customize workflows by integrating different connectors and processing tools.

By leveraging workflows, you can create an end-to-end automated data pipeline, ensuring efficient and accurate data processing from input to output.

*   [How It Works](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflows_intro/#how-it-works)
    
*   [Why Use Workflows?](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflows_intro/#why-use-workflows)

---

# Deploying LLMChallenge | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_deployment/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

We discussed earlier in [Developing and verifying LLMChallenge](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_dev_and_verification/)
 that while in Prompt Studio, you're only verifying if LLMChallenge is doing a good job of required the structured extraction. LLMChallenge needs to be deployed as part of [API Deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
, [ETL Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/set_up_etl_pipeline/)
, [Human Quality Review](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
 or [Task Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/set_up_task_pipeline/)
 workflow. In this section, let's see how, when deploying one of the previously mentioned types of workflows, we can enable LLMChallenge.

To understand this section fully, you need to already know how [Workflows](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflows_intro/)
 work in Unstract.

Enabling LLMChallenge[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_deployment/#enabling-llmchallenge "Direct link to Enabling LLMChallenge")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

It's pretty simple, really:

1.  First, open any Workflow project in which you want to enable LLMChallenge.
2.  Select the exported Prompt Studio project Tool from the Workflow steps.
3.  Choose a challenger LLM from the list of available LLMs.
4.  Enable the LLMChallenge checkbox from the Tool Settings panel on the right.

![img Enable LLMChallenge](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/deploying_llmchallenge-830c89f7c6ccec227419854a36ed87ad.png)

Once this is done, this workflow has LLMChallenge enabled. Irrespective of whether it's an API, ETL Pipeline, Human Quality Review or Task Pipeline, when the extraction actually occurs and the output from the extraction LLM is available, the LLMChallenge process kicks in increasing the accuracy and trust of the extraction.

*   [Enabling LLMChallenge](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_deployment/#enabling-llmchallenge)

---

# Developing and verifying LLMChallenge | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_dev_and_verification/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

When developing your prompts in [Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_intro/)
, you can enable LLMChallenge and see how it affects your extractions. Of course, in Prompt Studio, you're only developing prompts and defining the extraction schema. Ultimately, LLMChallenge runs as part of a deployed Prompt Studio project as one of the following integration types:

*   [API Deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
    
*   [ETL Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/set_up_etl_pipeline/)
    
*   [Human Quality Review](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
    
*   [Task Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/set_up_task_pipeline/)
    

However, as you engineer your prompts in Prompt Studio, it's important to verify if LLMChallenge is working as expected. For this reason, you can turn it on and also inspect the LLMChallenge log in the Prompt Studio UI.

Turning on LLMChallenge[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_dev_and_verification/#turning-on-llmchallenge "Direct link to Turning on LLMChallenge")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To turn on LLMChallenge, in any Prompt Studio project, go to `Settings`🞂`LLMChallenge`, select an LLM from the `Challenger LLM` list. Then, click on the `Enable LLMChallenge` checkbox.

![img Enabling LLMChallenge](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/enabling_llm_challenge-127ef658090fd7b4475a7b23375448dc.png)

Choosing a challenger LLM[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_dev_and_verification/#choosing-a-challenger-llm "Direct link to Choosing a challenger LLM")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ideally, the Large Language Model chosen as the challenger should have 2 following characteristics. Without this, you should expect LLMChallenge not to give you the results you expect:

1.  The challenger model should be a powerful, large model that is considered a flagship model. At the time of this writing, one can consider models like GPT-4o, Anthropic Claude Sonnet 3.5, Gemini Pro 1.5 and Meta Llama 3.2 to be such models.
2.  Ensure that the extractor LLM and the challenger LLM are from different vendors. For instance, using Anthropic Claude Sonnet 3.5 for extraction and Meta Llama 3.2 for challenging it is an example of a good set up. Both these models are available as part of AWS Bedrock and can be easily added to Unstract.

Inspecting the LLMChallenge log[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_dev_and_verification/#inspecting-the-llmchallenge-log "Direct link to Inspecting the LLMChallenge log")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The LLMChallenge log can be accessed by clicking on its designated button as illustrated in the screenshot below.

![img LLMChallenge log button](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llm_log_button-623b357c966effce51a997d892e0ab31.png)

Clicking on this button brings up the LLMChallenge log dialog. You can inspect the consensus conversation between the extractor LLM and the challenger LLM in this dialog.

![img LLMChallenge log](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llm_challenge_log-31727c61b2f91c488d974e972cf19640.png)

In the screenshot above, we're able to see the score the challenger LLM, in this case Gemini Pro 1.5 gave the extractor LLM, GPT-4-Turbo. By clicking on the stage of the LLMChallenge conversation on the left, you can inspect the result of that stage, which is shown in the left pane.

*   [Turning on LLMChallenge](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_dev_and_verification/#turning-on-llmchallenge)
    
*   [Choosing a challenger LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_dev_and_verification/#choosing-a-challenger-llm)
    
*   [Inspecting the LLMChallenge log](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/llm_challenge/llm_challenge_dev_and_verification/#inspecting-the-llmchallenge-log)

---

# Developing and verifying SummarizedExtraction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/developing_and_verifying_summarized_extraction/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

When developing your prompts in [Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_intro/)
, you can enable SummarizedExtraction and see how it affects your extractions. Of course, in Prompt Studio, you're only developing prompts and defining the extraction schema. Ultimately, SummarizedExtraction runs as part of a deployed Prompt Studio project as one of the following integration types:

*   [API Deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
    
*   [ETL Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/set_up_etl_pipeline/)
    
*   [Human Quality Review](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
    
*   [Task Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/set_up_task_pipeline/)
    

However, while developing prompts, it is important to verify the performance of SummarizedExtraction. Prompt Studio provides various tools for you to be able to do that.

Enabling SummarizedExtraction in Prompt Studio[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/developing_and_verifying_summarized_extraction/#enabling-summarizedextraction-in-prompt-studio "Direct link to Enabling SummarizedExtraction in Prompt Studio")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To enable SummarizedExtraction, open the Prompt Studio project's settings and navigate to `SummarizedExtraction`.

![img SummarizedExtraction settings](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/summarized_extraction_settings-4cfffb73d48e5f16a67fbb63f91824bf.png)

Next, select an LLM that can create a summarized document based on your prompts. After that, enable the `Summarize Context` to turn on summary generation. Finally, checking the `Use summarized context as source` uses the summarized context to generate extraction responses.

Please note that this only enabled SummarizedExtraction for you to verify if everything is working well during prompt engineering. For SummarizedExtraction to be deployed, you need to turn it on in the deployment workflow. Please see the [Deploying SummarizedExtraction section](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/deploying_summarized_extraction/)
 for details on how to do this.

Verifying SummarizedExtraction[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/developing_and_verifying_summarized_extraction/#verifying-summarizedextraction "Direct link to Verifying SummarizedExtraction")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once SummarizedExtraction is enabled in Prompt Studio, you need to run all the prompts if sample documents are already present in the Prompt Studio project for their summaries to be generated. When you add new documents and they are indexed, their summarized versions are automatically created since SummarizedExtraction is turned on.

![img SummarizedExtraction verification](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/summarized_extraction_summary_view-c13b9dcde75621660a89454daafd587a.png)

You can see that in `Summary View`, you can now see the summarized version of the sample document created based on your prompts. You can see how compact the summary is, as compared to the `Raw View` seen below.

![img SummarizedExtraction Raw View](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/summarized_extraction_raw_view-ba97e708fb6d3b098da961b1f1acae38.png)

It is important for you to verify that the individual prompt outputs look good.

*   [Enabling SummarizedExtraction in Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/developing_and_verifying_summarized_extraction/#enabling-summarizedextraction-in-prompt-studio)
    
*   [Verifying SummarizedExtraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/developing_and_verifying_summarized_extraction/#verifying-summarizedextraction)

---

# Managing Grammar | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Overview[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#overview "Direct link to Overview")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Grammar feature in Unstract allows you to define custom synonyms for words that may appear in different forms across various documents. This helps the system recognize and interpret different terminology that shares the same meaning.

Steps for Adding Grammar (Custom Synonyms)[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#steps-for-adding-grammar-custom-synonyms "Direct link to Steps for Adding Grammar (Custom Synonyms)")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Access Settings**: Click on the gear icon (⚙️) in the top-right corner of the Unstract interface.
    
2.  **Navigate to Grammar**: In the Settings sidebar on the left, select "Grammar".
    
3.  **View Custom Synonyms**: The main panel displays the "Custom Synonyms" section where you can manage your word-synonym pairs.
    
4.  **Add New Words and Synonyms**:
    
    *   In the "Word" column, enter the base term you want to define (as shown with examples like "debit" and "credit")
    *   In the "Synonyms" column, enter terms that should be treated as equivalent to the base word
    *   You can add multiple synonyms for each word
5.  **Add More Rows**: Click the "+ Rows" button to add additional word-synonym pairs to your grammar.
    
6.  **Remove Synonyms**: If needed, click the "×" next to any synonym to remove it.
    
7.  **Save Changes**: After adding your custom synonyms, click the "Save" button to apply your changes.
    
8.  **Navigate Between Pages**: If you have multiple pages of synonym entries, use the pagination controls at the bottom to navigate between them.
    

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_grammar-ab9da07eb30b86872d9e0b63519f63e5.png)

Examples of Custom Synonyms[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#examples-of-custom-synonyms "Direct link to Examples of Custom Synonyms")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Here are some additional examples you could add:

### Financial Document Examples:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#financial-document-examples "Direct link to Financial Document Examples:")

*   **Word**: payment **Synonyms**: remittance, disbursement, settlement, installment
    
*   **Word**: statement **Synonyms**: bill, invoice, account summary, transaction record
    
*   **Word**: balance **Synonyms**: total, sum, amount due, outstanding amount
    

### Medical Document Examples:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#medical-document-examples "Direct link to Medical Document Examples:")

*   **Word**: patient **Synonyms**: client, subject, individual, recipient
    
*   **Word**: diagnosis **Synonyms**: assessment, finding, determination, clinical impression
    

### Legal Document Examples:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#legal-document-examples "Direct link to Legal Document Examples:")

*   **Word**: agreement **Synonyms**: contract, arrangement, covenant, understanding
    
*   **Word**: party **Synonyms**: participant, entity, signatory, stakeholder
    

Benefits of Using Grammar[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#benefits-of-using-grammar "Direct link to Benefits of Using Grammar")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Improves extraction accuracy across documents that use different terminology
*   Allows the system to recognize semantic equivalence even when wording varies
*   Helps standardize extracted information regardless of the specific terms used in original documents
*   Reduces the need to create multiple prompts to capture the same information expressed differently

*   [Overview](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#overview)
    
*   [Steps for Adding Grammar (Custom Synonyms)](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#steps-for-adding-grammar-custom-synonyms)
    
*   [Examples of Custom Synonyms](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#examples-of-custom-synonyms)
    *   [Financial Document Examples:](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#financial-document-examples)
        
    *   [Medical Document Examples:](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#medical-document-examples)
        
    *   [Legal Document Examples:](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#legal-document-examples)
        
*   [Benefits of Using Grammar](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/managing_grammar/#benefits-of-using-grammar)

---

# Prompt Coverage | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_coverage/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Overview of Coverage Feature[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_coverage/#overview-of-coverage-feature "Direct link to Overview of Coverage Feature")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Coverage feature in Unstract shows how your prompts perform across multiple documents in your collection. It provides visibility into the extraction results for each prompt across all documents, allowing you to verify consistency and adjust prompts as needed.

How to Access and Understand Coverage[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_coverage/#how-to-access-and-understand-coverage "Direct link to How to Access and Understand Coverage")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **View Coverage Information**:
    
    *   When a prompt is created and executed, you'll see a "Coverage" indicator below each prompt (The anchor link in blue color).
    *   The format shows "Coverage: X of Y docs" where:
        *   X = The number of documents with successful extractions
        *   Y = The total number of documents processed by this prompt
2.  **Accessing Detailed Coverage**:
    
    *   Click on the "Coverage" link to open a detailed view showing results across all documents.
    *   This opens a modal window with comprehensive information about how the prompt performed.
3.  **Coverage Details Window**:
    
    *   The top section shows configuration details:
        *   Profile Name, LLM, Chunk Size, Vector Store, Embedding Model, etc.
        *   These indicate which components were used for processing
4.  **Document Variants Tab**:
    
    *   Lists all documents that were processed by this prompt
    *   Shows token count for each document
    *   Displays the extracted value for each document
    *   Green checkmarks indicate successful extractions
5.  **Navigation**:
    
    *   Use pagination controls at the bottom to navigate between multiple pages of results

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_coverage-4ed190a36e0ffe47d57ad8d3025de3c7.png)

Benefits and Use Cases[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_coverage/#benefits-and-use-cases "Direct link to Benefits and Use Cases")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Cross-Document Verification**:
    
    *   Verify that your prompt works consistently across different document formats and sources
    *   In the example, the "account\_holder\_name" prompt successfully extracted names from two different documents:
        *   "JOSEPH PAULSON" from "Amex.pdf"
        *   "NARENDRAN HARIPARANTHAMAN" from "BoFA\_creditcard.pdf"
2.  **Prompt Optimization**:
    
    *   If extraction fails or returns unexpected results for certain documents, you can adjust your prompt
    *   Compare successful vs. unsuccessful extractions to identify patterns and improve prompts
3.  **Quality Assurance**:
    
    *   Ensure that all your documents are being properly processed
    *   Identify any documents that may require special handling or different prompts

By effectively utilizing the Coverage feature, you can ensure your extraction prompts deliver consistent, reliable results across your entire document collection.

*   [Overview of Coverage Feature](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_coverage/#overview-of-coverage-feature)
    
*   [How to Access and Understand Coverage](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_coverage/#how-to-access-and-understand-coverage)
    
*   [Benefits and Use Cases](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_coverage/#benefits-and-use-cases)

---

# Deploying SummarizedExtraction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/deploying_summarized_extraction/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

We discussed earlier in [Developing and verifying SummarizedExtraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/developing_and_verifying_summarized_extraction/)
 that while in Prompt Studio, you're only verifying if SummarizedExtraction is doing a good job of the required structured extraction. SummarizedExtraction needs to be deployed as part of [API Deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
, [ETL Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/set_up_etl_pipeline/)
, [Human Quality Review](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
 or [Task Pipelines](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/set_up_task_pipeline/)
 workflow. In this section, let's see how, when deploying one of the previously mentioned types of workflows, we can enable SummarizedExtraction.

To understand this section fully, you need to already know how [Workflows](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflows_intro/)
 work in Unstract.

Enabling SummarizedExtraction[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/deploying_summarized_extraction/#enabling-summarizedextraction "Direct link to Enabling SummarizedExtraction")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

It's pretty simple, really:

1.  First, open any Workflow project in which you want to enable SummarizedExtraction.
2.  Select the exported Prompt Studio project Tool from the Workflow steps.
3.  Enable the SummarizedExtraction checkbox from the Tool Settings panel on the right.

![img Enable SinglePass Extraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/deploying_summarized_extraction-2e95c9ab48e496eafb5c9d86ec8d6066.png)

Once this is done, this workflow has SummarizedExtraction enabled. Irrespective of whether it's an API, ETL Pipeline, Human Quality Review or Task Pipeline, when the extraction actually occurs, your prompts will be read, a new, summarized version of the input document created and your individual prompts run against it.

*   [Enabling SummarizedExtraction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/summarized_extraction/deploying_summarized_extraction/#enabling-summarizedextraction)

---

# File History | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

File History keeps track of files that have been processed by your workflows. It helps prevent duplicate processing and lets you see the status of each file.

What File History Does[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#what-file-history-does "Direct link to What File History Does")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Tracks processed files**: Records every file that goes through your workflow
*   **Prevents duplicates**: Successfully processed files are automatically skipped in future runs
*   **Shows errors**: Displays which files failed and why
*   **Enables reprocessing**: Delete records to process files again

Accessing File History[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#accessing-file-history "Direct link to Accessing File History")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### From ETL Pipeline, Task Pipeline, or Workflow[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#from-etl-pipeline-task-pipeline-or-workflow "Direct link to From ETL Pipeline, Task Pipeline, or Workflow")

1.  Navigate to **ETL Pipelines**, **Task Pipelines**, or **Workflows** page
2.  Find your pipeline or workflow in the list
3.  Click the **Actions** menu (three dots)
4.  Select **View File History**

![Accessing File History from Actions Menu](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/file_history_menu-f6d5939bec75cde8e2413c487ce49f8b.png)

Understanding the File History Table[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#understanding-the-file-history-table "Direct link to Understanding the File History Table")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![File History Table](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/file_history_modal-3634399074fb7d643b94a54e1bfcf509.png)

### Table Columns[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#table-columns "Direct link to Table Columns")

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **File Path** | The location of the processed file |
| **Status** | Whether processing succeeded or failed |
| **Total Attempts** | Number of times the file was processed (e.g., "1 / 3" means 1 attempt out of 3 maximum) |
| **Last Attempt** | When the file was last processed |
| **Error** | Error message if processing failed |
| **Action** | Delete button to remove the record |

### Status Values[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#status-values "Direct link to Status Values")

| Status | Color | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Completed** | Green | File processed successfully. Won't be processed again |
| **Error** | Red | Processing failed. Will retry automatically (up to 3 times) |

Filtering Files[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#filtering-files "Direct link to Filtering Files")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use the filters at the top of the File History table to find specific files:

| Filter | What It Does |
| --- | --- |
| **File Path** | Search by file name or path |
| **Status** | Show only Completed or Error files |
| **Min Attempts** | Show files processed at least N times |
| **Max Attempts** | Show files processed at most N times |

After setting filters, click **Apply** to update the results. Click **Reset** to clear all filters.

Clearing File History[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#clearing-file-history "Direct link to Clearing File History")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Clear file history when you want to reprocess files that were already completed.

### Clear All Files[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#clear-all-files "Direct link to Clear All Files")

Quick way to clear everything:

1.  Go to your pipeline's **Actions** menu
2.  Select **Clear Processed File History**
3.  All records for that pipeline are removed

### Delete Individual Files[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#delete-individual-files "Direct link to Delete Individual Files")

1.  Open **View File History**
2.  Find the file in the table
3.  Click the **Delete** button in the Action column
4.  Confirm the deletion

### Delete Multiple Files[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#delete-multiple-files "Direct link to Delete Multiple Files")

1.  Open **View File History**
2.  Check the boxes next to files you want to delete
3.  Click **Delete Selected**
4.  Confirm the deletion

### Delete Files Matching Filters[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#delete-files-matching-filters "Direct link to Delete Files Matching Filters")

1.  Apply filters to show only the files you want to delete
2.  Click **Clear with Filters**
3.  Review the count and confirm

Common Questions[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#common-questions "Direct link to Common Questions")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Why isn't my file being processed?[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#why-isnt-my-file-being-processed "Direct link to Why isn't my file being processed?")

If a file was already processed successfully, it won't run again. To reprocess it:

1.  Open **View File History**
2.  Find the file
3.  Delete its record
4.  Run your pipeline again

### What happens when a file fails?[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#what-happens-when-a-file-fails "Direct link to What happens when a file fails?")

Files with errors will automatically retry on the next pipeline run, up to **3 times total**. After 3 failed attempts, the file stops retrying.

### How do I reprocess a file that failed 3 times?[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#how-do-i-reprocess-a-file-that-failed-3-times "Direct link to How do I reprocess a file that failed 3 times?")

1.  Check the **Error** column in File History to understand what went wrong
2.  Review the **Execution Logs** for more details (navigate to **MANAGE** → **Logs** in the left panel)
3.  Fix the issue with the file if possible
4.  Open **View File History** and delete the record
5.  Run your pipeline again

### How do I reprocess all files?[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#how-do-i-reprocess-all-files "Direct link to How do I reprocess all files?")

Use **Clear Processed File History** from the Actions menu to remove all records, then run your pipeline.

Tips[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/file_history/#tips "Direct link to Tips")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Check errors first**: Look at the Error column to understand why files failed
*   **Use filters**: When you have many files, filters help you find specific ones quickly
*   **Copy paths easily**: Click the copy icon next to file paths to copy them
*   **Refresh for updates**: Click the Refresh button to see the latest data

# Custom Data Variables | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Custom Data Variables allow you to inject dynamic, user-defined data into your prompts at runtime. This feature enables you to create flexible, reusable prompts that can adapt to different contexts without modifying the prompt text itself.

Overview[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#overview "Direct link to Overview")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Custom data variables use the syntax `{{custom_data.key}}` and support dot notation for accessing nested values. This feature is available in both:

*   **Prompt Studio (Sandbox)**: For testing and development with a visual JSON editor
*   **API Deployment**: For production use where custom data is passed via the API request

Use Cases[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#use-cases "Direct link to Use Cases")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Custom data variables are ideal for:

*   **Document context**: Passing document-specific information like policy numbers, account IDs, or customer names
*   **Processing parameters**: Injecting thresholds, date ranges, or configuration values
*   **Multi-tenant scenarios**: Customizing extraction based on client-specific requirements
*   **Dynamic validation**: Passing expected values to compare against extracted data

Syntax[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#syntax "Direct link to Syntax")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Basic Access[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#basic-access "Direct link to Basic Access")

    {{custom_data.key}}

### Nested Object Access[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#nested-object-access "Direct link to Nested Object Access")

    {{custom_data.user.name}}{{custom_data.settings.threshold}}{{custom_data.company.address.city}}

Using Custom Data in Prompt Studio[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#using-custom-data-in-prompt-studio "Direct link to Using Custom Data in Prompt Studio")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Prompt Studio provides a visual interface to define and test custom data variables before deploying to production.

### Accessing Custom Data Settings[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#accessing-custom-data-settings "Direct link to Accessing Custom Data Settings")

1.  Open your Prompt Studio project
2.  Click the **Settings** icon (gear) in the toolbar
3.  Select **Custom Data** from the menu

![Settings modal with Custom Data menu item highlighted](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/settings_modal_with_custom_data_menu_item_highlighted-d1ffde6cb2c3b642f0f3798645102bb8.png)

### Defining Custom Data[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#defining-custom-data "Direct link to Defining Custom Data")

The Custom Data settings page provides:

1.  **JSON Editor**: A Monaco-based editor for defining your custom data as JSON
2.  **Variables Preview**: Shows which `{{custom_data.xyz}}` variables are used in your active prompts
3.  **Validation Warnings**: Alerts you when referenced variables are not defined in your JSON

![Custom Data settings page showing JSON editor and variables preview](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/custom_data_settings_page_showing_JSON_editor_and_variables-b63bd2b342941db0deb50d4c98cdd91e.png)

### Example Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#example-configuration "Direct link to Example Configuration")

Define your custom data as a JSON object:

    {  "customer_name": "Acme Corporation",  "policy_number": "POL-2024-001234",  "effective_date": "2024-01-01",  "thresholds": {    "min_amount": 1000,    "max_amount": 50000  },  "contact": {    "name": "John Smith",    "email": "john@acme.com"  }}

### Using Variables in Prompts[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#using-variables-in-prompts "Direct link to Using Variables in Prompts")

Once defined, reference your custom data in any prompt:

    Extract the premium amount for customer {{custom_data.customer_name}}with policy number {{custom_data.policy_number}}.The amount should be between {{custom_data.thresholds.min_amount}}and {{custom_data.thresholds.max_amount}}.

### Testing Your Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#testing-your-configuration "Direct link to Testing Your Configuration")

1.  Define your custom data JSON in the settings
2.  Create or edit a prompt using `{{custom_data.key}}` syntax
3.  Run the prompt to verify variable replacement
4.  Check the Variables Preview panel to ensure all variables are defined

tip

The Variables Preview panel automatically detects all `{{custom_data.xyz}}` variables used in your active prompts. Variables shown with a warning icon indicate they are referenced but not defined in your JSON.

Using Custom Data in API Deployment[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#using-custom-data-in-api-deployment "Direct link to Using Custom Data in API Deployment")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you deploy your Prompt Studio project as an API, custom data is passed as a parameter in the API request.

### API Request Parameter[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#api-request-parameter "Direct link to API Request Parameter")

Include the `custom_data` parameter in your API request:

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| custom\_data | object | No  | JSON object for variable replacement in prompts |

### Example API Request[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#example-api-request "Direct link to Example API Request")

    curl -X POST "https://us-central.unstract.com/deployment/api/<org_id>/<api_name>/" \  -H "Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_API_KEY>" \  -H "Content-Type: multipart/form-data" \  -F "files=@document.pdf" \  -F 'custom_data={"customer_name": "Acme Corp", "policy_number": "POL-001"}'

### Python Example[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#python-example "Direct link to Python Example")

    import requestsurl = "https://us-central.unstract.com/deployment/api/<org_id>/<api_name>/"headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer <YOUR_API_KEY>"}files = {"files": open("document.pdf", "rb")}data = {    "custom_data": '{"customer_name": "Acme Corp", "policy_number": "POL-001"}'}response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, files=files, data=data)print(response.json())

For complete API documentation, see [Execution API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_execution_api/)
.

Error Handling[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#error-handling "Direct link to Error Handling")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If a prompt references a custom data variable that is not defined, you will receive a descriptive error message with guidance on how to fix it.

**In Prompt Studio:**

    Custom data error for variable '{{custom_data.missing_key}}':Key 'missing_key' not found in custom data.Please define this key in Prompt Studio Settings > Custom Data.

**In API Deployment:**

    Custom data error for variable '{{custom_data.missing_key}}':Key 'missing_key' not found in custom data.Please include this key in the 'custom_data' field of your API request.

Troubleshooting[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#troubleshooting "Direct link to Troubleshooting")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Issue | Solution |
| --- | --- |
| Variable not replaced | Ensure the variable name matches exactly (case-sensitive) |
| Nested value not found | Verify the full path exists in your JSON (e.g., `user.name` requires `{"user": {"name": "..."}}`) |
| Invalid JSON | Use the JSON editor's syntax highlighting to identify and fix JSON errors |
| Variable not detected | Ensure correct syntax: `{{custom_data.key}}` with double curly braces |

Best Practices[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#best-practices "Direct link to Best Practices")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Test in Prompt Studio first**: Always validate your custom data configuration in the Prompt Studio sandbox before deploying to production
    
2.  **Use descriptive key names**: Choose clear, self-documenting key names like `customer_name` instead of `cn`
    
3.  **Document your schema**: Maintain documentation of expected custom data fields for API consumers
    
4.  **Handle missing data gracefully**: Design prompts that work even if optional custom data fields are not provided
    
5.  **Validate in your application**: Before calling the API, validate that required custom data fields are present
    

Relationship to Other Variable Types[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/#relationship-to-other-variable-types "Direct link to Relationship to Other Variable Types")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Custom data variables work alongside other variable types in Unstract:

| Variable Type | Syntax | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Custom Data** | `{{custom_data.key}}` | User-provided data from settings or API |
| **Prompt Chaining** | `{{prompt_key}}` | Output from another prompt in the same project |
| **Dynamic Variables** | `{{[prompt_key] url}}` | Post-processed prompt output via webhook |

For more information on prompt chaining, see [Prompt Chaining](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_chaining/)
.

# Destination Configuration | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

This page details the configuration options available when setting up destination in your workflows. Destination configurations define where and how your processed data is stored.

File System Destination Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#file-system-destination-configuration "Direct link to File System Destination Configuration")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When using a File System as your destination connector (common in Task pipelines), you can configure where processed output files are stored.

### Output Folder[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#output-folder "Direct link to Output Folder")

Specifies the folder path where processed output files will be stored. This path is relative to the root of the configured filesystem connector.

**Best Practices:**

*   Use descriptive folder names that reflect the workflow purpose
*   Consider organizing outputs by date or workflow type
*   Ensure the output folder is different from input folders to avoid reprocessing

Database Destination Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#database-destination-configuration "Direct link to Database Destination Configuration")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When using a Database as your destination connector (common in ETL and HITL workflows), you can configure how extracted data is stored in database tables.

![Destination Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/destination_config-a25a04d2f32e2dfd41b7144947e2bd16.png)

### Table Name[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#table-name "Direct link to Table Name")

The database table where processed output will be stored. If your database supports schemas, use the format `schema.table` (e.g., `public.invoices` or `analytics.documents`).

info

*   The table will be created if does not exist with the database user configured with the connector
*   If the table already exists, ensure that the database user has INSERT permissions on the table

### Include 'created\_by' column[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#include-created_by-column "Direct link to Include 'created_by' column")

When enabled, adds a `created_by` column to each output row, identifying the agent or process that created the record.

### Agent Name[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#agent-name "Direct link to Agent Name")

The value to use for the `created_by` column. Takes effect only when `created_by` is enabled. Defaults to "Unstract/DBWriter".

**Use Cases:**

*   Track which workflow or process generated each record
*   Enable multi-workflow auditing in shared tables
*   Support compliance and data lineage requirements

### Include 'created\_at' column[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#include-created_at-column "Direct link to Include 'created_at' column")

When enabled, adds a `created_at` column to each output row, recording when the data was processed and inserted.

### File Path Column Name[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#file-path-column-name "Direct link to File Path Column Name")

The name of the column that will store the absolute path to the source file that was processed. This helps link database records to source documents

### Execution ID Column Name[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#execution-id-column-name "Direct link to Execution ID Column Name")

The name of the column that will store the unique execution identifier for each workflow run. This ID helps track executions and is useful while debugging and viewing logs of the execution.

### Select how you want to write the output[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#select-how-you-want-to-write-the-output "Direct link to Select how you want to write the output")

Determines how the extracted data is structured in the database table.

**Available Options:**

#### Write JSON to a single column[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#write-json-to-a-single-column "Direct link to Write JSON to a single column")

Stores all extracted data as a JSON object in a single database column. This is the most flexible option and is currently the primary supported format.

#### Single Column Name[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#single-column-name "Direct link to Single Column Name")

The name of the column that will contain the JSON data

Best Practices[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#best-practices "Direct link to Best Practices")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### For Database Destinations[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#for-database-destinations "Direct link to For Database Destinations")

1.  **Always include audit columns** in production workflows:
    
    *   Enable `created_at` for time tracking
    *   Enable `created_by` to identify the workflow
2.  **Use descriptive column names** that match your organization's naming conventions
    
3.  **Test table permissions** before deploying:
    
    *   Ensure INSERT permissions
    *   Verify column data types match expected output

### For Filesystem Destinations[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#for-filesystem-destinations "Direct link to For Filesystem Destinations")

1.  **Organize output folders** logically:
    
    *   Use date-based folders for time series data
    *   Separate folders by workflow type or data category
2.  **Avoid output/input folder overlap** to prevent circular processing
    
3.  **Plan storage capacity** based on expected file volumes
    

Configuration Storage[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#configuration-storage "Direct link to Configuration Storage")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All destination configuration settings are stored in the workflow's backend configuration as JSON. These settings can be modified after workflow creation by editing the workflow configuration.

*   [File System Destination Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#file-system-destination-configuration)
    *   [Output Folder](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#output-folder)
        
*   [Database Destination Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#database-destination-configuration)
    *   [Table Name](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#table-name)
        
    *   [Include 'created\_by' column](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#include-created_by-column)
        
    *   [Agent Name](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#agent-name)
        
    *   [Include 'created\_at' column](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#include-created_at-column)
        
    *   [File Path Column Name](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#file-path-column-name)
        
    *   [Execution ID Column Name](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#execution-id-column-name)
        
    *   [Select how you want to write the output](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#select-how-you-want-to-write-the-output)
        
*   [Best Practices](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#best-practices)
    *   [For Database Destinations](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#for-database-destinations)
        
    *   [For Filesystem Destinations](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#for-filesystem-destinations)
        
*   [Configuration Storage](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/#configuration-storage)

# Combined Output | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/combined_output/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Overview[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/combined_output/#overview "Direct link to Overview")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Combined Output feature in Unstract consolidates all extraction results from multiple prompts into a structured JSON format. This provides a unified view of all extracted data from a document, making it easier to integrate with other systems or to analyze the complete set of extracted information.

Accessing Combined Output[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/combined_output/#accessing-combined-output "Direct link to Accessing Combined Output")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  In the Document Extraction interface, click on the **"Combined Output"** tab next to the "Document Parser".
2.  This view displays all prompt results in a structured JSON format.

Understanding the JSON Structure[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/combined_output/#understanding-the-json-structure "Direct link to Understanding the JSON Structure")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Combined Output presents extracted data with the following structure:

*   Each prompt key becomes a JSON key in the output
*   The extracted answer to each prompt becomes the corresponding JSON value
*   The entire set of prompts and their responses is formatted as a valid JSON object

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_combined_output-912e86ea333d18e92e5999034225a7cf.png)

*   [Overview](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/combined_output/#overview)
    
*   [Accessing Combined Output](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/combined_output/#accessing-combined-output)
    
*   [Understanding the JSON Structure](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/combined_output/#understanding-the-json-structure)

---

# Output Analyzer | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Overview[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#overview "Direct link to Overview")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Output Analyzer is a feature in Unstract that provides a side-by-side comparison of your original documents and the extracted data. This tool allows you to visually validate extraction accuracy, troubleshoot extraction issues, and gain confidence in the data being extracted from your documents.

Accessing the Output Analyzer[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#accessing-the-output-analyzer "Direct link to Accessing the Output Analyzer")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **From Document Extraction Interface**:
    *   In the Document Extraction interface, locate the chart icon (📊) in the toolbar (highlighted with a red arrow in the first screenshot).
    *   Click this icon to launch the Output Analyzer.

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_output_analyzer-17b01f20890305d7e6aa1ee0f25bfe69.png)

Output Analyzer Interface[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#output-analyzer-interface "Direct link to Output Analyzer Interface")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Output Analyzer provides a split-screen view with two main components:

### Left Panel: Extracted Data[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#left-panel-extracted-data "Direct link to Left Panel: Extracted Data")

*   Displays the structured JSON output of all extractions
*   Shows all extracted fields with proper syntax highlighting
*   Includes line numbers for easy reference
*   Displays the exact data that has been extracted from the document

### Right Panel: Original Document[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#right-panel-original-document "Direct link to Right Panel: Original Document")

*   Shows the original document in its native format
*   Allows you to scroll through the document to locate source information
*   Provides context for the extracted data
*   Supports PDF navigation tools (zoom, page navigation, etc.)

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_output_analyzer_1-6fb79716278724d3587a39ae4a6d9718.png)

Key Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#key-features "Direct link to Key Features")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Side-by-Side Comparison**:
    
    *   Directly compare extracted data with its source in the original document
    *   Verify accuracy of extractions visually
2.  **Document Navigation**:
    
    *   Switch between different uploaded documents using the arrow button (>) in the top-right corner
    *   Review extraction results across multiple documents
3.  **Extraction Statistics**:
    
    *   View summary statistics about your extractions:
        *   Total top-level fields
        *   Fill rate percentage
        *   Number of filled and unfilled fields
4.  **Field Selection Tracking**:
    
    *   The interface shows how many fields are currently selected (e.g., "3 of 3 fields selected")
5.  **Document View Options**:
    
    *   Toggle between different document viewing modes using the icons in the right panel

Use Cases[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#use-cases "Direct link to Use Cases")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Quality Assurance**:
    
    *   Verify that extracted data accurately reflects the content in the original document
    *   Identify any mismatches or extraction errors
2.  **Troubleshooting**:
    
    *   Diagnose issues with extractions by seeing exactly where data is coming from
    *   Understand why certain fields might be extracted incorrectly
3.  **Prompt Refinement**:
    
    *   Use insights from the side-by-side comparison to refine your prompts
    *   Identify patterns or document areas that may require special handling
4.  **Demonstration and Validation**:
    
    *   Show stakeholders exactly how the system is extracting data
    *   Build confidence in the extraction process through visual validation

By effectively utilizing the Output Analyzer, you can ensure the accuracy and reliability of your document extractions, leading to higher quality data and more confident decision-making based on that data.

*   [Overview](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#overview)
    
*   [Accessing the Output Analyzer](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#accessing-the-output-analyzer)
    
*   [Output Analyzer Interface](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#output-analyzer-interface)
    *   [Left Panel: Extracted Data](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#left-panel-extracted-data)
        
    *   [Right Panel: Original Document](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#right-panel-original-document)
        
*   [Key Features](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#key-features)
    
*   [Use Cases](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/output_analyzer/#use-cases)

# Source Configuration | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

This page details the configuration options available when setting up source connectors in your workflows. Source configuration define how files are collected and prepared for processing.

File System Source Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#file-system-source-configuration "Direct link to File System Source Configuration")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When using a File System as your source connector, you can configure the following settings:

![Source Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/source_config-bd8d99bfe94ccba03363fdd481ae7e61.png)

### Folders to process[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#folders-to-process "Direct link to Folders to process")

Specify one or more folders in the connected filesystem from which to start processing files. Enter absolute paths of the file system or select a folder from the dialog and click `Add Folder` to add it to the list. Leave empty or use `/` to process from the root folder.

### Process sub-folders[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#process-sub-folders "Direct link to Process sub-folders")

When enabled, the workflow processes files in all subfolders recursively, up to 10 levels deep. When disabled, only files in the specified folders are processed.

**Recommendation:** Enable this option if your files are organized in nested folder structures.

### File types to process[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#file-types-to-process "Direct link to File types to process")

Limit processing to specific file types. If no types are selected, all [supported file types](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/supported_file_types/list_of_file_types_supported/)
 will be processed.

**Available Options:**

*   PDF documents
*   Text documents
*   Images

### Max files to process[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#max-files-to-process "Direct link to Max files to process")

Set the maximum number of files to process in a single workflow execution. This helps control resource usage and processing time.

**Recommendations:**

*   Start with smaller limits when testing workflows
*   Increase the limit based on your processing capacity
*   Consider file size and complexity when setting this value

### File processing order[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#file-processing-order "Direct link to File processing order")

Determines the order in which files are processed from the source.

**Available Options:**

| Option | Description | Use Case |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Unordered** | No explicit ordering; files are processed as enumerated by the operating system | Fastest option; use when order doesn't matter |
| **Oldest First (FIFO)** | Process files in ascending order of last-modified time | Process older files first; chronological processing |
| **Newest First (LIFO)** | Process files in descending order of last-modified time | Prioritize recent files; latest data first |

Performance Note

File ordering adds processing overhead. If you have more than 40,000 files, the ordering may not reflect all files in the source folder accurately.

### File metadata deletion behavior[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#file-metadata-deletion-behavior "Direct link to File metadata deletion behavior")

Controls how the workflow handles file metadata. This is used to keep track of files that have been processed before. Only file path is stored and not file content.

**Available Options:**

*   **Never delete**: Use this to automatically skip files that have already been processed
*   **Delete after**: Specify a duration after which this file metadata will be deleted and files will be reprocessed as a result

**Use Cases:**

*   Periodic reprocessing of updated documents that fall with the same name
*   Regular audits or compliance checks
*   Data refresh workflows

Configuration Storage[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#configuration-storage "Direct link to Configuration Storage")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All source configuration settings are stored in the workflow's backend configuration as JSON. These settings can be modified after workflow creation by editing the workflow configuration.

*   [File System Source Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#file-system-source-configuration)
    *   [Folders to process](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#folders-to-process)
        
    *   [Process sub-folders](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#process-sub-folders)
        
    *   [File types to process](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#file-types-to-process)
        
    *   [Max files to process](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#max-files-to-process)
        
    *   [File processing order](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#file-processing-order)
        
    *   [File metadata deletion behavior](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#file-metadata-deletion-behavior)
        
*   [Configuration Storage](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/#configuration-storage)
