# Getting Started | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/getting_started/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Prerequisites[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/getting_started/#prerequisites "Direct link to Prerequisites")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before using Agentic Prompt Studio, make sure:

*   You have an active **Unstract Cloud** account (Starter, Growth, or Enterprise plan)
    *   Or, you are an existing **Enterprise On-Premise** user.
*   You have at least **1 LLM adapter configured** in your Unstract workspace (e.g., OpenAI GPT-4o, Anthropic Claude Sonnet). You will need adapters for up to three roles (see [Project Settings](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/project_settings/)
    ).
*   You have a set of **sample documents** representing the variants you want to process. Use 3–5 representative samples that include layout and formatting variations. The more representative your samples are of real-world variation, the better the results.

Creating a Project[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/getting_started/#creating-a-project "Direct link to Creating a Project")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Open **Agentic Prompt Studio** from your Unstract dashboard. **Create a new project** or open an existing one.

1.  Open Prompt Studio from your Unstract dashboard.
2.  Create a new project or open an existing one.
3.  Upload your document samples using **Manage Documents** in the top-right corner.

After uploading, head to the **Status** tab on the project's home screen.

This section shows your document list with processing status columns for each pipeline stage, along with three action buttons: **Generate Schema**, **Create Prompt**, and **Process All**.

![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/main_screen-1eea9b9d35ff2ba895ef5ef6a51d7246.png)

*   [Prerequisites](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/getting_started/#prerequisites)
    
*   [Creating a Project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/getting_started/#creating-a-project)

---

# Agentic Prompt Studio: Introduction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/agentic_prompt_studio_intro/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Beta Notice

Agentic Prompt Studio is currently in beta. Features and behavior may change as the product evolves.

Introduction[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/agentic_prompt_studio_intro/#introduction "Direct link to Introduction")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Setting up reliable document extraction is harder than it looks. The documents themselves arrive as PDFs, scans, or digital files. The hard part is everything that comes after.

First, you need to define a schema: what fields exist in these documents, their data types, which ones are required, and how they nest.

For a single document variant, this is manageable.

But real-world document sets — credit card statements from five different issuers, invoices from dozens of vendors, contracts with varying clause structures — contain the same information labeled and formatted differently across every variant. Building a schema that accounts for all of these variations requires analyzing each variant, recognizing which fields are equivalent despite different names, and consolidating everything into a single coherent structure.

This typically takes hours to days of manual work, and the risk of overlooking fields that only appear in certain variants is high.

Next, you need to write an extraction prompt that tells the LLM exactly how to pull structured data from these documents. A generic prompt produces generic results. An effective prompt needs to account for the specific patterns, anchors, formatting conventions, and edge cases present in your document set — like where dates appear, how amounts are formatted, what to do when a field is missing, how to handle multi-line values.

Writing this prompt well requires deep familiarity with both the documents and the schema, and getting it right usually involves multiple rounds of trial and error.

Finally, once the prompt works, you need a way to know it keeps working. Every prompt edit carries the risk of fixing one field while silently breaking another. Without a systematic way to compare extraction results against known-correct baselines, regressions go undetected until they surface in production.

Agentic Prompt Studio addresses each of these challenges.

It uses multi-agent AI pipelines to automate schema generation, prompt creation, and accuracy tracking — replacing days of manual iteration with minutes of automated analysis, while keeping you in full control to review, edit, and refine every output.

Capabilities[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/agentic_prompt_studio_intro/#capabilities "Direct link to Capabilities")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Agentic Prompt Studio has three core capabilities:

*   **Agentic Schema Generation.** Analyzes your document samples and produces a production-ready JSON Schema. It handles field normalization across document variants, determines data types, and identifies nested structures.
*   **Agentic Extraction Prompt Generation.** Crafts a detailed, versioned extraction prompt tailored to your documents' specific patterns and formatting.
*   **Verification Sets and Accuracy Tracking.** Lets you maintain manually verified extraction data per document, then compares new extraction results against those baselines to catch regressions and quantify improvements.

Architecture[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/agentic_prompt_studio_intro/#architecture "Direct link to Architecture")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Agentic Prompt Studio is organized into two agent pipelines and a set of extraction and comparison services:

    Agentic Prompt Studio│├── Schema Pipeline│   ├── SummarizerAgent│   ├── UniformerAgent│   └── FinalizerAgent│├── Prompt Pipeline│   ├── PatternMinerAgent│   ├── PromptArchitectAgent│   └── CriticDryRunner│└── Extraction Services    ├── ExtractionService    ├── HighlightService    └── ComparisonService

End-to-End Data Flow[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/agentic_prompt_studio_intro/#end-to-end-data-flow "Direct link to End-to-End Data Flow")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The complete processing pipeline moves through 6 stages:

1.  **Document Upload**: Documents are processed through LLMWhisperer, which extracts raw text with line metadata.
2.  **Schema Generation**: The SummarizerAgent, UniformerAgent, and FinalizerAgent produce a JSON Schema.
3.  **Prompt Generation**: The PatternMinerAgent, PromptArchitectAgent, and CriticDryRunner produce an extraction prompt.
4.  **Data Extraction**: The ExtractionService runs the prompt against documents, producing structured JSON with source references.
5.  **Highlight Mapping**: The HighlightService maps extracted values back to coordinates in the original PDF.
6.  **Result Verification**: Users review results, the ComparisonService calculates accuracy metrics.

*   [Introduction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/agentic_prompt_studio_intro/#introduction)
    
*   [Capabilities](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/agentic_prompt_studio_intro/#capabilities)
    
*   [Architecture](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/agentic_prompt_studio_intro/#architecture)
    
*   [End-to-End Data Flow](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/agentic_prompt_studio_intro/#end-to-end-data-flow)

---

# Cloning Prompt Studio projects | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/cloning_prompt_studio_projects/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Overview[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/cloning_prompt_studio_projects/#overview "Direct link to Overview")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Project Cloning in Unstract is a feature that allows you to create a copy of an existing Prompt Studio project. This enables you to experiment with different prompts, settings, and extraction strategies without affecting your original working project. The cloned project maintains all the documents, prompts, and settings from the original, providing a safe environment for testing and improvement.

Purpose and Benefits[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/cloning_prompt_studio_projects/#purpose-and-benefits "Direct link to Purpose and Benefits")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Risk-Free Experimentation**: Modify prompts and extraction methods without affecting your production environment
*   **Iterative Improvement**: Test different extraction approaches to improve accuracy and reliability
*   **Version Management**: Maintain multiple versions of your extraction logic for different purposes
*   **Comparison**: Compare different prompt strategies to determine the most effective approach
*   **Training and Onboarding**: Create copies for team members to practice without affecting original projects

How to Clone a Project[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/cloning_prompt_studio_projects/#how-to-clone-a-project "Direct link to How to Clone a Project")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Access the Clone Feature**:
    
    *   Navigate to your existing Prompt Studio project
    *   Locate and click the "Clone project" button in the top toolbar
2.  **Name Your Clone**:
    
    *   In the dialog box that appears, enter a name for your new clone
    *   By default, it will suggest "Copy of Document Extraction"
    *   You can customize this name to better describe the purpose of the clone (e.g., "Credit Card Extraction - Enhanced Prompts")
3.  **Confirm Creation**:
    
    *   Click the "Clone" button to create your clone
    *   To cancel the operation, click "Cancel"
4.  **Access Your Cloned Project**:
    
    *   Once created, the system will automatically navigate you to the new cloned project
    *   The header will display the name you provided (e.g., "Copy of Document Extraction")

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_clone-202698ebb415cf3bfc8999e6422ca4a8.png) ![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_clone_1-abccda6120634de5377d92b7d30374c0.png) ![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_clone_2-a8a384e755ec949002960830f90d88b4.png)

What Gets Cloned[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/cloning_prompt_studio_projects/#what-gets-cloned "Direct link to What Gets Cloned")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The cloning process copies the following elements from your original project:

*   **All Prompts**: Every prompt you've created in the original project
*   **Document References**: Links to all documents used in the original project
*   **Settings**: All configuration settings including LLM profiles, preambles, postambles, etc.
*   **Grammar/Synonyms**: Any custom synonyms you've defined

By effectively utilizing the Project Cloning feature, you can continuously improve your document extraction quality while maintaining a stable production environment.

*   [Overview](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/cloning_prompt_studio_projects/#overview)
    
*   [Purpose and Benefits](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/cloning_prompt_studio_projects/#purpose-and-benefits)
    
*   [How to Clone a Project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/cloning_prompt_studio_projects/#how-to-clone-a-project)
    
*   [What Gets Cloned](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/cloning_prompt_studio_projects/#what-gets-cloned)

---

# Publicly sharing Prompt Studio projects | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/public_shares/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Overview[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/public_shares/#overview "Direct link to Overview")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Public Sharing feature in Unstract allows you to share your Prompt Studio projects with anyone in a read-only mode. Recipients can view extraction results and documents without requiring an Unstract account, and without the ability to modify prompts or use LLM profiles. This feature is perfect for stakeholders who need to see extraction results but don't need editing capabilities.

Key Benefits[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/public_shares/#key-benefits "Direct link to Key Benefits")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Simplified Sharing**: Share extraction results with stakeholders without requiring them to create accounts
*   **Read-Only Access**: Recipients can view but not modify any aspect of the project
*   **Secure**: Share via unique links that can be revoked at any time
*   **Resource Protection**: Shared projects don't consume your LLM tokens or credits
*   **Presentation-Ready**: Perfect for demonstrating extraction capabilities to clients or team members

How to Share a Project Publicly[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/public_shares/#how-to-share-a-project-publicly "Direct link to How to Share a Project Publicly")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Access the Share Feature**:
    
    *   Navigate to your Document Extraction project
    *   Click on the Export-->Create/Manage Public Sharing in the top toolbar.
2.  **Confirm Sharing Intent**:
    
    *   A confirmation dialog will appear asking if you want to "Share this project publicly in read-only mode?"
    *   The dialog explains that the project will be viewable by anyone who has the generated unique link
    *   Click "Yes, enable sharing" to proceed
3.  **Success Notification**:
    
    *   A success message will appear confirming "Project shared successfully"
    *   Click "Close" to dismiss this notification
4.  **Managing the Shared Link**:
    
    *   After sharing, a persistent notification banner appears at the top of your project indicating "This project is shared publicly in read-only mode"
    *   The unique sharing URL is displayed in this banner
    *   Two options are available:
        *   **Copy public link**: Copies the URL to your clipboard for easy sharing
        *   **Stop sharing**: Immediately revokes access to anyone using the shared link

![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_public_share_updated-bc192f7299dcbb050368edfefc2b1b36.png) ![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_public_share_1-010d23c096af3649decc9e688e3642ec.png) ![img Prompt Studio project ](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_studio_public_share_2-3d69673a864173f67722576960b25bc6.png)

What Recipients Can See[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/public_shares/#what-recipients-can-see "Direct link to What Recipients Can See")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When someone accesses your shared project via the public link, they will be able to view:

*   The original documents
*   All extraction results
*   Document Parser view and Combined Output view
*   PDF View, Raw View, and Summary View

What Recipients Cannot Do[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/public_shares/#what-recipients-cannot-do "Direct link to What Recipients Cannot Do")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Recipients using the public sharing link cannot:

*   Modify any prompts
*   Add new prompts
*   Edit documents
*   Upload new documents
*   Use any LLM resources
*   Access or use your LLM profiles
*   Change any settings

By using the Public Sharing feature effectively, you can seamlessly collaborate with stakeholders while maintaining control over your Unstract environment.

*   [Overview](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/public_shares/#overview)
    
*   [Key Benefits](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/public_shares/#key-benefits)
    
*   [How to Share a Project Publicly](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/public_shares/#how-to-share-a-project-publicly)
    
*   [What Recipients Can See](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/public_shares/#what-recipients-can-see)
    
*   [What Recipients Cannot Do](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/public_shares/#what-recipients-cannot-do)

---

# Workflow Setup Guide | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

This guide walks you through the common process of creating and configuring workflows in Unstract. All workflow types follow the same foundational setup process, with specific differences highlighted in their respective deployment guides.

Prerequisites[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#prerequisites "Direct link to Prerequisites")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before creating a workflow, you must [export a Prompt Studio project as a tool](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/exporting_a_project/)
. This exported tool will be used to process your data within the workflow.

Workflow Setup Process[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#workflow-setup-process "Direct link to Workflow Setup Process")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Workflows are configured through a streamlined 4-step wizard interface:

![Workflow Creation Wizard](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/workflow_wizard-4c5c84ff2782b34a62099eb6970afa8a.png)

### Step 1: Configure Source Connector[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#step-1-configure-source-connector "Direct link to Step 1: Configure Source Connector")

Select and configure where your input data comes from.

*   **Available Source Types**:
    *   File System (for ETL and Task pipelines)
    *   API (for API deployments)

See [Source Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/source_configuration/)
 for source specific settings

### Step 2: Configure Destination Connector[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#step-2-configure-destination-connector "Direct link to Step 2: Configure Destination Connector")

Select and configure where your processed data will be stored.

*   **Available Destination Types**:
    *   Database (for ETL and HITL workflows)
    *   File System (for Task pipelines)
    *   API (for API deployments - no configuration needed)

See [Destination Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/destination_configuration/)
 for destination specific settings

info

Refer [Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
 for filesystem or database connector setup

### Step 3: Select Exported Prompt Studio Project[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#step-3-select-exported-prompt-studio-project "Direct link to Step 3: Select Exported Prompt Studio Project")

Choose the tool that will process your data.

*   **Actions**:
    1.  Your previously exported Prompt Studio projects will be available for selection
    2.  Use **Change Prompt Studio project** to select a different project
    3.  Click **Configure Settings** to modify tool-specific settings

info

At this point, you can run and test the workflow, select `Actions` -> `Run Workflow` to ensure it's working as expected. This is optional but recommended.

### Step 4: Deploy Workflow[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#step-4-deploy-workflow "Direct link to Step 4: Deploy Workflow")

Deploy your workflow with the appropriate deployment type. After deployment, you'll see a confirmation message with your workflow identifier.

Deployment Types Overview[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#deployment-types-overview "Direct link to Deployment Types Overview")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Different deployment types are optimized for specific use cases:

| Deployment Type | Source | Destination | Primary Use Case |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **[ETL Pipeline](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/)<br>** | File System | Database | Automated data extraction and loading into databases |
| **[Task Pipeline](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/unstract_task_pipeline_intro/)<br>** | File System | File System | Automated data extraction and loading into filesystem |
| **[API Deployment](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)<br>** | API | API | On-demand API-based processing |
| **[HITL (Human-in-the-Loop)](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)<br>** | File System | Database | ETL with human review and approval workflow |

Monitoring and Debugging[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#monitoring-and-debugging "Direct link to Monitoring and Debugging")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Check the **Debug Panel** at the bottom of the interface for a high level view of what's being executed.
*   For more detailed logs, check the **Logs** side navigation menu -> **Workflow Sessions** -> select your desired workflow session.

*   [Prerequisites](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#prerequisites)
    
*   [Workflow Setup Process](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#workflow-setup-process)
    *   [Step 1: Configure Source Connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#step-1-configure-source-connector)
        
    *   [Step 2: Configure Destination Connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#step-2-configure-destination-connector)
        
    *   [Step 3: Select Exported Prompt Studio Project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#step-3-select-exported-prompt-studio-project)
        
    *   [Step 4: Deploy Workflow](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#step-4-deploy-workflow)
        
*   [Deployment Types Overview](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#deployment-types-overview)
    
*   [Monitoring and Debugging](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/workflows/workflow_overview/#monitoring-and-debugging)

# Creating API Deployments from Prompt Studio | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

The API deployment feature allows you to deploy your Prompt Studio project directly as an API without needing to create a separate workflow. This streamlined approach gets your document processing API up and running quickly.

Getting Started[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#getting-started "Direct link to Getting Started")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before creating an API deployment, ensure you have:

*   A completed Prompt Studio project that extracts the intended information well
*   Tested your project with sample documents
*   Verified the output quality meets your requirements

Step-by-Step Process[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#step-by-step-process "Direct link to Step-by-Step Process")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Step 1: Access the Export Options[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#step-1-access-the-export-options "Direct link to Step 1: Access the Export Options")

1.  **Open your Prompt Studio project** - Navigate to the project you want to deploy as an API
2.  **Deploy as API** - Click on Deploy as API and click on proceed.

![img Export API new](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_api_from_prompt_updated-b80a01350919a2b790f63516c752104c.png)

### Step 2: Deployment Details Setup[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#step-2-deployment-details-setup "Direct link to Step 2: Deployment Details Setup")

Configure the basic details for your API deployment:

1.  **Display Name**: Enter a descriptive name for your API deployment
    
    *   Example: `Credit Card Parser API`
2.  **Description**: Add a detailed description of what this API deployment does
    
    *   Example: `API deployment for Credit Card Parser`
3.  **API Name**: Specify the unique API name (this will be part of your API endpoint)
    
    *   Example: `credit_card_parser_1752483494579`
    *   Note: This name must be unique across your deployment
4.  Click **Next** to proceed to tool settings
    

![img Export API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/api_deploy_from_prompt_setup-2e0bf7efd083e67f27c72ce54f304983.png)

### Step 3: Tool Settings Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#step-3-tool-settings-configuration "Direct link to Step 3: Tool Settings Configuration")

Configure the tool settings for your API deployment:

1.  **Configure Tool Settings**: Enter the tool configuration name
    
    *   Example: `credit_card_parser`
2.  **Challenger LLM**: Select the LLM to use for LLMChallenge from the dropdown menu
    
    *   Description: LLM to use for LLMChallenge
3.  **Enable Options**: Check the boxes for features you want to enable:
    
    *   **Enable LLMChallenge**: Enables LLMChallenge functionality
    *   **Enable highlight**: Enables highlight feature
    *   **Enable SummarizedExtraction**: Enables SummarizedExtraction
    *   **Enable SinglePass Extraction**: Enables SinglePass Extraction
4.  Click **Create Deployment** to finalize the API deployment
    

![img Export API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/api_deploy_from_prompt_tool_settings-4f4e97b2991c51f7305e7908c3974bcb.png)

Navigation Options[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#navigation-options "Direct link to Navigation Options")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Previous**: Go back to the Deployment Details step
*   **Cancel**: Cancel the API deployment process
*   **Create Deployment**: Complete the API deployment

### Step 4: API Deployment Success[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#step-4-api-deployment-success "Direct link to Step 4: API Deployment Success")

After clicking **Create Deployment**, you'll see a success confirmation screen with the following information:

#### Deployment Confirmation[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#deployment-confirmation "Direct link to Deployment Confirmation")

🎉 **Your API deployment has been created successfully!**

The success screen displays:

*   **API Name**: The unique identifier for your API (e.g., `credit_card_parser_1752486232785`)
*   **Display Name**: Your chosen display name (e.g., `Credit Card Parser API`)
*   **Description**: The description you provided (e.g., `API deployment for Credit Card Parser`)

![img Export API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/api_deploy_from_prompt_success-9bdb9aaf46670c775da3684d55e72827.png)

#### Next Steps Options[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#next-steps-options "Direct link to Next Steps Options")

**What would you like to do next?**

You have two options to proceed:

1.  **View API Deployment** (Primary action)
    
    *   Takes you to the API deployment details page
    *   Shows deployment status, endpoint information, and usage details
2.  **Configure Workflow** (Secondary action)
    
    *   Allows you to create additional workflows using this deployed API
    *   Useful for building more complex processing pipelines

Click **Close** to dismiss the success dialog and return to Prompt Studio, or choose one of the next step options to continue working with your newly deployed API.

Using Your Deployed API[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#using-your-deployed-api "Direct link to Using Your Deployed API")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![API Deployments Page](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/api_deploy_from_prompt_postman-79417ebb86a525fd3165b22661a7cec4.png)

Once your API is deployed, you can access it from the API Deployments page:

1.  Navigate to **API Deployments** from the left sidebar
2.  Find your deployed API in the table showing API Name, Description, API Endpoint, Workflow, and status
3.  Click on the **Actions** button (⋮) for your API deployment
4.  Select **Download Postman Collection** to get a ready-to-use Postman collection for testing your API

The Postman collection will include all necessary API endpoints and configuration to start testing your document processing API immediately.

*   [Getting Started](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#getting-started)
    
*   [Step-by-Step Process](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#step-by-step-process)
    *   [Step 1: Access the Export Options](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#step-1-access-the-export-options)
        
    *   [Step 2: Deployment Details Setup](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#step-2-deployment-details-setup)
        
    *   [Step 3: Tool Settings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#step-3-tool-settings-configuration)
        
*   [Navigation Options](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#navigation-options)
    *   [Step 4: API Deployment Success](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#step-4-api-deployment-success)
        
*   [Using Your Deployed API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/#using-your-deployed-api)

---

# Exporting a Prompt Studio project | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_export_project/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Once a Prompt Studio project is able to extract the intended information well for a given set of documents, it can be exported and used in various types of Workflows.

Export Options[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_export_project/#export-options "Direct link to Export Options")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you click the **Export** button in Prompt Studio, you'll see three available options:

![img Workflow Studio Layout](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/export_button_updated-290114d3cd51761ea0f3c47474834e10.png)

### 1\. Export as Tool[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_export_project/#1-export-as-tool "Direct link to 1. Export as Tool")

Export your project as a reusable tool that can be integrated into Workflow Studio. This option allows you to:

*   **Build Task Pipelines** that can pick up documents from network/cloud file systems or object stores, structure them, and push the structured results into cloud file systems
*   **Build Unstructured data ETL Pipelines** that can pick up documents from network/cloud file systems or object stores, structure them, and push the structured results into a data warehouse or database

The exported tool becomes available in Workflow Studio, where you can create comprehensive workflows and deploy them as APIs.

**Export Process:**

*   Export via the "Export" button in Prompt Studio
*   System shows confirmation: "Custom tool exported successfully"
*   Creates a "Parser Tool" in Unstract
*   Tool becomes available in Workflow Studio for creating API/ETL and TASK workflows

### 2\. Export as JSON[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_export_project/#2-export-as-json "Direct link to 2. Export as JSON")

Export your project configuration as a JSON file that can be easily shared and imported. This option is perfect for:

*   **Sharing projects** with team members or other users
*   **Backup and version control** of your project configurations
*   **Template creation** for similar document types

To use an exported JSON file:

1.  Go to Prompt Studio
2.  Click **Import Project**
3.  Select the exported JSON file

The project will be imported with all its prompts, preamble, postamble and grammar

### 3\. Create/Manage Public Sharing[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_export_project/#3-createmanage-public-sharing "Direct link to 3. Create/Manage Public Sharing")

The Public Sharing feature in Unstract allows you to share your Prompt Studio projects with anyone in a read-only mode. Recipients can view extraction results and documents without requiring an Unstract account, and without the ability to modify prompts or use LLM profiles. This feature is perfect for stakeholders who need to see extraction results but don't need editing capabilities.

For detailed steps on creating an API deployment, see the [Creating API Deployments from Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/create_api_deployments_from_promptstudio/)
.

*   [Export Options](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_export_project/#export-options)
    *   [1\. Export as Tool](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_export_project/#1-export-as-tool)
        
    *   [2\. Export as JSON](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_export_project/#2-export-as-json)
        
    *   [3\. Create/Manage Public Sharing](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/prompt_studio_export_project/#3-createmanage-public-sharing)

---

# Run Platform | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/run_platform/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Use the following available options of `run-platform.sh` launch script to run Unstract Open Source Edition in Docker containers.

    # Pull and run entire Unstract platform with default env config../run-platform.sh# Pull and run docker containers with a specific version tag../run-platform.sh -v v0.1.0# Upgrade existing Unstract platform setup by pulling the latest available version../run-platform.sh -u# Upgrade existing Unstract platform setup by pulling a specific version../run-platform.sh -u -v v0.2.0# Build docker images locally as a specific version tag../run-platform.sh -b -v v0.1.0# Build docker images locally from working branch as `current` version tag../run-platform.sh -b -v current# Display the help information../run-platform.sh -h# Only do setup of environment files../run-platform.sh -e# Only do docker images pull with a specific version tag../run-platform.sh -p -v v0.1.0# Only do docker images pull by building locally with a specific version tag../run-platform.sh -p -b -v v0.1.0# Upgrade existing Unstract platform setup with docker images built locally from working branch as `current` version tag../run-platform.sh -u -b -v current# Pull and run docker containers in detached mode../run-platform.sh -d -v v0.1.0

Visit [http://frontend.unstract.localhost](http://frontend.unstract.localhost/)
 in your browser to access the platform.

**NOTE**: Modify the `.env` files present in each service folder to update its runtime behavior. Run `docker compose up -d` again for the changes to take effect.

---

# Importing a Prompt Studio project | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/import_prompt_studio_project/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Unstract Prompt Studio supports importing projects that were previously exported as JSON files. This makes it easy to reuse existing configurations or share them across teams.

### Steps to Import a Project[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/import_prompt_studio_project/#steps-to-import-a-project "Direct link to Steps to Import a Project")

1.  ### **Go to Prompt Studio**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/import_prompt_studio_project/#go-to-prompt-studio "Direct link to go-to-prompt-studio")
    
    *   Navigate to the **Prompt Studio** section from the left-hand menu.
    *   You'll see a list of available projects.
2.  ### **Click “Import Project”**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/import_prompt_studio_project/#click-import-project "Direct link to click-import-project")
    
    *   This button is located in the top-right corner.
    *   A popup window will appear prompting you to upload a file.
    
    ![img Workflow Studio Layout](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/import_project-c091d7018477bf9ea1e3ccc2a78919bc.png)
    
3.  ### **Upload the JSON File**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/import_prompt_studio_project/#upload-the-json-file "Direct link to upload-the-json-file")
    
    *   Either click inside the upload area or drag and drop the `.json` file exported from another Prompt Studio project.
    *   Click **Import** to continue.
    
    ![img Workflow Studio Layout](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/import_project_1-2c4642146ef45e55bc3463221aaac0d6.png)
    
4.  ### **Select Adapters for Import**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/import_prompt_studio_project/#select-adapters-for-import "Direct link to select-adapters-for-import")
    
    After uploading the file, you’ll be prompted to select the required adapters. These are needed for the imported project to function correctly.
    
    ![img Workflow Studio Layout](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/import_project_2-12d94f03d59f40d345fde90a932ecafa.png)
    
    You need to select:
    
    *   **LLM (Large Language Model)**: For generating responses and processing prompts.
        
    *   **Vector Database**: For storing and retrieving document embeddings.
        
    *   **Embedding Model**: For converting text into vector representations.
        
    *   **X2Text Adapter**: For handling different file formats during text extraction.
        
    
    > **Note**: These adapters form the LLM profile for the imported project. You can modify them later in the project's profile configuration.
    
5.  ### **Complete Import**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/import_prompt_studio_project/#complete-import "Direct link to complete-import")
    
    *   Once all required adapters are selected, click **Import Project**.
    *   Your project will be created and added to the list in Prompt Studio.

*   [Steps to Import a Project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/import_prompt_studio_project/#steps-to-import-a-project)
    
*   [**Go to Prompt Studio**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/import_prompt_studio_project/#go-to-prompt-studio)
    
*   [**Click “Import Project”**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/import_prompt_studio_project/#click-import-project)
    
*   [**Upload the JSON File**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/import_prompt_studio_project/#upload-the-json-file)
    
*   [**Select Adapters for Import**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/import_prompt_studio_project/#select-adapters-for-import)
    
*   [**Complete Import**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/import_prompt_studio_project/#complete-import)

---

# Generate Extraction Prompt | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_extraction_prompt/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

warning

A schema must be available before you can generate a prompt.

Once you have a schema (generated or manually defined), prompt generation uses a separate three-agent pipeline to create a complete extraction prompt tailored to your documents.

How It Works[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_extraction_prompt/#how-it-works "Direct link to How It Works")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Prompt generation uses a separate three-agent pipeline:

**Agent 1: PatternMinerAgent.** Examines your sample documents to discover extraction-relevant patterns. It identifies field anchors and labels (e.g., "Account No:", "Invoice Date:", "Total Amount"), formatting conventions (date formats, currency symbols, number separators), and layout patterns (where fields tend to appear in the document).

**Agent 2: PromptArchitectAgent.** Uses the mined patterns along with the schema and document summaries to construct a comprehensive extraction prompt. The generated prompt follows a structured format that includes a task overview, the output schema, field-specific guidance, disambiguation rules, edge case handling, and output format specifications.

**Agent 3: CriticDryRunner.** Stress-tests the draft prompt by simulating an extraction. It checks whether the output would be valid JSON, whether it conforms to the schema, and whether the instructions are unambiguous. It identifies potential failure modes and suggests revisions. The final prompt has been vetted for common issues.

Generating a Prompt[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_extraction_prompt/#generating-a-prompt "Direct link to Generating a Prompt")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_generation-24faa48939c543f01122c28c12cca54a.png)

1.  From the **Status** tab, click **Create Prompt**.
2.  In the dialog, select an **LLM Connector** to run the agent pipeline.
3.  Click **Generate**.

The system generates the prompt and saves it as a new version. The prompt appears in the **Extraction Prompt** tab.

Viewing the Prompt[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_extraction_prompt/#viewing-the-prompt "Direct link to Viewing the Prompt")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Extraction Prompt** tab displays the generated prompt in a code editor. The prompt includes the complete extraction instructions: task overview, schema, field-specific guidance, edge cases, and output format specifications.

The toolbar shows the current version number (e.g., "v1") and provides access to **Regenerate**, **Edit**, **History**, and **Compare** actions.

To generate a prompt from scratch, click **Regenerate**. The Regenerate dialog allows you to select a different LLM connector when regenerating. ![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/regenerate_prompt-394bd1a109c93bfd359ac2c0c74be8af.png)

Editing a Prompt[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_extraction_prompt/#editing-a-prompt "Direct link to Editing a Prompt")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/edited_auto_generated_prompt-7ed5ae3680b869614810d29aaba45720.png)

1.  Click **Edit** in the Extraction Prompt toolbar.
2.  Modify the prompt text in the code editor. You can adjust extraction instructions, add field-specific guidance, change edge case rules, or modify formatting requirements.
3.  Click **Save** to save your changes.
4.  In the **Save Prompt Version** dialog, enter a **Short Description** (max 50 chars), a brief label for this version (e.g., "Minor Changes"), and a **Long Description** (max 255 chars) explaining what changed.
5.  Click **Save Version**. ![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/save_prompt_edit_changes-3b9bceb3f4c4767b9666ada100357937.png)

Prompt Version History[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_extraction_prompt/#prompt-version-history "Direct link to Prompt Version History")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Every generated or manually saved prompt is stored as a version. Click **History** in the Extraction Prompt toolbar to open the **Prompt Version History** dialog.

The history table shows:

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Version** | The version number (v1, v2, v3, etc.). The current active version is labeled "Current". |
| **Short Description** | The label given when the version was saved. |
| **Long Description** | The detailed description of changes. |
| **Created** | The timestamp when the version was created. |
| **Accuracy** | The accuracy score for this version (populated after running extraction and calculating accuracy). |
| **Actions** | **Load** to switch to a different version, or the compare icon to open version comparison. |

![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_version_history-11f696992c2a5a2625e17876ba5314eb.png)

Comparing Prompt Versions[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_extraction_prompt/#comparing-prompt-versions "Direct link to Comparing Prompt Versions")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Click **Compare** in the Extraction Prompt toolbar (or the compare icon in Version History) to open the **Compare Prompt Versions** dialog.

This side-by-side diff view highlights differences between any two prompt versions:

*   Select a **Left Version** and **Right Version** from the dropdowns.
*   Lines removed from the left version are highlighted in **red**.
*   Lines added in the right version are highlighted in **green**.
*   Unchanged lines appear without highlighting.

This is useful for understanding exactly what changed between an auto-generated prompt and a manually edited version, or between two regenerated versions. ![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/prompt_change_comparison-1c23b0b4b2e5a941574b5b4483c3faf0.png)

*   [How It Works](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_extraction_prompt/#how-it-works)
    
*   [Generating a Prompt](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_extraction_prompt/#generating-a-prompt)
    
*   [Viewing the Prompt](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_extraction_prompt/#viewing-the-prompt)
    
*   [Editing a Prompt](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_extraction_prompt/#editing-a-prompt)
    
*   [Prompt Version History](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_extraction_prompt/#prompt-version-history)
    
*   [Comparing Prompt Versions](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_extraction_prompt/#comparing-prompt-versions)

---

# Highlight data API | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_highlight_data_api/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Use the Highlight Data API to obtain line coordinates for each line number returned by the [Execution API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_execution_api/)
.  
Ensure that the `Enable Highlight` option is enabled in the exported tool settings in the workflow otherwise, highlight data will not be returned.

Request[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_highlight_data_api/#request "Direct link to Request")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Endpoint | `/deployment/api/<organization_id>/<api_name>/highlight/` |
| URL | `https://us-central.unstract.com/deployment/api/<organization_id>/<api_name>/highlight/` |
| Method | `GET` |
| Headers | `Authorization: Bearer <YOUR API KEY>` |

Parameters[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_highlight_data_api/#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Default | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| whisper\_hash | string | \-- | Yes | The Whisper hash returned in the Execution API response and you can find it in the below json path: `result.metadata.whisper_hash` |
| line\_numbers | string | \-- | Yes | The Line numbers returned in the Execution API response and you can find it in the below json path: `result.metadata.line_numbers`. Specify Comma-separated list of line numbers like 2,3,6 |
| text\_extractor\_name | string | \-- | Yes | The name of the text extractor adapter that is used in the Prompt studio e.g `llm-whisperer-v2` |

Example Curl Request[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_highlight_data_api/#example-curl-request "Direct link to Example Curl Request")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    curl -X GET --location 'https://us-central.unstract.com/deployment/api/org_HJqNgodUQsA99S3A/mar17/highlight/?whisper_hash=REPLACE_WITH_WHISPER_HASH&line_numbers=REPLACE_WITH_LINE_NUMBERS&text_extractor_name=REPLACE_WITH_TEXT_EXTRACTOR_NAME' \--header 'Authorization: Bearer <Your API Key>'

### Example `200` Response[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_highlight_data_api/#example-200-response "Direct link to example-200-response")

    {   "highlights":{      "2":{         "base_y":155,         "base_y_percent":4.8927,         "height":51,         "height_percent":1.6098,         "page":0,         "page_height":3168,         "raw":[            0,            155,            51,            3168,            1.0         ]      },      "3":{         "base_y":187,         "base_y_percent":5.9028,         "height":26,         "height_percent":0.8207,         "page":0,         "page_height":3168,         "raw":[            0,            187,            26,            3168,            1.0         ]      },      "6":{         "base_y":300,         "base_y_percent":9.4697,         "height":38,         "height_percent":1.1995,         "page":0,         "page_height":3168,         "raw":[            0,            300,            38,            3168,            1.0         ]      }   }}

### 400: Missing required fields[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_highlight_data_api/#400-missing-required-fields "Direct link to 400: Missing required fields")

This response occurs when any of required parameter is missing or empty.

    {   "type":"validation_error",   "errors":[      {         "code":"blank",         "detail":"This field may not be blank.",         "attr":"whisper_hash"      },      {         "code":"blank",         "detail":"This field may not be blank.",         "attr":"text_extractor_name"      },      {         "code":"blank",         "detail":"This field may not be blank.",         "attr":"line_numbers"      }   ]}

### Line Numbers[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_highlight_data_api/#line-numbers "Direct link to Line Numbers")

This data can be used show the bounding box of each line of text extracted from the document in the original document. Useful for highlighting the extracted text in the original document.

The line metadata contains the bounding box coordinates for each line of text extracted from the document. For each line, an array of arrays is provided with the bounding box coordinates. The bounding box information is represented as an array of five values: `[page_no, base_y, height, page_height, confidence]` where:

*   `page_no`: The page number of the document.
*   `base_y`: The y-coordinate of the bounding box (bottom).
*   `height`: The height of the bounding box.
*   `page_height`: The height of the original page.
*   `confidence`: The confidence score of the extracted line, ranging from `0.0` to `1.0`.

info

Highlighting is for the entire row of the original document. The x coordinates are not provided as they will effectively be the width of the page. `x1=0` and `x2=page_width` always.

      (0,y-height) +-------------------------+ (page_width,y-height)               |                         |               |                         |         (0,y) +-------------------------+ (page_width,y)

    highlight_data = {  "2": { # Example highlight data for line 2    "base_y": 155, # Y-coordinate    "base_y_percent": 4.8927,    "height": 51, # Height    "height_percent": 1.6098,    "page": 0, # Page number    "page_height": 3168, # Page height    "raw": [      0, # Page number      155, # Y-coordinate      51, # Height      3168, # Page height      1.0 # Confidence (0.0 to 1.0)    ]  }}

*   [Request](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_highlight_data_api/#request)
    
*   [Parameters](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_highlight_data_api/#parameters)
    
*   [Example Curl Request](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_highlight_data_api/#example-curl-request)
    *   [Example `200` Response](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_highlight_data_api/#example-200-response)
        
    *   [400: Missing required fields](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_highlight_data_api/#400-missing-required-fields)
        
    *   [Line Numbers](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_highlight_data_api/#line-numbers)

# Configure Project Settings | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/project_settings/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Open the **Settings** tab to configure the LLM connectors used by your project. There are 4 connector slots, each serving a different role.

Extractor LLM[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/project_settings/#extractor-llm "Direct link to Extractor LLM")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The model used for running extraction prompts against documents. This is the model that performs the actual data extraction.

Select any configured connector from the dropdown.

![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/project_configuration_extractor_llm-24b0540acdbdcee22b942d6fd2af9f8a.png)

Agent LLM[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/project_settings/#agent-llm "Direct link to Agent LLM")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This is the LLM used for schema generation and prompt generation.

This model powers the SummarizerAgent, UniformerAgent, FinalizerAgent, PatternMinerAgent, PromptArchitectAgent, and CriticDryRunner.

![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/project_configuration_agent_llm-882aa100ba9e8370be4f09455648114b.png)

LLMWhisperer Connector[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/project_settings/#llmwhisperer-connector "Direct link to LLMWhisperer Connector")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The connector used for PDF text extraction. LLMWhisperer converts your PDF documents into structured raw text with line-level metadata, which the agents and extraction prompts use as input.

![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/project_configuration_text_extraction-b1471dd74443e31a154cf7bef1b92862.png)

Lightweight LLM[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/project_settings/#lightweight-llm "Direct link to Lightweight LLM")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

An LLM used for lightweight tasks like generating prompt metadata (descriptions, labels). This can be a smaller, less expensive model since it handles simpler tasks.

After making changes, click **Save Changes** to apply.

![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/project_configuration_lightweight_llm-d82358384e986bc013673343aa5eb91c.png)

*   [Extractor LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/project_settings/#extractor-llm)
    
*   [Agent LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/project_settings/#agent-llm)
    
*   [LLMWhisperer Connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/project_settings/#llmwhisperer-connector)
    
*   [Lightweight LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/project_settings/#lightweight-llm)
