# Generate Schema | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_schema/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

warning

The document's Raw Text & Summaries must be available before you can generate a schema.

Schema generation takes the document summaries and produces a unified JSON Schema that defines the fields, data types, and structure for extraction.

How It Works[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_schema/#how-it-works "Direct link to How It Works")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Schema generation uses a three-agent pipeline that runs sequentially:

**Agent 1: Summarizer Agent.** Processes each uploaded document independently. For every document, it identifies all data fields present: names, data types, descriptions, and example values. Because it analyzes each variant separately, it captures fields that may only appear in certain document formats or layouts.

**Agent 2: Uniformer Agent.** Takes the individual summaries from Agent 1 and normalizes them. It recognizes that fields labeled differently across documents — like "Invoice No.", "Invoice #", and "Inv Number" — refer to the same data point. It merges equivalent fields, selects consistent naming conventions, and consolidates descriptions to reflect all observed variations.

**Agent 3: Finalizer Agent.** Converts the unified field map into a complete JSON Schema (Draft 07) with descriptions, example values, data types, required field designations, and nested structures (such as line items or address objects).

Generating a Schema[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_schema/#generating-a-schema "Direct link to Generating a Schema")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  From the **Status** tab, click **Generate Schema**. ![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/schema_generation-30292edecbe85951c4e83f296635cb7e.png)
2.  In the dialog, select a **Generation Type**:
    *   **Regular (Requires processed documents).** Requires that document summaries are already available. Use this if you have already processed your documents' raw text.
    *   **Lazy (Auto-process documents if needed).** Resolves missing dependencies. If raw text or summaries have not been generated yet, the system processes them first before running schema generation. This is the most convenient option for new projects. ![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/schema_generation_select_type-6d0fa112ee1e8b1f051e340be7aa3ffc.png)
3.  Select an **LLM Connector** from the dropdown. This determines which model runs the agent pipeline.
4.  Click **Generate**. ![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/schema_preview-1e330ca9543e878db7ffd7aa38e62cce.png)

When complete, a success notification appears and the schema becomes available in the **Schema** tab. You can also preview the schema from the status module.

Viewing the Schema[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_schema/#viewing-the-schema "Direct link to Viewing the Schema")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Open the **Schema** tab to view the generated JSON Schema. The schema is displayed in a code editor alongside a PDF preview of the currently selected document. ![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/schema_full_view-5fe2faee4bc5df0978115ebf8887b065.png)

Editing the Schema[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_schema/#editing-the-schema "Direct link to Editing the Schema")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If the generated schema needs adjustment — like adding a missing field, renaming a field, changing a data type, or updating descriptions:

1.  Click **Edit** in the Schema tab toolbar.
2.  Modify the JSON directly in the code editor.
3.  Click **Save** to apply your changes, or **Cancel** to discard them.

Deleting Schema Fields[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_schema/#deleting-schema-fields "Direct link to Deleting Schema Fields")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To remove specific fields from the schema without manually editing JSON:

![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/delete_schema_fields-4038e094bc6db94c95b13a8fb9ec7b6f.png)

1.  Click **Delete Fields** in the Schema tab toolbar.
2.  A dialog displays all fields in the schema with their names, types, and descriptions.
3.  Select the fields you want to remove using the checkboxes. Use the search bar to filter fields by name, type, or description.
4.  Click **Delete Selected (N)** to remove the selected fields. Nested fields within a selected parent are removed as well.

*   [How It Works](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_schema/#how-it-works)
    
*   [Generating a Schema](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_schema/#generating-a-schema)
    
*   [Viewing the Schema](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_schema/#viewing-the-schema)
    
*   [Editing the Schema](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_schema/#editing-the-schema)
    
*   [Deleting Schema Fields](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/generate_schema/#deleting-schema-fields)

---

# Calculating extraction costs | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/calculating_extraction_cost/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

As a customer planning to use Unstract to extract structured data from unstructured documents, you might want to be sure about total cost of ownership. While it's easy to understand how much the Unstract Cloud or Unstract On-Prem editions might cost for the kind of volume you expect to deal with, calculating how much you might expect to spend on Large Language Models is a little bit more involved, but fairly easy and straightforward to calculate.

Components of the total cost[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/calculating_extraction_cost/#components-of-the-total-cost "Direct link to Components of the total cost")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The full cost of extracting structured data using the Unstract platform can be arrived at by adding up the following costs:

1.  Cost of the Unstract platform (usually per page processed)
2.  Cost of the text extraction service (e.g: LLMWhisperer; usually per page processed)
3.  Cost of the extraction LLM (number of tokens consumed).
4.  Cost of the challenger LLM (number of tokens consumed).
5.  Cost of the embedding model. This is usually a tiny fraction and can ignored for all practical purposes.

Cost of items 3-5 depends on the average lengths of the documents being processed, the number of fields being extracted, etc.

Calculating total cost[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/calculating_extraction_cost/#calculating-total-cost "Direct link to Calculating total cost")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

One convenient way to calculate total cost is by following the procedure outlined below:

1.  [Deploy your Prompt Studio project as an API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/quick_start/exporting_a_project/)
    .
2.  [Enable API metadata](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_execution_api/)
     and run extractions for 10-15 files. Ensure that when you choose these sample files, they're a good representative sample set of the kind of variants you'll see in production. Increase the sample set size should you want to increase the accuracy of your calculation or you're likely yo see wide-ranging variants in production. Please see below for details on cost metadata as returned by API Deployment responses.
3.  Gather the costs of the extractor LLM, challenger LLM and embedding model. The cost of the text extraction service is easy to calculate since it's usually charged per page. You should now have the per-document total extraction costs.
4.  Average the per-document extraction costs and you should have a very good estimate of what extraction costs are going to be for you in production.

Cost Metadata[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/calculating_extraction_cost/#cost-metadata "Direct link to Cost Metadata")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you call an API Deployment RESTful API passing in an unstructured document to get structured data back, you can \[enable API metadata\] so that the API response includes cost and other details.

Here are details of the number of tokens consumed and cost of the extractor LLM along with the cost of the embedding model for this particular document:

![img extraction LLM and embedding cost](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/embedding_and_extraction_llm_costs-28a958c15e0229fa03655127a9747048.png)

If LLMChallenge is enabled, cost for the challenger LLM, along with the tokens consumed is also made available as part of the metadata returned.

![img challenger LLM cost](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/challenger_llm_costs-fb27ac54c0e17a74a2d80c9824eade5b.png)

Automating batch extractions[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/calculating_extraction_cost/#automating-batch-extractions "Direct link to Automating batch extractions")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

An [open source batch processing script](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-api-deployments-batch-run)
 is available that should allow easily call your deployed API endpoint against a batch of sample documents. The JSON responses from the API are saved in a SQLite database flat file, from where you can retrieve the API response JSON values and easily calculate the average cost of processing a document. Other useful information like response times, etc are also saved in the database. We recommend that you use an open source tool like [DBeaver](https://dbeaver.io/)
 to retrieve data from the SQLite DB flat file.

Need help?[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/calculating_extraction_cost/#need-help "Direct link to Need help?")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you need help calculating costs, you can always reach out to us on the [Unstract Community Slack](https://join-slack.unstract.com/)
.

*   [Components of the total cost](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/calculating_extraction_cost/#components-of-the-total-cost)
    
*   [Calculating total cost](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/calculating_extraction_cost/#calculating-total-cost)
    
*   [Cost Metadata](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/calculating_extraction_cost/#cost-metadata)
    
*   [Automating batch extractions](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/calculating_extraction_cost/#automating-batch-extractions)
    
*   [Need help?](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/calculating_extraction_cost/#need-help)

# Contribute to Frontend | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Local Development[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#local-development "Direct link to Local Development")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See [frontend documentation](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract/tree/main/frontend)
.

React Strict Mode[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#react-strict-mode "Direct link to React Strict Mode")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_React Strict Mode_ is enabled by default, which will **mount all components twice** during local development.

This helps in verifying the integrity of the React components, and is in alignment with all the frontend use cases known and envisioned currently.

Responsive Design[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#responsive-design "Direct link to Responsive Design")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Grid Layout**
    
    _**Use Ant Design’s Grid system to create responsive layouts. It provides a flexible system for different screen sizes.**_
    
          import { Row, Col } from 'antd';  <Row gutter={16}>    <Col xs={24} md={12} lg={8}>      Content 1    </Col>    <Col xs={24} md={12} lg={8}>      Content 2    </Col>    <Col xs={24} md={24} lg={8}>      Content 3    </Col>  </Row>
    
    This layout ensures that on small screens (xs), each item takes the full width. On medium screens (md), each item takes half the width, and on large screens (lg), they take one-third.
    

Responsive Visibility[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#responsive-visibility "Direct link to Responsive Visibility")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_**Use Ant Design’s Grid and Responsive utilities to control the visibility of components at different screen sizes.**_

*   Media Queries:
    
          import { useMediaQuery } from 'react-responsive';  const isDesktop = useMediaQuery({ query: '(min-width: 1224px)' });  const isTablet = useMediaQuery({ query: '(max-width: 1224px)' });  return (    <>      {isDesktop && <div>Show on Desktop</div>}      {isTablet && <div>Show on Tablet</div>}    </>  );
    

Avoid Inline Styling[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#avoid-inline-styling "Direct link to Avoid Inline Styling")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Instead of inline styles, prefer to use CSS classes, styled-components, or Ant Design’s built-in styling with LESS. This ensures consistency and easier maintenance.

Testing on Multiple Devices[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#testing-on-multiple-devices "Direct link to Testing on Multiple Devices")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Regularly test the application on various screen sizes and devices, ensuring that the layout is consistent. Use the developer tools in your browser for quick testing.

Separate Class/Modifier for New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#separate-classmodifier-for-new-features "Direct link to Separate Class/Modifier for New Features")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_**When adding new features or plugins, avoid overriding shared styles. Instead, use specific class names to prevent regressions in existing features.**_

      .button {      background-color: blue;  }    .button--new-feature {      background-color: green;      font-size: 20px;  }

JavaScript Style Guide[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#javascript-style-guide "Direct link to JavaScript Style Guide")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_**Follow the Google JavaScript Style Guide for consistent code quality.**_

*   Module exports should be at the end of the file.
*   Avoid inline comments except when necessary.
*   Use JSX extensions for all new React components.

File Structure[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#file-structure "Direct link to File Structure")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_**Ensure a clear, maintainable file structure**_

*   All components should be grouped under the /src directory.
*   There will be an index.js file in the /src folder containing all component exports.
*   Use JSX extensions for all new React components.

Use ESLint[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#use-eslint "Direct link to Use ESLint")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_**Linting: Install the linter locally to catch and fix issues early.**_

*   Extends from "react-app" and "react-app/jest".
*   Includes Google's style guide with eslint-config-google.
*   Prettier integration to ensure formatting consistency across the codebase.
*   Run ESLint and Prettier on staged files before each commit

![vscode extension](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/vscode_eslint_ext-2507941f4909f29a4110dc63b9255ae1.png)

Supported Browsers[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#supported-browsers "Direct link to Supported Browsers")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_**Ensure the code is compatible with the following browsers:**_

*   Production:
    
    Coverage: Browsers with >0.2% market share, excluding obsolete browsers ("not dead") and Opera Mini.
    
*   Development:
    
    Latest versions of Chrome, Firefox, and Safari.
    

*   [Local Development](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#local-development)
    
*   [React Strict Mode](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#react-strict-mode)
    
*   [Responsive Design](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#responsive-design)
    
*   [Responsive Visibility](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#responsive-visibility)
    
*   [Avoid Inline Styling](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#avoid-inline-styling)
    
*   [Testing on Multiple Devices](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#testing-on-multiple-devices)
    
*   [Separate Class/Modifier for New Features](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#separate-classmodifier-for-new-features)
    
*   [JavaScript Style Guide](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/contribute-frontend/#javascript-style-guide)

# Limitations and Best Practices | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Limitations and Edge Cases[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#limitations-and-edge-cases "Direct link to Limitations and Edge Cases")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Beta status.** Agentic Prompt Studio is in beta. Agent behavior, pipeline structure, and output formats may change in future releases.
*   **Sample quality matters.** The agents can only learn patterns present in your uploaded samples. If your production documents include variants not represented in the samples, the generated schema and prompts may not cover those cases. Upload the widest range of representative samples you can.
*   **Complex nested structures.** While the FinalizerAgent handles nested structures (e.g., line items, addresses), deeply nested or highly irregular structures may require manual schema adjustments after generation.
*   **Prompt generation depends on schema quality.** If the schema is incomplete or inaccurate, the generated prompt will reflect those gaps. Review and validate the schema before generating prompts.
*   **Verification Sets require manual effort upfront.** You need to provide manually verified JSON for each baseline document. This initial investment pays off during iterative prompt development, but the baselines themselves must be accurate.
*   **LLM adapter required.** The agentic pipelines use LLM calls under the hood, so a configured LLM adapter with valid API keys is required.
*   **JSON parsing edge cases.** LLM responses may occasionally be malformed, truncated, or wrapped in markdown code fences. The system uses a multi-method parsing strategy (direct parse → markdown extraction → JSON repair → empty fallback), but rare edge cases can produce incomplete output.
*   **Context window limitations.** Documents exceeding the LLM's context window cannot be processed in a single pass. Keep documents under 100 pages when possible. Intelligent context window management with chunking and multi-pass extraction is planned for a future release.
*   **No built-in cost tracking.** There is currently no way to track LLM usage costs within Agentic Prompt Studio. Monitor spending through your LLM provider's dashboard.

Best Practices[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#best-practices "Direct link to Best Practices")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Document Preparation[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#document-preparation "Direct link to Document Preparation")

*   Use 3–5 representative samples that cover the range of document variants you expect in production.
*   Include edge cases: documents with missing fields, unusual formatting, or multi-page layouts.
*   Ensure OCR quality by using LLMWhisperer or a comparable preprocessing tool.
*   Prefer documents under 100 pages where possible, as longer documents may approach LLM context limits.

### Schema Design[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#schema-design "Direct link to Schema Design")

*   Review auto-generated schemas before generating prompts. The schema directly shapes the prompt, so gaps or errors in the schema carry forward.
*   Add or refine field descriptions to improve extraction guidance.
*   Use consistent naming conventions (e.g., `snake_case` for all field names).
*   Test the schema against diverse samples to confirm it covers all expected fields.

### Prompt Optimization[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#prompt-optimization "Direct link to Prompt Optimization")

*   Start with the auto-generated prompt and iterate from there. The agents have already incorporated document-specific patterns, so targeted edits are more effective than rewriting from scratch.
*   Tune the prompt after verifying at least 10 documents to establish a reliable accuracy baseline.
*   Use the **Compare Prompt Versions** feature to understand the impact of each change.
*   Add version notes when saving edits to maintain a clear change history.

### Verification Workflow[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#verification-workflow "Direct link to Verification Workflow")

*   Build verified data early. The sooner you have baselines, the sooner accuracy tracking becomes useful.
*   Verify consistently — use the same standards for what constitutes "correct" extraction across all documents.
*   Use PDF highlighting to cross-reference extracted values against source documents.
*   Focus prompt refinement on high-error fields identified by the Field Comparison and Mismatch Matrix.
*   Re-verify and recalculate accuracy every 10–20 prompt edits to track trends.

### Quality Monitoring[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#quality-monitoring "Direct link to Quality Monitoring")

*   Run regular accuracy checks against your verification set.
*   Review the Mismatch Matrix to spot systemic extraction issues — these are fields that fail across multiple documents.
*   Track accuracy trends across prompt versions using the Version History accuracy column.
*   Define a production accuracy threshold and do not deploy until that threshold is met.

*   [Limitations and Edge Cases](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#limitations-and-edge-cases)
    
*   [Best Practices](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#best-practices)
    *   [Document Preparation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#document-preparation)
        
    *   [Schema Design](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#schema-design)
        
    *   [Prompt Optimization](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#prompt-optimization)
        
    *   [Verification Workflow](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#verification-workflow)
        
    *   [Quality Monitoring](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/limitations_and_best_practices/#quality-monitoring)

# ETL Pipeline Introduction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Unstract's ETL Pipeline extracts and transforms unstructured data from various sources and loads it into databases.

Key Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/#key-features "Direct link to Key Features")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Extract** from [Filesystem Sources](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/index/)
    
*   **Transform** data using [Tool exported from Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/)
    
*   **Load** to popular [Database Destinations](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/index/)
    

Smart File Handling[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/#smart-file-handling "Direct link to Smart File Handling")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Unstract's ETL pipelines are designed for maximum efficiency and reliability:

*   Keeps track of new files in each pipeline run to avoid reprocessing
*   Skips duplicate files (same name, path, and content) automatically
*   Executes files in parallel for faster batch processing
*   Retries execution on failure to ensure reliability

Getting Started[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/#getting-started "Direct link to Getting Started")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To create your first ETL pipeline, see [Set Up ETL Pipeline](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/set_up_etl_pipeline/)

Execution and Monitoring[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/#execution-and-monitoring "Direct link to Execution and Monitoring")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Execution Modes:**

*   **Manual**: Instant execution via UI (**ETL Pipeline** > **Actions** > **Sync Now**)
*   **Scheduled**: Automated cron-based execution configured in pipeline settings
*   **API**: REST integration via **ETL Pipeline** > **Actions** > **Download Postman Collection**

**Monitoring:**

*   **Real-time status**: Dashboard view and run details
*   **Execution history**: **MANAGE** > **Logs** > **ETL Sessions**

**Notifications**:

*   **ETL Pipeline** > **Actions** > **Setup Notifications**

Next Steps[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/#next-steps "Direct link to Next Steps")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Set Up ETL Pipeline](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/set_up_etl_pipeline/)
    

*   [Key Features](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/#key-features)
    
*   [Smart File Handling](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/#smart-file-handling)
    
*   [Getting Started](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/#getting-started)
    
*   [Execution and Monitoring](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/#execution-and-monitoring)
    
*   [Next Steps](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/etl_pipeline/unstract_etl_pipeline_intro/#next-steps)

# Unstructured.io Enterprise Edition Text Extractor | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/text_extractors/unstructured_io_enterprise_text_extractor/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

![img Unstructured.io Enterprise Edition Text Extractor Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/unstructred_io_enterprise-97696ab7d323447cf9e43a80bd096160.png)

---

# Exporting Projects | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/exporting_projects/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Click the **Export** button in the top-right corner of the project to export your project configuration. The export includes the schema, prompt, verified data, and project settings.

---

# Analytics Dashboard | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/analytics_dashboard/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

The **Analytics** tab provides a high-level summary of extraction performance across your entire document set.

While the Mismatch Matrix shows field-by-document detail, the Analytics Dashboard gives you the aggregate picture at a glance.

![img Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/analytics_dashboard-2fd807333184295fced4085c29b5f43e.png)

At the top, the dashboard displays four summary metrics:

| Metric | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Total Documents** | Total documents in the project |
| **Total Fields** | Total fields identified across all documents |
| **Overall Accuracy** | Accuracy as a percentage |
| **Failed Fields** | Fields with at least one mismatch across any document |

Below the summary, the **Top Mismatched Fields** table lists the fields with the lowest accuracy, ranked by error count. Each row shows the field path, its accuracy percentage, and the number of errors.

This tells you immediately which fields are causing the most extraction failures across your document set, and therefore where prompt refinement effort will have the most impact.

Clicking a **field name** in the Top Mismatched Fields table opens a **Field Detail** dialog. This dialog shows the field's accuracy percentage and lists every document where a mismatch occurred, with the verified value and extracted value displayed side by side for each.

Click **Refresh** to update the dashboard after making changes to prompts, extraction results, or verified data.

---

# Cascading Workflows | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-cascading-workflows/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Enterprise Feature

Cascading workflows enable you to promote changes across **DEV**, **TEST**, **PROD** environments available with the **Enterprise SSO** feature.

Use Platform APIs to move Prompt Studio projects, adapters, and deployments between Unstract environments.

Promote Prompt Studio Project[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-cascading-workflows/#promote-prompt-studio-project "Direct link to Promote Prompt Studio Project")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Step | Action | API | Details |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1   | **Export** project from source | `GET /api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/project-transfer/{tool_id}` | [Prompt Studio — Export Project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-prompt-studio/#export-project-download) |
| 2a  | **Import** by creating a new project on target | `POST /api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/project-transfer/` | [Prompt Studio — Import Project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-prompt-studio/#import-project) |
| 2b  | **Or sync** prompts into existing project on target | `POST /api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/{tool_id}/sync-prompts/` | [Prompt Studio — Sync Prompts](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-prompt-studio/#sync-prompts) |
| 3   | **Configure adapters** on target (environment-specific) | `POST /api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/adapter/`, `POST /api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/adapter/default_triad/` | [Adapters](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-adapters/) |
| 4   | **Export tool** to registry for deployment on target | `POST /api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/export/{tool_id}` | [Prompt Studio — Export Tool](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-prompt-studio/#export-tool-to-registry) |
| 5   | **Create deployment** on target | `POST /api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/deployment/` | [API Deployments — Create](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-api-deployments/#create-deployment) |

tip

Step 2a creates a new project on the target. Use step 2b instead if the project already exists and you only want to update the prompts.

*   [Promote Prompt Studio Project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-cascading-workflows/#promote-prompt-studio-project)

---

# Platform API Keys | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-platform-keys/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Platform API keys provide Bearer token authentication for accessing the Unstract Platform API. Keys are managed through the Unstract UI by organization admins.

UI Only

Platform API key management (create, list, update, rotate, delete) is available exclusively through the Unstract UI under **Platform > Platform API Keys**. These operations are not exposed as Platform API endpoints.

![Platform API Keys management UI](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/platform_api_keys_ui-885fcc698c2e99511080efb9aaf89fd4.png)

Key Management[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-platform-keys/#key-management "Direct link to Key Management")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Organization admins can perform the following operations from the UI:

*   **Create** a new API key with a name, description, and permission level
*   **List** all API keys for the organization
*   **Retrieve** details of a specific key
*   **Update** a key's description, active status, or permission level (name is not updatable)
*   **Delete** a key and its associated service account
*   **Rotate** a key to generate a new secret (the old key is invalidated immediately)

* * *

Permission Enum[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-platform-keys/#permission-enum "Direct link to Permission Enum")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Value | Label |
| --- | --- |
| `read` | Read |
| `read_write` | Read/Write |

Input Validation[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-platform-keys/#input-validation "Direct link to Input Validation")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `name` and `description` fields are validated against the following pattern:

    ^[a-zA-Z0-9 \-_.,:()/]+$

Allowed characters: alphanumeric characters, spaces, hyphens, underscores, periods, commas, colons, parentheses, and forward slashes. HTML tags and angle brackets are rejected.

*   [Key Management](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-platform-keys/#key-management)
    
*   [Permission Enum](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-platform-keys/#permission-enum)
    
*   [Input Validation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-platform-keys/#input-validation)

---

# API Deployments | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-api-deployments/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

API Deployments expose workflows as REST APIs for document processing. Once a workflow is deployed, external systems can submit documents and receive structured extraction results.

Authentication

All endpoints require **session or token auth**.

**Pagination:** All list endpoints support `page` (default `1`) and `page_size` (default `50`, max `1000`) query parameters.

* * *

### Deployment Management

Create, list, update, and delete API deployments for workflows.

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/deployment/`

List all API deployments for the current organization.▾

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/deployment/`

Create a new API deployment for a workflow.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/deployment/{id}/`

Retrieve a single deployment by its ID.▾

PUT`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/deployment/{id}/`

Fully update a deployment. Requires owner permission.▾

PATCH`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/deployment/{id}/`

Partially update a deployment. Requires owner permission.▾

DELETE`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/deployment/{id}/`

Delete a deployment. Returns 204 on success. Requires owner permission.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/deployment/{id}/users/`

List users the deployment is shared with.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/deployment/by-prompt-studio-tool/`

Get deployments associated with a specific Prompt Studio tool.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/postman_collection/{id}/`

Download a Postman collection JSON file for the deployment.▾

* * *

### Deployment API Keys

Manage API keys used to authenticate execution requests against deployments.

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/keys/api/{api_id}/`

List all API keys for a specific deployment.▾

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/keys/api/{api_id}/`

Create a new API key for a deployment or pipeline.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/keys/{id}/`

Retrieve a single API key by its ID.▾

PUT`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/keys/{id}/`

Update an API key. Typically used to toggle is\_active or update description.▾

DELETE`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/api/keys/{id}/`

Delete an API key. Returns 204 on success.▾

---

# Workflows | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-workflows/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Workflow endpoints manage the full lifecycle of workflows in Unstract — including CRUD operations, execution control, endpoint configuration (source/destination connectors), execution monitoring, and file history tracking.

Permissions

*   **List / Retrieve / Schema / Shared Users:** `IsOwnerOrSharedUserOrSharedToOrg`
*   **Update / Delete:** `IsOwner`

* * *

Pagination

All paginated endpoints use `page` (default `1`) and `page_size` (default `50`, max `1000`) query parameters and return responses in the format: `{ count, next, previous, results }`.

* * *

### Workflow CRUD

Create, list, retrieve, update, and delete workflows.

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/`

List all workflows accessible to the current user.▾

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/`

Create a new workflow. The is\_active field is automatically set to true and workflow\_owner is set to the requesting user.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/{id}/`

Retrieve a single workflow by its ID.▾

PUT`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/{id}/`

Fully update a workflow. Requires IsOwner permission. PATCH is also supported for partial updates.▾

DELETE`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/{id}/`

Delete a workflow. Requires IsOwner permission. Returns 204 No Content on success.▾

* * *

### Workflow Actions

Execute workflows, manage activation state, check update permissions, clear file markers, and retrieve schemas.

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/execute/`

Execute a workflow. Accepts multipart/form-data (for file uploads) or JSON. Use execution\_action to control flow for multi-step executions.▾

PUT`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/active/{id}/`

Toggle the active state of a workflow.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/{id}/can-update/`

Check whether the current user has permission to update the specified workflow.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/{id}/clear-file-marker/`

Clear the file processing marker for the specified workflow, allowing previously processed files to be re-processed.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/schema/`

Retrieve the JSON schema for workflow source or destination connector configuration.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/{id}/users/`

List the users a workflow is shared with, including organization-wide sharing status.▾

* * *

### Workflow Executions

List, retrieve, and inspect execution logs for workflow runs.

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/{id}/execution/`

List all executions for a specific workflow. Requires IsOwner permission.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/execution/{id}/`

Retrieve details of a single execution by its ID.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/execution/{id}/logs/`

Retrieve paginated execution logs. Filter by file execution or log level.▾

* * *

### File History

Track and manage file processing history for workflows. View which files have been processed, their status, and execution counts.

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/{workflow_id}/file-histories/`

List file histories for a workflow. Supports filtering by status, execution count, and file path prefix.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/{workflow_id}/file-histories/{id}/`

Retrieve a single file history entry.▾

DELETE`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/{workflow_id}/file-histories/{id}/`

Delete a single file history entry. Returns 204 No Content on success.▾

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/workflow/{workflow_id}/file-histories/clear/`

Bulk delete file histories matching the specified filters. At least one filter must be provided.▾

---

# Prompt Studio | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-prompt-studio/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Prompt Studio endpoints manage projects (called "Custom Tools" internally), prompts, LLM profiles, documents, and tool exports.

Authentication

These endpoints require a **Bearer token** or **session auth**. Permission varies by action — most endpoints use `IsOwnerOrSharedUserOrSharedToOrg`, while delete operations require `IsOwner`.

* * *

### Projects (Custom Tools)

Create, list, retrieve, update, and delete Prompt Studio projects.

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/`

List all Prompt Studio projects accessible to the current user.▾

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/`

Create a new Prompt Studio project.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/{tool_id}/`

Retrieve a single Prompt Studio project by its ID.▾

PUT`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/{tool_id}/`

Fully update a Prompt Studio project. All writable fields should be provided.▾

PATCH`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/{tool_id}/`

Partially update a Prompt Studio project. Only provided fields are updated.▾

DELETE`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/{tool_id}/`

Delete a Prompt Studio project. Returns 204 No Content on success. Fails with 409 Conflict if the tool has been exported and is currently used in workflows.▾

* * *

### Project Transfer (Export/Import)

Export projects as JSON files for backup or transfer, and import them into other environments.

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/project-transfer/{tool_id}`

Export a Prompt Studio project as a downloadable JSON file.▾

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/project-transfer/`

Import a Prompt Studio project from a JSON file. Uses multipart/form-data encoding.▾

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/{tool_id}/sync-prompts/`

Sync prompts from an exported project JSON into an existing project. Optionally creates a backup copy before syncing.▾

* * *

### Tool Export (for Deployment)

Export a Prompt Studio project as a tool to the registry for use in workflows and pipelines.

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/export/{tool_id}`

Export a Prompt Studio project as a tool to the tool registry, making it available for use in workflows.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/export/{tool_id}`

Get export information for a project. Returns 204 No Content if the tool has not been exported.▾

* * *

### Document Management

Upload, fetch, and delete documents associated with a Prompt Studio project.

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/file/{tool_id}`

Upload one or more files to a Prompt Studio project. Uses multipart/form-data encoding.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/file/{tool_id}`

Fetch the contents of a document associated with a Prompt Studio project.▾

DELETE`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/file/{tool_id}`

Delete a document from a Prompt Studio project.▾

* * *

### Prompts

Create, retrieve, update, delete, and reorder prompts within a Prompt Studio project.

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/prompt-studio-prompt/{tool_id}/`

Create a new prompt within a Prompt Studio project.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/prompt/{prompt_id}/`

Retrieve a single prompt by its ID.▾

PUT`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/prompt/{prompt_id}/`

Fully update a prompt.▾

PATCH`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/prompt/{prompt_id}/`

Partially update a prompt. Only provided fields are updated.▾

DELETE`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/prompt/{prompt_id}/`

Delete a prompt. Returns 204 No Content on success.▾

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/prompt/reorder/`

Reorder a prompt by moving it from one sequence position to another.▾

* * *

### LLM Profiles

Manage LLM profiles that define which models, vector stores, and retrieval strategies are used within a project. Maximum 4 profiles per project.

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/prompt-studio-profile/{tool_id}/`

List all LLM profiles for a Prompt Studio project.▾

PATCH`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/prompt-studio-profile/{tool_id}/`

Set the default LLM profile for a Prompt Studio project.▾

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/profilemanager/{tool_id}`

Create a new LLM profile for a project. Maximum 4 profiles per project.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/profile-manager/{profile_id}/`

Retrieve a single LLM profile by its ID.▾

PUT`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/profile-manager/{profile_id}/`

Fully update an LLM profile.▾

PATCH`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/profile-manager/{profile_id}/`

Partially update an LLM profile. Only provided fields are updated.▾

DELETE`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/profile-manager/{profile_id}/`

Delete an LLM profile. Returns 204 No Content on success.▾

* * *

### Execution

Index documents, run individual prompts, and perform single-pass extractions.

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/index-document/{tool_id}`

Index a document for retrieval-augmented generation within the project.▾

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/fetch_response/{tool_id}`

Run a single prompt against a document and fetch the LLM response.▾

POST`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/single-pass-extraction/{tool_id}`

Run all active prompts against a document in a single pass. Requires single\_pass\_extraction\_mode to be enabled on the project.▾

* * *

### Other

Miscellaneous endpoints for shared users, deployment checks, and configuration choices.

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/users/{tool_id}`

List shared users for a Prompt Studio project.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/{tool_id}/check_deployment_usage/`

Check whether an exported tool is currently used in any deployments.▾

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/select_choices/`

Returns available dropdown field values for Prompt Studio forms (e.g., enforce\_type options, prompt\_type options).

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/adapter-choices/`

Returns available adapter choices for use in LLM profiles, including LLMs, embedding models, vector stores, and text extractors.

GET`/api/v1/unstract/{org_id}/prompt-studio/{tool_id}/get_retrieval_strategies/`

Returns metadata about available retrieval strategies for the project.▾

---

# N8N Custom Node | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_n8n_custom_node/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

N8N is a workflow automation tool that allows you to automate tasks and workflows. This guide will show you how to use the Unstract custom node for N8N that interacts with a deployed Unstract API.

The N8N custom node is available in npm as `n8n-nodes-unstract`  
[https://www.npmjs.com/package/n8n-nodes-unstract](https://www.npmjs.com/package/n8n-nodes-unstract)
.

Installation[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_n8n_custom_node/#installation "Direct link to Installation")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Installation](https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/community-nodes/installation/gui-install/)
 documentation from N8N for custom community nodes.

Once installed it would be available in Community Nodes.

![Confirmation of installation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/n8n_installation_confirmation-e513ebcda70272f1b8def8b70691060d.png)

info

After installing a community node, it will only become visible after restarting n8n.

Authentication Credentials[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_n8n_custom_node/#authentication-credentials "Direct link to Authentication Credentials")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Credentials are required to authenticate with the Unstract API. New credentials can be created in N8N using the credentials section in overview. Enter "Unstract API" when asked to "Select an app or service to connect to". Enter the API key and the Organization ID.

![Input Parameters](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/n8n_unstract_creds-b5ab00722ee76041c94b176c2bf4d15a.png)

🔑 How to Get Your API Key, Organization ID, and Deployment Name[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_n8n_custom_node/#-how-to-get-your-api-key-organization-id-and-deployment-name "Direct link to 🔑 How to Get Your API Key, Organization ID, and Deployment Name")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Follow these steps to retrieve the required details from the Unstract API Deployments screen:

### 1\. Navigate to **API Deployments**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_n8n_custom_node/#1-navigate-to-api-deployments "Direct link to 1-navigate-to-api-deployments")

*   From the sidebar, click on **API Deployments** (highlighted with marker **①**).

### 2\. Get the **Organization ID** and **API Deployment Name**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_n8n_custom_node/#2-get-the-organization-id-and-api-deployment-name "Direct link to 2-get-the-organization-id-and-api-deployment-name")

*   Look at the **API Endpoint** column.
    
*   The endpoint URL contains both:
    
    *   **Organization ID** → It's the segment after `/org_`, e.g., `org_HJqNgodUQsA99S3A` (**②**).
    *   **API Deployment Name** → It appears immediately after the organization ID, e.g., `credit_card_api_new1` (**⑤**).

### 3\. Open the **Actions Menu**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_n8n_custom_node/#3-open-the-actions-menu "Direct link to 3-open-the-actions-menu")

*   Click the three-dot menu next to your deployment (**③**).

### 4\. Select **Manage Keys**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_n8n_custom_node/#4-select-manage-keys "Direct link to 4-select-manage-keys")

*   From the dropdown, click **Manage Keys** (**④**) to view or generate your **API Key**.

![img n8n LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/n8n_api_key-89f9b9e628e1e961c0eabf919d9d64a2.png)

Usage[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_n8n_custom_node/#usage "Direct link to Usage")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Input Parameters[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_n8n_custom_node/#input-parameters "Direct link to Input Parameters")

Note that **File Contents** is binary file contents loaded from any previous node.

![Input Parameters](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/n8n_unstract_parameters-ac79d2f708565c2aca4746488f8db579.png)

*   API Deployment name:👉 For instructions on how to get the Organization ID and API Deployment Name, refer to [this section](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_n8n_custom_node/#2-get-the-organization-id-and-api-deployment-name)
    .
*   File Contents: The Key name of the binary File from the previous step.

A typical workflow would look like this:

# Task Pipeline Introduction | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/unstract_task_pipeline_intro/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Unstract's Task Pipeline handles file transfer and processing workflows between storage systems. Unlike ETL Pipelines, it focuses on moving files rather than data transformation.

Key Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/unstract_task_pipeline_intro/#key-features "Direct link to Key Features")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Extract** from [Filesystem Sources](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/index/)
    
*   **Transform** data using [Tool exported from Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/)
    
*   **Load** to popular [Filesystem Destinations](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/index/)
    

Smart File Handling[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/unstract_task_pipeline_intro/#smart-file-handling "Direct link to Smart File Handling")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Unstract's Task pipelines are designed for maximum efficiency and reliability:

*   Keeps track of new files in each pipeline run to avoid reprocessing
*   Skips duplicate files (same name, path, and content) automatically
*   Executes files in parallel for faster batch processing
*   Retries execution on failure to ensure reliability

Getting Started[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/unstract_task_pipeline_intro/#getting-started "Direct link to Getting Started")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To create your first Task Pipeline, see [Set Up Task Pipeline](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/set_up_task_pipeline/)

Execution and Monitoring[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/unstract_task_pipeline_intro/#execution-and-monitoring "Direct link to Execution and Monitoring")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Execution Modes:**

*   **Manual**: Instant execution via UI (**Task Pipeline** > **Actions** > **Sync Now**)
*   **Scheduled**: Automated cron-based execution configured in pipeline settings
*   **API**: REST integration via **Task Pipeline** > **Actions** > **Download Postman Collection**

**Monitoring:**

*   **Real-time status**: Dashboard view and run details
*   **Execution history**: **MANAGE** > **Logs** > **Task Sessions**

**Notifications**:

*   **Task Pipelines** > **Actions** > **[Setup Notifications](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/notification/unstract_notification_intro/)
    **

Next Steps[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/unstract_task_pipeline_intro/#next-steps "Direct link to Next Steps")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Set Up Task Pipeline](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/set_up_task_pipeline/)
    

*   [Key Features](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/unstract_task_pipeline_intro/#key-features)
    
*   [Smart File Handling](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/unstract_task_pipeline_intro/#smart-file-handling)
    
*   [Getting Started](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/unstract_task_pipeline_intro/#getting-started)
    
*   [Execution and Monitoring](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/unstract_task_pipeline_intro/#execution-and-monitoring)
    
*   [Next Steps](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/task_pipeline/unstract_task_pipeline_intro/#next-steps)
