# SSO Group-to-Role Mapping Guide | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

This guide explains how to map your identity provider's groups to Unstract roles, enabling role-based access control through Enterprise SSO.

info

This step is **only required if you have configured Enterprise SSO** for your Unstract deployment. If you are not using Enterprise SSO, you can skip this guide.

Prerequisites

Before proceeding, ensure:

*   Unstract is deployed and running per the [On-Prem Deployment Guide](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_deployment/)
    .
*   Your identity provider (IdP) is configured per the [Azure AD](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/azure_ad/)
     or [Okta](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/enterprise_sso/okta/)
     Enterprise SSO guide.

Step 1: Prepare the Configuration File[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#step-1-prepare-the-configuration-file "Direct link to Step 1: Prepare the Configuration File")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Create a JSON configuration file (e.g., `domain-config.json`) with the following structure:

    {    "domain": "<your-domain>",    "client_id": "<your-sso-client-id>",    "org_name": "<your-organization-name>",    "role_mapping": [        {            "group": "<idp-group-name>",            "unstract_role": "<unstract-role>",            "provider": "<identity-provider>"        }    ]}

### Field Reference[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#field-reference "Direct link to Field Reference")

| Field | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `domain` | Yes | The application domain (same as `ZIPSTACK_ID_DOMAIN` in your Helm secret values) |
| `client_id` | Yes | The Auth0 client ID (same as `ZIPSTACK_ID_CLIENT_ID` in your Helm secret values) |
| `org_name` | No  | The organization name created on the Auth0 side. Contact the Unstract team to get this value. |
| `role_mapping` | No  | Array of group-to-role mappings (see below). Technically optional, but must be provided to enable group-to-role assignment. |

### Role Mapping Fields[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#role-mapping-fields "Direct link to Role Mapping Fields")

Each entry in `role_mapping` requires all three fields:

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `group` | The group name as configured in your identity provider |
| `unstract_role` | The Unstract role to assign (see supported roles table below) |
| `provider` | The identity provider type (e.g., `okta`, `azure_ad`, `okta_saml`) |

### Supported Unstract Roles[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#supported-unstract-roles "Direct link to Supported Unstract Roles")

Each Unstract role must be mapped to a corresponding group in your identity provider's directory.

| Unstract Role | Customer Directory Group (example) |
| --- | --- |
| `unstract_user` | `customer_unstract_user_dev` |
| `unstract_admin` | `customer_unstract_admin_dev` |
| `unstract_supervisor` | `customer_unstract_supervisor_dev` |
| `unstract_reviewer` | `customer_unstract_reviewer_dev` |

note

The customer directory group names above are examples. Replace them with the actual group names configured in your identity provider.

### Example Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#example-configuration "Direct link to Example Configuration")

    {    "domain": "acme.com",    "client_id": "xYz789AbCdEf",    "org_name": "Acme Corporation",    "role_mapping": [        {            "group": "customer_unstract_admin_dev",            "unstract_role": "unstract_admin",            "provider": "okta"        },        {            "group": "customer_unstract_user_dev",            "unstract_role": "unstract_user",            "provider": "okta"        }    ]}

Step 2: Identify the Backend Pod[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#step-2-identify-the-backend-pod "Direct link to Step 2: Identify the Backend Pod")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    kubectl get pods -n <namespace> | grep backend

Step 3: Run the Init Domain Command[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#step-3-run-the-init-domain-command "Direct link to Step 3: Run the Init Domain Command")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Run the following command to write the configuration and initialize the domain:

    kubectl exec -n <namespace> <backend-pod-name> -- \    bash -c 'source /app/.venv/bin/activate && cat > /tmp/domain-config.json << '\''EOF'\''{    "domain": "acme.com",    "client_id": "xYz789AbCdEf",    "org_name": "Acme Corporation",    "role_mapping": [        {            "group": "customer_unstract_admin_dev",            "unstract_role": "unstract_admin",            "provider": "okta"        },        {            "group": "customer_unstract_user_dev",            "unstract_role": "unstract_user",            "provider": "okta"        }    ]}EOFpython manage.py init_domain /tmp/domain-config.json && rm /tmp/domain-config.json'

note

Replace the JSON values above with your actual domain, client ID, organization name, and role mappings.

On success, you should see output similar to:

    Created CustomDomains: acme.comCreated CustomDomainOrg: acme.com -> Acme CorporationCreated CustomDomainRoles: customer_unstract_admin_dev -> unstract_adminCreated CustomDomainRoles: customer_unstract_user_dev -> unstract_userDomain initialization complete.

Step 4: Verify the Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#step-4-verify-the-configuration "Direct link to Step 4: Verify the Configuration")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Run the list command to confirm everything was set up correctly:

    kubectl exec -n <namespace> <backend-pod-name> -- \    bash -c "source /app/.venv/bin/activate && python manage.py list_domains"

Important Notes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#important-notes "Direct link to Important Notes")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Idempotent**: The command is safe to re-run. If the domain already exists, it will update the existing configuration.
*   **Role mappings are replaced**: Each time you run the command, all existing role mappings for that domain are deleted and recreated from the JSON file. Ensure your configuration file contains the complete set of role mappings.
*   **Atomic operation**: All changes are applied together. If any step fails, none of the changes are saved.
*   **Invalid role entries are skipped**: If a role mapping is missing any required field (`group`, `unstract_role`, or `provider`), that entry is skipped with a warning. Other valid entries are still processed.

*   [Step 1: Prepare the Configuration File](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#step-1-prepare-the-configuration-file)
    *   [Field Reference](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#field-reference)
        
    *   [Role Mapping Fields](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#role-mapping-fields)
        
    *   [Supported Unstract Roles](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#supported-unstract-roles)
        
    *   [Example Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#example-configuration)
        
*   [Step 2: Identify the Backend Pod](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#step-2-identify-the-backend-pod)
    
*   [Step 3: Run the Init Domain Command](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#step-3-run-the-init-domain-command)
    
*   [Step 4: Verify the Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#step-4-verify-the-configuration)
    
*   [Important Notes](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_sso_role_mapping/#important-notes)

---

# On-Prem Release Notes | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

This page lists all stable Unstract on-prem releases. Release notes are organized by year — select a year below to view the full changelog.

*   [2026](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2026/)
     — Latest releases
*   [2025](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2025/)
     — Earlier releases

Download Configuration Files[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/#download-configuration-files "Direct link to Download Configuration Files")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use the command below to extract the configuration files for a specific release directly from the Helm chart registry. Replace `<VERSION>` with the target release version (e.g., `0.158.0`).

note

You must be authenticated to the Helm registry before running this command. See [Step 2 of the Deployment Guide](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_deployment/#2-installation-one-time)
 for authentication instructions.

    VERSION=<VERSION>TMP_DIR="/tmp/unstract-extract-$(date +%s)"trap 'rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"' EXITmkdir -p "$TMP_DIR"helm pull oci://us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/pandoras-tamer/charts/unstract-platform \  --version "$VERSION" --untar --untardir "$TMP_DIR"cp "$TMP_DIR/unstract-platform/sample.on-prem.values.yaml" "./sample.on-prem.values-$VERSION.yaml"cp "$TMP_DIR/unstract-platform/sample.on-prem.secret.yaml" "./sample.on-prem.secret-$VERSION.yaml"cp "$TMP_DIR/unstract-platform/values-multi-az.yaml" "./values-multi-az-$VERSION.yaml"

This extracts three files:

| File | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `sample.on-prem.values-<VERSION>.yaml` | Non-sensitive configuration — rename to `on-prem.values.yaml` and fill in `# <REQUIRED>` placeholders |
| `sample.on-prem.secret-<VERSION>.yaml` | Sensitive credentials — rename to `on-prem.secret.yaml` and fill in `# <REQUIRED>` placeholders |
| `values-multi-az-<VERSION>.yaml` | HA/Multi-AZ override — apply this in addition to the above for [HA deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_ha_deployment/) |

*   [Download Configuration Files](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/#download-configuration-files)

---

# On-Prem Disaster Recovery SOP | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

This document provides disaster recovery procedures for Unstract and LLMWhisperer on-premise deployments. It covers recovery from AZ or region-wide failures using a **cold standby approach**. Cold standby is recommended since Unstract is usually business critical but not mission critical.

| Objective | Target |
| --- | --- |
| Recovery Time Objective (RTO) | 6 hours |
| Recovery Point Objective (RPO) | 24 hours |

1\. Scope[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#1-scope "Direct link to 1. Scope")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Unstract platform recovery
*   LLMWhisperer service recovery
*   Configuration and data restoration
*   Application-specific validation

### Customer Responsibilities[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#customer-responsibilities "Direct link to Customer Responsibilities")

*   Infrastructure provisioning in DR region
*   Database backup/restore operations
*   Network, SSL and DNS configuration
*   Cloud Object Storage replication setup (required for Prompt Studio sample files to be restored)

### Data Loss Considerations[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#data-loss-considerations "Direct link to Data Loss Considerations")

| Component | Impact of 24-hour RPO |
| --- | --- |
| Processed documents | May need re-processing |
| Workflows in progress | Will remain in limbo state, can be retriggered |
| Configuration changes | Reverted to last backup |
| LLMWhisperer APIs | In-flight requests lost, must be retried |
| Usage data | Potential mismatch with vendor records |

2\. Prerequisites[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#2-prerequisites "Direct link to 2. Prerequisites")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Documentation Requirements[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#documentation-requirements "Direct link to Documentation Requirements")

*   [ ]  Current `values.yaml` and `secret.yaml` files (version controlled)
*   [ ]  `artifact-key.json` for image registry access
*   [ ]  Deployment documentation reference
*   [ ]  DR region infrastructure specifications

### Infrastructure Prerequisites[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#infrastructure-prerequisites "Direct link to Infrastructure Prerequisites")

**Customer must have the ability to provision within 4 hours:**

*   [ ]  Recreate the same infrastructure required as per the [deployment docs](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_infra_requirements/)
    
*   [ ]  Restore the backed up database to a new instance, or use a cross-region replica if available
*   [ ]  For cloud object storage, use the replicated storage

### Backup Requirements[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#backup-requirements "Direct link to Backup Requirements")

*   [ ]  Database automated backups (daily minimum)
*   [ ]  Cloud Object Storage cross-region replication active
*   [ ]  Configuration files in version control or another secure location

3\. Backup Procedures[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#3-backup-procedures "Direct link to 3. Backup Procedures")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Automated Backups (Customer Managed)[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#automated-backups-customer-managed "Direct link to Automated Backups (Customer Managed)")

*   **Databases**: Daily automated snapshots with 7-day retention
*   **Cloud Object Storage**: Real-time cross-region replication
*   **Monitoring**: Backup success/failure alerts

### Kubernetes Resource Backup (Optional)[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#kubernetes-resource-backup-optional "Direct link to Kubernetes Resource Backup (Optional)")

Kubernetes resources do not need to be backed up as they can be restored using Helm. However, if you have directly made resource changes in Kubernetes or added additional resources, you may choose to back them up.

4\. Recovery Procedures[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#4-recovery-procedures "Direct link to 4. Recovery Procedures")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Phase 1: Environment Preparation (2 hours)[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#phase-1-environment-preparation-2-hours "Direct link to Phase 1: Environment Preparation (2 hours)")

#### Infrastructure Provisioning[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#infrastructure-provisioning "Direct link to Infrastructure Provisioning")

**Customer Action Required** — Provision the following in the DR region:

*   Kubernetes cluster matching production specs
*   PostgreSQL instances (2 databases)
*   Cloud Storage buckets with replication
*   Load balancers and networking
*   DNS entries (can be updated later)

#### Database Restoration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#database-restoration "Direct link to Database Restoration")

**Customer Action Required:**

*   Restore both PostgreSQL databases from latest backup
*   Verify connectivity and update endpoints

### Phase 2: Configuration Update (30 minutes)[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#phase-2-configuration-update-30-minutes "Direct link to Phase 2: Configuration Update (30 minutes)")

Update your `values.yaml` and `secret.yaml` files with:

*   New database endpoints
*   New storage bucket names/endpoints
*   New ingress domains
*   Any region-specific configurations

danger

Ensure `ENCRYPTION_KEY` in `secret.yaml` remains unchanged to decrypt existing data.

### Phase 3: Application Deployment (1 hour)[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#phase-3-application-deployment-1-hour "Direct link to Phase 3: Application Deployment (1 hour)")

Deploy applications following the standard deployment procedures:

1.  **Deploy Unstract**: Follow the [Deployment Guide — Installation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_deployment/#2-installation-one-time)
     section
2.  **Deploy LLMWhisperer**: Follow the [LLMWhisperer deployment guide](https://docs.unstract.com/llmwhisperer/llm_whisperer/on_prem/on_prem_deployment/)
    

Use the same Helm commands and values files as documented in your original deployment, with the updated DR configuration files.

### Phase 4: Network Configuration (Customer Managed)[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#phase-4-network-configuration-customer-managed "Direct link to Phase 4: Network Configuration (Customer Managed)")

*   Update DNS to point to DR region
*   Configure SSL certificates
*   Verify ingress timeout settings (900 seconds)

5\. Post-Recovery Validation[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#5-post-recovery-validation "Direct link to 5. Post-Recovery Validation")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### System Health Checks[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#system-health-checks "Direct link to System Health Checks")

Ensure all health checks at pod level (and at ingress level, if configured) are passing.

    kubectl get pods -n unstract

All pods should be in `Running` state with zero restarts.

### Functional Validation[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#functional-validation "Direct link to Functional Validation")

1.  **Unstract Platform:**
    
    *   [ ]  Access web interface
    *   [ ]  Login
    *   [ ]  Create test workflow / View existing workflows
    *   [ ]  Upload and process test document
    *   [ ]  Verify API deployments
2.  **LLMWhisperer:**
    
    *   [ ]  Access dashboard
    *   [ ]  Test extract endpoint
    *   [ ]  Verify usage tracking

### Known Issues Post-Recovery[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#known-issues-post-recovery "Direct link to Known Issues Post-Recovery")

*   In-flight workflow executions could remain as in-progress in the DB record at the time of the last snapshot. Anything after that will be lost.
*   LLMWhisperer in-flight requests are lost
*   Usage data may be inconsistent with vendor records
*   Configuration changes made in the last 24 hours (last backup point) to adapters, Prompt Studio, workflows, etc. may be lost

6\. Testing Guidelines[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#6-testing-guidelines "Direct link to 6. Testing Guidelines")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Testing Schedule[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#testing-schedule "Direct link to Testing Schedule")

*   **Frequency**: Semi-annually
*   **Type**: Table-top exercise or partial test

### Test Procedure[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#test-procedure "Direct link to Test Procedure")

1.  Document review and update
2.  Verify backup availability
3.  Test Kubernetes resource restoration
4.  Validate configuration files
5.  Deploy to test namespace (optional)

### Success Criteria[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#success-criteria "Direct link to Success Criteria")

*   All pods running without crashes
*   API endpoints responding
*   Test document processed successfully
*   No critical errors in logs

Appendix[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#appendix "Direct link to Appendix")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### A. Quick Reference Commands[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#a-quick-reference-commands "Direct link to A. Quick Reference Commands")

    # Check deployment statushelm list -n unstracthelm list -n llmwhisperer# View current configurationhelm get values unstract-platform -n unstract > current-unstract-values.yamlhelm get values whisperer -n llmwhisperer > current-llmwhisperer-values.yaml# Debug pod issueskubectl describe pod [POD_NAME] -n [NAMESPACE]kubectl logs [POD_NAME] -n [NAMESPACE] --previous

### B. Troubleshooting[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#b-troubleshooting "Direct link to B. Troubleshooting")

1.  **Image Pull Errors**
    
    *   Verify `gcr-artifact-secret` exists in namespace
    *   Check `artifact-key.json` validity
    *   Ensure Helm registry login succeeded
2.  **Database Connection Failed**
    
    *   Verify database endpoints in `secret.yaml`
    *   Check network connectivity
    *   Validate credentials
3.  **Storage Access Denied**
    
    *   Verify IAM policies match production
    *   Check bucket names in `values.yaml`
    *   Validate credentials in `secret.yaml`
4.  **Pods in CrashLoopBackOff**
    
    *   Check pod logs for specific errors
    *   Verify all secrets are present
    *   Ensure resource limits are adequate

### C. Contact Information[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_disaster_recovery/#c-contact-information "Direct link to C. Contact Information")
