# On-Prem Release Notes - 2026 | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2026/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Jump to version:Select versionv0.158.4v0.158.0v0.148.3v0.139.3v0.130.0v0.128.1v0.128.0v0.122.8v0.118.18v0.116.20v0.116.18v0.113.0

### v0.158.4 — March 27, 2026[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2026/#v0-158-4 "Direct link to v0.158.4 — March 27, 2026")

**New Features**

*   **AWS S3 IRSA authentication**: Support for IAM Roles for Service Accounts (IRSA) on both S3 storage and [S3 connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/)
    , eliminating the need for static credentials in EKS deployments
*   **HTTP session lifecycle management**: Managed HTTP session pooling for workers API clients, improving connection reuse and reliability

**Fixes & Improvements**

*   Added TTL to [API HITL](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
     settings and fixed NaN TTL display
*   Security hardening: cookie security attributes and XSS prevention headers
*   Added input validation and Content Security Policy (CSP) headers across backend and frontend
*   Frontend CSP adjustments for RJSF form rendering (`unsafe-eval`) and PDF viewer (`blob:`)
*   Switched litellm to Zipstack fork after PyPI quarantine
*   Upgraded litellm to 1.82.3 to fix Azure OpenAI connection errors
*   Monkey-patched litellm Cohere embed timeout for Bedrock embeddings
*   Added LLMCompat bridge class to fix retriever LLM compatibility with llama-index
*   Handle LLM refusal responses to prevent NoneType errors
*   Include adapter name in error messages for easier debugging

**Helm Configuration Changes**

*   **Hardcoded image references** now configurable via `values.yaml` — previously hardcoded container image paths can be overridden for air-gapped or custom registry deployments

* * *

### v0.158.0 — March 19, 2026[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2026/#v0-158-0 "Direct link to v0.158.0 — March 19, 2026")

**New Features**

*   **Platform API keys** for programmatic access to the Unstract Platform API. Organization admins can create, list, update, rotate, and delete API keys via the UI under **Platform > Platform API Keys**. Keys provide Bearer token authentication for all Platform API endpoints. See [Platform API Keys documentation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-platform-keys/)
     for details.
*   **Service account access bypass** for pluggable apps, enabling automated integrations without manual permission grants
*   **Agentic Prompt Studio** (beta): new backend and frontend for agentic document extraction workflows. See [Agentic Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/agentic_prompt_studio_intro/)
     for details.
*   **HITL enhancements**: sidebar navigation, queue deletion, nested table support, fetch-specific for targeted document review, reviewer name display on in-review documents, and default TTL changed from unlimited to 90 days. See [HITL documentation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
     for details.
*   **CSV, TXT, and Excel file support** in [Prompt Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/adding_and_managing_documents/)
     file converter
*   **Dashboard metrics system** with plan banner, welcome card, and subscription usage tab
*   **Card-based layout** for Pipelines and [API Deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
     listing pages
*   **Profile page** now displays role and organization info
*   **1M context support** for [Anthropic LLM adapters](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anthropic_llm/)
    
*   **Vertex AI `vertex_location`** support for regional endpoint configuration. See [Gemini Pro adapter](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_pro_llm/)
     for details.
*   **`total_pages_processed`** exposed in [execution API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_execution_api/)
     response and worker destination metadata
*   **SharePoint/OneDrive connector** for filesystem integration. See [Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
     for details.
*   **Azure AI Foundry adapter** for LLM access
*   **Redis Sentinel HA support** with dual-mode configuration for backend, Celery, cache, tool containers, sidecars, and manual review queue. See [HA Deployment](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_ha_deployment/)
     for details.
*   **RabbitMQ HA** with configurable quorum queues. See [HA Deployment](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_ha_deployment/)
     for details.
*   **MinIO HA** support via optional MinIO Operator. See [HA Deployment](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_ha_deployment/)
     for details.
*   **OAuth product scope** added to login/signup authorization requests
*   **Dynamic plugin loading** infrastructure for enterprise components, migrations, and rule engine
*   **Sidebar expand-on-hover** UX improvement
*   **Documentation link popover** for connectors in the UI
*   **Workflow deletion errors** now show specific pipeline/API deployment names

**Fixes & Improvements**

*   **HITL reliability**: PostgreSQL count mismatch and slow query optimizations, bulk queries with lrange, soft-delete for DB-synced records, TTL display in days, rule engine nested array flattening fix, API rules evaluation fix after removal, add-row race condition fix, and queue metadata backfill migration
*   **Custom data** support added to single pass extraction; fixed string values being wrapped in extra quotes. See [Custom Data](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/)
     for details.
*   **PostgreSQL race condition** in concurrent table creation handled
*   **Memory and resource leak fixes**: database cursor closure in subscription usage handler, platform-service resource leaks
*   **Ollama adapters** fixed post LiteLLM migration. See [Ollama adapter](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/)
     for details.
*   **Packet processing** final fetch response API fix
*   **Role update failures** for existing users resolved
*   **LLMWhisperer** API key lookup database fallback added; client retry backoff configuration added
*   **SIGTERM trap handlers** for graceful container shutdown
*   **Worker API timeout** increased to prevent stuck executions during cron storms
*   **Vertex AI** thinking config skip for pro models when disabled
*   **Azure OpenAI** cost tracking now uses actual model name
*   **Worker query optimization** and retry configuration improvements
*   **fsspec directory listing cache** fix on connectors (including Azure listings expiry regression)
*   **SharePoint** `walk()` now supports `detail=True`
*   **Secure cookie settings** and CSRF cookie secure attribute enabled
*   **Forbidden email handling** in Auth0 OAuth callback
*   **PDF viewer** error fallback when document fails to load
*   **Frontend migrated** from Create React App to Vite
*   **Export reminder state** persists across page reloads
*   **HTTP 409** returned when tool image not found in container registry
*   **Legacy Celery file processing workers** and dead code removed

**Helm Configuration Changes**

*   **Redis Sentinel HA**: new `REDIS_SENTINEL_MODE` env var added to prompt, runner, and multi-az values for dual-mode (standalone/sentinel) support
*   **RabbitMQ HA**: configurable quorum queues support added
*   **MinIO HA**: optional MinIO Operator support (operator deployed separately)
*   **HITL worker secrets** added to on-prem secret template
*   **Agentic Studio** apps and URLs added to on-prem configuration
*   **Backfill metrics** enabled by default for on-prem deployments
*   **`MODEL_PRICES_TTL_IN_DAYS`** changed from 7 to 1
*   **Legacy worker templates** removed: `useUnifiedWorkers` toggle and `workerLogging` config no longer needed

*   [v0.158.4 — March 27, 2026](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2026/#v0-158-4)
    
*   [v0.158.0 — March 19, 2026](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/release_notes/on-prem/on-prem-2026/#v0-158-0)

---

# Azure OpenAI Embedding | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/azure_openai_embedding/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Azure OpenAI Embeddings are a way to convert text data into numerical representations that capture semantic meaning. This allows you to find similar text content or compare documents based on their ideas, even if the wording differs.

Getting started with Azure OpenAI Embedding[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/azure_openai_embedding/#getting-started-with-azure-openai-embedding "Direct link to Getting started with Azure OpenAI Embedding")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Quicksteps- How to use Azure OpenAI Embedding[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/azure_openai_embedding/#quicksteps--how-to-use-azure-openai-embedding "Direct link to Quicksteps- How to use Azure OpenAI Embedding")

1.  Submit the [Azure OpenAI Registration form](https://aka.ms/oaiapply)
    
2.  Create an instance in [Azure portal](https://portal.azure.com/)
    .
    
3.  Use Azure [OpenAI studio](https://oai.azure.com/)
     to deploy the model.
    

### Detailed steps to create Azure OpenAI Embedding[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/azure_openai_embedding/#detailed-steps-to-create-azure-openai-embedding "Direct link to Detailed steps to create Azure OpenAI Embedding")

1.  Azure openAI requires registration and is currently only available to approved enterprise customers and partners.Submit the [Registration form](https://aka.ms/oaiapply)
     for approval.
    
2.  Once the submitted form is approved, Navigate to [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com/)
     and login.
    
3.  Search for `Azure OpenAI` resource and click on create. If the resource is not listed,search in more services.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai_resource-92842411c0ddafaae135b34c41912a35.png)
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai-37b7b02bb1866fa15593cd3b05c8d29a.png)
    
4.  Fill all the mandatory fields in `Basic` section as shown below.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai1-bbc0ffaab958af1d76f5c853c7f59f45.png)
    
5.  Choose the preferred option in the `Network` section as shown below.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai2-dcc6b4f90f67870defca09b22c11faec.png)
    
6.  Enter the tag name if required, it's an option section.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai3-6354a97c5dacff65cced5d66b79db7cf.png)
    
7.  Review the terms and create the resource.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai4-0043c2305c17d1f2b1b551c92b095fab.png)
    
8.  After the creation of OpenAI resource, click on **`Go to Resource`**
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai5-799724a78c305db2efabf912cade268b.png)
    
9.  Click on `Click here to view Endpoints` and get the `Endpoint` and the `API Key`.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai6-f4069f6b4fb1724fa5e632f813b89cce.png) ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai7-573910bfaa49133bc0120b81edc4ad8d.png)
    
10.  To enable to resource, at least one model should be created. Click on the `Alert message shown` 🞂 `Manage Deployments`, which navigates to [Azure OpenAI Studio](https://oai.azure.com/)
    . ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai8-09f30187eac63bf30b77013e30f86fd5.png) ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai9-5a4ba0622a01808817f81569f0f050ea.png)
    
11.  `Create new Deployment` as shown below.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai10-103d1e1e86162b7414c92852440cd309.png) ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai11-6e7b3f156c086d0e48691ef2c39bd23d.png)
    
12.  Get the `Model Name` and `Deployment Name`.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai12-8543e526d19e9bdadf358a481b95dce6.png)
    

Setting up the Azure OpenAI connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/azure_openai_embedding/#setting-up-the-azure-openai-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Azure OpenAI connector in Unstract")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have all the required details, we can use it to set up an embedding profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `Embedding`
    
*   Click on the `New Embedding Profile` button.
    
*   From the list of LLMs, choose `AzureOpenAIEmbedding`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_openai_embedding_unstract-cd2c8627bab74b8d50a3280a668673c1.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector
    
*   For `Models`, enter the LLM model name used in the deployment.Refer step 12.
    
*   For `Deployment Name`, enter the name provided for LLM deployment.Refer step 12.
    
*   In `API Key`, enter the key created in the above section.Refer step 9.
    
*   Leave `Max retries`, `Timeout`, `API Version` to their default values.
    
*   In `Azure Endpoint`, enter the LLM deployed endpoint. Refer step 9.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new Embedding Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with Azure OpenAI Embedding](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/azure_openai_embedding/#getting-started-with-azure-openai-embedding)
    *   [Quicksteps- How to use Azure OpenAI Embedding](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/azure_openai_embedding/#quicksteps--how-to-use-azure-openai-embedding)
        
    *   [Detailed steps to create Azure OpenAI Embedding](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/azure_openai_embedding/#detailed-steps-to-create-azure-openai-embedding)
        
*   [Setting up the Azure OpenAI connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/azure_openai_embedding/#setting-up-the-azure-openai-connector-in-unstract)

---

# Anthropic | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anthropic_llm/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Anthropic LLM, also known as Claude, is a large language model focused on safety and helpfulness. It can generate text, answer questions, and perform many other tasks with a focus on avoiding harmful or misleading outputs.

Extended Thinking Support

Anthropic now supports **Extended Thinking** capabilities with step-by-step transparency!

**Key Features:**

*   ✅ **Enable Extended Thinking**: Enhance reasoning for complex tasks with transparent thought processes
*   🎯 **Thinking Budget Tokens**: Configure the maximum tokens for Claude's internal reasoning
*   🔍 **Step-by-step Transparency**: See how Claude approaches complex problems
*   ⚠️ **Cost Impact**: Extended thinking consumes additional tokens, increasing usage costs
*   📋 **Availability**: Available for **Claude 3.7 Sonnet and newer versions**

Getting started with Anthropic[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anthropic_llm/#getting-started-with-anthropic "Direct link to Getting started with Anthropic")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  [Sign up for an Anthropic account.](https://console.anthropic.com/)
    .
    
2.  Purchase credits.
    
3.  Create an API key as shown below.
    
    ![img Anthropic Embeddings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/anthropic_apikey-248c2885d6b797d7cc13e8ec0644e7da.png)
    
    ![img Anthropic Embeddings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/anthropic_apikey1-d745ea38dfa1cc78e71e66421c67a48e.png)
    
    ![img Anthropic Embeddings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/anthropic_apikey2-ee897fa69f28a659c5dcbd5a289d8ddf.png)
    
    ![img Anthropic Embeddings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/anthropic_apikey3-5da1c9451ba61d614eecbc5fc6d561be.png)
    
4.  Select a plan or claim the free $5 credits.
    
    ![img Anthropic Embeddings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/anthropic_apikey4-b4023d1fab1144c1840defb55dd7ce85.png)
    

Setting up the Anthropic LLM model in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anthropic_llm/#setting-up-the-anthropic-llm-model-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Anthropic LLM model in Unstract")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from Anthropic, we can use it to set up an LLM profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `LLMs`
*   Click on the `New LLM Profile` button.
*   From the list of LLMs, choose `Anthropic`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.

Extended Thinking Configuration

If you're using **Claude 3.7 Sonnet or newer versions**, you'll have additional options:

**Enable Extended Thinking**

*   ☑️ Check this option to activate enhanced reasoning with step-by-step transparency
*   Available for Claude 3.7 Sonnet and newer model versions

**Thinking Budget Tokens**

*   🎯 **Token Budget**: Set the maximum tokens for Claude's internal reasoning
*   💡 **Recommendation**: Start with 3000 tokens for most use cases
*   📈 **Higher Budget**: More tokens = more detailed reasoning but increased costs

⚠️ **Important**: Extended thinking consumes additional tokens, which will increase your usage costs

![img Google Anthropic LLM Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/anthropic_unstract-32f04120a14b81ca05ca932b8240d38c.png)

*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
*   In the `Model Name` enter the Anthropic LLM model. To get the Anthropic model names, refer [Anthropic Models Section](https://docs.anthropic.com/claude/docs/models-overview#model-comparison)
    
*   Leave the `Max retries` and `Timeout` fields to their default values.
*   In the `API Key` field, paste the API key we created on the Anthropic website.
*   **For Claude 3.7 Sonnet and newer**: Enable extended thinking and set your preferred thinking budget tokens
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new LLM Profile for use in your Unstract projects.

*   [Getting started with Anthropic](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anthropic_llm/#getting-started-with-anthropic)
    
*   [Setting up the Anthropic LLM model in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anthropic_llm/#setting-up-the-anthropic-llm-model-in-unstract)

---

# Palm Embeddings | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/palm_embedding/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Palm embedding is a technique that converts text into numerical vectors, capturing meaning and context. Similar text snippets have close embeddings, allowing for tasks like semantic search and text classification.

Getting started with PALM Embedding[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/palm_embedding/#getting-started-with-palm-embedding "Direct link to Getting started with PALM Embedding")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  To get the PALM API key, navigate to [PALM API setup page](https://ai.google.dev/palm_docs/setup)
    
2.  Click on `Get an API Key` and sign in using google account.
    
    ![img Palm Embeddings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/palm_api_key-54fc0fd5467c8af757fb3db7d8cf6c6d.png)
    
3.  Create an API key for the existing project or new project.
    
    ![img Palm Embeddings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/palm_get_key-26dbcda48b598a93505add4bb5957ed8.png)
    
    ![img Palm Embeddings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/palm_api_key1-d7cf749defb7fe9af9f6a9e811516c40.png)
    

Setting up the PALM Embedding model in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/palm_embedding/#setting-up-the-palm-embedding-model-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the PALM Embedding model in Unstract")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from PALM, we can use it to set up an Embedding profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `Embedding`
    
*   Click on the `New Embedding Profile` button.
    
*   From the list of Embeddings, choose `PALM`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img Palm Embeddings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/palm_embedding-bdbc4de703fd2bb79007e560e02c7cab.png)
    
*   For `Name`,enter a name for this connector.
    
*   In the `Model Name` enter the PALM model which supports embedding prefixed with 'models/'.For eg "**models/embedding-gecko-001**". To get the PALM model names, refer [PALM Models Section](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/learn/models#palm-models)
    
*   In the `API Key` field, paste the API key we created on the PALM website.
    
*   Leave the `Embed Batch Size` field to the default value.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new Embedding Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with PALM Embedding](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/palm_embedding/#getting-started-with-palm-embedding)
    
*   [Setting up the PALM Embedding model in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/palm_embedding/#setting-up-the-palm-embedding-model-in-unstract)

---

# Postgres Vector Database | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/postgres_vectordb/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Postgres itself isn't a vector database, but the open-source pgvector extension lets it store and search vector data efficiently, like finding similar text embeddings.

Getting started with Postgres Vector DB[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/postgres_vectordb/#getting-started-with-postgres-vector-db "Direct link to Getting started with Postgres Vector DB")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `pgvector` extension enables you to store vector embeddings and perform vector similarity search in Postgres.To enable the extension follow the below steps,

1.  Check the `vector` extension is available using command (It's only available in newer versions)
    
        SHOW rds.extensions;
    
2.  Install it using
    
        CREATE EXTENSION vector;
    
3.  Verify using
    
        \dx
    
    (or)
    
        SELECT * FROM pg_extension;
    

Setting up the Postgres Vector DB in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/postgres_vectordb/#setting-up-the-postgres-vector-db-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Postgres Vector DB in Unstract")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from Qdrant, we can use it to set up a Vector DB profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `Vector DBs`
    
*   Click on the `New Vector DB Profile` button
    
*   From the list of supported Vector DBs, choose `Postrges`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img Postgres Vector DB Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/postgres-f836042202118fa2a9f948ca4f60aac0.png)
    
*   Postgres Connection string/URL is split in below format.
    
    *   postgresql://\[user\]:\[password\]@\]\[host\]:\[port\]/\[database name\]
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
    
*   For `Database`, enter the Database name
    
*   For `Host`, enter the Host
    
*   For `Port`, enter the Port
    
*   For `User`, enter the username
    
*   For `Password`, enter the password
    

*   [Getting started with Postgres Vector DB](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/postgres_vectordb/#getting-started-with-postgres-vector-db)
    
*   [Setting up the Postgres Vector DB in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/postgres_vectordb/#setting-up-the-postgres-vector-db-in-unstract)

---

# Azure OpenAI | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_openai_llm/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Azure OpenAI LLM is a cloud-based service from Microsoft that provides access to OpenAI's powerful large language models (LLMs). These AI systems can generate text, translate languages, write different kinds of creative content, and answer your questions in an informative way, all within the secure environment of Microsoft Azure.

Reasoning Models Support

Azure OpenAI now supports **reasoning models** (O series models) with advanced reasoning capabilities!

**Key Features:**

*   ✅ **Enable Reasoning**: Allow the model to apply extra reasoning for complex tasks
*   ⚙️ **Configurable Reasoning Effort**: Choose from three levels:
    *   **Low**: Basic reasoning with minimal latency impact
    *   **Medium**: Balanced reasoning and performance
    *   **High**: Maximum reasoning capability
*   ⚠️ **Cost Impact**: May slightly increase latency and cost (typically 20-50% depending on selected level)
*   📋 **Availability**: Only applicable for **O series models**

Getting started with Azure OpenAI LLMs[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_openai_llm/#getting-started-with-azure-openai-llms "Direct link to Getting started with Azure OpenAI LLMs")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Quicksteps- How to use Azure OpenAI LLM[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_openai_llm/#quicksteps--how-to-use-azure-openai-llm "Direct link to Quicksteps- How to use Azure OpenAI LLM")

1.  Submit the [Azure OpenAI Registration form](https://aka.ms/oaiapply)
    
2.  Create an instance in [Azure portal](https://portal.azure.com/)
    .
    
3.  Use Azure [OpenAI studio](https://oai.azure.com/)
     to deploy the model.
    

### Detailed steps to create Azure OpenAI LLM[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_openai_llm/#detailed-steps-to-create-azure-openai-llm "Direct link to Detailed steps to create Azure OpenAI LLM")

1.  Azure openAI requires registration and is currently only available to approved enterprise customers and partners.Submit the [Registration form](https://aka.ms/oaiapply)
     for approval.
    
2.  Once the submitted form is approved, Navigate to [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com/)
     and login.
    
3.  Search for `Azure OpenAI` resource and click on create. If the resource is not listed,search in more services.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai_resource-92842411c0ddafaae135b34c41912a35.png)
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai-37b7b02bb1866fa15593cd3b05c8d29a.png)
    
4.  Fill all the mandatory fields in `Basic` section as shown below.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai1-bbc0ffaab958af1d76f5c853c7f59f45.png)
    
5.  Choose the preferred option in the `Network` section as shown below.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai2-dcc6b4f90f67870defca09b22c11faec.png)
    
6.  Enter the tag name if required, it's an option section.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai3-6354a97c5dacff65cced5d66b79db7cf.png)
    
7.  Review the terms and create the resource.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai4-0043c2305c17d1f2b1b551c92b095fab.png)
    
8.  After the creation of OpenAI resource, click on **`Go to Resource`**
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai5-799724a78c305db2efabf912cade268b.png)
    
9.  Click on `Click here to view Endpoints` and get the `Endpoint` and the `API Key`.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai6-f4069f6b4fb1724fa5e632f813b89cce.png) ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai7-573910bfaa49133bc0120b81edc4ad8d.png)
    
10.  To enable to resource, at least one model should be created. Click on the `Alert message shown` 🞂 `Manage Deployments`, which navigates to [Azure OpenAI Studio](https://oai.azure.com/)
    . ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai8-09f30187eac63bf30b77013e30f86fd5.png) ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai9-5a4ba0622a01808817f81569f0f050ea.png)
    
11.  `Create new Deployment` as shown below.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai10-103d1e1e86162b7414c92852440cd309.png) ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai11_1-480c29c1af38a50f49569d7724e1614b.png)
    
12.  Get the `Model Name` and `Deployment Name`.
    
    ![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_azure_openai12_1-002ea58f882b05fa81aec9066c1bf1b4.png)
    

Setting up the Azure OpenAI connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_openai_llm/#setting-up-the-azure-openai-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Azure OpenAI connector in Unstract")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have all the required details, we can use it to set up an LLM profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `LLMs`
*   Click on the `New LLM Profile` button.
*   From the list of LLMs, choose `AzureOpenAI`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.

Reasoning Models Configuration

If you're using **O series models**, you'll have additional options:

**Enable Reasoning**

*   ☑️ Check this option to activate advanced reasoning capabilities
*   Only available for O series models (e.g., o1-preview, o1-mini)

**Reasoning Effort Levels**

*   **Low**: Quick responses with basic reasoning
*   **Medium**: Recommended for most use cases
*   **High**: Maximum reasoning for complex tasks

⚠️ **Important**: Higher reasoning effort may increase response time and costs by 20-50%

![img Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_openai_unstract-ff0bb3bc906056ebd6cd2c8d00314dfe.png)

*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector
*   For `Models`, enter the LLM model name used in the deployment.Refer step 12.
*   For `Deployment Name`, enter the name provided for LLM deployment.Refer step 12.
*   In `API Key`, enter the key created in the above section.Refer step 9.
*   Leave `Max retries`, `Timeout`, `API Version` to their default values.
*   In `Azure Endpoint`, enter the LLM deployed endpoint. Refer step 9.
*   **For O series models**: Enable reasoning and select your preferred reasoning effort level (Low/Medium/High)
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new LLM Profile for use in your Unstract projects.

*   [Getting started with Azure OpenAI LLMs](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_openai_llm/#getting-started-with-azure-openai-llms)
    *   [Quicksteps- How to use Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_openai_llm/#quicksteps--how-to-use-azure-openai-llm)
        
    *   [Detailed steps to create Azure OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_openai_llm/#detailed-steps-to-create-azure-openai-llm)
        
*   [Setting up the Azure OpenAI connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_openai_llm/#setting-up-the-azure-openai-connector-in-unstract)

---

# Qdrant Vector Database | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/qdrant_vectordb/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Qdrant vectorDB is a high-performance database for storing and searching high-dimensional data like image and text encodings. It lets you find similar items using vector similarity search and filter results based on additional data.

Set up Qdrant Vector Database[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/qdrant_vectordb/#set-up-qdrant-vector-database "Direct link to Set up Qdrant Vector Database")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

info

Qdrant Cloud features a "forever free" managed cloud plan that is plenty enough for us to cover our example use cases.To increase the usage limit check [Qdrant Pricing section](https://qdrant.tech/pricing/)

Sign up for Qdrant Cloud[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/qdrant_vectordb/#sign-up-for-qdrant-cloud "Direct link to Sign up for Qdrant Cloud")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Go to the Qdrant website and click on the `Cloud` icon on the top right. That should take you to a sign-up / login page. If you're logging in for the first time, a new account is automatically created. You should land in a page similar to the one in the screenshot below.

![img Create free Qdrant Vector DB cluster](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_qdrant_vector_db-3c7d022c2291867361f1e9f4550eb7e9.png)

*   In the `Create your first free tier cluster` section, provide `for_unstract` as the cluster name.
*   Click on the `Create free tier cluster` button.
*   You should be greeted with a success message like the one below if everything went well.

![img Qdrant success message](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_qdrant_vector_db_2-241e051d6a4102284a53a81172d01f1a.png)

*   Click on `Continue`.
*   We'll now generate and copy an API key for your new Qdrant account which we'll use in the Unstract Platform.

![img Copy Qdrant API key](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_qdrant_vector_db_3-2877f2d93538fcbb4edd81b3747e5129.png)

*   Copy the API key and keep hold it safely somewhere until we're ready to enter details into the Unstract Platform in subsequent steps. If you lose this key, while you can't retrieve the same, you can always create a new one.
*   Click on `Continue` to eventually go to the dashboard where you can fine details of your newly created free Qdrant cluster.

![img Qdrant cluster details](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/add_qdrant_vector_db_4-bde565f9fea5049c39fa6bd4607f424e.png)

*   Click on the little arrow / triangle to disclose more details about the cluster.
*   Copy the `Cluster URL`. This along with the already copied API key is what we'll need to set up our new Vector DB Profile on the Unstract Platform. Let's do that now.

Setting up the Qdrant Vector DB in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/qdrant_vectordb/#setting-up-the-qdrant-vector-db-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Qdrant Vector DB in Unstract")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from Qdrant, we can use it to set up a Vector DB profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `Vector DBs`
    
*   Click on the `New Vector DB Profile` button
    
*   From the list of supported Vector DBs, choose `Qdrant`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img Qdrant Vector Database Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/Qdrant-77c5caabbf8e115085617d44d10fc5e3.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
    
*   In the `URL` field, paste in the `Cluster URL` copied earlier from the Qdrant console.
    
*   In the `API Key` field, paste the API key we created and copied earlier from the Qdrant console.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new Vector DB Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Set up Qdrant Vector Database](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/qdrant_vectordb/#set-up-qdrant-vector-database)
    
*   [Sign up for Qdrant Cloud](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/qdrant_vectordb/#sign-up-for-qdrant-cloud)
    
*   [Setting up the Qdrant Vector DB in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/qdrant_vectordb/#setting-up-the-qdrant-vector-db-in-unstract)

---

# Weaviate Vector Database | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/weaviate_vectordb/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Weaviate is an open-source vector database that stores both objects and vectors, allowing for the combination of vector search with structured filtering with the fault tolerance and scalability of a cloud-native database.

Getting started with Weaviate[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/weaviate_vectordb/#getting-started-with-weaviate "Direct link to Getting started with Weaviate")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  [Register a Weaviate account](https://console.weaviate.cloud/)
     as mentioned in the [Weaviate documentation](https://weaviate.io/developers/wcs/create-account)
    .
2.  Create a **Cluster** as mentioned [here](https://weaviate.io/developers/wcs/create-instance#create-a-cluster)
    .
3.  Navigate to `Dashboard` 🞂 `Cluster Details` and grab the **Cluster URL**.

![img Weaviate Cluster Details](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/weaviate_cluster_details-c3b9e643e20f5a0113c021588d5f2f6a.png)

4.  To get **API keys**, click the `API keys` button.

Setting up the Weaviate connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/weaviate_vectordb/#setting-up-the-weaviate-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Weaviate connector in Unstract")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `Vector DBs`
    
*   Click on the `New Vector DB Profile` button
    
*   From the list of supported Vector DBs, choose `Weaviate`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img Weaviate Vector Database Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/weaviate_vectordb-04668895640c0d4ed1963aebf631914d.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
    
*   For `URL`, paste in the value of `Cluster Details` copied in the earlier section.
    
*   For `Api Key`, paste in the value copied in the earlier section.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new VectorDB Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with Weaviate](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/weaviate_vectordb/#getting-started-with-weaviate)
    
*   [Setting up the Weaviate connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/weaviate_vectordb/#setting-up-the-weaviate-connector-in-unstract)

---

# Gemini (Google AI Studio) | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_llm/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Gemini is Google's family of multimodal large language models, accessible through [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/)
. It can generate text, analyze documents, reason over complex inputs, and handle a wide range of tasks across text, code, and images.

Google AI Studio vs. Vertex AI

This adapter uses a **Google AI Studio API key** — the simplest way to use Gemini. If you need GCP-managed access with service account credentials, use the [Google / VertexAI Gemini Pro](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_pro_llm/)
 adapter instead.

Getting started with Gemini[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_llm/#getting-started-with-gemini "Direct link to Getting started with Gemini")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Sign in to [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/)
     using your Google account. Google AI Studio provides a free tier that's sufficient for evaluation — you can start without enabling billing.
    
2.  From the left side navigation, choose `API keys`.
    
3.  Click on `Create API key` in the top right corner.
    
    ![img Gemini API Key Creation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/gemini_apikey-b544f6d9618ba434666dabbea93c997f.png)
    
4.  Select or create a Google Cloud project for the key, then copy the generated API key and keep it safe.
    

Setting up the Gemini LLM model in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_llm/#setting-up-the-gemini-llm-model-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Gemini LLM model in Unstract")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from Google AI Studio, we can use it to set up an LLM profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `LLMs`
    
*   Click on the `New LLM Profile` button.
    
*   From the list of LLMs, choose `Gemini`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img Gemini Adapter Selection](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/gemini_adapter_list-099fc4bbb35750b8d2b7574897cfbff1.png)
    
    ![img Gemini LLM Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/gemini_unstract-54ab0028f767b3ee34dc7851a9211c78.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a unique name for this adapter instance. Example: `gemini-group-1`.
    
*   In the `API Key` field, paste the API key copied from Google AI Studio.
    
*   In the `Model` field, enter the Gemini model name. Supported models: `gemini-2.0-flash`, `gemini-2.5-pro`, `gemini-2.5-flash`, `gemini-1.5-pro`, `gemini-1.5-flash`. The `gemini/` prefix will be added automatically if omitted. Refer to [Gemini Models](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models)
     for the full list.
    
*   For `Temperature`, set a sampling temperature between 0 and 2. Default is `0.1`.
    
*   For `Maximum Output Tokens`, set the maximum number of output tokens. Default is `8192`. The maximum possible value differs from model to model.
    
*   Leave `Timeout` (in seconds) and `Max Retries` at their default values unless you have specific requirements.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new LLM Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with Gemini](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_llm/#getting-started-with-gemini)
    
*   [Setting up the Gemini LLM model in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_llm/#setting-up-the-gemini-llm-model-in-unstract)

---

# Ollama | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Ollama is a user-friendly platform that lets you run powerful large language models (LLMs) directly on your own computer, even utilizing your graphics card for faster processing.

Getting started with Ollama[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#getting-started-with-ollama "Direct link to Getting started with Ollama")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ollama LLM offers three primary ways to run and interact with large language models:

*   Local System.
*   Docker Image.
*   Cloud GPUs(Preferred one).

info

Running Ollama in Local System and Docker Image are not recommended, and you can't directly connect the Local ollama instance/ Dockerized ollama in Unstract cloud, you need to use tools like [Ngrok](https://ngrok.com/)
 to achieve this.

### Local System.[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#local-system "Direct link to Local System.")

#### Installation:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#installation "Direct link to Installation:")

*   Download the installer for your operating system (Windows, macOS, or Linux) from the [Official Ollama website](https://ollama.com/download)
     or [GitHub repository](https://github.com/ollama/ollama)
    .
*   Follow the installation instructions. Ollama will automatically detect available GPUs on your system.

#### Download and install the LLM Model:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#download-and-install-the-llm-model "Direct link to Download and install the LLM Model:")

*   Ollama doesn't come with pre-installed models. Choose one from the [Ollama model library](https://ollama.com/library)
     that's compatible with your system.
*   Use the ollama run command followed by the model name (e.g., ollama run llama3). Ollama will download the model if necessary.

### Docker Container.[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#docker-container "Direct link to Docker Container.")

Ollama is available as an official Docker image. Refer the [Ollama official documentation](https://ollama.com/blog/ollama-is-now-available-as-an-official-docker-image)
 for the steps.

### Cloud GPU's.[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#cloud-gpus "Direct link to Cloud GPU's.")

#### Choose a Cloud Provider:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#choose-a-cloud-provider "Direct link to Choose a Cloud Provider:")

*   Popular options include Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud Platform (GCP), and Microsoft Azure.

#### Launch a GPU Instance:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#launch-a-gpu-instance "Direct link to Launch a GPU Instance:")

*   Select a GPU-enabled VM instance type that meets your needs (amount of RAM, GPU type, VRAM).Ollama supports Nvidia GPU's, refer [GPU's section](https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/gpu.md)
     for more details.
*   Follow the cloud provider's specific instructions for launching instances.

#### Connect and Install Ollama:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#connect-and-install-ollama "Direct link to Connect and Install Ollama:")

*   Connect to the cloud instance using SSH or a web console.
*   Install Ollama on the instance following the instructions for your operating system. You may need to install additional dependencies like CUDA Toolkit or ROCm for GPU support. Refer to [Ollama's documentation](https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/README.md)
    .

#### Optional GPU Configuration:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#optional-gpu-configuration "Direct link to Optional GPU Configuration:")

*   Some cloud providers might require additional configuration to ensure Ollama utilizes the GPU. Consult the provider's documentation.

#### Run Ollama with GPU Acceleration:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#run-ollama-with-gpu-acceleration "Direct link to Run Ollama with GPU Acceleration:")

*   Use the ollama run command followed by the model name (e.g., ollama run llama3). Ollama should automatically detect the GPU and leverage it for faster.

Setting up the Ollama LLM model in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#setting-up-the-ollama-llm-model-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Ollama LLM model in Unstract")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have a URL of instance in which ollama is running, we can use it to set up an LLM profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `LLMs`
    
*   Click on the `New LLM Profile` button.
    
*   From the list of LLMs, choose `Ollama`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img Google Anthropic LLM Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/ollama_oss-e54f85dd2b4ccc52957452655507c294.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
    
*   In the `Model Name` enter the Ollama LLM model. To get the Ollama model names, refer [Ollama Models Section](https://ollama.com/library)
    
*   In the `URL` field, enter the URL in which the ollama is running.
    
    *   Try [http://host.docker.internal:11434](http://host.docker.internal:11434/)
         or [http://localhost:11434](http://localhost:11434/)
        
    *   11434 is default port of ollama. Change the port accordingly, if its running in different one.
*   In the `Context Window` field,enter the maximum number of context tokens for the model. Check for the Model context size by choosing any [Ollama Model](https://ollama.com/library)
    .
    
*   Leave the `Timeout` fields to its default value.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new LLM Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with Ollama](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#getting-started-with-ollama)
    *   [Local System.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#local-system)
        
    *   [Docker Container.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#docker-container)
        
    *   [Cloud GPU's.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#cloud-gpus)
        
*   [Setting up the Ollama LLM model in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/#setting-up-the-ollama-llm-model-in-unstract)

---

# Google / VertexAI Gemini Pro | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_pro_llm/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Vertex AI Gemini Pro is a powerful large language model (LLM) by Google, accessible through their Vertex AI platform. It excels in various tasks like text generation, translation, and code analysis, making it a versatile tool for developers and businesses.

Getting started with Google / VertexAI Gemini Pro LLMs[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_pro_llm/#getting-started-with-google--vertexai-gemini-pro-llms "Direct link to Getting started with Google / VertexAI Gemini Pro LLMs")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Vertex AI is provided by Google Cloud Platform.Navigate to [Google Cloud Platform](https://console.cloud.google.com/)
     and signup using google account.
    
2.  Create an Organization and first project. Click on the `APIs and services` from quick access.
    
    ![img Gemini Pro LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/gemini_org_project-cf840a9b2555b97fd4c45e91e909a4ab.png)
    
3.  Click on **`ENABLE APIS AND SERVICES`**
    
    ![img Gemini Pro LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/enable_api-967f227c8ccdd751b91f1439c171441f.png)
    
4.  Search for **`Vertex AI API`** and click on the search result.
    
    ![img Gemini Pro LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/vertex_ai_result_click-253f39967d2ccf905ba61fd21a1193b2.png)
    
5.  Now **`Enable`** the **'Vertex AI API'**.
    
    ![img Gemini Pro LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/vertex_ai_enable-e0d957a19ebfebbb32ffece4c5298138.png)
    
6.  select `Credentials` 🞂 `Create Credentials` 🞂 `Service Account`.
    
    ![img Gemini Pro LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_credentials-ba5f44b937d05ef683be637eb8985614.png)
    
7.  Create the **`Service Account`** as shown below.
    
    ![img Gemini Pro LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/service_account_1-ad33a2579c52ff68864a7d3e11de960e.png)
    
    ![img Gemini Pro LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/service_account_2-89aa1a1eaa37362e858c4397f0929629.png)
    
    ![img Gemini Pro LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/service_account_3-785ab14c1082c57d27d8ae8f801708e5.png)
    
8.  Click on the created **Service Account** link.
    
    ![img Gemini Pro LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/service_account_created-8cbe95d059282b737ddbb821cd4307e3.png)
    
9.  Create the **`Key`** in json format as shown below.
    
    ![img Gemini Pro LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/key_creation-7f32bb28098191343add3769124022c7.png)
    

Setting up the Google / VertexAI Gemini Pro connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_pro_llm/#setting-up-the-google--vertexai-gemini-pro-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Google / VertexAI Gemini Pro connector in Unstract")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have all the required details, we can use it to set up an LLM profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `LLMs`
    
*   Click on the `New LLM Profile` button.
    
*   From the list of LLMs, choose `VertexAI`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img Anyscale Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/gemini_pro_llm-f34a3ab69dddeb64632c210b3ee0cae9.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
    
*   In `Credentials`field, paste in the whole downloaded service account key we copied from the text editor earlier.
    
*   For `Models` field,enter `gemini-1.5-pro`.For more models refer to [Gemini Models](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/learn/models#gemini-models)
    .
    
*   For `Max output tokens`,enter `8192`. For more models and its token limit refer to [Gemini Models](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/learn/models#gemini-models)
    .
    
*   For `Project`, enter the Google project name in which the Vertex AI is enabled (or) Check the `project_id` in credentials json.
    
*   Leave `Max Retries` value to its default.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new LLM Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with Google / VertexAI Gemini Pro LLMs](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_pro_llm/#getting-started-with-google--vertexai-gemini-pro-llms)
    
*   [Setting up the Google / VertexAI Gemini Pro connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_pro_llm/#setting-up-the-google--vertexai-gemini-pro-connector-in-unstract)

---

# Mistral AI | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/mistral_llm/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Mistral LLM is a powerful AI model trained on massive amounts of text data. It can generate different creative text formats, translate languages, and answer your questions in an informative way. It's known for its strong performance on text-based tasks.

Getting started with Mistral LLMs[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/mistral_llm/#getting-started-with-mistral-llms "Direct link to Getting started with Mistral LLMs")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  [Register an Mistral account](https://auth.mistral.ai/ui/registration)
    .
    
2.  Click on `API key`
    
    ![img Mistral AI](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/mistral_api_key-0259d30e73da1f118d91479ff10a7823.png)
    
3.  Complete the `Billing Configuration`, after that `Create API Key` button will be enabled. Create a new `API key`.
    

Setting up the Mistral LLM in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/mistral_llm/#setting-up-the-mistral-llm-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Mistral LLM in Unstract")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from Mistral, we can use it to set up an LLM profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `LLMs`
    
*   Click on the `New LLM Profile` button.
    
*   From the list of LLMs, choose `Mistral`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img Mistral AI](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/mistral_llm-93e7c4954b5ccab80d08d1e97ecef5f5.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
    
*   In the `API Key` field, paste the API key we created on the Mistral website.
    
*   In the `Model` enter the Mistral LLM model. To get the Mistral model names, refer [Mistral Model names](https://docs.mistral.ai/guides/model-selection/)
    
*   Leave the `Timeout` field to the default value.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new LLM Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with Mistral LLMs](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/mistral_llm/#getting-started-with-mistral-llms)
    
*   [Setting up the Mistral LLM in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/mistral_llm/#setting-up-the-mistral-llm-in-unstract)

---

# Amazon Bedrock Cross-Region Inference | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

📘 Introduction[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-introduction "Direct link to 📘 Introduction")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Amazon Bedrock Cross-Region Inference allows you to access foundation models across different AWS regions, providing enhanced flexibility and operational benefits for your AI applications.

### Why Cross-Region Inference is Required[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#why-cross-region-inference-is-required "Direct link to Why Cross-Region Inference is Required")

Cross-Region Inference addresses several critical business and technical requirements:

1.  **Model Availability** – Some foundation models may not be available in all AWS regions. Cross-region access ensures you can use the specific models your application requires regardless of your primary region.
2.  **Latency Optimization** – Accessing models in regions closer to your end users reduces response times and improves UX.
3.  **Disaster Recovery** – Provides redundancy and failover options across AWS regions.
4.  **Compliance and Data Residency** – Choose regions that meet your specific regulatory or data residency needs.
5.  **Cost Optimization** – Choose the most cost-effective region based on pricing or capacity.
6.  **Load Distribution** – Distribute workloads to avoid service limits and ensure performance.

🔗 **[Amazon Bedrock User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html)
**

* * *

🛠️ Step 1: Accessing Cross-Region Inference in Bedrock Console[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#%EF%B8%8F-step-1-accessing-cross-region-inference-in-bedrock-console "Direct link to 🛠️ Step 1: Accessing Cross-Region Inference in Bedrock Console")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### ✅ Prerequisites[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-prerequisites "Direct link to ✅ Prerequisites")

*   AWS account with necessary permissions
*   Knowledge of model regions (e.g., Claude 3 in `us-east-1`, Llama 3 in `us-west-2`)
*   IAM user/role with Bedrock permissions

🔗 **[Setup Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/setting-up.html)
**

### 🚀 Access Bedrock Console[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-access-bedrock-console "Direct link to 🚀 Access Bedrock Console")

1.  Sign in to [AWS Console](https://aws.amazon.com/console/)
    
2.  Search for **Amazon Bedrock** and open it
3.  Switch to the region where your model resides (e.g., `us-east-1`)

### 🌐 Navigate to Cross-Region Inference[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-navigate-to-cross-region-inference "Direct link to 🌐 Navigate to Cross-Region Inference")

1.  In the left menu, click **Model access** or **Cross-region inference** (if visible)
2.  View models and regions in the access matrix
3.  Enable cross-region access via toggle/button if available

🔗 **[Cross-Region Inference Doc](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/cross-region-inference.html)
**

* * *

🔓 Step 2: Request Access to Models[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-step-2-request-access-to-models "Direct link to 🔓 Step 2: Request Access to Models")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### ✅ Enable Cross-Region Inference[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-enable-cross-region-inference "Direct link to ✅ Enable Cross-Region Inference")

*   Enable the **cross-region inference** option in the Bedrock console
*   Accept the terms and conditions

### 📍 Select Regions and Models[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-select-regions-and-models "Direct link to 📍 Select Regions and Models")

1.  Pick primary and secondary regions
2.  Select required foundation models (e.g., Claude 3, Titan)
3.  Click **Request Access** for each model if not already enabled

🔗 **[Model Access](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-access.html)
**

### 📈 Monitor Access Status[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-monitor-access-status "Direct link to 📈 Monitor Access Status")

*   Use the **status matrix** to check:
    
    *   ✅ Access Granted
    *   ⏳ Pending Approval
    *   ❌ Denied

* * *

🔐 Step 3: IAM Setup for Cross-Region Inference[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-step-3-iam-setup-for-cross-region-inference "Direct link to 🔐 Step 3: IAM Setup for Cross-Region Inference")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### ✅ Create IAM User or Role[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-create-iam-user-or-role "Direct link to ✅ Create IAM User or Role")

1.  Go to [IAM Console](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/)
    
2.  Create a user with **Programmatic Access** or role for Lambda/SageMaker

### 📜 Attach Permissions[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-attach-permissions "Direct link to 📜 Attach Permissions")

Use the following policy:

    {  "Version": "2012-10-17",  "Statement": [    {      "Effect": "Allow",      "Action": [        "bedrock:InvokeModel",        "bedrock:InvokeModelWithResponseStream"      ],      "Resource": [        "arn:aws:bedrock:us-east-1:*:model/*",        "arn:aws:bedrock:us-west-2:*:model/*",        "arn:aws:bedrock:eu-central-1:*:model/*",        "arn:aws:bedrock:ap-northeast-1:*:model/*"      ]    },    {      "Effect": "Allow",      "Action": [        "bedrock:ListFoundationModels",        "bedrock:GetFoundationModel"      ],      "Resource": "*"    },    {      "Effect": "Allow",      "Action": [        "bedrock:ListCrossRegionInferenceProfiles",        "bedrock:GetCrossRegionInferenceProfile"      ],      "Resource": "*"    }  ]}

🔗 **[IAM Policy Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/security-iam.html)
**

* * *

🧪 Step 4: Test Cross-Region Inference[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-step-4-test-cross-region-inference "Direct link to 🧪 Step 4: Test Cross-Region Inference")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### ✅ Python SDK Example[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-python-sdk-example "Direct link to ✅ Python SDK Example")

    import boto3import jsonbedrock = boto3.client(    service_name='bedrock-runtime',    region_name='us-east-1')response = bedrock.invoke_model(    modelId='anthropic.claude-3-sonnet-20240229-v1:0',    body=json.dumps({        "prompt": "Explain AI governance.",        "max_tokens_to_sample": 300    }),    contentType='application/json',    accept='application/json')print(response['body'].read().decode())

### ✅ AWS CLI Example[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-aws-cli-example "Direct link to ✅ AWS CLI Example")

    aws bedrock-runtime invoke-model \  --region us-east-1 \  --model-id anthropic.claude-3-sonnet-20240229-v1:0 \  --content-type application/json \  --accept application/json \  --body '{"prompt":"Hello Claude","max_tokens_to_sample":300}' \  output.json

* * *

💸 Step 5: Pricing & Considerations[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-step-5-pricing--considerations "Direct link to 💸 Step 5: Pricing & Considerations")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Bedrock pricing is **per 1,000 tokens** for input/output
*   No extra charges for using cross-region inference
*   Data transfer between regions may incur standard AWS fees

🔗 [Bedrock Pricing](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/pricing/)

* * *

🧰 Step 6: Integration with Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-step-6-integration-with-unstract "Direct link to 🧰 Step 6: Integration with Unstract")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To use this model in **Unstract**:

Extended Thinking Configuration

If you're using **Anthropic Claude 3.7 Sonnet** through Bedrock, you'll have additional options:

**Enable Extended Thinking**

*   ☑️ Check this option to activate enhanced reasoning with step-by-step transparency
*   Available for **Claude 3.7 Sonnet and newer versions**

**Thinking Budget Tokens**

*   🎯 **Token Budget**: Set the maximum tokens for Claude's internal reasoning
*   💡 **Recommendation**: Start with 5000 tokens for most use cases
*   📈 **Higher Budget**: More tokens = more detailed reasoning but increased costs

⚠️ **Important**: Extended thinking consumes additional tokens, which will increase your AWS usage costs

1.  Go to **Settings > LLMs**
2.  Select **New LLM Profile**
3.  Select **Amazon Bedrock**
4.  Configure:
    *   LLM Name: Bedrock
    *   Region: The region in which model is enabled
    *   Model Name & Access Keys
5.  Optional: **For Claude 3.7 Sonnet and newer**: Enable extended thinking and set your preferred thinking budget tokens
6.  Test and Save

![img Google Anthropic LLM Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/bedrock_anthropic-7e6d2639111c2a9122e1739b4e2a8fd6.png)

*   [📘 Introduction](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-introduction)
    *   [Why Cross-Region Inference is Required](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#why-cross-region-inference-is-required)
        
*   [🛠️ Step 1: Accessing Cross-Region Inference in Bedrock Console](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#%EF%B8%8F-step-1-accessing-cross-region-inference-in-bedrock-console)
    *   [✅ Prerequisites](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-prerequisites)
        
    *   [🚀 Access Bedrock Console](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-access-bedrock-console)
        
    *   [🌐 Navigate to Cross-Region Inference](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-navigate-to-cross-region-inference)
        
*   [🔓 Step 2: Request Access to Models](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-step-2-request-access-to-models)
    *   [✅ Enable Cross-Region Inference](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-enable-cross-region-inference)
        
    *   [📍 Select Regions and Models](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-select-regions-and-models)
        
    *   [📈 Monitor Access Status](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-monitor-access-status)
        
*   [🔐 Step 3: IAM Setup for Cross-Region Inference](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-step-3-iam-setup-for-cross-region-inference)
    *   [✅ Create IAM User or Role](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-create-iam-user-or-role)
        
    *   [📜 Attach Permissions](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-attach-permissions)
        
*   [🧪 Step 4: Test Cross-Region Inference](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-step-4-test-cross-region-inference)
    *   [✅ Python SDK Example](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-python-sdk-example)
        
    *   [✅ AWS CLI Example](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-aws-cli-example)
        
*   [💸 Step 5: Pricing & Considerations](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-step-5-pricing--considerations)
    
*   [🧰 Step 6: Integration with Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/#-step-6-integration-with-unstract)
