# Google Palm | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/palm_llm/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Google PaLM LLM (Large Language Model) is a super-sized AI model trained on a massive dataset of text and code. It can understand and generate human language, translate languages, write different kinds of creative content, and answer your questions in an informative way. It's known for its ability to learn new things from just a few examples.

Getting started with PALM LLM[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/palm_llm/#getting-started-with-palm-llm "Direct link to Getting started with PALM LLM")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  To get the PALM API key, navigate to [PALM API setup page](https://ai.google.dev/palm_docs/setup)
    
2.  Click on `Get an API Key` and sign in using google account.
    
    ![img Palm Embeddings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/palm_api_key-54fc0fd5467c8af757fb3db7d8cf6c6d.png)
    
3.  Create an API key for the existing project or new project.
    
    ![img Palm Embeddings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/palm_get_key-26dbcda48b598a93505add4bb5957ed8.png)
    
    ![img Palm Embeddings Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/palm_api_key1-d7cf749defb7fe9af9f6a9e811516c40.png)
    

Setting up the PALM LLM model in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/palm_llm/#setting-up-the-palm-llm-model-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the PALM LLM model in Unstract")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from PALM, we can use it to set up an LLM profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `LLMs`
    
*   Click on the `New LLM Profile` button.
    
*   From the list of LLMs, choose `PALM`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img Google Palm LLM Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/palm_llm-9b95c43d5137cde9af9d87f84665e13d.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
    
*   In the `API Key` field, paste the API key we created on the PALM website.
    
*   In the `Model Name` enter the PALM LLM model. To get the PALM model names, refer Model code in [PALM Models Section](https://ai.google.dev/palm_docs/palm#model-variations)
    
*   Leave the `Number of Output Tokens` field to the default value.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new LLM Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with PALM LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/palm_llm/#getting-started-with-palm-llm)
    
*   [Setting up the PALM LLM model in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/palm_llm/#setting-up-the-palm-llm-model-in-unstract)

---

# Ollama in Unstract Open source | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm_in_oss/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Ollama is a user-friendly platform that lets you run powerful large language models (LLMs) directly on your own computer, even utilizing your graphics card for faster processing.

Getting started with Ollama[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm_in_oss/#getting-started-with-ollama "Direct link to Getting started with Ollama")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can use Ollama in the Unstract open-source software through the following methods:

*   Running it on your local system.
*   Using a Docker image.

### Local System.[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm_in_oss/#local-system "Direct link to Local System.")

#### Installation:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm_in_oss/#installation "Direct link to Installation:")

*   Download the installer for your operating system (Windows, macOS, or Linux) from the [Official Ollama website](https://ollama.com/download)
     or [GitHub repository](https://github.com/ollama/ollama)
    .
*   Follow the installation instructions. Ollama will automatically detect available GPUs on your system.

#### Download and install the LLM Model:[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm_in_oss/#download-and-install-the-llm-model "Direct link to Download and install the LLM Model:")

*   Ollama doesn't come with pre-installed models. Choose one from the [Ollama model library](https://ollama.com/library)
     that's compatible with your system.
*   Use the ollama run command followed by the model name (e.g., ollama run llama3). Ollama will download the model if necessary.

### Docker Container.[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm_in_oss/#docker-container "Direct link to Docker Container.")

Ollama is available as an official Docker image. Refer the [Ollama official documentation](https://ollama.com/blog/ollama-is-now-available-as-an-official-docker-image)
 for the steps.

Setting up the Ollama LLM model in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm_in_oss/#setting-up-the-ollama-llm-model-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Ollama LLM model in Unstract")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have a URL of instance in which ollama is running, we can use it to set up an LLM profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `LLMs`
    
*   Click on the `New LLM Profile` button.
    
*   From the list of LLMs, choose `Ollama`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img Google Anthropic LLM Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/ollama_oss-e54f85dd2b4ccc52957452655507c294.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
    
*   In the `Model Name` enter the Ollama LLM model. To get the Ollama model names, refer [Ollama Models Section](https://ollama.com/library)
    
*   In the `URL` field, enter the URL in which the ollama is running.
    
    *   Try [http://host.docker.internal:11434](http://host.docker.internal:11434/)
         or [http://localhost:11434](http://localhost:11434/)
        
    *   11434 is default port of ollama. Change the port accordingly, if its running in different one.
*   In the `Context Window` field,enter the maximum number of context tokens for the model. Check for the Model context size by choosing any [Ollama Model](https://ollama.com/library)
    .
    
*   Leave the `Timeout` fields to its default value.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new LLM Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with Ollama](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm_in_oss/#getting-started-with-ollama)
    *   [Local System.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm_in_oss/#local-system)
        
    *   [Docker Container.](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm_in_oss/#docker-container)
        
*   [Setting up the Ollama LLM model in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm_in_oss/#setting-up-the-ollama-llm-model-in-unstract)

---

# Amazon Bedrock | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Amazon Bedrock is a fully managed service that provides access to powerful foundation models (FMs) from leading AI providers like Anthropic, Meta, and Amazon. It allows developers to easily build and scale generative AI applications without managing infrastructure.

Extended Thinking Support

Amazon Bedrock now supports **Extended Thinking** capabilities for Anthropic Claude models with step-by-step transparency!

**Key Features:**

*   ✅ **Enable Extended Thinking**: Enhance reasoning for complex tasks with transparent thought processes
*   🎯 **Thinking Budget Tokens**: Configure the maximum tokens for Claude's internal reasoning
*   🔍 **Step-by-step Transparency**: See how Claude approaches complex problems
*   ⚠️ **Cost Impact**: Extended thinking consumes additional tokens, increasing usage costs
*   📋 **Availability**: Available for **Claude 3.7 Sonnet and newer versions**

info

For enabling models in different AWS regions,refer this [cross region inference page](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock_cross_region_inference/)

Getting started with Amazon Bedrock[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#getting-started-with-amazon-bedrock "Direct link to Getting started with Amazon Bedrock")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can choose your model based on your requirements; the following steps only show how to configure Anthropic in Bedrock.

### Step 1: Set Up Your AWS Account[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-1-set-up-your-aws-account "Direct link to Step 1: Set Up Your AWS Account")

### Pre-requisites[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#pre-requisites "Direct link to Pre-requisites")

*   Active AWS account.
*   Necessary permissions: Ensure that your AWS user or role has the necessary permissions to access and use Amazon Bedrock.

### Step 2: Access Amazon Bedrock[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-2-access-amazon-bedrock "Direct link to Step 2: Access Amazon Bedrock")

### Via AWS Console[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#via-aws-console "Direct link to Via AWS Console")

1.  Go to the [AWS Management Console](https://aws.amazon.com/console/)
    .
2.  Search for **Amazon Bedrock** in the search bar.
3.  Open **Amazon Bedrock** service page.

### Step 3: Model Access[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-3-model-access "Direct link to Step 3: Model Access")

info

The **Model access** page in Amazon Bedrock has been retired. Serverless foundation models are now automatically enabled across all AWS commercial regions when first invoked in your account — you no longer need to manually activate model access.

For models served from AWS Marketplace, a user with [AWS Marketplace permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/marketplace/latest/buyerguide/buyer-iam-users-groups-policies.html)
 must invoke the model once to enable it account-wide for all users. First-time users of Anthropic models may need to submit use case details before they can access the model.

Steps to Get AWS Access Key and Secret Key for Bedrock[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#steps-to-get-aws-access-key-and-secret-key-for-bedrock "Direct link to Steps to Get AWS Access Key and Secret Key for Bedrock")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Step 1: Sign In to the AWS Management Console[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-1-sign-in-to-the-aws-management-console "Direct link to Step 1: Sign In to the AWS Management Console")

1.  Go to the [AWS Management Console](https://aws.amazon.com/console/)
    .
2.  Log in using your **AWS root account** or an **IAM user** with sufficient permissions.

### Step 2: Create an IAM User with Programmatic Access[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-2-create-an-iam-user-with-programmatic-access "Direct link to Step 2: Create an IAM User with Programmatic Access")

1.  **Navigate to IAM:**
    
    *   In the AWS Management Console, search for **IAM** (Identity and Access Management).
    *   Click on **IAM** to open the IAM dashboard.
2.  **Create New User:**
    
    *   In the left sidebar, click on **Users**.
    *   Click the **Add user** button.
3.  **Set Permissions:**
    
    *   In the **User details** step, provide a **User name**.
    *   Under **Access type**, select **Programmatic access** to allow access through the AWS CLI, SDKs, or APIs.
4.  **Assign Permissions:**
    
    *   In the **Set permissions** step, you have two options:
        
        *   **Attach policies directly**: Search for and attach policies that grant the necessary permissions to use Amazon Bedrock.
        
        **Basic policy for on-demand models** (e.g., Claude 3 Haiku):
        
            {    "Version": "2012-10-17",    "Statement": [        {            "Effect": "Allow",            "Action": [                "bedrock:InvokeModel",                "bedrock:InvokeModelWithResponseStream"            ],            "Resource": [                "arn:aws:bedrock:us-east-1::foundation-model/anthropic.claude-3-haiku-20240307-v1:0"            ]        }    ]}
        
        **Policy for newer models requiring inference profiles** (e.g., Claude Haiku 4.5, Claude Sonnet 4.6):
        
        Newer Anthropic models (Claude Haiku 4.5, Claude Sonnet 4.6, etc.) do **not** support on-demand invocation. They must be accessed through a cross-region inference profile (e.g., `us.anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0`). The IAM policy must include **both** the foundation model ARN and the inference profile ARN:
        
            {    "Version": "2012-10-17",    "Statement": [        {            "Effect": "Allow",            "Action": [                "bedrock:InvokeModel",                "bedrock:InvokeModelWithResponseStream"            ],            "Resource": [                "arn:aws:bedrock:*::foundation-model/anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0",                "arn:aws:bedrock:*:<ACCOUNT_ID>:inference-profile/us.anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0"            ]        },        {            "Effect": "Allow",            "Action": [                "aws-marketplace:ViewSubscriptions",                "aws-marketplace:Subscribe"            ],            "Resource": "*"        }    ]}
        
        Important Notes
        
        *   Replace `<ACCOUNT_ID>` with your AWS account ID.
        *   The **foundation model** resource uses a wildcard region (`*`) because the cross-region inference profile may route requests to any US region.
        *   The **inference profile** resource also needs a wildcard region (`*`) for the same reason.
        *   The `aws-marketplace` actions require `Resource: "*"` — they do not support resource-level restrictions. These permissions are needed for first-time Anthropic model access and can be removed afterward.
        
        For more information, see [Amazon Bedrock IAM policies](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/iam-policies.html)
        .
        
        *   **Add user to group**: If you have an existing group with appropriate permissions, you can select that group.
        *   **Attach customer managed policies**: For fine-grained control, create a custom policy (such as restricting access only to Bedrock).
    *   Once selected, click **Next**.
        

### Step 3: Review and Create User[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-3-review-and-create-user "Direct link to Step 3: Review and Create User")

1.  **Review User Details:**
    
    *   Verify the permissions and access settings.
2.  **Create User:**
    
    *   Click **Create user**.
    *   After creation, AWS will display a **success message** with the **Access Key ID** and **Secret Access Key**.
    
    **Important:** Copy the **Secret Access Key** immediately, as you won't be able to view it again.
    

### Step 4: Store the Access Keys Securely[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-4-store-the-access-keys-securely "Direct link to Step 4: Store the Access Keys Securely")

*   Store the **Access Key ID** and **Secret Access Key** in a secure location (for example, AWS Secrets Manager, environment variables, or an encrypted file).
*   You will use these keys to connect to Unstract.

Setting up the Anthropic LLM model in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#setting-up-the-anthropic-llm-model-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Anthropic LLM model in Unstract")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an model deployed and the required keys, we can use it to set up an LLM profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `LLMs`
*   Click on the `New LLM Profile` button.
*   From the list of LLMs, choose `Bedrock`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.

Extended Thinking Configuration

If you're using **Anthropic Claude 3.7 Sonnet** through Bedrock, you'll have additional options:

**Enable Extended Thinking**

*   ☑️ Check this option to activate enhanced reasoning with step-by-step transparency
*   Available for **Claude 3.7 Sonnet and newer versions**

**Thinking Budget Tokens**

*   🎯 **Token Budget**: Set the maximum tokens for Claude's internal reasoning
*   💡 **Recommendation**: Start with 5000 tokens for most use cases
*   📈 **Higher Budget**: More tokens = more detailed reasoning but increased costs

⚠️ **Important**: Extended thinking consumes additional tokens, which will increase your AWS usage costs

![img Google Anthropic LLM Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/bedrock_anthropic-7e6d2639111c2a9122e1739b4e2a8fd6.png)

*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
*   In the `Model Name` enter the model which you deployed.
*   For `AWS Secret Key` and `AWS Access Key` enter the keys downloaded from AWS
*   Leave the `Max retries` and `Timeout` fields to their default values.
*   For the `Maximum Output Tokens`, enter the maximum token limit supported by the model, or leave it empty to use the default maximum.
*   **For Claude 3.7 Sonnet and newer**: Enable extended thinking and set your preferred thinking budget tokens
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new LLM Profile for use in your Unstract projects.

### Using Cross-Region Inference Models[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#using-cross-region-inference-models "Direct link to Using Cross-Region Inference Models")

Amazon Bedrock supports cross-region inference, which allows you to access foundation models across different AWS regions for improved availability and performance. With Unstract's Bedrock adapter, you can easily use cross-region inference models.

#### Understanding Inference Profiles[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#understanding-inference-profiles "Direct link to Understanding Inference Profiles")

Newer Anthropic models on Bedrock (Claude Haiku 4.5, Claude Sonnet 4.6, and newer) **require** cross-region inference profiles. They cannot be invoked directly using the foundation model ID.

*   **On-demand models** (e.g., `anthropic.claude-3-haiku-20240307-v1:0`): Can be invoked directly with the model ID.
*   **Inference-profile-only models** (e.g., `anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0`): Must use the inference profile ID with the `us.` prefix (e.g., `us.anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0`).

If you use a model ID without the `us.` prefix for these newer models, you will receive the error: _"Invocation of model ID ... with on-demand throughput isn't supported. Retry your request with the ID or ARN of an inference profile that contains this model."_

#### Configuring Cross-Region Inference in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#configuring-cross-region-inference-in-unstract "Direct link to Configuring Cross-Region Inference in Unstract")

1.  **Access the Cross-Region Inference Console:**
    
    *   In the Amazon Bedrock console, navigate to the **Cross-region inference** section.
    *   Here you'll find the available models with their corresponding **Model IDs** and **Model ARNs**.
2.  **Configure in Unstract:**
    
    *   When setting up your LLM profile in Unstract, use the **inference profile Model ID** (e.g., `us.anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0`) in the `Model Name` field.
3.  **Ensure IAM Permissions:**
    
    *   Your IAM policy must include both the **foundation model** and **inference profile** resource ARNs with wildcard regions (see [IAM policy example above](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-2-create-an-iam-user-with-programmatic-access)
        ).

![Cross-Region Inference Model Selection](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/cross_region_inference-c9a6e6042a5da04fb6c49bba5f751451.png)

For more detailed information about cross-region inference, refer to the [AWS Bedrock Cross-Region Inference documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/cross-region-inference.html)
.

**Note:** All other configuration steps remain the same - you only need to replace the model identifier in the `Model Name` field.

Current Limitations

**APAC Region Costing:** Costing information for models in the APAC region are currently unavailable. This is expected to be fixed in the future.

*   [Getting started with Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#getting-started-with-amazon-bedrock)
    *   [Step 1: Set Up Your AWS Account](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-1-set-up-your-aws-account)
        
    *   [Pre-requisites](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#pre-requisites)
        
    *   [Step 2: Access Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-2-access-amazon-bedrock)
        
    *   [Via AWS Console](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#via-aws-console)
        
    *   [Step 3: Model Access](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-3-model-access)
        
*   [Steps to Get AWS Access Key and Secret Key for Bedrock](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#steps-to-get-aws-access-key-and-secret-key-for-bedrock)
    *   [Step 1: Sign In to the AWS Management Console](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-1-sign-in-to-the-aws-management-console)
        
    *   [Step 2: Create an IAM User with Programmatic Access](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-2-create-an-iam-user-with-programmatic-access)
        
    *   [Step 3: Review and Create User](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-3-review-and-create-user)
        
    *   [Step 4: Store the Access Keys Securely](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#step-4-store-the-access-keys-securely)
        
*   [Setting up the Anthropic LLM model in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#setting-up-the-anthropic-llm-model-in-unstract)
    *   [Using Cross-Region Inference Models](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/amazon_bedrock/#using-cross-region-inference-models)

---

# OpenAI | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/openai_llm/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

OpenAI LLMs are powerful AI systems trained on massive amounts of text data, allowing them to generate text, translate languages, write different kinds of creative content, and answer your questions in an informative way.

Reasoning Models Support

OpenAI now supports **reasoning models** (O series models) with advanced reasoning capabilities!

**Key Features:**

*   ✅ **Enable Reasoning**: Allow the model to apply extra reasoning for complex tasks
*   ⚙️ **Configurable Reasoning Effort**: Choose from three levels:
    *   **Low**: Basic reasoning with minimal latency impact
    *   **Medium**: Balanced reasoning and performance
    *   **High**: Maximum reasoning capability
*   ⚠️ **Cost Impact**: May slightly increase latency and cost (typically 20-50% depending on selected level)
*   📋 **Availability**: Only applicable for **O series models**

Getting started with OpenAI LLMs[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/openai_llm/#getting-started-with-openai-llms "Direct link to Getting started with OpenAI LLMs")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  [Signup an OpenAI account](https://platform.openai.com/signup/)
    .
    
2.  Choose your plan
    
3.  From the side navigation menu, choose `API Keys` and create a new API key.
    
    ![img OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/openai_key-f6cfbd713cef76fdf1e785099a5ffd57.png) ![img OpenAI LLM](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/openai_key1-cecf2965ad18100cfd7f6988dcaf46ec.png)
    

Setting up the OpenAI LLM model in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/openai_llm/#setting-up-the-openai-llm-model-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the OpenAI LLM model in Unstract")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from OpenAI, we can use it to set up an LLM profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign into the Unstract Platform
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `LLMs`
*   Click on the `New LLM Profile` button
*   From the list of LLMs, choose `OpenAI`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.

Reasoning Models Configuration

If you're using **O series models**, you'll have additional options:

**Enable Reasoning**

*   ☑️ Check this option to activate advanced reasoning capabilities
*   Only available for O series models (e.g., o1-preview, o1-mini)

**Reasoning Effort Levels**

*   🔹 **Low**: Quick responses with basic reasoning
*   🔹 **Medium**: Recommended for most use cases
*   🔹 **High**: Maximum reasoning for complex tasks

⚠️ **Important**: Higher reasoning effort may increase response time and costs by 20-50%

![img OpenAI LLM Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/openai_llm-3e1305626de301e39b231a146b61434e.png)

*   For `Name`,enter a name for this connector.
*   In the `API Key` field, paste the API key we created on the OpenAI website.
*   For `Model`, refer to [OpenAI model names](https://platform.openai.com/docs/models)
    
*   Leave the `Max retries`,`Timeout` and the `API Base` field to their default values.
*   **For O series models**: Enable reasoning and select your preferred reasoning effort level (Low/Medium/High)
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new LLM Profile for use in your Unstract projects.

*   [Getting started with OpenAI LLMs](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/openai_llm/#getting-started-with-openai-llms)
    
*   [Setting up the OpenAI LLM model in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/openai_llm/#setting-up-the-openai-llm-model-in-unstract)

---

# Anyscale | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anyscale_llm/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Running your own GPU instance can be a pretty expensive affair. Services like Anyscale or Replicate allow you to be charged for the exact duration of your API calls and let you use open source LLMs on their infrastructure.

Getting started with Anyscale[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anyscale_llm/#getting-started-with-anyscale "Direct link to Getting started with Anyscale")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Anyscale service you need to run LLMs is called [Anyscale Endpoints](https://www.anyscale.com/endpoints)
. To get started, you can do the following to acquire an API key before heading over to Unstract to connect your Anyscale account.

1.  [Register an Anyscale Endpoints account](https://app.endpoints.anyscale.com/welcome)
    
2.  [Generate an API key](https://app.endpoints.anyscale.com/credentials)
    
3.  From the list of supported models they feature in the [Anyscale documentation](https://docs.endpoints.anyscale.com/)
    , copy the name of the model you want to use. For instance:
    *   google/gemma-7b-it
    *   meta-llama/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf
    *   meta-llama/Llama-2-13b-chat-hf
    *   meta-llama/Llama-2-70b-chat-hf
    *   codellama/CodeLlama-70b-Instruct-hf
    *   mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.1
    *   mistralai/Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1
    *   mlabonne/NeuralHermes-2.5-Mistral-7B

Setting up the Anyscale connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anyscale_llm/#setting-up-the-anyscale-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Anyscale connector in Unstract")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Head over to the Unstract console, From the side navigation, select `LLMs` 🞂 `New LLM Profile` 🞂 `Anyscale`.

![img Anyscale Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/Anyscale_LLM-9fff63dabe82c5e5f4e364b7543ccde5.png)

*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector
*   For `Model`, enter one of model names as exactly mentioned in the [Anyscale documentation](https://docs.endpoints.anyscale.com/)
    . Some examples are mentioned above, though more might have been added or removed recently. Please refer to the Anyscale documentation for the latest list. Please note that some names contain a `/`. The names have to be entered as is along with the slash.
*   You can leave `Additional kwargs` empty
*   You can leave `Max retries` at the default value of `5`
*   You can leave `API Base` at the default value present
*   For `API Key`, paste in the value copied in the earlier section from the [Anyscale console](https://app.endpoints.anyscale.com/credentials)
    

*   [Getting started with Anyscale](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anyscale_llm/#getting-started-with-anyscale)
    
*   [Setting up the Anyscale connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anyscale_llm/#setting-up-the-anyscale-connector-in-unstract)

---

# LLMWhisperer Text Extractor-v2 | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/text_extractors/llm_whisperer_text_extractor_v2/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

LLMWhisperer is a text extraction service that extracts and presents data from complex documents (different designs and formats) to LLMs in a way that they can best understand.

**Advantages of using LLMWhisperer v2:**

*   The main benefit is parallel page processing, which results in faster extraction of large documents.
*   Webhooks are introduced to notify once extraction is complete.This will be useful when LLMWhisperer v2 API is used separately.

Getting started with LLMWhisperer Text Extractor-v2[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/text_extractors/llm_whisperer_text_extractor_v2/#getting-started-with-llmwhisperer-text-extractor-v2 "Direct link to Getting started with LLMWhisperer Text Extractor-v2")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

info

When you sign up for LLM whisperer-v2, you will be provided with lifetime free access with a limit of 100 pages per day.

1.  [Sign-up for an LLM Whisperer account](https://us-central.unstract.com/)
     and choose the product as "**LLM Whisperer**"
    
    ![img LLMWhisperer Text Extractor Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llmwhisperer_v2_1-e4d52522f052cf7517f21ca7335421a7.png)
    
2.  Click on "**Get Started**" to start the free subscription to LLM Whisperer account
    
    ![img LLMWhisperer Text Extractor Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llmwhisperer_v2_2-7f4a901e816d4ea5163fe95fd28a1acc.png)
    
3.  Click on "**API Keys**" and copy the `LLM Gateway URL` and `API Key` as shown below.
    
    ![img LLMWhisperer Text Extractor Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llmwhisperer_v2_3-9bb988302eab5e06f475eada8587853b.png)
    

Setting up the LLM Whisperer connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/text_extractors/llm_whisperer_text_extractor_v2/#setting-up-the-llm-whisperer-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the LLM Whisperer connector in Unstract")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from LLM Whisperer, we can use it to set up a Text Extractor profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `Text Extractor`
    
*   Click on the `New Text Extractor` button.
    
*   From the list of Text Extractor, choose `LLMWhisperer V2`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img LLMWhisperer Text Extractor Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llmwhisperer_v2_4-c3bf88f9a5f23874d2e7cffab90707a3.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
    
*   Leave the `URL` field to the default value.
    
*   In `Unstract Key`, enter the `key` created in the above section.
    
*   In `Mode`, choose
    
    *   `Native Text` - To process text based files.
    *   `Low Cost` - To process high quality pdf documents.
    *   `High Quality` - To process Medium/low quality scanned PDFs and Handwritten documents.
    *   `Form` - To process pdf with Checkbox and radio button detection
    *   Check the below image for the comparison of modes ![img LLMWhisperer Text Extractor Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/llmwhisperer_v2_5-28143586fc22b0c7060a32c3a3a5dd64.png)
*   In `Output Mode`, choose
    
    *   `layout_preserving` - To extract the text line by line.
    *   `text` - Keeps the context of the document in place.
*   `Line Splitter Tolerance` - Default value is 0.4 .Reduce this value to split lines less often, increase to split lines more often. Useful when PDFs have multi-column layout with text in each column that is not aligned.
    
*   `Horizontal Stretch Factor` - Default value is 1 .Increase this value to stretch text horizontally, decrease to compress text horizontally. Useful when multi-column text merge with each other.
    
*   `Page number(s) or range to extract`\- Specify the range of pages to extract (e.g., 1-5, 7, 10-12, 50-). Leave it empty to extract all pages.
    
*   `Page separator` - Specify a pattern to separate the pages in the document `(e.g., <<< {{page_no}} >>>, <<< >>>)`. This pattern will be inserted at the end of every page. Omit page\_no if you don't want to include the page number in the separator.
    
*   `Mark Vertical Lines` & `Mark Horizonatal Lines`
    
    *   Both buttons must be in the same state (either both checked or both unchecked) for the operation to proceed.
    *   If checked, it will extract the tables structure(border and internal lines) with dotted lines.Check this buttons if the table is outlined properly in the document.
*   Leave other values to its default.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new Text Extractor Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with LLMWhisperer Text Extractor-v2](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/text_extractors/llm_whisperer_text_extractor_v2/#getting-started-with-llmwhisperer-text-extractor-v2)
    
*   [Setting up the LLM Whisperer connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/text_extractors/llm_whisperer_text_extractor_v2/#setting-up-the-llm-whisperer-connector-in-unstract)

---

# Pinecone Vector Database | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/pinecone_vectordb/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Pinecone is a cloud-based vector database designed for machine learning applications. It excels at storing and searching high-dimensional vector data, enabling fast and accurate similarity searches for tasks like recommendation systems and image recognition.

Getting started with Pinecone[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/pinecone_vectordb/#getting-started-with-pinecone "Direct link to Getting started with Pinecone")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  [Signup a free Pinecone account](https://www.pinecone.io/)
    .
    
2.  By default, the Organization and API key will be generated. Get the API key as shown below,
    
    ![img Pinecone Vector DB Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/pinecone_apikey-e50b86a867ddc0d544c2c7c6c50b2632.png)
    

info

This is just a starter plan, check [pricing section](https://www.pinecone.io/pricing/)
 for plan upgrade.

Setting up the Pinecone Vector DB in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/pinecone_vectordb/#setting-up-the-pinecone-vector-db-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the Pinecone Vector DB in Unstract")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from Pinecone, we can use it to set up a Vector DB profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `Vector DBs`
    
*   Click on the `New Vector DB Profile` button
    
*   From the list of supported Vector DBs, choose `Pinecone`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img Pinecone Vector Database Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/pinecone_unstract-4a6c23966e7258fefaed7618941a1483.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
    
*   In the `Environment` field, enter the pinecone indexes environment name. It's `gcp-starter` in this case.
    
*   In the `API Key` field, paste the API key we copied on pinecone website.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new VectorDB Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with Pinecone](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/pinecone_vectordb/#getting-started-with-pinecone)
    
*   [Setting up the Pinecone Vector DB in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/vector_dbs/pinecone_vectordb/#setting-up-the-pinecone-vector-db-in-unstract)

---

# OpenAI Embeddings | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/openai_embedding/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

OpenAI embedding is used for converting text into numerical representations, enabling machines to understand and process language efficiently in various natural language processing tasks such as sentiment analysis, text classification, and language translation.

Getting started with OpenAI Embedding[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/openai_embedding/#getting-started-with-openai-embedding "Direct link to Getting started with OpenAI Embedding")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

info

When you sign up for OpenAI, you'll get $5 in free credits. This credit limit is more than sufficient to run a simple example.To increase a usage limit,visit [OpenAI Billing section](https://platform.openai.com/account/billing/overview)

1.  [Signup an OpenAI account](https://platform.openai.com/signup/)
    .
    
2.  From the side navigation menu, choose `API Keys` and create a new API key.
    
    ![img OpenAI Embeddings](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/openai_key-f6cfbd713cef76fdf1e785099a5ffd57.png)
    
    ![img OpenAI Embeddings](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/openai_key1-cecf2965ad18100cfd7f6988dcaf46ec.png)
    

Setting up the OpenAI Embedding model in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/openai_embedding/#setting-up-the-openai-embedding-model-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up the OpenAI Embedding model in Unstract")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have an API key from OpenAI, we can use it to set up an Embedding profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign into the Unstract Platform
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `Embedding`
    
*   Click on the `New Embedding Profile` button
    
*   From the list of Embeddings, choose `OpenAI`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![img OpenAI Embeddings](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/openai_embedding-6a3c2a849f6a85a97545a89019289d61.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a name for this connector.
    
*   In the `API Key` field, paste the API key we created on the OpenAI website.
    
*   Leave the `Embed Batch Size` field and the `API Base` field to their default values.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new Embedding Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with OpenAI Embedding](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/openai_embedding/#getting-started-with-openai-embedding)
    
*   [Setting up the OpenAI Embedding model in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/embedding/openai_embedding/#setting-up-the-openai-embedding-model-in-unstract)

---

# Azure AI Foundry | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_ai_foundry_llm/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Azure AI Foundry is Microsoft's unified platform for building, deploying, and managing AI models. It provides access to a wide catalog of models from various providers — including OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, Meta, and more — all deployable as managed endpoints within your Azure subscription.

Getting started with Azure AI Foundry[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_ai_foundry_llm/#getting-started-with-azure-ai-foundry "Direct link to Getting started with Azure AI Foundry")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Prerequisites[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_ai_foundry_llm/#prerequisites "Direct link to Prerequisites")

*   An active Azure subscription.
*   An Azure AI Foundry project. If you don't have one, create a project from the [Azure AI Foundry portal](https://ai.azure.com/)
    .

### Deploying a model[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_ai_foundry_llm/#deploying-a-model "Direct link to Deploying a model")

1.  Navigate to the [Azure AI Foundry portal](https://ai.azure.com/)
     and open your project.
    
2.  Click on the **Build** tab in the top navigation. From the left sidebar, click on **Models**. You will see the **Deployments** tab listing your existing model deployments.
    
    ![Azure AI Foundry Deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/deployments_page-b287c3a6de9f4e0cc218da1b9182a441.png)
    
3.  To deploy a new model, switch to the model catalog by clicking **Discover** in the top navigation. Browse or search for the model you want to deploy.
    
    ![Azure AI Foundry Model Catalog](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/model_catalog-e268603d3dc20291a25d800eef92a224.png)
    
4.  Select the model you want to deploy (e.g., `claude-sonnet-4-6`). On the model details page, click the **Deploy** button.
    
    ![Azure AI Foundry Model Details](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/model_deploy-7f7c0c513432fb96a35efa76272d54a3.png)
    
5.  A dropdown will appear with deployment options. Choose the appropriate deployment type (e.g., **Default settings**, **Custom settings**, etc.).
    
    ![Azure AI Foundry Deploy Dropdown](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/deploy_dropdown-9176f50d703292e8f7e1e0cffef749af.png)
    
6.  Review the terms of use and pricing information in the deployment dialog. Select your preferred options and click **Agree and proceed**.
    
    ![Azure AI Foundry Deploy Dialog](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/deploy_dialog-19837bb24327c5532dce92ed63328cf2.png)
    
7.  The model will begin deploying. Wait for the provisioning state to change from **Creating** to **Succeeded**.
    
    ![Azure AI Foundry Deploying Model](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/deploying_model-e418b20288a79ca92d976893437c290a.png)
    
    ![Azure AI Foundry Deployment Succeeded](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/deployment_succeeded-5e3543a9fa6f20a5067e08f5e04fc551.png)
    
8.  Once the deployment succeeds, navigate to the deployment **Details** tab. Note down the **Target URI** and the **Key** — you will need these to configure the LLM in Unstract.
    
    ![Azure AI Foundry Deployment Details](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/deployment_details-4ac4ec0ce6414613fb506e1042120b16.png)
    

Endpoint URL

The **Target URI** displayed on the deployment details page is the full endpoint URL (e.g., `https://<resource-name>.services.ai.azure.com/anthropic/v1/messages`). For the Unstract configuration, you only need the base URL up to and including the resource domain — for example: `https://<resource-name>.services.ai.azure.com/`.

Alternatively, you can use the inference endpoint format: `https://<resource-name>-<deployment-name>-serverless.<region>.inference.ai.azure.com/` or `https://<region>.inference.ai.azure.com/`.

Setting up Azure AI Foundry LLM in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_ai_foundry_llm/#setting-up-azure-ai-foundry-llm-in-unstract "Direct link to Setting up Azure AI Foundry LLM in Unstract")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now that we have the endpoint URL and API key from Azure AI Foundry, we can use them to set up an LLM profile on the Unstract platform. For this:

*   Sign in to the Unstract Platform.
    
*   From the side navigation menu, choose `Settings` 🞂 `LLMs`.
    
*   Click on the `New LLM Profile` button.
    
*   From the list of LLMs, choose `Azure AI Foundry`. You should see a dialog box where you enter details.
    
    ![Azure AI Foundry LLM Setup in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/unstract_llm_setup-3391120dd87c06c047b91ed81b41ad49.png)
    
*   For `Name`, enter a unique name for this adapter instance. Example: `azure-ai-foundry-1`.
    
*   In the `API Key` field, paste the key copied from the Azure AI Foundry deployment details page (refer step 8).
    
*   For `Endpoint URL`, enter the Azure AI Foundry endpoint URL. Example: `https://unstract-dev-foundry-resource.services.ai.azure.com/`. Refer to the info box above for URL format details.
    
*   In the `Model` field, enter the model name deployed in Azure AI Foundry. Examples: `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `command-r-plus`, `mistral-large-latest`, `gpt-4o`.
    
*   Leave `Maximum Output Tokens` empty to use the model default, or set a value to limit LLM reply length.
    
*   Leave `Max Retries` and `Timeout` fields to their default values.
    
*   Click on `Test Connection` and ensure it succeeds. You can finally click on `Submit` and that should create a new LLM Profile for use in your Unstract projects.
    

*   [Getting started with Azure AI Foundry](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_ai_foundry_llm/#getting-started-with-azure-ai-foundry)
    *   [Prerequisites](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_ai_foundry_llm/#prerequisites)
        
    *   [Deploying a model](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_ai_foundry_llm/#deploying-a-model)
        
*   [Setting up Azure AI Foundry LLM in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/azure_ai_foundry_llm/#setting-up-azure-ai-foundry-llm-in-unstract)

---

# Unstract Cloud Changelog - 2026 | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Jump to version:Select versionv0.159.1v0.158.4v0.158.2v0.158.1v0.158.0v0.157.0v0.156.2v0.156.1v0.156.0v0.155.0v0.154.0v0.153.0v0.152.1v0.152.0v0.151.0v0.150.1v0.150.0v0.149.0v0.148.3v0.147.0v0.146.0v0.143.0v0.140.0v0.139.0v0.138.0v0.135.0v0.132.0v0.130.0v0.128.0v0.123.0v0.118.0v0.110.0v0.102.0

v0.159.1[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-159-1 "Direct link to v0.159.1")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: April 16, 2026

### New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#new-features "Direct link to New Features")

*   **Gemini LLM adapter**: Added a dedicated [Gemini adapter for Google AI Studio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_llm/)
    , enabling direct access to Google's Gemini models via an API key without requiring a GCP service account.

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   **SharePoint OAuth**: Fixed multi-tenant authentication and a duplicate OAuth button on the [SharePoint connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/)
     setup flow.
*   **Connector forms**: Connector configuration forms now pre-populate with schema defaults, reducing setup effort.
*   **Dashboard polish**: Hid the welcome card on the dashboard, corrected a usage count mismatch on the subscription tab, and aligned the dashboard header style with other pages.
*   **Null-response handling**: Improved handling of null responses from upstream services to prevent occasional errors during extraction.

* * *

v0.158.4[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-158-4 "Direct link to v0.158.4")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: March 27, 2026

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-1 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   **Extraction playground cleanup**: Removed the TSV view and download button from the extraction playground for a streamlined experience.

* * *

v0.158.2[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-158-2 "Direct link to v0.158.2")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: March 25, 2026

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-2 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   **Security improvements**: Added input validation and Content Security Policy (CSP) headers across the platform for improved protection against cross-site scripting.

* * *

v0.158.1[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-158-1 "Direct link to v0.158.1")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: March 24, 2026

### New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#new-features-1 "Direct link to New Features")

*   **AWS S3 IRSA authentication**: You can now authenticate S3 storage and [S3 connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/)
     using IAM Roles for Service Accounts (IRSA), eliminating the need for static credentials in EKS deployments.
*   **Usage sync failure alerts**: Automated Slack notifications are now sent when usage synchronization encounters errors, improving operational visibility.
*   **Improved error messages**: Adapter names are now included in error messages, making it easier to identify which adapter is causing issues.

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-3 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Fixed TTL settings and NaN display in [API HITL](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
     review configuration.
*   Improved cookie security attributes and XSS prevention headers.
*   Fixed Azure OpenAI connection errors by upgrading the underlying LLM library.
*   Fixed LLM compatibility issues with retriever-based workflows.
*   Fixed an issue where LLM refusal responses could cause unexpected errors.

* * *

v0.158.0[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-158-0 "Direct link to v0.158.0")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: March 19, 2026

### New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#new-features-2 "Direct link to New Features")

*   **Platform API keys**: Added Platform API key support for programmatic access to the Unstract Platform API. Organization admins can create, list, update, rotate, and delete API keys via the UI under **Platform > Platform API Keys**. Keys provide Bearer token authentication for all Platform API endpoints. See [Platform API Keys documentation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_documentation/versions/v1-platform-keys/)
     for details.
*   **Service account access bypass**: Service accounts now have visibility into and can bypass access restrictions for pluggable apps, enabling automated integrations without manual permission grants.

* * *

v0.157.0[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-157-0 "Direct link to v0.157.0")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: March 17, 2026

### New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#new-features-3 "Direct link to New Features")

*   **High-availability infrastructure**: Added Redis Sentinel and RabbitMQ quorum queue support for improved reliability and automatic failover in production deployments. See [HA Deployment](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_ha_deployment/)
     for details.
*   **HITL reviewer name display**: Documents in review now show the reviewer's name in the HITL queue. See [Human in the Loop (HITL) documentation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
     for details.
*   **Improved HITL permissions**: Streamlined permission checks for HITL operations.

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-4 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Fixed Redis Sentinel and MinIO HA compatibility issues across multiple services.
*   Fixed Agentic Prompt Studio configuration for on-prem deployments.

* * *

v0.156.2[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-156-2 "Direct link to v0.156.2")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: March 16, 2026

### New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#new-features-4 "Direct link to New Features")

*   **Redis Sentinel HA support**: Added Redis Sentinel support for backend, Celery, cache, tool containers, sidecars, and metrics services with automatic failover. See [HA Deployment](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_ha_deployment/)
     for details.
*   **RabbitMQ HA with quorum queues**: Added configurable quorum queue support for RabbitMQ broker. See [HA Deployment](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_ha_deployment/)
     for details.
*   **LLMWhisperer client retry backoff**: Added configurable retry and backoff for LLMWhisperer client requests.

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-5 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Fixed Redis Sentinel stability issues across multiple backend services.
*   Fixed TTL time display to show days instead of only hours.
*   Fixed profile dropdown menu items alignment.
*   Fixed Agentic Prompt Studio build by including prompt files.

* * *

v0.156.1[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-156-1 "Direct link to v0.156.1")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: March 11, 2026

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-6 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Fixed MinIO Operator Helm repo configuration in GCR push workflow.

* * *

v0.156.0[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-156-0 "Direct link to v0.156.0")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: March 11, 2026

### New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#new-features-5 "Direct link to New Features")

*   **HITL default TTL changed to 90 days**: Default TTL for HITL records changed from unlimited to 90 days. See [HITL documentation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
     for details.
*   **HITL Fetch Specific**: Added targeted document review — fetch specific documents from the HITL queue by identifier. See [HITL documentation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
     for details.
*   **HITL rule string in API responses**: API responses now include `rule_string` and display TTL in days. See [HITL Rule Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/hitl_rule_configuration/)
     for details.
*   **MinIO HA support**: Added optional MinIO HA via MinIO Operator for high-availability object storage. See [HA Deployment](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/user_guides/on_prem_ha_deployment/)
     for details.
*   **Azure AI Foundry adapter**: Added Azure AI Foundry as a new LLM adapter option.
*   **SharePoint/OneDrive connector**: Added SharePoint and OneDrive filesystem connector support. See [Working with Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
     for details.
*   **Secure cookie settings**: Enabled secure cookie configuration in Helm values.
*   **HubSpot integration plugin**: Added HubSpot contact property update integration for user journey tracking.

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-7 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Fixed HITL API rules still being evaluated after removal.
*   Fixed secure cookie configuration for improved session security.
*   Fixed connector directory listing cache, including Azure storage expiry regression.
*   Added PDF viewer error fallback when document fails to load.
*   Fixed landing page content clipping on laptop/tablet screens.
*   Fixed consistent empty state when search returns no results.
*   Fixed SharePoint directory listing not returning file details.
*   Improved model pricing cache refresh frequency.

* * *

v0.155.0[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-155-0 "Direct link to v0.155.0")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: March 6, 2026

### New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#new-features-6 "Direct link to New Features")

*   **Vertex AI `vertex_location` support**: Added `vertex_location` field for Vertex AI adapters to configure regional endpoints. See [Google / VertexAI Gemini Pro adapter](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/gemini_pro_llm/)
     for details.
*   **`total_pages_processed` in API response**: Execution API response and worker destination metadata now include `total_pages_processed` count. See [Execution API](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_execution_api/)
     for details.

* * *

v0.154.0[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-154-0 "Direct link to v0.154.0")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: March 5, 2026

### New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#new-features-7 "Direct link to New Features")

*   **CSV, TXT, and Excel file support in Prompt Studio**: Prompt Studio file converter now supports CSV, TXT, and Excel files in addition to PDF and images. See [Adding and Managing Documents](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/adding_and_managing_documents/)
     for details.
*   **HITL sidebar navigation and queue management**: HITL moved to sidebar navigation (removed from profile dropdown), added queue deletion support and nested table row management. See [Human in the Loop (HITL)](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
     for details.
*   **Dashboard metrics system**: Added plan banner, welcome card, and subscription usage tab to the dashboard with metrics worker Helm deployment.
*   **Card-based layout for deployments**: Pipelines and API Deployments listing pages redesigned with card-based layout. See [API Deployments](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/api_deployment/unstract_api_deployment_intro/)
     for details.
*   **Profile page with role and organization info**: Profile page now displays user role and organization details.
*   **Sidebar expand-on-hover**: Enhanced sidebar UX to expand on hover for better navigation.
*   **Documentation link popover**: Added documentation link popover for connectors in the UI.
*   **Workflow deletion error detail**: Deletion error messages now show specific pipeline/API deployment names.
*   **Login page redesign**: Redesigned login form with light-themed right panels.
*   **User account migration**: Added support for handling email changes during OAuth authentication.

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-8 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Fixed workflow executions getting stuck during high-load periods.
*   Fixed Vertex AI pro models incorrectly applying thinking mode when disabled.
*   Fixed Azure OpenAI cost tracking displaying incorrect model names.
*   Added beta tag to Agentic Prompt Studio navigation item.
*   Fixed logs screen pagination in card-based deployment layout.
*   Fixed Prompt Studio tool settings not preserving registry metadata on save.
*   Improved error response when a required tool image is unavailable.
*   Fixed admin access verification on navigation to admin routes.
*   Improved frontend build stability and plugin compatibility.
*   Cleaned up legacy worker infrastructure for better maintainability.

* * *

v0.153.0[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-153-0 "Direct link to v0.153.0")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: February 26, 2026

### New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#new-features-8 "Direct link to New Features")

*   **OAuth product scope**: Added product scope to OAuth authorization requests for login/signup, enabling product-specific authentication flows.
*   **Plugin loading infrastructure**: Added dynamic plugin loading for enterprise components, cloud migrations, and rule engine.

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-9 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Fixed forbidden email error handling during Auth0 OAuth callback.
*   Optimized worker queries and retry configuration for worker base client.
*   Improved dev experience with compose debug override.

* * *

v0.152.1[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-152-1 "Direct link to v0.152.1")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: February 12, 2026

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-10 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Added optimized HITL queue metadata backfill migration for improved performance.

* * *

v0.152.0[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-152-0 "Direct link to v0.152.0")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: February 10, 2026

### New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#new-features-9 "Direct link to New Features")

*   **HITL PostgreSQL migration**: Moved HITL file handling from Redis to PostgreSQL for improved reliability and persistence. See [Human in the Loop (HITL)](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/human_quality_review/human_quality_review_intro/)
     for details.

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-11 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Significantly improved HITL queue performance with optimized database queries and faster queue details loading.
*   Fixed HITL TTL display incorrectly showing "Never expires" when a TTL was configured.
*   Fixed expired records still appearing in HITL queue counts.
*   Improved LLMWhisperer extraction logging for better troubleshooting.

* * *

v0.151.0[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-151-0 "Direct link to v0.151.0")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: February 3, 2026

### New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#new-features-10 "Direct link to New Features")

*   **Agentic Prompt Studio**: New backend and frontend for agentic document extraction workflows (beta). Includes SDK integration, registry support, and dedicated navigation. See [Agentic Prompt Studio documentation](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/agentic_prompt_studio/agentic_prompt_studio_intro/)
     for details.
*   **Profile page redesign**: Added role and organization info display with enterprise design.
*   **Copy-to-clipboard for file execution ID**: Added copy button for execution ID in LogModal.

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-12 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Fixed HITL rule engine nested array flattening for multi-COA documents.
*   Fixed highlight offset issue.
*   Fixed SIGTERM trap handlers for graceful container shutdown.
*   Fixed incorrect constant reference breaking cloud config loading.
*   Improved save UX in Prompt Studio settings components.
*   Fixed dashboard icon always appearing highlighted.
*   Removed success alerts from deployments and pipelines.

* * *

v0.150.1[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-150-1 "Direct link to v0.150.1")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: January 30, 2026

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-13 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Fixed incorrect constant reference breaking cloud config loading.

* * *

v0.150.0[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-150-0 "Direct link to v0.150.0")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: January 22, 2026

### New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#new-features-11 "Direct link to New Features")

*   **1M context for Anthropic LLM adapters**: Added support for 1M context window for Anthropic LLM adapters. See [Anthropic LLM adapter](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/anthropic_llm/)
     for details.
*   **Billing portal rename**: Removed "Past Invoices" button and renamed to "Manage Billing" in subscription UI.

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-14 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Fixed custom data string values being wrapped in extra quotes. See [Custom Data Variables](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/)
     for details.
*   Added custom data support to single pass extraction. See [Custom Data Variables](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/features/prompt_studio/custom_data/)
     for details.
*   Fixed PostgreSQL race condition in concurrent table creation.
*   Fixed memory leak by closing database cursors in subscription usage handler.
*   Fixed resource leaks in platform-service.
*   Added improved logging for retrieval operations.

* * *

v0.149.0[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#v0-149-0 "Direct link to v0.149.0")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Release Date**: January 20, 2026

### New Features[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#new-features-12 "Direct link to New Features")

*   **Stripe billing portal**: Added Stripe billing portal integration for LLMWhisperer subscription page.
*   **Usage billing control**: Added ability to disable usage billing for specific subscriptions in API Hub.

### Bug Fixes[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/changelog/cloud/2026/#bug-fixes-15 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Fixed packet processing final fetch response API.
*   Added database fallback for API key lookup in LLMWhisperer Bridge.
*   Fixed role update failures for existing users.
*   Fixed Ollama adapters failing post LiteLLM migration. See [Ollama adapter](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/adapters/llms/ollama_llm/)
     for details.
*   Fixed export reminder state persistence across page reloads.
