# BigQuery Database Connector | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/bigquery_database/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

BigQuery is a cloud-based data warehouse designed for massive-scale analytics with SQL querying capabilities.

info

For the common steps on adding connectors in Unstract, see [Working with Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
.

Getting started with BigQuery[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/bigquery_database/#getting-started-with-bigquery "Direct link to Getting started with BigQuery")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  [Create BigQuery account](https://console.cloud.google.com/freetrial/signup/)
    
2.  Create project ![img BigQuery create project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/1_create_project-e7608e1d09da1cb2ee834e084341f089.png) ![img BigQuery create project 2](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/2_create_project-2b929afbc2fca3dac22133c12821f989.png)
3.  Create studio ![img BigQuery create project 2](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/3_create_studio-97bef033e4330e0f63dc6b0f1a6c5d58.png)
4.  Create Dataset ![img BigQuery create project 2](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/4_create_dataset-5a04070fad2f2ba804a4bcf9385fc080.png)
5.  Set neccessary permissions if required ![img BigQuery create project 2](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/5_optional_set_persmission-3b59b85928a2d9459c66812824a6abf8.png) ![img BigQuery create project 2](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/6_optional_set_permission-0a0481b3c2ea603738a59d9bef85d886.png)
6.  Create Keys and download Credentials json ![img BigQuery key creation configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/7_big_query_keys-d05d3e1f46f6985633f7e94a172a185a.png)

Adding BigQuery Connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/bigquery_database/#adding-bigquery-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Adding BigQuery Connector in Unstract")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When configuring the BigQuery connector, you'll need to provide the following connection details:

### Required Configuration Fields[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/bigquery_database/#required-configuration-fields "Direct link to Required Configuration Fields")

*   **Name of the connector**: Enter a descriptive name for your BigQuery connector
*   **Credentials**: Paste the service account JSON credentials created in Google Cloud

![BigQuery Connector Filled](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/bigquery-7f5120040322364a1de04f164c6f5813.png)

### Credentials Format[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/bigquery_database/#credentials-format "Direct link to Credentials Format")

The BigQuery connector requires a service account JSON key file with the following structure:

    {  "type": "service_account",  "project_id": "your-project-id",  "private_key_id": "key-id",  "private_key": "-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----\n...\n-----END PRIVATE KEY-----\n",  "client_email": "service-account@your-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com",  "client_id": "123456789",  "auth_uri": "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth",  "token_uri": "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"}

Refer to the [Google Cloud documentation](https://developers.google.com/workspace/guides/create-credentials#create_credentials_for_a_service_account)
 for detailed instructions on creating service account credentials.

*   [Getting started with BigQuery](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/bigquery_database/#getting-started-with-bigquery)
    
*   [Adding BigQuery Connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/bigquery_database/#adding-bigquery-connector-in-unstract)
    *   [Required Configuration Fields](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/bigquery_database/#required-configuration-fields)
        
    *   [Credentials Format](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/bigquery_database/#credentials-format)

---

# Contribute an Adapter | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/sdk/contribute-adapter/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

What is an adapter?
===================

In the Unstract code, every LLM / Embedding / VectorDB / X2Text provider is termed as an adapter. Navigating through the [adapters code](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/tree/main/src/unstract/sdk/adapters)
, there are categorisations for adapters like LLM, Embedding, VectorDB, X2Text etc. Each category has the supported adapter providers nested under it. One can see, for example, support for Azure OpenAI [here](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/tree/main/src/unstract/sdk/adapters/llm/azure_open_ai)
.

What can one contribute here?
=============================

There could be cases where a user will need support for a new adapter in the Unstract system. In such cases, one can contribute the support for the same and begin to use it for their use-cases.

Easy steps to start contributing a new adapter
==============================================

1.  Choose the category of adapter (LLM, Embedding, VectorDB, X2Text, etc.)
2.  Duplicate an existing provider under that category and give it an appropriate name.
3.  Each provider folder has a [src](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/tree/main/src/unstract/sdk/adapters/llm/azure_open_ai/src)
     folder which contains the relevant code to support an adapter.
4.  The [static folder](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/tree/main/src/unstract/sdk/adapters/llm/azure_open_ai/src/static)
     under this contains the [json\_schema](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/blob/main/src/unstract/sdk/adapters/llm/azure_open_ai/src/static/json_schema.json)
     which will define the required fields that should show up in the UI when user chooses to configure this adapter.
5.  Also, there is a [Python class](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/blob/main/src/unstract/sdk/adapters/llm/azure_open_ai/src/azure_open_ai.py)
     for each adapter which implements the [base class](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/blob/main/src/unstract/sdk/adapters/llm/llm_adapter.py)
     depending on the category of adapter. Make sure to put in all the configuration and instantiation logic in this class.
6.  Make sure to generate a [new UUID](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/blob/c48e21f45b92a158ab22d770a67afb5abdc0fe60/src/unstract/sdk/adapters/llm/azure_open_ai/src/azure_open_ai.py#L32)
     for the adapter that is being added.
7.  The new adapter will also need a [logo](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/blob/c48e21f45b92a158ab22d770a67afb5abdc0fe60/src/unstract/sdk/adapters/llm/azure_open_ai/src/azure_open_ai.py#L48)
    . Make sure to copy an appropriate icon for the same in the [Unstract repo](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract/tree/main/frontend/public/icons/adapter-icons)
     so that it shows up appropriately in UI
8.  The newly added adapter can be subjected to unit testing by a [simple test](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/blob/main/tests/test_llm.py)
    . The unit test uses a env file similar to this [sample](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/blob/main/tests/sample.env)
    .

Once this is done, the new adapter should be ready to use. At this stage, a PR can be raised for the above-mentioned changes post incrementing the version.

Things to keep in mind
======================

1.  The existing LLM, Embedding and VectorDB adapters supported in Unstract are using the [LlamaIndex framework](https://www.llamaindex.ai/open-source)
     underneath. So while adding a new adapter, one can always check for the support of this adapter in [LlamaIndex](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index)
    . This will keep the functionality of this new adapter intact with other existing ones.
2.  Some of the Llama-index functions for LLM like [complete](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/blob/c48e21f45b92a158ab22d770a67afb5abdc0fe60/src/unstract/sdk/llm.py#L69)
     and [stream\_complete](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/blob/c48e21f45b92a158ab22d770a67afb5abdc0fe60/src/unstract/sdk/llm.py#L115)
     are wrapped by Unstract functions. While contributing, it is good to be mindful of the same and make suitable changes accordingly
3.  In order to see the adapters in action across the platform, the SDK needs to be built and published in [pypi](https://pypi.org/project/unstract-sdk/)
     with a [new version](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract-sdk/blob/c48e21f45b92a158ab22d770a67afb5abdc0fe60/src/unstract/sdk/__init__.py#L1)
    . Once that is done, the dependent modules/services can use the same in order to pull the latetst changes.

---

# Minio Filesystem Connector | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_minio_filesystem/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

File system connectors like Minio enable seamless integration between applications and cloud storage services, facilitating efficient file management, sharing, and storage across platforms.

Getting started with Minio[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_minio_filesystem/#getting-started-with-minio "Direct link to Getting started with Minio")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To get started, you can do the following to acquire an Access key and Secret before heading over to Unstract to connect your Minio account.

1.  [Deploy Minio server](https://min.io/docs/minio/linux/index.html)
    
2.  Create Access key and Secret ![img S3/Minio Filesysem Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_key_secret-53bc5ba6fa24c9a1031c41495f609796.png) ![img S3/Minio Filesysem Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_key_secret_2-6e983356c54b73bc8ca425f75864ebd2.png)
3.  Create bucket ![img S3/Minio Filesysem Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/create_bucket-c80f5d5f69b2ef0ac61e051dcd9f6c09.png)

Adding Minio Connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_minio_filesystem/#adding-minio-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Adding Minio Connector in Unstract")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

info

For the common steps on adding connectors in Unstract, see [Working with Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
.

When configuring the Minio connector, you'll need to provide the following connection details:

### Required Configuration Fields[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_minio_filesystem/#required-configuration-fields "Direct link to Required Configuration Fields")

*   **Name of the connector**: Enter a descriptive name for this connector (e.g., "My Minio Server")
*   **Key**: Enter the Minio Access Key ID
*   **Secret**: Enter the Minio Secret Access Key
*   **Endpoint URL**: Enter your Minio server URL (e.g., `http://localhost:9000`)
*   **Region Name**: For Minio, leave this field blank (it's primarily for AWS S3)

![Filled Minio Configuration Form](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/minio-connector-form-filled-9a13287294b539988f3ef5bf0be59cc9.png)

*   [Getting started with Minio](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_minio_filesystem/#getting-started-with-minio)
    
*   [Adding Minio Connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_minio_filesystem/#adding-minio-connector-in-unstract)
    *   [Required Configuration Fields](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_minio_filesystem/#required-configuration-fields)

# PostgreSQL Database Connector | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/postgresql_database/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

PostgreSQL is relational databases offering robust data storage, management, and querying features tailored for different scales and use cases.

info

For the common steps on adding connectors in Unstract, see [Working with Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
.

Adding PostgreSQL Connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/postgresql_database/#adding-postgresql-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Adding PostgreSQL Connector in Unstract")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When configuring the PostgreSQL connector, you'll need to provide the following connection details:

### Required Configuration Fields[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/postgresql_database/#required-configuration-fields "Direct link to Required Configuration Fields")

*   **Name of the connector**: Enter a descriptive name for your PostgreSQL connector
*   **Connection Method**: Can be one of `Postgres URL` or `Postgres Params`
*   If the `Connection Method` is `Postgres URL`, enter your PostgreSQL connection string in the format:
    
        postgresql://username:password@hostname:port/database
    
    For example: `postgresql://myuser:mypassword@db.example.com:5432/mydatabase`

![PostgreSQL Connector Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/postgresql-connector-configuration-e1fae4f6a210f28542d7f67f7881ada3.png)

*   If the `Connection Method` is `Postgres Params`, fill in the required fields
*   For `Host`, enter the Host
*   For `Port`, enter the Port
*   For `Database`, enter the database name to connect to
*   For `Schema`, enter the database schema to connect to. Defaults to `public`
*   For `User`, enter the DB user for authentication. Queries will be run as this user
*   For `Password`, enter the password for authentication

*   [Adding PostgreSQL Connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/postgresql_database/#adding-postgresql-connector-in-unstract)
    *   [Required Configuration Fields](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/postgresql_database/#required-configuration-fields)

---

# S3 Filesystem Connector | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

File system connectors like S3 enable seamless integration between applications and cloud storage services, facilitating efficient file management, sharing, and storage across platforms.

Getting started with S3[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#getting-started-with-s3 "Direct link to Getting started with S3")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To get started, you can do the following to acquire an Access key, Secret key and endpoint URL before heading over to Unstract to connect your S3 account.

### **1\. Sign in to AWS IAM Console**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#1-sign-in-to-aws-iam-console "Direct link to 1-sign-in-to-aws-iam-console")

*   Go to the [AWS IAM Console](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/)
    
*   Log in with your AWS account.

### **2\. Create a New IAM User (Recommended)**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#2-create-a-new-iam-user-recommended "Direct link to 2-create-a-new-iam-user-recommended")

1.  Navigate to **Users** → Click **Add User**.
2.  Provide a **User Name**.
3.  Under **Access Type**, check **Programmatic access** (this will generate an Access Key and Secret Key).
4.  Click **Next: Permissions**.

### **3\. Assign Permissions**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#3-assign-permissions "Direct link to 3-assign-permissions")

*   Choose one of the following:
    *   **Attach existing policies directly**: Select **AmazonS3FullAccess** (or create a custom policy with restricted access).
    *   **Add the user to a group**: If you have a group with S3 permissions, add the user to it.

### **4\. Generate Access Keys**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#4-generate-access-keys "Direct link to 4-generate-access-keys")

*   After setting permissions, click **Next: Tags** → **Next: Review** → **Create user**.
*   You will see the **Access Key ID** and **Secret Access Key**.
*   **Download the credentials** (CSV file) and store them securely.

### **4.S3 Region**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#4s3-region "Direct link to 4s3-region")

*   Note down the region of the s3.It will be used to construct the endpoint url

Adding S3 Connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#adding-s3-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Adding S3 Connector in Unstract")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

info

For the common steps on adding connectors in Unstract, see [Working with Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
.

When configuring the S3 connector, you'll need to provide the following connection details:

### Required Configuration Fields[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#required-configuration-fields "Direct link to Required Configuration Fields")

*   **Name of the connector**: Enter a descriptive name for this connector
*   **Key**: Enter your S3 Access Key ID
*   **Secret**: Enter your S3 Secret Access Key
*   **Endpoint URL**: Enter the S3 connection URL in the format:
    *   `https://s3.(your_s3_region).amazonaws.com`
    *   Example: If your S3 region is `ap-south-1`, use `https://s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com`
*   **Region Name** (Mandatory): Enter the region name of your AWS S3 account
    *   Example: `ap-south-1`

![S3 Connector Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/s3_connector_filled_form-a0d07f2b6b3d54ec190dba2ae1e3c50d.png)

*   [Getting started with S3](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#getting-started-with-s3)
    *   [**1\. Sign in to AWS IAM Console**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#1-sign-in-to-aws-iam-console)
        
    *   [**2\. Create a New IAM User (Recommended)**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#2-create-a-new-iam-user-recommended)
        
    *   [**3\. Assign Permissions**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#3-assign-permissions)
        
    *   [**4\. Generate Access Keys**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#4-generate-access-keys)
        
    *   [**4.S3 Region**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#4s3-region)
        
*   [Adding S3 Connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#adding-s3-connector-in-unstract)
    *   [Required Configuration Fields](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/s3_filesystem/#required-configuration-fields)

---

# Redshift Database Connector | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/redshift_database/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Amazon Redshift is a fully managed, petabyte-scale data warehouse service in the cloud. It's designed for high-performance analysis using SQL and existing business intelligence tools, making it ideal for data analytics and business reporting workloads.

info

For the common steps on adding connectors in Unstract, see [Working with Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
.

Getting started with Redshift[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/redshift_database/#getting-started-with-redshift "Direct link to Getting started with Redshift")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  [Setting up AWS Redshit serverless, creating database, and Workgroup](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eq4o26Hpuac)
    
2.  Navigate to `Amazon Redshift Sevice` 🞂 `Workgroup configuration` 🞂 `workgroup name` to get the JDBC url ![img Redshift Database Connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/redshift_1-3bf9cf74618c79779de51d13e0748ff5.png)
3.  Set the rule ![img Redshift Database Connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/redshift_2-cb5d69a42243a714f014944f7db2b673.png) ![img Redshift Database Connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/redshift_4-02b5e92b653aaf2151318f6bf90baada.png) ![img Redshift Database Connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/redshift_5-2aaacce8d3dbad6eacf8ae9712e589c6.png) ![img Redshift Database Connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/redshift_6-43152b1a4efcc3698bc3a9b4bbf5e97b.png) ![img Redshift Database Connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/redshift_7-e31a8f3889ff9a93fe01983f45802622.png) ![img Redshift Database Connector](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/redshift_8-33304fd7085b545ff783e582f3a56e2a.png)

Adding Redshift Connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/redshift_database/#adding-redshift-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Adding Redshift Connector in Unstract")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When configuring the Redshift connector, you'll need to provide the following connection details:

### Required Configuration Fields[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/redshift_database/#required-configuration-fields "Direct link to Required Configuration Fields")

*   **Name of the connector**: Enter a descriptive name for your Redshift connector
*   **Host**: Enter your Redshift cluster endpoint (e.g., `your-cluster.abc123.us-west-2.redshift.amazonaws.com`)
*   **Port**: The port number (default is 5439 for Redshift)
*   **Database**: The name of the database you want to connect to
*   **Schema**: The schema name (default is `public`, optional)
*   **User**: Your database username
*   **Password**: Your database password

![Redshift Connector Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/redshift-connector-configuration-0068dcfe9f7d0ea0120c46e1c810281b.png)

*   [Getting started with Redshift](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/redshift_database/#getting-started-with-redshift)
    
*   [Adding Redshift Connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/redshift_database/#adding-redshift-connector-in-unstract)
    *   [Required Configuration Fields](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/redshift_database/#required-configuration-fields)

# Google Drive Filesystem Connector | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/google_drive_filesystem/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

File system connectors like Google Drive enable seamless integration between applications and cloud storage services, facilitating efficient file management, sharing, and storage across platforms.

Adding Google Drive Connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/google_drive_filesystem/#adding-google-drive-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Adding Google Drive Connector in Unstract")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

info

For the common steps on adding connectors in Unstract, see [Working with Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
.

The Google Drive connector uses OAuth authentication to securely connect to your Google Drive account.

### Required Configuration[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/google_drive_filesystem/#required-configuration "Direct link to Required Configuration")

*   **Name of the connector**: Enter a descriptive name for your connector (e.g., "My Google Drive Connector")
*   **Authentication**: Click **Authenticate with Google** to begin the OAuth process

### Authentication Process[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/google_drive_filesystem/#authentication-process "Direct link to Authentication Process")

1.  Click **Authenticate with Google** in the connector configuration form
2.  You'll be redirected to Google's authentication page
3.  Grant Unstract access to your Google Drive
4.  Once successful, the button will show **Authenticated**

![Google Drive Authenticated](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/google_drive_modal_authenticated-aeaa34eeacfac816b992a15aa6e2ec7c.png)

*   [Adding Google Drive Connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/google_drive_filesystem/#adding-google-drive-connector-in-unstract)
    *   [Required Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/google_drive_filesystem/#required-configuration)
        
    *   [Authentication Process](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/google_drive_filesystem/#authentication-process)

# Run Unstract Platform On Host | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Setup Python services for local development[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#setup-python-services-for-local-development "Direct link to Setup Python services for local development")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[![uv](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/astral-sh/uv/main/assets/badge/v0.json)](https://github.com/astral-sh/uv)

### Prerequisites[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#prerequisites "Direct link to Prerequisites")

*   Linux (Debian/Ubuntu)
    
        apt install build-essential libmagic-dev pandoc pkg-config tesseract-ocr
    
*   Mac
    
        brew install freetds libmagic pkg-config poppler
    
*   [UV](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/)
    

### Manage virtual env[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#manage-virtual-env "Direct link to Manage virtual env")

    # Setup venv for a service../dev-env-cli.sh -e -s <service># Show commands to activate venv for a service../dev-env-cli.sh -a -s <service># Destroy venv for a service../dev-env-cli.sh -d -s <service>

### Install dependencies[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#install-dependencies "Direct link to Install dependencies")

    # Install dependencies in the venv for a service../dev-env-cli.sh -i -s <service>

### Update dependencies[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#update-dependencies "Direct link to Update dependencies")

**NOTE**: All commands assume that you are inside the service dir.

    # Add a dependency to the pyproject.toml of a service.uv add <package_from_pypi># Add a local relative path dependency as an editable install.uv add --editable <relative_path_to_local_package># List all dependencies.uv tree# Generate lock file.uv lock

### Other utilities[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#other-utilities "Direct link to Other utilities")

**NOTE**: All commands assume that you are inside the service dir.

    # Install specific dev dependency group.uv sync  --group lint# Install production dependencies only.uv run --frozen

### Backend[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#backend "Direct link to Backend")

See [backend README.md](https://github.com/Zipstack/unstract/blob/main/backend/README.md)
 for more details on running the backend.

Setup NodeJS services for local development[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#setup-nodejs-services-for-local-development "Direct link to Setup NodeJS services for local development")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Frontend[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#frontend "Direct link to Frontend")

*   Install dependencies with `npm install`
*   Start the server with `npm start`

*   [Setup Python services for local development](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#setup-python-services-for-local-development)
    *   [Prerequisites](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#prerequisites)
        
    *   [Manage virtual env](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#manage-virtual-env)
        
    *   [Install dependencies](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#install-dependencies)
        
    *   [Update dependencies](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#update-dependencies)
        
    *   [Other utilities](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#other-utilities)
        
    *   [Backend](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#backend)
        
*   [Setup NodeJS services for local development](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#setup-nodejs-services-for-local-development)
    *   [Frontend](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/contributing/unstract/local-development/run-on-host/#frontend)

# SharePoint Filesystem Connector | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

File system connectors like SharePoint enable Unstract to read files from SharePoint Online document libraries and OneDrive for Business. It supports Microsoft 365 work/school accounts from any organization.

Prerequisites[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#prerequisites "Direct link to Prerequisites")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   A **Microsoft 365 work/school account** (Business Basic or higher) with a SharePoint Online or OneDrive for Business license assigned to the user.
*   Personal Microsoft accounts (`@outlook.com`, `@hotmail.com`) are not supported.

Adding SharePoint Connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#adding-sharepoint-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Adding SharePoint Connector in Unstract")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

info

For the common steps on adding connectors in Unstract, see [Working with Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
.

### OAuth (Recommended)[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#oauth-recommended "Direct link to OAuth (Recommended)")

With OAuth, you sign in with your existing Microsoft work/school account. **No Azure AD app registration is needed on your side** — the Unstract platform handles this.

1.  Select **OAuth (Recommended)** as the Authentication Type.
2.  Click **Sign in with Microsoft** and sign in with your work/school account.
3.  Grant consent when prompted ("Unstract wants to access your files").
4.  The button will switch to **Authenticated**.

note

Your organization's Azure AD admin may need to grant admin consent before you can sign in. If you see a "Need admin approval" screen, contact your IT admin.

![SharePoint OAuth connector form](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/unstract_oauth_form-0431ec485e0dd877874544537b75442d.png)

### Client Credentials[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#client-credentials "Direct link to Client Credentials")

With Client Credentials, you authenticate using an Azure AD app registration that you manage. This is useful for service accounts and automation scenarios where no user sign-in is involved.

You will need to [set up an Azure AD app registration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#azure-ad-app-setup-for-client-credentials)
 first, then provide:

*   **Client ID (Application ID)**: from your app registration
*   **Tenant ID**: from your app registration
*   **Client Secret**: from your app registration
*   **User Email**: required **only** for OneDrive with Client Credentials (not needed for SharePoint)

![SharePoint Client Credentials connector form](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/unstract_client_credentials_form-74119d750714e7a27b82caebb1e11563.png)

### Configuration Fields[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#configuration-fields "Direct link to Configuration Fields")

*   **Name of the connector**: A descriptive name to identify this connector.
*   **Site URL**: SharePoint site URL (e.g., `https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/mysite`). Leave empty for OneDrive for Business root.
*   **Drive ID (Optional)**: Specific Drive or Document Library ID. Leave empty to use the default drive.
*   **Authentication Type**: Select **OAuth (Recommended)** or **Client Credentials**.

Azure AD App Setup for Client Credentials[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#azure-ad-app-setup-for-client-credentials "Direct link to Azure AD App Setup for Client Credentials")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

info

This section is only needed if you are using **Client Credentials** authentication. If you are using **OAuth**, skip this section entirely.

### **1\. Sign in to the Microsoft Entra admin center**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#1-sign-in-to-the-microsoft-entra-admin-center "Direct link to 1-sign-in-to-the-microsoft-entra-admin-center")

*   Go to the [Microsoft Entra admin center](https://entra.microsoft.com/)
    .
*   Sign in with an account that can create app registrations in your tenant.

### **2\. Open your app registration**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#2-open-your-app-registration "Direct link to 2-open-your-app-registration")

*   In the left navigation, go to **Entra ID → App registrations**.
*   Either create a new registration (**\+ New registration**) or open the existing app you want Unstract to use.

### **3\. Copy the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#3-copy-the-application-client-id-and-directory-tenant-id "Direct link to 3-copy-the-application-client-id-and-directory-tenant-id")

On the app's **Overview** page, copy the following values from the **Essentials** panel:

*   **Application (client) ID**
*   **Directory (tenant) ID**

![App registration Overview](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/app_overview-8b6deb1f69b1a9bccc05411365883a41.png)

### **4\. Create a client secret**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#4-create-a-client-secret "Direct link to 4-create-a-client-secret")

*   Click **Certificates & secrets** in the left navigation.
*   On the **Client secrets** tab, click **\+ New client secret**.
*   Provide a description and expiry, then click **Add**.
*   Copy the new secret's **Value** immediately — Azure masks it once you navigate away. If lost, delete it and create a new one.

![Client secrets](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/client_secrets-5c8daca84db9f27deb853a75adf1e677.png)

### **5\. Add Microsoft Graph API permissions**[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#5-add-microsoft-graph-api-permissions "Direct link to 5-add-microsoft-graph-api-permissions")

*   Click **API permissions → + Add a permission → Microsoft Graph → Application permissions**.
*   Add: `Files.ReadWrite.All`, `Sites.ReadWrite.All`
*   Click **Grant admin consent for `<tenant>`**. All rows should then show a green check in the **Status** column.

![API permissions with admin consent granted](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/api_permissions-416fd05feff6aa813f7b9e5a49737b4b.png)

*   [Prerequisites](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#prerequisites)
    
*   [Adding SharePoint Connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#adding-sharepoint-connector-in-unstract)
    *   [OAuth (Recommended)](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#oauth-recommended)
        
    *   [Client Credentials](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#client-credentials)
        
    *   [Configuration Fields](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#configuration-fields)
        
*   [Azure AD App Setup for Client Credentials](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#azure-ad-app-setup-for-client-credentials)
    *   [**1\. Sign in to the Microsoft Entra admin center**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#1-sign-in-to-the-microsoft-entra-admin-center)
        
    *   [**2\. Open your app registration**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#2-open-your-app-registration)
        
    *   [**3\. Copy the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#3-copy-the-application-client-id-and-directory-tenant-id)
        
    *   [**4\. Create a client secret**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#4-create-a-client-secret)
        
    *   [**5\. Add Microsoft Graph API permissions**](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/sharepoint_filesystem/#5-add-microsoft-graph-api-permissions)

---

# Dropbox Filesystem Connector | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/dropbox_filesystem/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Dropbox](https://www/dropbox.com)
 is a cloud-based file hosting service that offers cloud file hosting and other services.

note

**NOTE:** Please note that this is currently in `alpha` and is limited by the access token's short expiry time (4 hours). Does not support OAuth yet.

Generating a Dropbox API Key[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/dropbox_filesystem/#generating-a-dropbox-api-key "Direct link to Generating a Dropbox API Key")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To connect to Dropbox, you'll first need to generate a unique Dropbox API key:

1.  Navigate to the [Dropbox App Console](https://www.dropbox.com/developers/apps)
     and click **Create app**
2.  Complete the form, making sure to choose the following options:

*   **API**: Scoped Access
*   **Access Type**:
    *   **App Folder** will restrict Unstract to only a single folder and its subdirectories.
    *   **Full Dropbox** will allow Unstract to read the contents of all folders

3.  Name your app, then choose **Create app**
4.  In your new app's **Settings** page, click the **Permissions** tab
5.  Choose the following scopes, making sure to **Submit** your changes

*   `files.metadata.read`
    
*   `files.content.read`
    
    ![img Dropbox App Scopes](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/dropbox_api_scopes-6355d9653015f36cb793ba12c2b25d47.png)
    

6.  After saving your changes, return to the **Settings** tab.
    
7.  Finally, press the **Generate** button located in the **Generated Access Token** section of `OAuth2`
    
8.  Copy the generated key to your clipboard. It should look like this:
    
    `sl.xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx`
    

**Note:** The Dropbox access token must be re-generated any time there is a change to the app's `Scope`.

Adding Dropbox Connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/dropbox_filesystem/#adding-dropbox-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Adding Dropbox Connector in Unstract")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

info

For the common steps on adding connectors in Unstract, see [Working with Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
.

When configuring the Dropbox connector, you'll need to provide the following connection details:

### Required Configuration Fields[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/dropbox_filesystem/#required-configuration-fields "Direct link to Required Configuration Fields")

*   **Name of the connector**: Provide a descriptive name for your connector
*   **Access Token**: Paste your Dropbox API Key (access token) from the app you created above

![Dropbox Connector Configuration Form](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/dropbox_modal_filled-3a3db720af86203990b6c1288a89ec9e.png)

### Need a hand?[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/dropbox_filesystem/#need-a-hand "Direct link to Need a hand?")

Talk to us on [slack](https://join.slack.com/t/unstract/shared_invite/zt-2fyv2bkye-RVfk2kxZQbB9PDEizOMh1g)
! We're here to help :)

*   [Generating a Dropbox API Key](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/dropbox_filesystem/#generating-a-dropbox-api-key)
    
*   [Adding Dropbox Connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/dropbox_filesystem/#adding-dropbox-connector-in-unstract)
    *   [Required Configuration Fields](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/dropbox_filesystem/#required-configuration-fields)
        
    *   [Need a hand?](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/dropbox_filesystem/#need-a-hand)

---

# MSSQL Database Connector | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/mssql_database/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

MSSQL (Microsoft SQL Server) is a relational database management system developed by Microsoft, designed to store and retrieve data for various applications. It supports a wide range of data types, transactions, and analytics, making it suitable for enterprise-level database needs.

info

For the common steps on adding connectors in Unstract, see [Working with Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
.

Getting started with MSSQL[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/mssql_database/#getting-started-with-mssql "Direct link to Getting started with MSSQL")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  [Sign up or Sign in for an Azure account](https://portal.azure.com/)
    .
    
2.  Search for `SQL Databases` in the global search and click on the search result and click on `create`.
    
    ![img mssql create project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/mssql1-edf6c0025fc2b13827808abde6e7ad2f.png) ![img mssql create project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/mssql2-cff9985486dfbef7b20c6ba82d400213.png)
    
3.  Now click on `create new` button below server field to create a new database server.
    
    ![img mssql create project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/mssql3-391ca70a0971a2f728fe0d3961f1435c.png)
    
4.  Enter the **Server Name**, **Location**, and choose the **SQL authentication** as login type and finish the form.
    
    ![img mssql create project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/mssql4-6d00041f49189b1a6b9aba300fc7cb93.png)
    
5.  Now choose the **Database Name** and choose **Environment** as `Production` and submit it. For more customization click on next pages before submitting.
    
    ![img mssql create project](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/mssql5-7fcfcf876c1324184a42fe9641bae0d7.png)
    
6.  Wait for the database to get deployed.
    

Adding MSSQL Connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/mssql_database/#adding-mssql-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Adding MSSQL Connector in Unstract")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When configuring the MSSQL connector, you'll need to provide the following connection details:

### Required Configuration Fields[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/mssql_database/#required-configuration-fields "Direct link to Required Configuration Fields")

*   **Name of the connector**: Enter a descriptive name for your MSSQL connector
*   **Server**: Enter your MSSQL server hostname or IP address (for Azure SQL Database, use the format `server-name.database.windows.net`)
*   **Port**: The port number (default is 1433 for MSSQL)
*   **Database**: The name of the database you want to connect to
*   **User**: Your database username
*   **Password**: Your database password

![MSSQL Connector Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/mssql-connector-filled-form-d46de9223c9202ae9862ea575ee358fd.png)

### Need a hand?[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/mssql_database/#need-a-hand "Direct link to Need a hand?")

Talk to us on [Slack](https://join.slack.com/t/unstract/shared_invite/zt-2fyv2bkye-RVfk2kxZQbB9PDEizOMh1g)
! We're here to help :)

*   [Getting started with MSSQL](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/mssql_database/#getting-started-with-mssql)
    
*   [Adding MSSQL Connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/mssql_database/#adding-mssql-connector-in-unstract)
    *   [Required Configuration Fields](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/mssql_database/#required-configuration-fields)
        
    *   [Need a hand?](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/databases/mssql_database/#need-a-hand)

---

# Azure Cloud Storage Connector | Unstract Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/azure_cloud_storage/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Azure Cloud Storage](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-in/products/category/storage)
 is a scalable, secure service for storing large amounts of unstructured data, including text, images, and video. It supports various storage types like blobs, files, queues, and tables, making it versatile for different storage needs.

Getting started with Azure Cloud Storage[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/azure_cloud_storage/#getting-started-with-azure-cloud-storage "Direct link to Getting started with Azure Cloud Storage")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  [Sign up or Sign in for an Azure account](https://portal.azure.com/)
    .
    
2.  Search for `Storage account` in the global search and click on the search result.
    
    ![img Azure cloud Scopes](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_cloud1-fef73130a2ff90d3165ba21e06ca6d76.png)
    
3.  Click on `Create Storage Account`
    
    ![img Azure cloud Scopes](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_cloud2-17404338698afe12cae5470fe95ddea3.png)
    
4.  Enter the `Resource Group Name` and the `Storage Name`. If you want more customization, then visit the remaining tabs, or else just click `Review + Create`
    
    ![img Azure cloud Scopes](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_cloud4-275b989f319dd987f9f3dfa347d3c41e.png)
    
5.  Verify the selected options and click on `Create`
    
    ![img Azure cloud Scopes](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_cloud5-f7ce0bde4443d58c8b79f2e8991adef1.png)
    
6.  Now create the container in the storage account by doing the following, Click **Data Storage**\-->**Containers**\-->**+Container**\-->Enter **Name**\-->**Save** it.
    
    ![img Azure cloud Scopes](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_cloud6-cb2799b284ca47ccc8ae4db71cd224bf.png) ![img Azure cloud Scopes](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_cloud7-867535308b65f898c179466f49990c6f.png)
    
7.  Upload the required files in the container.
    
    ![img Azure cloud Scopes](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_cloud8-55d8bdff9841a6d096b581a4426d7418.png)
    
8.  To get the Access key,navigate to **Security+Networking**\-->**Access Keys** and click on show and copy the key.
    
    ![img Azure cloud Scopes](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_cloud9-7b75cf5ebb7bde17e42f8220af0d7535.png)
    

Adding Azure Cloud Storage Connector in Unstract[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/azure_cloud_storage/#adding-azure-cloud-storage-connector-in-unstract "Direct link to Adding Azure Cloud Storage Connector in Unstract")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

info

For the common steps on adding connectors in Unstract, see [Working with Connectors](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/)
.

When configuring the Azure Cloud Storage connector, you'll need to provide the following connection details:

### Required Configuration Fields[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/azure_cloud_storage/#required-configuration-fields "Direct link to Required Configuration Fields")

*   **Name of the connector**: Enter a descriptive name (e.g., "Unstract's Azure cloud storage")
*   **Account Name**: Enter the `Storage Name` you created in step 4 of the Azure setup above
*   **Access Key**: Paste the access key you copied in step 8 of the Azure setup above
*   **Bucket Name**: Enter the container name you created in step 6 of the Azure setup above

![Azure Cloud Storage Configuration](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/assets/images/azure_cloud10-fbef80994655ef59d44dc6ff8f36b868.png)

### Need a hand?[​](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/azure_cloud_storage/#need-a-hand "Direct link to Need a hand?")

Talk to us on [Slack](https://join.slack.com/t/unstract/shared_invite/zt-2fyv2bkye-RVfk2kxZQbB9PDEizOMh1g)
! We're here to help :)

*   [Getting started with Azure Cloud Storage](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/azure_cloud_storage/#getting-started-with-azure-cloud-storage)
    
*   [Adding Azure Cloud Storage Connector in Unstract](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/azure_cloud_storage/#adding-azure-cloud-storage-connector-in-unstract)
    *   [Required Configuration Fields](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/azure_cloud_storage/#required-configuration-fields)
        
    *   [Need a hand?](https://docs.unstract.com/unstract/unstract_platform/connectors/filesystems/azure_cloud_storage/#need-a-hand)

---
